STG-03-04_LTS.name: Little Tennis Stars (Ages 3–4)
STG-03-04_LTS.mix: Play-based 80–90% · Skill exposure 10–20% · No formal competition
STG-03-04_LTS.tech: Racket hold comfort; tap/bounce; push/roll; simple forehand/backhand shapes; tracking and contact games.
STG-03-04_LTS.tact: Awareness of space; send ball to big target; start/stop on cue; simple 'aim to the middle'.
STG-03-04_LTS.phys: Run/jump/hop/skip; balance; throw/catch; rotate; stop-start control.
STG-03-04_LTS.mental: Confidence, curiosity, following 1-step instructions, turn-taking, celebrating effort.

STG-05-07_RED.name: Red Ball (Ages 5–7)
STG-05-07_RED.mix: Play-heavy: ~70% games · ~30% skill building · Competition exposure 0–10%
STG-05-07_RED.tech: Ready position; forehand/backhand to big target; serve/overhand throw pattern via games; simple volley catch/punch.
STG-05-07_RED.tact: Aim to big targets (middle/deep); recover to home base; 'crosscourt safer' concept.
STG-05-07_RED.phys: Agility, balance, coordination; side shuffle; crossover intro; stop-start; reaction games.
STG-05-07_RED.mental: Enjoyment, 'next ball' reset, effort praise, simple routines (bounce-breath).

STG-07-09_ORANGE.name: Orange Ball (Ages 7–9)
STG-07-09_ORANGE.mix: Balanced games + skill: ~50% play · ~50% practice · Competition 10–20%
STG-07-09_ORANGE.tech: Forehand/backhand with consistent contact; topspin feel; serve rhythm; return ready + split; first volley basics.
STG-07-09_ORANGE.tact: Crosscourt default; short-ball recognition; simple defense (height/depth to reset); home base positioning.
STG-07-09_ORANGE.phys: Footwork patterns (shuffle/crossover); coordination ladders; reaction; acceleration/deceleration.
STG-07-09_ORANGE.mental: Process goals; basic reset routine; learning language; confidence through challenge calibration.

STG-09-11_GREEN.name: Green Dot (Ages 9–11)
STG-09-11_GREEN.tech: Topspin consistency; serve fundamentals (targets); return depth; approach + first volley; overhead basics.
STG-09-11_GREEN.tact: Crosscourt control; change direction triggers; defense-to-neutral; basic serve+1/return+1.
STG-09-11_GREEN.phys: Agility + speed base; coordination; intro strength with bodyweight/medball; injury prevention habits.
STG-09-11_GREEN.mental: Process goals; reset routine compliance; confidence tracking; sportsmanship habits.

STG-11-14_YELLOW.name: Youth Development Yellow Ball (Ages 11–14)
STG-11-14_YELLOW.tech: Serve development (targets + 2nd serve shape); return neutralization; groundstroke pace+margin; transition skills.
STG-11-14_YELLOW.tact: Offense/neutral/defense recognition; short-ball attack; defense-to-offense transitions; basic scouting and plan A/B.
STG-11-14_YELLOW.phys: Speed/agility progressions; strength fundamentals; recovery education (sleep, hydration).
STG-11-14_YELLOW.mental: Pre-point routine; between-point reset; emotional regulation; MPI tracking intro.

STG-14-18_YELLOW.name: Youth Development Yellow Ball (Ages 14–18)
STG-14-18_YELLOW.tech: Serve weaponization; aggressive/neutral returns; reliable attacking ball; defensive tools (height/spin).
STG-14-18_YELLOW.tact: Plan A/B/C; scoreboard management; adjustments (return position, patterns); opponent exploitation.
STG-14-18_YELLOW.phys: Periodized strength/power; mobility; injury prevention; recovery protocols.
STG-14-18_YELLOW.mental: Pressure routines (breath, cue, quiet eye); post-match reflection; self-regulation; identity separation from results.

STG-09-13_HP.name: High-Performance Yellow Ball Academy (Ages 9–13)
STG-09-13_HP.mix: High volume skill + patterns; competition integrated weekly
STG-09-13_HP.tech: Advanced groundstrokes; serve/return; transition and finishing skills; shot variation.
STG-09-13_HP.tact: Pattern library; opponent targeting; defensive transitions; match analytics.
STG-09-13_HP.phys: Agility/speed/strength with safe load; readiness tracking.
STG-09-13_HP.mental: Deliberate routine training; pressure tolerance; reflection and journaling.

STG-13P_CBI.name: College-Bound Intensive (Ages 13+, UTR 7+)
STG-13P_CBI.mix: Serve/return + patterns dominate; match simulation daily; data-driven
STG-13P_CBI.tech: Serve weaponization; return aggression; first 4 shots efficiency; net finishing.
STG-13P_CBI.tact: Opponent scouting; plan A/B; doubles systems; scoreboard management.
STG-13P_CBI.phys: Strength & conditioning; injury prevention; recovery and peaking strategies.
STG-13P_CBI.mental: Pressure routines; resilience; autonomy; competition reflection.

STG-12-18_FT.name: Full-Time College & Pro Pathway (Ages 12–18)
STG-12-18_FT.mix: Daily tennis + S&C + mental + academics; quarterly evaluations
STG-12-18_FT.tech: Automated technique under pressure; adaptability; serve/return; specialty shots.
STG-12-18_FT.tact: Advanced patterns; surface/style adaptation; scouting; match analytics.
STG-12-18_FT.phys: Periodized strength/power; mobility; recovery; injury prevention; load management.
STG-12-18_FT.mental: Sports psych skills; attention control; resilience; leadership and professionalism.

STG-ADULT_BEGINNER.name: Adult Beginner (USTA ~2.0–2.5)
STG-ADULT_BEGINNER.mix: Skill building + simple games; low injury risk; high enjoyment
STG-ADULT_BEGINNER.tech: Reliable contact; simple swing shapes; basic serve motion; volley fundamentals.
STG-ADULT_BEGINNER.tact: Big targets; keep ball in play; recover to home base; simple doubles positioning.
STG-ADULT_BEGINNER.phys: Mobility, balance, low-impact agility; injury-prevention warm-up habits.
STG-ADULT_BEGINNER.mental: Reduce fear of missing; focus on process; supportive environment.

STG-ADULT_COMP.name: Adult Competitive (USTA ~3.0+)
STG-ADULT_COMP.mix: High-leverage practice: serve/return + patterns + pressure points; injury prevention priority
STG-ADULT_COMP.tech: Serve placement; return depth; forehand/backhand margin; net skills for singles/doubles.
STG-ADULT_COMP.tact: 2 core patterns; error management; exploit weaknesses; doubles communication.
STG-ADULT_COMP.phys: Strength/mobility for longevity; load management; warm-up and recovery compliance.
STG-ADULT_COMP.mental: Between-point reset; self-talk hygiene; confidence from KPI tracking.

DRL-ADULT-001.n: 2-Pattern Points (Plan A / Plan B for Adults)
DRL-ADULT-001.o: Give adult competitors 2 simple patterns that win at their level and practice switching under pressure.
DRL-ADULT-001.su: Define Pattern A and Pattern B (e.g., A: heavy crosscourt to backhand; B: serve body then +1 to open). Write on whiteboard. Points start with serve.
DRL-ADULT-001.ex:
1) Play points to 11.
2) Odd points: must attempt Pattern A.
3) Even points: must attempt Pattern B.
4) Coach tags pattern appearance and UFEs.
5) Debrief: which pattern is more reliable?
DRL-ADULT-001.ef:
• Over-complication → simplify patterns to 2 shots.
• Pattern forgotten → verbalize before serve.
• Too aggressive → enforce big targets and net clearance.
DRL-ADULT-001.rg: Only Pattern A for full game.
DRL-ADULT-001.pg: Add opponent style constraints; add time clock; start games down 0-3.
DRL-ADULT-001.sm: Higher pattern execution rate and reduced unforced errors.
DRL-ADULT-001.sf: Adults: monitor injury risk; include warm-up and mobility.

DRL-ADULT-002.o: Prioritize the highest leverage phase: first 4 shots. Force intentionality on serve/return and next ball.
DRL-ADULT-002.su: Play points but stop after 4 shots and award point based on advantage: Winner/Forced error = point; Neutral = replay; UFE = opponent.
DRL-ADULT-002.ex:
1) Start points with serve.
2) Count shots: serve, return, +1, +1.
3) Stop at shot 4 if neutral; replay (keeps reps high).
4) Track 1st serve in%, return depth%, and pattern appearance.
DRL-ADULT-002.cu:
• 'Own the first four.'
• 'Deep return.'
• '+1 with margin.'
DRL-ADULT-002.ef:
• Players frustrated by replays → explain rep goal; keep tempo.
• Too many UFEs early → bigger targets, simplify patterns.
DRL-ADULT-002.pg: Play full point after 4 shots; add pressure scoring.
DRL-ADULT-002.sm: Improved serve/return KPIs and better early-point execution.
DRL-ADULT-002.sf: High intensity; manage rest and hydration.

DRL-ADULT-003.o: Improve doubles communication, shifting, and coverage responsibilities.
DRL-ADULT-003.su: 4 players. Start crosscourt 'crossfire' rally: deuce-court crosscourt only. Net players must call 'mine/yours' and 'switch' on lobs.
DRL-ADULT-003.ex:
1) Start cooperative crosscourt.
2) After 6 balls, make it live.
3) Rule: each team must make a verbal call every point (mine/yours/switch).
4) Rotate after 10 points.
DRL-ADULT-003.cu:
• 'Talk early.'
• 'Shift with the ball.'
• 'Cover the line when partner moves.'
DRL-ADULT-003.ef:
• Silence → enforce point loss if no call.
• Both go for ball → define priority (forehand/middle).
• Net player drifting → mark net home base spot.
DRL-ADULT-003.rg: No live points; cooperative only with calls.
DRL-ADULT-003.pg: Add poach signals; add Australian/I formation sequences.
DRL-ADULT-003.sm: More consistent communication and fewer doubles confusion errors.
DRL-ADULT-003.sf: Racket clashes: define spacing and calling priority.

DRL-ANALYTICS-020.o: Turn match play into development by tagging key stats and patterns.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020.su: Play a short set (first to 4 games). Coach/assistant tags: 1st serve in%, return depth%, pattern appeared, UFEs by pattern.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020.ex:
1) Brief pre-set goal: 1 pattern + 1 KPI.
2) Play set.
3) Between changeovers: 30-second coaching on KPI only.
4) Post-set: review tag sheet and choose next session focus.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020.cu:
• 'Plan before point.'
• 'Deep returns.'
• 'Pattern first, improvise later.'
DRL-ANALYTICS-020.ef:
• Too much feedback → constrain coaching to one KPI.
• Players forget plan → write plan on towel/whiteboard.
• Tagging inconsistency → standardize tags and definitions.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020.pg: Add opponent scouting notes; add video clips; expand stats set.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020.sm: Clear KPI trends and actionable next-step focus from set play.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020.sf: Match intensity: monitor heat, hydration, and injury risk.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-ANALYTICS-040.o: Turn video into a habit: tag clips to KPIs and convert into next session action items.
DRL-ANALYTICS-040.su: Capture 10–15 minutes of serve/return or match play. Use tagging sheet: serve routine, return depth, pattern adherence, reset routine.
DRL-ANALYTICS-040.ex:
1) Watch 10 clips.
2) Tag each clip with 1 KPI label.
3) Identify 1 technical cue + 1 tactical decision rule.
4) Write next-session drill prescription (IDs).
DRL-ANALYTICS-040.cu:
• 'One cue, one rule.'
• 'Tag, then act.'
• 'Keep it short.'
DRL-ANALYTICS-040.ef:
• Overanalyzing → timebox to 20 minutes.
• Too many cues → choose the one with biggest ROI.
• No action plan → require drill prescription output.
DRL-ANALYTICS-040.pg: Athlete self-tags; compare coach vs athlete tags; integrate into RAG research notes.
DRL-ANALYTICS-040.sm: Consistent video habit and clear carryover into practice plan.
DRL-ANALYTICS-040.sf: Ensure privacy/permissions for recording; follow facility policies.
DRL-ANALYTICS-040.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-CARDIO-002.n: Around the World Baseline (Continuous Rally Rotation)
DRL-CARDIO-002.o: Create continuous movement and rally reps in a fun group format.
DRL-CARDIO-002.su: Players line up at baseline on one side; coach feeds crosscourt; after hitting, player runs to end of line on opposite side (around the world).
DRL-CARDIO-002.ex:
1) Coach feeds ball to first player.
2) Player hits to big target and immediately runs around behind line to other side.
3) Next player hits.
4) If player misses, they do quick penalty (5 squats) and rejoin.
DRL-CARDIO-002.cu:
• 'Move fast between balls.'
• 'Hit safe targets.'
• 'Stay balanced.'
DRL-CARDIO-002.ef:
• Collisions in rotation → clear running lane behind baseline.
• Too many misses → slow feed; allow 2 bounces.
• Fatigue → shorten duration.
DRL-CARDIO-002.rg: Allow catch-throw for juniors; slower pace.
DRL-CARDIO-002.pg: Add direction call; add volley station.
DRL-CARDIO-002.sm: High engagement and improved movement endurance.
DRL-CARDIO-002.sf: High group speed: enforce lanes and spacing; monitor heat.

DRL-COMP-010.o: Transfer a single skill focus into live points with simple scoring and high engagement.
DRL-COMP-010.su: Choose ONE constraint aligned to the day: e.g., 'crosscourt only', 'must hit deep middle on defense', 'serve to body'.
DRL-COMP-010.ex:
1) Play points to 7.
2) If a player violates constraint, point counts for opponent OR replay (choose based on level).
3) Rotate opponents every 7 points.
4) Coach tracks one KPI during play.
DRL-COMP-010.cu:
• 'One thing today.'
• 'Commit to the rule.'
• 'Reset and go again.'
DRL-COMP-010.ef:
• Too many rules → remove extras; keep one.
• Players gaming the rule → clarify allowed shots.
• Low engagement → shorten games and rotate more.
DRL-COMP-010.rg: Cooperative points; larger court scaling; underhand serve start.
DRL-COMP-010.pg: Add score deficits (start 0-3); add time clock; add consequences.
DRL-COMP-010.sm: Players demonstrate skill in live context; KPI improves over multiple rounds.
DRL-COMP-010.sf: Ensure intensity appropriate; rotate rest and hydration.

DRL-COMP-030.o: Train calm decision-making when behind in games and reduce panic errors.
DRL-COMP-030.su: Play short sets to 4 games. Every service game starts at 0-30 for the server.
DRL-COMP-030.ex:
1) Start each game at 0-30.
2) Server focuses on high percentage targets and routine.
3) Coach tracks: first serve in%, UFEs, and routine compliance.
4) Debrief after set: what changed under pressure?
DRL-COMP-030.ef:
• Panic aggression → enforce deep-middle rule on neutral balls.
• Double faults → move forward and prioritize 2nd serve shape.
• Emotional tilt → use reset routine.
DRL-COMP-030.pg: Start at 0-40; add time clock; add consequence ladder.
DRL-COMP-030.sm: Reduced UFEs when behind; improved break point conversion and MPI stability.
DRL-COMP-030.sf: High stress and load; balance with recovery and positive reinforcement.

DRL-COMP-040.n: Serve Spots Only Set Play (Tactical Serving Discipline)
DRL-COMP-040.o: Force serve discipline: choose spot with intent and execute under match scoring.
DRL-COMP-040.su: Play a short set. Server must call serve spot (W/B/T) before serving. If serve does not land in called zone, point is replayed (or lost based on level).
DRL-COMP-040.ex:
1) Start set.
2) Before each serve, call spot.
3) Track called vs hit.
4) After set, review serve distribution and effectiveness.
DRL-COMP-040.cu:
• 'Call it, then own it.'
• 'Routine first.'
• 'Use body serve under pressure.'
DRL-COMP-040.ef:
• Too strict for level → use replay instead of point loss.
• Serve clusters of misses → enlarge zones and reduce speed.
DRL-COMP-040.rg: Call only side (deuce/ad), not exact zone.
DRL-COMP-040.pg: Add second serve call; add serve+1 requirement.
DRL-COMP-040.sm: Improved serve placement intent and reduced random serving.

DRL-COMP-041.o: Improve depth discipline and eliminate short, attackable balls.
DRL-COMP-041.su: Mark 'no-man's-land' as the zone between service line and baseline (or narrow band). Rule: if you hit into NML, you lose point.
DRL-COMP-041.ex:
1) Play points to 11.
2) Any ball landing in NML = immediate loss.
3) Encourage deep-middle as safety target.
4) Debrief: how did targets change?
DRL-COMP-041.cu:
• 'Deep or short; not middle.'
• 'Height + spin for depth.'
• 'Recover after depth.'
DRL-COMP-041.ef:
• Too many immediate losses → widen allowed zone or use replay.
• Players push short → allow higher net clearance and slower pace.
DRL-COMP-041.rg: Use replay instead of loss; narrower no-man's-land zone.
DRL-COMP-041.pg: Add pattern requirement before finishing; add serve start.
DRL-COMP-041.sm: Reduced short balls and improved depth consistency in match play.
DRL-COMP-041.sf: High engagement: manage intensity and hydration.

DRL-CTRL-001.o: Improve net clearance margin while maintaining depth and rally quality.
DRL-CTRL-001.su: Place a rope/tape 2–4 feet above net (or use visual marker). Mark deep target zone with cones 3 feet inside baseline.
DRL-CTRL-001.ex:
1) Cooperative rally crosscourt.
2) Rule: every ball must clear the rope/tape.
3) After 8 in a row, lower the rope marker slightly OR shrink target.
4) If ball hits net or goes long, reset ladder step.
DRL-CTRL-001.cu:
• 'Aim above the tape.'
• 'Brush up for spin.'
• 'High, heavy, deep.'
DRL-CTRL-001.ef:
• Balls flying long → cue more spin, lower swing speed, aim deeper target.
• Net errors → exaggerate height and follow-through.
• Players pushing → cue 'accelerate through contact'.
DRL-CTRL-001.rg: No rope; bigger deep zone; allow mid-court rally.
DRL-CTRL-001.pg: Add scoring; add 'one ball must be lower' (variety); add change direction after 10.
DRL-CTRL-001.sm: Longer rallies with consistent net clearance and fewer net errors.
DRL-CTRL-001.sf: Monitor elbow/shoulder fatigue; keep swing intensity appropriate.

DRL-CTRL-002.o: Teach depth as a tactical tool: reset neutral, build offense, and avoid short balls.
DRL-CTRL-002.su: Mark 3 zones on each side with cones: Short (inside service line), Neutral (between SL and 3 ft from baseline), Deep (last 3 ft).
DRL-CTRL-002.ex:
1) Cooperative rally; each landing in Deep = 2 pts, Neutral = 1 pt, Short = 0.
2) First pair to 15 wins.
3) After each rally, players call out the zone aloud (awareness).
4) Add rule: when you hit Deep 3 times in a rally, you can 'go' for angle or DTL.
DRL-CTRL-002.cu:
• 'Deep buys time.'
• 'Neutral is OK; short is danger.'
• 'Recover after depth.'
DRL-CTRL-002.ef:
• Forcing deep and missing long → cue higher net clearance and spin.
• No awareness → require verbal zone call.
• Standing too far back → teach baseline positioning relative to depth.
DRL-CTRL-002.pg: Add opponent pressure (live points); add serve start; add specific patterns after deep ball.
DRL-CTRL-002.sm: Higher percentage of deep balls; better decision timing for offense; fewer short balls.
DRL-CTRL-002.sf: Use appropriate ball and feed pace to avoid overuse.

DRL-CTRL-040.o: Teach players to control pace intentionally while keeping depth and margin.
DRL-CTRL-040.su: Mark deep target zone. Use 3 rounds: 50%, 60%, 70% perceived speed. Coach helps calibrate.
DRL-CTRL-040.ex:
1) Round 1: rally at 50% pace, must hit deep target.
2) Round 2: 60% pace.
3) Round 3: 70% pace.
4) If miss target or error, drop back a pace level.
DRL-CTRL-040.cu:
• 'Same target, different pace.'
• 'Keep margin.'
• 'Breathe and balance.'
DRL-CTRL-040.ef:
• Pace too high too early → enforce ladder and reset.
• Pushing at 50% → cue smooth acceleration and spin.
DRL-CTRL-040.pg: Add direction change at 70%; add pressure scoring.
DRL-CTRL-040.sm: Better pace control and fewer errors when increasing intensity.

DRL-DBL-020.o: Train poach timing and decision-making with clear cues and communication.
DRL-DBL-020.su: 4 players: server, returner, net player, opponent net. Net player has 2 options: stay or poach. Use hand signals.
DRL-DBL-020.ex:
1) Server serves to planned target.
2) Returner returns crosscourt.
3) Net player decides to poach or stay based on return quality and cues.
4) Play out point. Score net player's decision correctness (not just outcome).
5) Rotate positions every 6 points.
DRL-DBL-020.cu:
• 'Poach on a weak return.'
• 'Stay on a strong return.'
• 'Signal early; commit.'
DRL-DBL-020.ef:
• Random poaching → define cues (slow/floaty return).
• Late move → start earlier and split-step.
• Poor communication → enforce signals and partner calls.
DRL-DBL-020.rg: Coach calls poach/stay; return is fed predictably.
DRL-DBL-020.pg: Add Australian formation; add fake poach; add return variety.
DRL-DBL-020.sm: Higher correct decision rate; improved net aggression with discipline.
DRL-DBL-020.sf: Net player safety; avoid racket clashes; enforce spacing.

DRL-DBL-021.n: Doubles Serve + First Volley Pattern (Serve & Close)
DRL-DBL-021.o: Develop serve-and-volley patterns and partner movement coordination.
DRL-DBL-021.su: 4 players. Server starts, partner at net. Mark serve target and first volley target (deep middle).
DRL-DBL-021.ex:
1) Server serves and immediately closes.
2) Returner returns crosscourt.
3) Server hits first volley to deep middle, partner adjusts.
4) Play out point. Repeat 8 points then rotate.
DRL-DBL-021.cu:
• 'Serve then close hard.'
• 'First volley deep middle.'
• 'Partner shifts with the ball.'
DRL-DBL-021.ef:
• Server not closing → add sprint to service line before volley.
• First volley too low → cue 'punch through'.
• Partner static → teach 'shift and cover line'.
DRL-DBL-021.rg: Coach hand-feeds return; shorter court start.
DRL-DBL-021.pg: Add poach; add serve variety; add Australian/I formation options.
DRL-DBL-021.sm: Higher net conversion; improved partner coordination and positioning.

DRL-DBL-040.o: Train doubles serving formations and communication to create confusion and first-strike advantages.
DRL-DBL-040.su: Server and net partner practice signals (poach/stay; direction). Use Australian and I-formation positioning.
DRL-DBL-040.ex:
1) Run 8 serves in Australian: serve target + net partner movement.
2) Run 8 serves in I-formation: net player crouches center, moves based on signal.
3) Add returner and play out point.
4) Rotate roles.
DRL-DBL-040.cu:
• 'Signal early and commit.'
• 'Serve target supports formation.'
• 'Partner shifts with movement.'
DRL-DBL-040.ef:
• Confusion on signals → simplify to 2 signals first.
• Net player late → start movement earlier.
• Server misses targets → enlarge target and reduce speed.
DRL-DBL-040.pg: Add fake signals; add return variety; add pressure scoring.
DRL-DBL-040.sm: Cleaner communication and successful formation execution under live returns.
DRL-DBL-040.sf: Net safety; communication to avoid collisions.

DRL-DEF-011.n: Wide Ball Neutralize to Middle (Defense Decision Rule)
DRL-DEF-011.o: Teach defensive rule: when pulled wide, neutralize to big middle/deep target rather than going for lines.
DRL-DEF-011.su: Coach feeds wide to FH or BH corner. Mark big middle deep target (center 6 feet).
DRL-DEF-011.ex:
1) Athlete runs wide and hits neutralizing shot to big middle target.
2) Athlete recovers.
3) Coach feeds next ball to opposite side.
4) After 8 reps, play point live but require first defensive ball to middle.
DRL-DEF-011.cu:
• 'When stretched: middle and deep.'
• 'High and heavy.'
• 'Recover to center.'
DRL-DEF-011.ef:
• Trying DTL winner → enforce rule and restart.
• Defensive shot short → cue higher net clearance.
• Late contact → earlier prep and better first step.
DRL-DEF-011.rg: Reduce feed width; allow neutral to crosscourt big target.
DRL-DEF-011.pg: Add pressure scoring; add opponent attack; add time clock.
DRL-DEF-011.sm: Reduced errors from wide positions and improved reset ability.
DRL-DEF-011.sf: High-intensity lateral movement; manage reps; ensure proper warm-up.

DRL-DIR-001.o: Build directional control and rally tolerance, then execute a controlled change of direction.
DRL-DIR-001.su: Two players crosscourt. Mark a target rectangle (middle-deep) crosscourt. Optional: add a 'DTL gate' with cones.
DRL-DIR-001.ex:
1) Cooperative: reach 10 in a row in target.
2) If successful, continue to 20.
3) After 20, player A gets one controlled DTL attempt through gate; if in, point live.
4) Rotate roles every 4 minutes.
DRL-DIR-001.cu:
• 'Crosscourt is home.'
• 'Heavy to the target.'
• 'Change direction only from balance.'
DRL-DIR-001.ef:
• DTL errors → require deeper crosscourt before unlocking.
• Short balls → emphasize depth zone.
• Players too close to sidelines → teach recovery to center.
DRL-DIR-001.rg: Unlock change after 6; allow two DTL attempts.
DRL-DIR-001.pg: Shrink target; require DTL only on specific ball (short); add score pressure.
DRL-DIR-001.sm: Longer crosscourt streaks; improved quality and timing of change direction shots.
DRL-DIR-001.sf: Monitor intensity; rotate to avoid repetitive stress.

DRL-DIR-002.n: Crosscourt → DTL Trigger Game (Attack the Short Ball)
DRL-DIR-002.o: Train the decision rule: build crosscourt until you earn a short ball, then change direction with margin.
DRL-DIR-002.su: Play crosscourt only until a ball lands inside service line (short). Mark 'attack zone' inside baseline.
DRL-DIR-002.ex:
1) Start crosscourt rally.
2) When a short ball appears, the next hitter must go DTL (or inside-out) to attack.
3) Point becomes live and can be finished.
4) Score to 7 with quick rotations.
DRL-DIR-002.cu:
• 'Earn the change.'
• 'Attack with margin (net clearance + spin).' 
• 'Recover forward after attack.'
DRL-DIR-002.ef:
• Forcing change too early → enforce crosscourt-only rule strictly.
• Overhitting short ball → cue 'heavy not hard'.
• Not moving forward → add approach requirement after attack.
DRL-DIR-002.rg: Define short ball as inside service line + 3 ft; allow attack to big middle target.
DRL-DIR-002.pg: Add 2nd trigger: if opponent defends deep middle, you re-attack; add time pressure.
DRL-DIR-002.sm: Better patience in crosscourt; higher success rate on attack decision; improved transition behavior.
DRL-DIR-002.sf: Warm up thoroughly; manage high-intensity bursts.

DRL-FITNESS-020.n: On-Court Speed Endurance — Pattern HIIT (20s On / 40s Off)
DRL-FITNESS-020.o: Build tennis-specific speed endurance while maintaining technical shape under fatigue.
DRL-FITNESS-020.su: Coach feeds alternating corners or use live rally with pattern constraint. Timer: 20 seconds work, 40 seconds rest x 8 rounds.
DRL-FITNESS-020.ex:
1) Work interval: athlete executes pattern (e.g., FH corner → recover → BH corner) and hits controlled targets.
2) Rest interval: walk + breath.
3) Track quality: if 3 errors in a round, reduce pace or increase target size.
DRL-FITNESS-020.cu:
• 'Quality first.'
• 'Breathe on effort.'
• 'Stick the split step.'
DRL-FITNESS-020.ef:
• Technique breaks down → slow feed, bigger targets.
• Poor recovery → cue 3-step recovery.
• Over-fatigue → reduce rounds.
DRL-FITNESS-020.pg: Add ball recognition; add point finish; add medicine ball between rounds (advanced).
DRL-FITNESS-020.sm: Sustains movement and shot quality across rounds; reduced drop-off in last rounds.
DRL-FITNESS-020.sf: High intensity: contraindicated if injury/overuse risk; ensure warm-up, hydration, and appropriate scaling.
DRL-FITNESS-020.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT
DRL-FITNESS-020.ct: Full court (coach feeding or live ball)

DRL-FITNESS-030.o: Reduce common tennis overuse risk by strengthening rotator cuff/scap/core/hips.
DRL-FITNESS-030.su: Bands + mini-bands + light med ball. 3 stations: (1) band external rotations, (2) scap retractions, (3) dead bug or Pallof press, (4) hip airplanes or lateral band walks.
DRL-FITNESS-030.ex:
1) 30 seconds work / 15 seconds rest per station.
2) 2 rounds.
3) Finish with 60 seconds breathing + thoracic rotation.
DRL-FITNESS-030.cu:
• 'Control, not speed.'
• 'Ribs down, breathe.'
• 'Feel shoulder blade move.'
DRL-FITNESS-030.ef:
• Using too heavy band → reduce resistance.
• Poor posture → coach sets alignment.
• Rushing → add tempo count.
DRL-FITNESS-030.pg: Add eccentric control; add single-leg balance; add med-ball rotational throws (advanced).
DRL-FITNESS-030.sm: Consistent compliance and improved movement quality; fewer soreness flags over cycles.
DRL-FITNESS-030.sf: General fitness guidance only. For pain/injury, consult qualified medical professional.
DRL-FITNESS-030.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-FITNESS-040.o: Improve reaction time and split-step timing with unpredictable bounces.
DRL-FITNESS-040.su: Pairs. One athlete drops reaction ball; other starts in athletic stance with split-step on drop.
DRL-FITNESS-040.ex:
1) Dropper says 'ready' and drops ball.
2) Receiver split-steps as ball hits ground and reacts to catch after bounce.
3) 6 reps then switch.
4) Progress: add lateral start positions or add racket catch on strings.
DRL-FITNESS-040.ef:
• Upright posture → cue low hips.
• Late split → slow down, exaggerate timing.
• Reaching → move feet first.
DRL-FITNESS-040.rg: Use normal tennis ball; predictable bounce.
DRL-FITNESS-040.pg: Add second ball; add sprint to cone after catch; add feed and hit after reaction.
DRL-FITNESS-040.sm: Faster first step and better split timing in returns/rallies.
DRL-FITNESS-040.st: STG-07-09_ORANGE, STG-09-11_GREEN, STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-FITNESS-041.o: Develop rotational power and sequencing safely to support serve and groundstroke speed.
DRL-FITNESS-041.su: Wall space or partner. Use appropriate med ball weight. Mark safe throw line.
DRL-FITNESS-041.ex:
1) 6 standing rotational throws each side (hip-shoulder separation).
2) 6 step-in rotational throws each side.
3) 6 overhead slams.
4) Rest as needed; prioritize quality.
DRL-FITNESS-041.cu:
• 'Hips lead, shoulders follow.'
• 'Brace core; exhale on throw.'
• 'Quality reps only.'
DRL-FITNESS-041.ef:
• Using arms only → reduce weight; cue hip drive.
• Losing balance → widen stance; slow down.
• Over-arching back → ribs down; brace.
DRL-FITNESS-041.rg: Lighter ball; no wall; mimic pattern with band.
DRL-FITNESS-041.pg: Add shot-put throws; add reactive catches.
DRL-FITNESS-041.sm: Improved coordination and power without pain; better sequencing carryover to tennis.
DRL-FITNESS-041.sf: General fitness guidance; supervise closely; avoid if pain; keep volume modest.
DRL-FITNESS-041.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-FOOT-001.o: Teach split-step timing in a fun, low-threat format.
DRL-FOOT-001.su: Coach plays music or claps rhythm. Players bounce in athletic stance on their home spot.
DRL-FOOT-001.ex:
1) Players move lightly; on clap/stop-music they perform a split step and freeze in ready position.
2) Add ball cue: coach tosses a ball left/right after freeze; player makes 1st step to catch.
3) Repeat 6–10 reps.
DRL-FOOT-001.cu:
• 'Land soft.'
• 'Feet under hips.'
• 'Freeze with racket up.'
DRL-FOOT-001.ef:
• Jumping too high → cue 'small hop'.
• Feet too wide → mark two footprints on court.
• Late reaction → slow down cue, then speed up.
DRL-FOOT-001.pg: Add racket; after freeze, hit a fed ball; add split-step on partner contact in mini rally.
DRL-FOOT-001.sm: More consistent split-step timing and faster first step response.
DRL-FOOT-001.sf: Ensure safe spacing; avoid slippery surfaces.

DRL-GAME-001.o: Increase movement intensity while reinforcing listening, teamwork, and boundaries.
DRL-GAME-001.su: Scatter 30–50 balls on half court. Place 4 'treasure chests' (baskets) in corners. Split into 2 teams.
DRL-GAME-001.ex:
1) On go, players collect one ball at a time and place in their team's basket.
2) Add movement constraints each round: shuffles only, hop to ball, sprint back.
3) After 3 rounds, count treasure; discuss effort + teamwork.
DRL-GAME-001.cu:
• 'One ball at a time.'
• 'Eyes up, avoid collisions.'
• 'Quick feet, soft hands.'
DRL-GAME-001.ef:
• Players grabbing many balls → enforce rule and restart.
• Collisions → assign lanes and 'no crossing' zones.
• Over-excitement → shorten rounds; add calm breath between.
DRL-GAME-001.rg: Smaller area; fewer balls; walking only.
DRL-GAME-001.pg: Add racket carry; add 'collect only red balls' (sorting); add time clock.
DRL-GAME-001.sm: High engagement, good spacing, improved agility and compliance to rules.
DRL-GAME-001.sf: Strict spacing and boundaries; keep baskets away from fences to avoid crashes.

DRL-GAME-002.o: Build aim + controlled power via target games with simple scoring.
DRL-GAME-002.su: Place 3–6 cone 'castles' on far side. Players hit from close distance. Teams or individual.
DRL-GAME-002.ex:
1) Players feed themselves or coach feeds.
2) Each hit that knocks a cone earns 1 point.
3) After score threshold, step back 2 feet.
4) Rotate roles (feed/hit) if in pairs.
DRL-GAME-002.cu:
• 'Aim small, miss small.'
• 'Smooth swing; hit through the target.'
• 'Recover to ready.'
DRL-GAME-002.ef:
• Over-hitting → move closer; lower swing speed.
• Missing wide → add larger target zone; focus on contact.
DRL-GAME-002.rg: Bigger cones; closer; allow two bounces.
DRL-GAME-002.pg: Smaller targets; require crosscourt only; add rally before target shot.
DRL-GAME-002.sm: Improving accuracy over rounds; players can modulate speed to hit targets.
DRL-GAME-002.sf: Ensure targets are safe cones, not hard objects; manage retrieval.

DRL-LIVE-001.o: High-tempo doubles reps without traditional serving; develop reactions and teamwork.
DRL-LIVE-001.su: 4 players per court. Coach feeds in to start point. Rules: challengers need 3 points to replace champions; defenders need 2 points to stay.
DRL-LIVE-001.ex:
1) Coach feeds ball into play.
2) Play point to completion.
3) Keep running score per rules.
4) Rotate players as winners/losers based on format.
5) Coach provides quick tactical cues between points.
DRL-LIVE-001.cu:
• 'Make the first ball.'
• 'High percentage targets.'
• 'Communicate and cover.'
DRL-LIVE-001.ef:
• Chaos in rotation → clearly designate champ/challenger sides.
• Overhitting → reinforce big targets and net clearance.
• Fatigue → add 60-second water break every 15 minutes.
DRL-LIVE-001.rg: Challengers need 2, defenders need 2; slower feeds.
DRL-LIVE-001.sm: High engagement and improvement in doubles positioning/reaction decisions.
DRL-LIVE-001.sf: High intensity; ensure warm-up; manage heat and hydration; screen for injury risk.

DRL-MENTAL-001.o: Install a simple reset habit (breath + cue) that keeps sessions positive.
DRL-MENTAL-001.su: Coach teaches: (1) hands on racket, (2) 3 slow breaths, (3) cue word ('next ball').
DRL-MENTAL-001.ex:
1) After any miss in drills/games, player steps to a marked 'reset spot'.
2) Performs 3 breaths and says cue word.
3) Returns to ready stance and continues.
4) Coach reinforces with praise for compliance, not outcome.
DRL-MENTAL-001.cu:
• 'Reset spot, 3 breaths.'
• 'Say it: NEXT BALL.'
• 'Strong body language.'
DRL-MENTAL-001.ef:
• Forgetting routine → coach uses silent hand signal.
• Overthinking → keep it playful: 'blow out candles'.
DRL-MENTAL-001.rg: 1 breath + cue for very young; parent models off-court.
DRL-MENTAL-001.pg: Add visual focus point; add 'plan' (target) after cue for older players.
DRL-MENTAL-001.sm: Fewer emotional spirals; quicker return to task; improved resilience in points.
DRL-MENTAL-001.sf: Keep it supportive; avoid shaming. If a child is distressed, pause and address safety/comfort first.

DRL-MENTAL-010.o: Create a repeatable routine that stabilizes performance and reduces emotional volatility.
DRL-MENTAL-010.su: Define 3-step routine: (1) physical reset (turn away), (2) breath, (3) plan + cue word. Write on whiteboard.
DRL-MENTAL-010.ex:
1) During any point game, coach tracks compliance.
2) Every time athlete uses full routine, they earn a 'routine point'.
3) First to 8 routine points wins (independent of tennis score).
4) Debrief: what step was hardest?
DRL-MENTAL-010.cu:
• 'Turn away, breathe, plan.'
• 'Cue word: heavy / early / trust.'
• 'Routine points matter.'
DRL-MENTAL-010.ef:
• Skipping steps → start with 2-step routine.
• Overthinking plan → make plan one word.
• Negative self-talk → replace with cue word.
DRL-MENTAL-010.rg: Routine only after errors; 1 breath + cue.
DRL-MENTAL-010.pg: Add pressure scenarios; require routine on every point; integrate 'quiet eye' for serve/return.
DRL-MENTAL-010.sm: Routine compliance % increases and emotional recovery improves.
DRL-MENTAL-010.sf: Avoid punitive tone; routine is skill acquisition.

DRL-MENTAL-020.o: Improve attention control by pairing a routine with a stable final visual focus (quiet eye).
DRL-MENTAL-020.su: Define quiet-eye spot: serve = back of ball at toss; return = server contact zone. Use 3-step routine + 1-second quiet eye.
DRL-MENTAL-020.ex:
1) Athlete rehearses routine without ball 5 times.
2) Athlete serves 10 balls: must pause 1 second on quiet-eye spot before motion.
3) Athlete returns 10 feeds: must hold gaze on contact zone before split.
4) Reflect: rate focus 1–5.
DRL-MENTAL-020.cu:
• 'One calm look.'
• 'Breath, focus, go.'
• 'Eyes steady, body calm.'
DRL-MENTAL-020.ef:
• Rushing → enforce pause count.
• Overthinking → keep cue simple.
• Eyes wandering → use visual marker.
DRL-MENTAL-020.pg: Add pressure scoring; integrate into tiebreak; add distractions.
DRL-MENTAL-020.sm: Better routine compliance and reduced rushed errors on serve/return.
DRL-MENTAL-020.sf: Avoid making it rigid; routine must remain functional in match tempo.
DRL-MENTAL-020.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-MENTAL-040.o: Translate goals into actionable behaviors using if–then plans that hold up under pressure.
DRL-MENTAL-040.su: Whiteboard: KPI goals and 3 'if–then' plans (e.g., If I feel tight, then I breathe + aim big).
DRL-MENTAL-040.ex:
1) Athlete chooses 1 KPI for the week.
2) Athlete writes 3 if–then plans for common scenarios.
3) Athlete rehearses plans verbally.
4) Integrate into pressure points session; coach tags plan usage.
DRL-MENTAL-040.cu:
• 'One KPI.'
• 'If–then must be simple.'
• 'Practice the plan, not the hope.'
DRL-MENTAL-040.ef:
• Plans too complex → reduce to 3 words.
• Athlete forgets → write on towel card.
• Judgment tone → keep it curious and constructive.
DRL-MENTAL-040.rg: Coach provides template; athlete fills blanks.
DRL-MENTAL-040.pg: Add post-match review; adjust plans based on data.
DRL-MENTAL-040.sm: Improved routine compliance and more consistent responses to common triggers.
DRL-MENTAL-040.sf: Not therapy; refer to qualified professional for mental health concerns.

DRL-MENTAL-041.o: Improve confidence and decision clarity by mentally rehearsing high-quality reps.
DRL-MENTAL-041.su: Athlete chooses one skill (serve target, return depth, pattern). Find quiet spot.
DRL-MENTAL-041.ex:
1) Close eyes and visualize 10 perfect reps with sensory detail (feel, sound, target).
2) Immediately perform 10 real reps.
3) Compare: what matched? what drifted?
4) Repeat once.
DRL-MENTAL-041.cu:
• 'See it, feel it, do it.'
• 'One image, one cue.'
• 'Then execute.'
DRL-MENTAL-041.ef:
• Vague images → add target and body sensation details.
• Overthinking → keep to 10 reps.
• No transfer → pair immediately with real reps.
DRL-MENTAL-041.pg: Use before pressure tiebreak; integrate with quiet eye.
DRL-MENTAL-041.sm: Better focus and smoother execution in subsequent reps.
DRL-MENTAL-041.sf: Keep it practical; avoid making it feel forced.

DRL-MINI-001.o: Teach rally rhythm and spacing before full hitting.
DRL-MINI-001.su: Pairs across mini-net or rope; each pair has one foam/red ball.
DRL-MINI-001.ex:
1) Player A underhand tosses to partner; partner catches after one bounce.
2) Partner tosses back; continue cooperative 'rally'.
3) Add target: toss must land on 'island' (cone square).
4) Optional: toss → trap on racket → lift to partner.
DRL-MINI-001.cu:
• 'Soft toss to the island.'
• 'Ready hands.'
• 'Bounce, catch, look.'
DRL-MINI-001.ef:
• Wild tosses → shorten distance; teach 'pendulum arm'.
• Not moving feet → add 'step to the bounce'.
• Poor catching → use bigger ball, two hands.
DRL-MINI-001.rg: Catch without bounce; closer distance; no targets.
DRL-MINI-001.pg: Catch with one hand; add side shuffle; transition to gentle hit after bounce.
DRL-MINI-001.sm: Longer cooperative sequences; improved attention and movement to the bounce spot.
DRL-MINI-001.sf: Maintain pair spacing; avoid crossing courts during retrieval.

DRL-MINI-002.o: Build rally tolerance and basic directional control with a simple constraint.
DRL-MINI-002.su: Pairs rally mini tennis. Mark a large target rectangle in each service box (e.g., 2 cones deep-middle).
DRL-MINI-002.ex:
1) Cooperative rally: ball must bounce once and land inside target zone.
2) After 5 in a row, players can 'unlock' and hit to full service box.
3) Add challenge: every 3rd ball must go crosscourt (if using half boxes).
4) Rotate partners every 4 minutes.
DRL-MINI-002.cu:
• 'High over the net.'
• 'Aim middle-deep.'
• 'Recover to home base.'
DRL-MINI-002.ef:
• Too hard → cue '80% swing'; use slower ball.
• Net errors → cue 'aim higher'; add net clearance rope.
• Standing still → cue 'split then step'.
DRL-MINI-002.rg: Allow two bounces; use foam ball; remove target zones.
DRL-MINI-002.pg: Shrink target; add scoring; add 'change direction' ball after 6 in a row.
DRL-MINI-002.sm: Average rally length increases; fewer net errors; better spacing and recovery.

DRL-MOVE-001.o: Automate recovery steps after each hit and reduce drifting.
DRL-MOVE-001.su: Place a 'home base' cone slightly behind baseline center. Coach feeds alternating FH/BH.
DRL-MOVE-001.ex:
1) Athlete hits ball, then must take 3 recovery steps back toward home base cone before next feed.
2) Coach feeds next ball only when athlete touches cone (or crosses a line).
3) After 10 reps, remove cone and keep habit.
DRL-MOVE-001.cu:
• 'Hit, recover, ready.'
• '3 steps to home.'
• 'Split before the bounce.'
DRL-MOVE-001.ef:
• Athlete watches shot → cue 'eyes back to coach' after hit.
• Lazy steps → shorten recovery distance; add timing goal.
• Over-recovering → set cone slightly forward.
DRL-MOVE-001.rg: 2 recovery steps; slower feeds; smaller movement.
DRL-MOVE-001.pg: Random feeds; add live points; add serve start and +1 recovery.
DRL-MOVE-001.sm: Faster ready position, improved defense, fewer late contacts in rallies.

DRL-MOVE-040.n: Crossover + Drop Step for Wide Balls (First Step Mechanics)
DRL-MOVE-040.o: Improve first step efficiency to wide balls using correct drop step/crossover mechanics.
DRL-MOVE-040.su: Mark start spot and wide cone targets. Coach points left/right as cue.
DRL-MOVE-040.ex:
1) Athlete split-steps.
2) On cue, athlete executes drop step + crossover to wide cone.
3) Recover with shuffle to start.
4) Add racket shadow swing on arrival.
5) 8 reps each side.
DRL-MOVE-040.cu:
• 'Drop step, then go.'
• 'Hips turn first.'
• 'Stay low.'
DRL-MOVE-040.ef:
• Shuffling first → demonstrate drop step.
• Upright posture → cue low hips.
• Slow recovery → shorten distance, build quality.
DRL-MOVE-040.pg: Add fed ball to hit on arrival; add reaction randomization.
DRL-MOVE-040.sm: Faster wide-ball coverage and better balance on arrival.
DRL-MOVE-040.sf: Manage knee/ankle stress; ensure proper warm-up.

DRL-NET-001.o: Introduce net positioning and volley contact without swing complexity.
DRL-NET-001.su: Pairs at 6–10 ft distance. Start with hands; then rackets.
DRL-NET-001.ex:
1) Hand volley: partner tosses softly; player catches and 'pushes' back with two hands.
2) Racket trap: catch ball on strings (no swing) and pop it back.
3) Punch volley: short backswing, 'block' to target.
4) Add split-step before contact for older stages.
DRL-NET-001.cu:
• 'Racket out front.'
• 'Step to the ball.'
• 'Short punch, no big swing.'
DRL-NET-001.ef:
• Swinging too much → cue 'block like a wall'.
• Racket drops → cue 'hands up'.
• Late contact → start closer; slower toss.
DRL-NET-001.rg: Use balloon; allow catch with hands only.
DRL-NET-001.pg: Add direction targets; add 2-volley combo (FH then BH); add approach + volley sequence.
DRL-NET-001.sm: Cleaner volley contact, improved ready position, and better split-step timing.
DRL-NET-001.sf: Keep volleys controlled; avoid face-level tosses for kids.

DRL-NET-010.o: Train approach shot selection + forward movement + split-step timing + first volley quality.
DRL-NET-010.su: Coach feeds short ball to baseline. Mark approach target deep corner and volley target open court.
DRL-NET-010.ex:
1) Athlete hits approach (high clearance + depth) to target.
2) Athlete moves forward; split-step at opponent contact (coach toss).
3) Athlete volleys to open court target.
4) Repeat 6 reps per set; 3 sets with rest.
DRL-NET-010.cu:
• 'Approach deep with margin.'
• 'Follow the ball in.'
• 'Split then punch.'
• 'Volley in front.'
DRL-NET-010.ef:
• Approach short → aim higher + deeper target.
• No split-step → add audible cue 'split!'.
• Volley swing → cue 'block and steer'.
DRL-NET-010.rg: Hand-feed; shorter approach distance; trap volley.
DRL-NET-010.pg: Add second volley; add opponent pass attempt; make point live after volley.
DRL-NET-010.sm: Higher approach depth; cleaner first volley; improved net positioning.
DRL-NET-010.sf: Monitor knee/ankle deceleration; keep volume appropriate.

DRL-NET-011.n: Volley Direction Game (Two Targets, One Decision)
DRL-NET-011.o: Improve volley control and teach simple decision: volley back behind or away from opponent.
DRL-NET-011.su: Feeder at net with basket. Volleyer at service line. Two targets: deep-middle and angle.
DRL-NET-011.ex:
1) Feeder tosses/feeds.
2) Volleyer must call target ('middle' or 'angle') before contact.
3) Score 1 if ball lands in target.
4) Switch after 12 feeds.
DRL-NET-011.cu:
• 'Racket out front.'
• 'Step to the ball.'
• 'Aim, then punch.'
DRL-NET-011.ef:
• Over-swing → cue 'short punch'.
• Late contact → start closer, slower feed.
• Missing target → enlarge target and re-focus on contact.
DRL-NET-011.rg: Trap and push; use foam ball.
DRL-NET-011.pg: Add moving volleys; add approach into volley; add point live after volley.
DRL-NET-011.sm: Increased target hits and steadier volley contact.
DRL-NET-011.sf: Keep feeds controlled; avoid face-level feeds.

DRL-NET-020.n: Transition Decision: Approach or Reset (2-Option Rule)
DRL-NET-020.o: Teach transition decision: approach only on the right ball; otherwise reset neutral.
DRL-NET-020.su: Coach feeds mixed balls: some short and some deep. Define criteria for approach (short + above net height).
DRL-NET-020.ex:
1) Athlete plays rally.
2) On short ball, athlete must approach and play at net.
3) On deep ball, athlete must rally neutral and stay back.
4) Coach scores decision correctness + point outcome.
DRL-NET-020.cu:
• 'Approach only the right ball.'
• 'Neutral is a weapon.'
• 'Commit forward when you go.'
DRL-NET-020.ef:
• Approaching on deep balls → enforce penalty point.
• Hesitation on short ball → add cue 'green light' and require approach.
• Poor approach quality → set depth target for approach.
DRL-NET-020.pg: Opponent passes/ lobs live; add scoring for correct decisions.
DRL-NET-020.sm: Decision accuracy improves; fewer transition errors; better net positioning.

DRL-NET-040.n: Swing Volley + Overhead Combo (Attack the Floater)
DRL-NET-040.o: Convert floaters into offense with controlled swing volley and overhead technique.
DRL-NET-040.su: Coach feeds floaty mid-court balls and then lobs. Mark finishing targets (open court).
DRL-NET-040.ex:
1) Athlete attacks floater with swing volley (compact) to big target.
2) Coach feeds lob; athlete hits overhead to finish.
3) Repeat 8 sequences.
DRL-NET-040.cu:
• 'Compact swing volley.'
• 'Attack with margin.'
• 'Turn-point-move on lob.'
DRL-NET-040.ef:
• Swing volley overhit → shorten swing; bigger target.
• Overhead footwork poor → emphasize turn/point/shuffle.
• Rushing → enforce split-step before volley.
DRL-NET-040.rg: Punch volley instead of swing volley; easier lobs.
DRL-NET-040.pg: Make point live after swing volley; add defender trying to pass.
DRL-NET-040.sm: Higher conversion rate on floaters and fewer missed overheads.

DRL-OH-010.o: Build safe overhead mechanics and tracking footwork patterns.
DRL-OH-010.su: Coach feeds lobs to overhead position. Mark 'smash zone' target on opposite court.
DRL-OH-010.ex:
1) Athlete starts at service line.
2) Coach lobs.
3) Athlete turns sideways, points with non-dominant hand, shuffles back, then hits overhead to target.
4) Reset quickly; 10 reps.
DRL-OH-010.cu:
• 'Turn, point, move.'
• 'Contact high.'
• 'Snap and land balanced.'
DRL-OH-010.ef:
• Backpedaling → cue side shuffle; use drop step.
• Contact too low → earlier movement; cue 'reach tall'.
• Over-swing → emphasize smooth snap, not muscle.
DRL-OH-010.rg: Catch lob; shadow swing; hit from closer toss.
DRL-OH-010.pg: Add recovery to net; add opponent counter feed; add overhead from baseline (running).
DRL-OH-010.sm: Improved tracking, fewer mishits, better balance on landing.
DRL-OH-010.sf: Shoulder safety: limit volume; stop if pain; reinforce safe landing and spacing.

DRL-PASS-040.n: Passing Shot vs Approach (Drive / Dip / Lob Options)
DRL-PASS-040.o: Build passing shot selection and execution against an approaching opponent.
DRL-PASS-040.su: One player approaches after a short ball; defender at baseline. Mark passing targets (down the line and crosscourt) and lob zone.
DRL-PASS-040.ex:
1) Coach feeds short ball to attacker.
2) Attacker approaches.
3) Coach feeds ball to defender.
4) Defender chooses pass: drive, dip (low), or lob.
5) Play out point; rotate roles.
DRL-PASS-040.cu:
• 'Read the net player's position.'
• 'Dip crosscourt is high %.'
• 'Lob when they crowd.'
DRL-PASS-040.ef:
• Defender forces line too early → emphasize crosscourt dip.
• Pass into net → aim higher; add spin.
• Lob short → aim higher/deeper; use topspin lob.
DRL-PASS-040.rg: Attacker starts at service line (less pressure); defender chooses only one option first.
DRL-PASS-040.pg: Attacker chooses approach direction; live feed; add pressure scoring.
DRL-PASS-040.sm: Better passing decisions and reduced errors vs net pressure.
DRL-PASS-040.sf: High intensity; monitor sprinting and decel.
DRL-PASS-040.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-PATTERN-020.o: Build a reliable Plan A pattern and automate it under live ball conditions.
DRL-PATTERN-020.su: Choose ONE Plan A (e.g., serve wide → +1 to open court → +2 behind opponent → finish). Mark targets for each ball.
DRL-PATTERN-020.ex:
1) Run 8 pattern reps with cooperative partner (partner returns predictably).
2) Run 8 reps with semi-live return (partner chooses between two options).
3) Run 8 reps live.
4) Track pattern appearance and success.
DRL-PATTERN-020.cu:
• 'Know your pattern.'
• 'Each ball has a job.'
• 'High margin early, tighter later.'
DRL-PATTERN-020.ef:
• Athlete improvises too early → enforce targets.
• Over-forcing finish → require 2 heavy balls before finishing.
• Poor spacing → add recovery cone or split-step cue.
DRL-PATTERN-020.rg: Start from feed instead of serve; fewer balls (serve+1 only).
DRL-PATTERN-020.pg: Add opponent style constraints; add pressure score; add Plan B if blocked.
DRL-PATTERN-020.sm: Pattern execution rate increases; errors reduce in early pattern balls.
DRL-PATTERN-020.sf: High intensity; ensure warm-up and hydration; rotate partners.
DRL-PATTERN-020.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-PATTERN-021.o: Improve perception and timing: identify attackable ball and execute high-percentage aggression.
DRL-PATTERN-021.su: Define 'attackable ball' as: inside baseline + above net height (or short). Mark attack target (inside court, not lines).
DRL-PATTERN-021.ex:
1) Rally neutral crosscourt.
2) When attackable ball appears, hitter must go to attack target and move forward.
3) If hitter attacks from non-attackable ball, they lose point immediately (discipline).
4) Play to 11.
DRL-PATTERN-021.cu:
• 'Attack only the right ball.'
• 'Heavy to safe target.'
• 'Move forward to finish.'
DRL-PATTERN-021.ef:
• Attacking too early → tighten definition and enforce penalties.
• Attacking too late → add verbal cue from coach to train recognition.
• Overhitting → shrink swing speed, increase net clearance.
DRL-PATTERN-021.rg: Allow any attack; no penalty; coach calls attack ball.
DRL-PATTERN-021.pg: Add second trigger (inside-in); add opponent counter; add time pressure.
DRL-PATTERN-021.sm: Fewer premature attacks; more successful forward movement and finishing.

DRL-PRESS-020.n: Deuce Game / Break Point Simulation (Pressure Reps)
DRL-PRESS-020.o: Build pressure tolerance by repeating the highest leverage points (deuce/ad, break points).
DRL-PRESS-020.su: Play games that start at Deuce or at 30-40. Track break points converted and routine compliance.
DRL-PRESS-020.ex:
1) Start game at Deuce.
2) Play best of 7 points (deuce/ad cycles).
3) Switch server each game.
4) Add rule: routine must be visible or point doesn't count.
DRL-PRESS-020.cu:
• 'Simplify targets.'
• 'Routine on every point.'
• 'High percentage first 4 shots.'
DRL-PRESS-020.ef:
• Tight swings → cue breath + bigger targets.
• Rushing between points → enforce routine.
• Over-aggression → require deep-middle rule on neutral balls.
DRL-PRESS-020.rg: Start at 15-15; allow coaching between points.
DRL-PRESS-020.pg: Time clock; consequences; play 2 deuce games in a row with stats.
DRL-PRESS-020.sm: Improved routine compliance and break point conversion trend.
DRL-PRESS-020.sf: High stress: keep supportive tone; manage volume and rest.
DRL-PRESS-020.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-PRESS-021.o: Increase decision speed and routine discipline by limiting time between points.
DRL-PRESS-021.su: Play a tiebreak to 7 with a 15-second clock between points. Coach times it. Require routine within the clock.
DRL-PRESS-021.ex:
1) Start tiebreak.
2) After each point, players have 15 seconds to start next point.
3) If late, lose 1 point.
4) Track unforced errors and routine compliance.
DRL-PRESS-021.cu:
• 'Breathe quickly, pick a plan.'
• 'Commit to the target.'
• 'Short memory.'
DRL-PRESS-021.ef:
• Panic decisions → simplify to deep-middle.
• Skipping routine → reinforce routine points.
• Too many double faults → allow 20 seconds for serve only initially.
DRL-PRESS-021.sm: Reduced hesitation; improved composure; fewer UFEs late in games.
DRL-PRESS-021.sf: Manage intensity; ensure hydration; stop if heat risk.

DRL-RETURN-010.o: Improve return timing and first step using a clear contact cue and simple targets.
DRL-RETURN-010.su: Server (or coach) feeds moderate serves. Receiver starts in ready position with two return targets (deep middle + crosscourt).
DRL-RETURN-010.ex:
1) Receiver practices split-step timed with server contact.
2) Receiver returns to deep-middle target 8/10 reps.
3) Next set: return crosscourt target.
4) Add 'call': coach says 'middle' or 'cross' just before contact.
DRL-RETURN-010.ef:
• Late split → exaggerate: split earlier then adjust.
• Big swing → cue 'block and guide'.
• Returns short → cue higher net clearance and deeper target.
DRL-RETURN-010.rg: Coach hand-feed from service line; allow catch-return toss for young.
DRL-RETURN-010.pg: Increase serve pace; add body serve; add return +1 live.
DRL-RETURN-010.sm: More consistent returns beyond service line and improved timing/footwork.
DRL-RETURN-010.sf: Manage ball feed pace; protect shoulders; avoid overuse in young athletes.
DRL-RETURN-010.ct: Half court or full return position

DRL-RETURN-011.o: Raise return depth reliability and reduce short returns.
DRL-RETURN-011.su: Mark a 'depth line' at service line. Server serves moderate pace; receiver aims beyond depth line.
DRL-RETURN-011.ex:
1) Receiver hits 20 returns.
2) Score 1 point for each return beyond service line, 2 points if in deep zone (last 3 ft).
3) Goal score set by level (e.g., 18/40).
4) Switch roles.
DRL-RETURN-011.cu:
• 'Meet it early.'
• 'Aim high enough.'
• 'Deep middle is safe.'
DRL-RETURN-011.ef:
• Net errors → cue higher clearance, earlier prep.
• Long errors → reduce swing speed, add spin.
• Shanking wide → contact in front; shorten backswing.
DRL-RETURN-011.rg: Hand-feed; bigger target; allow bounce return from closer.
DRL-RETURN-011.pg: Add pressure: down 30-40 scenario; add second serve returns; tag quality index.
DRL-RETURN-011.sm: Return depth % trends upward week to week.
DRL-RETURN-011.sf: Limit return volume if shoulder/elbow discomfort arises; ensure rest and technique quality.

DRL-RETURN-020.o: Train return as offense/neutral tool and execute first groundstroke (+1) with intent.
DRL-RETURN-020.su: Server serves to realistic locations. Returner has a planned return target (deep middle or cross). After return, coach feeds next ball to simulate server +1.
DRL-RETURN-020.ex:
1) Returner focuses on depth/quality (neutralize).
2) On next ball, returner executes +1 pattern (e.g., heavy cross then attack). 
3) Play out point for max 4 shots.
4) 10 reps each side.
DRL-RETURN-020.cu:
• 'Split on contact.'
• 'Return deep first.'
• '+1 is your first attack.'
DRL-RETURN-020.ef:
• Return errors → shorten backswing; contact earlier; deep middle.
• +1 rushed → build earlier prep; use recovery steps.
• Plan unclear → pick ONE return target per set.
DRL-RETURN-020.rg: Coach hand-feeds serve; return to big middle target only.
DRL-RETURN-020.pg: Live server +1; add score pressure; alternate return positions (inside/outside).
DRL-RETURN-020.sm: Return depth % and pattern execution rate improve in match-like reps.
DRL-RETURN-020.sf: High intensity; monitor reps and rest.
DRL-RETURN-020.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-RETURN-030.o: Teach tactical return positioning based on serve type and opponent tendencies.
DRL-RETURN-030.su: Mark three return starting positions: (1) inside baseline, (2) on baseline, (3) 1–2 steps back. Server alternates serve types.
DRL-RETURN-030.ex:
1) Play 6 returns from each position.
2) Tag return quality and depth.
3) Discuss: which position neutralizes best vs each serve?
4) Finish with live points where receiver chooses position each point and states why.
DRL-RETURN-030.cu:
• 'Position is a tactic.'
• 'Split on contact.'
• 'Choose, don't drift.'
DRL-RETURN-030.ef:
• Standing too far back always → show tradeoffs; test inside position.
• Late reactions inside → shorter backswing and earlier split.
• Returns short → aim higher and deeper target.
DRL-RETURN-030.rg: Only two positions; coach calls position.
DRL-RETURN-030.pg: Add serve variety and disguise; add pressure points; add doubles return positions.
DRL-RETURN-030.sm: Improved return quality and smarter positioning choices.
DRL-RETURN-030.sf: Manage return volume; protect lower back with good warm-up.

DRL-RKT-001.o: Develop racket comfort, basic control, and listening skills through simple constraints.
DRL-RKT-001.su: Each player with racket and foam/red ball. Mark a 'home spot' with a dot/cone.
DRL-RKT-001.ex:
1) Tap ball up on strings (self-rally). Count taps.
2) On coach call 'Freeze!' players stop the ball on strings and hold still on home spot.
3) Repeat with variations: tap high/low, tap while walking to cone and back.
4) Finish: 3 attempts to beat personal best.
DRL-RKT-001.cu:
• 'Soft hands, quiet wrist.'
• 'Racket like a table.'
• 'Eyes on the ball; freeze like a statue.'
DRL-RKT-001.ef:
• Ball flies off → lower height, bigger ball, two-hand assist on handle.
• Over-gripping → 'hold like a bird'.
• Not freezing → practice freeze without ball first.
DRL-RKT-001.rg: Use balloon; allow bounce between taps; reduce to 3–5 taps goals.
DRL-RKT-001.pg: Add walking, side shuffles, or 180° turn on cue; add tap patterns (2 low + 1 high).
DRL-RKT-001.sm: Tap streak improves; freeze compliance improves; players stay on task with minimal reminders.
DRL-RKT-001.sf: Keep rackets below shoulder height; ensure spacing between players.

DRL-RKT-002.o: Build tracking, timing, and hand-eye coordination using predictable bounce/catch rhythm.
DRL-RKT-002.su: Each player with ball (no racket initially). Mark a bounce line with tape/cones.
DRL-RKT-002.ex:
1) Drop ball, let it bounce once, catch with two hands.
2) Progress: bounce on the line, catch above waist.
3) Add movement: take 2 steps back after each catch.
4) Add racket: bounce, catch on strings (trap), then lift.
DRL-RKT-002.cu:
• 'Watch it bounce.'
• 'Hands together like a basket.'
• 'Catch soft, like an egg.'
DRL-RKT-002.ef:
• Reaching late → start closer and slow down.
• Slapping at ball → cue 'receive' not 'grab'.
• Poor bounce height → demonstrate consistent drop.
DRL-RKT-002.rg: Use balloon; allow two bounces; remove movement.
DRL-RKT-002.pg: Add alternating hands catch; add clap once before catch; add partner toss-bounce-catch.
DRL-RKT-002.sm: Consistent bounce-and-catch rhythm; improved focus and calm hands.
DRL-RKT-002.sf: Space players; avoid chasing balls into other groups.

DRL-RKT-003.o: Teach 'aiming' and spacing using the simplest ball trajectory (rolling).
DRL-RKT-003.su: Create 3 big targets (hoops/flat cones) at 8–12 feet. Players in pairs with one ball per pair.
DRL-RKT-003.ex:
1) Player A rolls ball with hand toward a target.
2) Player B traps with hand or racket, returns roll to a chosen target.
3) Add 'coach call' target color to train listening.
4) Score: 1 point for hitting target; first to 5.
DRL-RKT-003.cu:
• 'Point your belly button at the target.'
• 'Roll it like bowling.'
• 'Trap, then aim.'
DRL-RKT-003.ef:
• Rolling too hard → shorten distance, cue 'gentle'.
• Not trapping → teach 'stop the ball' first.
• Chaos around targets → assign lanes and clear rules.
DRL-RKT-003.rg: Bigger targets, shorter distance, no scoring.
DRL-RKT-003.pg: Roll with racket push; add backhand-side rolls; add 'fake then go' decision.
DRL-RKT-003.sm: Players consistently hit big targets and demonstrate turn-taking.
DRL-RKT-003.sf: Ensure players roll within lanes; avoid collisions.

DRL-SERVE-001.o: Build the overhand throwing pattern and consistent toss pathway before serving.
DRL-SERVE-001.su: Each player with ball; optional targets on opposite side. No racket initially for younger players.
DRL-SERVE-001.ex:
1) Toss up and catch at full reach (no jump).
2) Step and throw to a big target (underhand then overhand).
3) Add racket: shadow serve motion with pause at trophy; then gentle serve from service line.
DRL-SERVE-001.cu:
• 'Reach tall.'
• 'Throw up then forward.'
• 'Front shoulder points at target.'
DRL-SERVE-001.ef:
• Toss too far → set toss 'window' cone in front.
• Sidearm throw → cue 'elbow leads, hand follows'.
• Jumping wildly → require 'feet stay down' until consistent.
DRL-SERVE-001.rg: Use balloon; toss lower; throw from closer distance.
DRL-SERVE-001.pg: Add accuracy scoring; add serve to deuce/ad boxes; add pre-serve routine (bounce-breath).
DRL-SERVE-001.sm: Consistent toss into window; improved throw accuracy; smoother serve rhythm.
DRL-SERVE-001.sf: Shoulder safety: keep volume low for young kids; stop if pain; emphasize smooth rhythm.

DRL-SERVE-002.n: Underhand Serve Start Game (Start the Point)
DRL-SERVE-002.o: Teach point initiation with high success and early return habits.
DRL-SERVE-002.su: Pairs. Server underhand serves from service line to a big target zone. Receiver starts in ready position.
DRL-SERVE-002.ex:
1) Underhand serve into box.
2) Receiver catches after bounce OR blocks back gently (depending on stage).
3) Play point out to 3 shots max, then reset.
4) Rotate server/receiver every 6 points.
DRL-SERVE-002.cu:
• 'Serve to the island.'
• 'Receiver: split then step.'
• 'Keep it simple: big targets.'
DRL-SERVE-002.ef:
• Missed serves → move closer; bigger target.
• Receiver frozen → add 'split-step clap' cue.
• Too long points → cap at 3–5 shots for success.
DRL-SERVE-002.pg: Move serve back; require return beyond service line; play full point to 7.
DRL-SERVE-002.sm: High serve-in rate; improved readiness for points; reduced fear of serving.
DRL-SERVE-002.sf: Keep points short for young kids to avoid fatigue and chaos.

DRL-SERVE-010.o: Build reliable serve rhythm and placement with progressive target difficulty.
DRL-SERVE-010.su: Mark 3 targets in service box: wide, body, T (big cones/rectangles). Use a serve scorecard: hit each target 3 times.
DRL-SERVE-010.ex:
1) Serve 10 balls aiming wide target. Count hits.
2) Serve 10 balls aiming body target.
3) Serve 10 balls aiming T.
4) If athlete hits 3/10 in a target, shrink target for next session; if <2/10, enlarge and focus routine.
DRL-SERVE-010.cu:
• 'Same routine every time.'
• 'Toss to the window.'
• 'Smooth acceleration.'
DRL-SERVE-010.ef:
• Double faults → move forward; reduce speed; focus toss.
• Missing wide → cue 'hit up and out' and align shoulders.
• Toss inconsistency → use toss target (hula hoop).
DRL-SERVE-010.rg: Serve from service line; underhand start; bigger targets.
DRL-SERVE-010.pg: Add pressure: must hit target on score 30-30; add second serve shape.
DRL-SERVE-010.sm: Serve-in % increases and target hit % trends upward across weeks.
DRL-SERVE-010.sf: Limit serve volume for young shoulders; ensure warm-up and rest.

DRL-SERVE-011.o: Train first-strike pattern: serve to target, anticipate return, execute +1 to open court.
DRL-SERVE-011.su: Player A serves; Player B returns crosscourt to a big target. Mark +1 target to opposite corner.
DRL-SERVE-011.ex:
1) A serves to planned target (e.g., wide).
2) B returns crosscourt (or coach feeds return at first).
3) A hits +1 to open court target.
4) Play out point for 2 more shots max.
5) Switch after 10 serves each side.
DRL-SERVE-011.cu:
• 'Serve to earn a predictable return.'
• 'Recover fast and load early.'
• '+1 is heavy to the open court.'
DRL-SERVE-011.ef:
• Late to +1 → use 3-step recovery habit; reduce return pace.
• +1 goes for too much → require big target first.
• Serve misses → move target bigger; focus routine.
DRL-SERVE-011.rg: Coach feeds return; +1 to middle target.
DRL-SERVE-011.pg: Return becomes live; require +1 to specific zone; add score pressure.
DRL-SERVE-011.sm: Higher pattern execution rate; improved +1 depth; more points finished early.
DRL-SERVE-011.sf: Keep serve volumes appropriate; rotate servers to manage fatigue.

DRL-SERVE-020.o: Improve serve reliability + placement under a structured pressure protocol.
DRL-SERVE-020.su: Create 3 targets per box (wide/body/T). Athlete serves 5 balls to each target (deuce then ad) = 30 serves total. Track in% and target hits.
DRL-SERVE-020.ex:
1) Athlete runs full routine each serve.
2) Serve 5 to wide, 5 to body, 5 to T (deuce).
3) Repeat on ad side.
4) Pressure rule: if athlete misses 3 in a row, they must do 10-second reset and re-start that target set.
DRL-SERVE-020.cu:
• 'Routine wins.'
• 'Toss to the same window.'
• 'Hit shape to target (not just speed).'
DRL-SERVE-020.ef:
• Target obsession reduces in% → separate metrics and scale target size.
• Tightness under pressure → add breath + longer exhale.
• Toss drift → toss marker drill.
DRL-SERVE-020.rg: 20-ball protocol; bigger targets; serve from 1 step inside baseline for younger.
DRL-SERVE-020.pg: Add second serve requirement; add scoreboard: down 30-40 must hit T; add serve+1 after each make.
DRL-SERVE-020.sm: First serve in% and target hit% trend upward; reduced 'clusters' of misses.
DRL-SERVE-020.sf: Serve volume management; stop if shoulder pain; prioritize quality.
DRL-SERVE-020.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-SERVE-021.o: Build a safe, repeatable second serve with intentional spin and target.
DRL-SERVE-021.su: Mark a 'second serve safe box' target (middle 60% of service box). Optionally mark net clearance height (rope).
DRL-SERVE-021.ex:
1) Hit 10 kick/slice second serves to safe box.
2) If 7/10 in, shrink target slightly; if <6/10, move forward 1 step and focus shape.
3) Add 5 serves to body as variation.
4) Finish with 5 'scoreboard' serves: simulate 30-40 and must make.
DRL-SERVE-021.cu:
• 'Shape over speed.'
• 'Brush and reach.'
• 'High net clearance.'
DRL-SERVE-021.ef:
• Second serve into net → cue higher toss/arc and more brush.
• Double fault streaks → reset routine; simplify target.
• Over-rotating → focus on balance and landing.
DRL-SERVE-021.rg: Serve from service line; use slice only; bigger targets.
DRL-SERVE-021.pg: Add returner; play point live after second serve; require +1 pattern.
DRL-SERVE-021.sm: Second serve in% improves; athlete trusts second serve under pressure.
DRL-SERVE-021.sf: Shoulder safety; avoid excessive volume; ensure warm-up.

DRL-SERVE-030.o: Add variability to serve placement while maintaining routine and shape.
DRL-SERVE-030.su: Create a randomizer: draw cards (W/B/T) or use phone spinner. Mark targets accordingly.
DRL-SERVE-030.ex:
1) Athlete draws target.
2) Executes full routine and serves to target.
3) Track in% and target hit%.
4) After 10 serves, increase pressure: two misses in a row = reset routine + 10-second pause.
DRL-SERVE-030.cu:
• 'Routine stays constant; target changes.'
• 'Hit shape to target.'
• 'Breathe before motion.'
DRL-SERVE-030.ef:
• Rushing after draw → enforce pause.
• Target misses → adjust target size and aim window.
• Tight arm → cue smooth acceleration.
DRL-SERVE-030.pg: Add serve+1 after each serve; add score-based randomizer (choose target based on score).
DRL-SERVE-030.sm: Stable serve in% with improved adaptability and placement.

DRL-TACT-040.o: Build inside-out forehand pattern with correct footwork, spacing, and recovery to avoid leaving court exposed.
DRL-TACT-040.su: Coach feeds to backhand corner; athlete runs around to hit inside-out forehand to crosscourt target. Mark recovery cone toward center.
DRL-TACT-040.ex:
1) Feed to BH corner.
2) Athlete uses crossover/drop step to run around.
3) Hit inside-out FH to target.
4) Recover with 3 steps to cone.
5) Repeat 8–12 reps, then play live point starting from same feed.
DRL-TACT-040.cu:
• 'Create space.'
• 'Inside-out to big target.'
• 'Recover fast; don't admire.'
DRL-TACT-040.ef:
• Getting jammed → start run earlier; bigger spacing.
• Leaving court open → enforce recovery cone touch.
• Going for too much angle → big target first.
DRL-TACT-040.rg: Feed slower; start closer; hit to middle target.
DRL-TACT-040.pg: Add inside-in option on short ball; add pressure scoring; add opponent counter.
DRL-TACT-040.sm: More reliable inside-out pattern without exposing court; improved recovery speed.
DRL-TACT-040.sf: High lateral load; manage reps and rest.
DRL-TACT-040.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-TACT-041.n: Backhand Cross → DTL Ladder (Earn the Line)
DRL-TACT-041.o: Improve backhand crosscourt stability and disciplined DTL changes from the right ball.
DRL-TACT-041.su: Crosscourt rally on backhand side. Mark crosscourt target and DTL gate.
DRL-TACT-041.ex:
1) Hit 8 crosscourt BHs in target to unlock 1 DTL attempt.
2) If DTL goes in, point becomes live.
3) If DTL misses, reset ladder.
4) Rotate after 6 minutes.
DRL-TACT-041.cu:
• 'Earn the line.'
• 'DTL only from balance.'
• 'Aim higher over the net on DTL.'
DRL-TACT-041.ef:
• DTL into net → raise aim and add spin.
• Crosscourt drift → tighten target and stance.
• Late contact → early unit turn emphasis.
DRL-TACT-041.rg: Unlock after 4; allow 2 DTL attempts; bigger gate.
DRL-TACT-041.pg: Require DTL only on short ball; add pressure scoring.
DRL-TACT-041.sm: Better BH consistency and smarter direction changes with fewer errors.
DRL-TACT-041.sf: Monitor volume on BH for developing athletes; alternate sides.
DRL-TACT-041.st: STG-09-11_GREEN, STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-TECH-BH-001.o: Create early backhand shape with two-hand stability and directional intent.
DRL-TECH-BH-001.su: Players with two-handed backhand grip; big target in opposite service box.
DRL-TECH-BH-001.ex:
1) Coach underhand toss to backhand side; player catches ball lightly on strings (trap) then pushes it over mini-net to target.
2) Progress to bounce-hit (no trap) using compact swing.
3) Add footwork: 'turn + step' before contact.
4) Score: 5 pushes to target then 5 bounce-hits.
DRL-TECH-BH-001.cu:
• 'Two hands, one unit.'
• 'Turn your shoulders.'
• 'Step, then push through.'
DRL-TECH-BH-001.ef:
• Arms separate → cue 'glue hands together'.
• No shoulder turn → show 'logo to the fence'.
• Hitting with wrists → cue 'quiet wrists'.
DRL-TECH-BH-001.rg: Trap and push only; use foam ball; shorter distance.
DRL-TECH-BH-001.pg: Add topspin brush; require target zones; alternate FH/BH every ball.
DRL-TECH-BH-001.sm: Stable two-hand contact with predictable direction; improved readiness to rally.
DRL-TECH-BH-001.sf: Use slower balls; avoid overloading with long lines.

DRL-TECH-BH-010.n: Backhand Contact Window (Cone Gate + Unit Turn)
DRL-TECH-BH-010.o: Build reliable backhand contact with early unit turn and stable posture.
DRL-TECH-BH-010.su: Cone gate in contact zone. Coach feeds to BH side. Target zone crosscourt deep-middle.
DRL-TECH-BH-010.ex:
1) Split step.
2) Unit turn (shoulders/hips) and set spacing.
3) Contact through gate and finish.
4) Repeat 10–12 reps, then add one 'change direction' ball to DTL.
DRL-TECH-BH-010.cu:
• 'Turn the shoulders early.'
• 'Create space; contact in front.'
• 'Finish balanced.'
DRL-TECH-BH-010.ef:
• Jammed contact → increase spacing cue; start farther from ball.
• Late turn → coach calls 'turn' at feed.
• Slice-only default → require 70% topspin reps.
DRL-TECH-BH-010.rg: Trap and push for younger; slower feed.
DRL-TECH-BH-010.pg: Add movement feed; add return-style feed; shrink target.
DRL-TECH-BH-010.sm: Improved BH rally stability; better spacing; fewer frame hits.
DRL-TECH-BH-010.sf: Limit volume for developing shoulders; emphasize smooth acceleration.

DRL-TECH-FH-001.o: Develop consistent contact point and simple swing path to a large target.
DRL-TECH-FH-001.su: Targets: 2 large cones/hoops in opposite service box. Players start 6–10 feet from mini-net. One ball each or coach feed.
DRL-TECH-FH-001.ex:
1) Player drops ball, lets it bounce, and hits forehand over mini-net into big target zone.
2) Collect and repeat; aim for 10 quality reps.
3) Add movement: start behind cone, shuffle to contact spot, then hit.
4) Scoring: 1 point if ball lands in target; first to 7.
DRL-TECH-FH-001.cu:
• 'Turn, drop, hit.'
• 'Contact in front.'
• 'Finish to the shoulder.'
• External cue: 'Brush the ball up the mountain' (topspin feel).
DRL-TECH-FH-001.ef:
• Late contact → move closer; cue 'hit earlier'.
• Over-swing → cue 'smooth, not hard'; use slower ball.
• Ball into net → raise target zone, cue 'hit up'.
DRL-TECH-FH-001.rg: Allow catch after bounce then gentle push; use foam ball; shorten distance.
DRL-TECH-FH-001.pg: Coach feeds; require 3-in-a-row to advance; shrink target; add crosscourt vs DTL targets.
DRL-TECH-FH-001.sm: Increasing target hits and cleaner contact; fewer mishits; more consistent height over net.
DRL-TECH-FH-001.sf: Ensure safe spacing; players retrieve balls after 'freeze' call.

DRL-TECH-FH-010.o: Stabilize contact point and swing path while moving to the ball.
DRL-TECH-FH-010.su: Place a 'contact gate' of two cones 18–24 inches apart at contact zone. Coach feeds or player self-feeds. Target zone crosscourt.
DRL-TECH-FH-010.ex:
1) Athlete starts in ready. Coach feeds moderate ball.
2) Athlete turns and steps so contact happens through cone gate.
3) Finish high and recover to home base.
4) 10 balls, then switch to 'moving feed' (wider).
DRL-TECH-FH-010.cu:
• 'Turn first, then step.'
• 'Contact in front through the gate.'
• 'Finish to the shoulder; recover.'
DRL-TECH-FH-010.ef:
• Late contact → widen gate and move it forward.
• Over-rotation → cue 'finish on balance'.
• Arm-only swing → cue 'hips then arm'.
DRL-TECH-FH-010.rg: Shorter distance; slower feed; bounce-hit from drop.
DRL-TECH-FH-010.pg: Randomize feed depth; require target zone; add 2-ball sequence (FH then BH).
DRL-TECH-FH-010.sm: Higher contact consistency; fewer shanks; improved balance and recovery steps.
DRL-TECH-FH-010.sf: Keep feeding pace appropriate; avoid excessive volume if fatigue increases.

DRL-TECH-LOB-001.o: Use the offensive lob as a tactical option against aggressive net players while maintaining recovery discipline.
DRL-TECH-LOB-001.su: 4 players in doubles formation. Poacher starts active. Mark deep lob target zones (last 3 ft).
DRL-TECH-LOB-001.ex:
1) Coach feeds neutral ball to baseliner.
2) Baseliner chooses either drive crosscourt or offensive lob over poacher.
3) If lob chosen, must land deep and allow team to reset positions.
4) Play out point.
DRL-TECH-LOB-001.cu:
• 'Lob must be high AND deep.'
• 'Sell drive, then lift.'
• 'Recover after lob.'
DRL-TECH-LOB-001.ef:
• Lob short → aim higher; more lift; use topspin lob.
• Lob too flat → emphasize arc.
• Players forget recovery → assign cone home spots.
DRL-TECH-LOB-001.rg: Cooperative lob practice without live point.
DRL-TECH-LOB-001.pg: Poacher reads and moves; add signals; add pressure scoring.
DRL-TECH-LOB-001.sm: Higher deep-lob percentage and better doubles problem-solving.
DRL-TECH-LOB-001.sf: Net player safety with overheads; manage overhead volume.

DRL-WUP-001.o: Build physical literacy (run, hop, balance, rotate) while establishing attention + safety routines.
DRL-WUP-001.su: Create 4–6 stations in a loop: (1) hopscotch dots, (2) cone weave, (3) balance beam (tape line), (4) two-hand throw to target, (5) sprint to 'home base' spot. Use visual cues (animal pictures).
DRL-WUP-001.ex:
1) Coach demonstrates each station with an 'animal'.
2) Players travel the loop for 2–3 rounds.
3) Add a simple command: when coach calls 'Freeze!' players stop in athletic stance.
4) Finish with a 20-second 'fast feet' and then a calm breath.
DRL-WUP-001.cu:
• 'Quiet feet on the line.'
• 'Eyes forward, soft knees.'
• 'Freeze like a statue: racket up / hands ready.'
• Use stories: 'cheetah sprint', 'flamingo balance'.
DRL-WUP-001.ef:
• Chaos/spacing → set clear start spots and one-way traffic.
• Falling off balance line → widen the line or allow 'toe touches'.
• Throwing wildly → use bigger target, shorter distance, two-hand chest pass.
DRL-WUP-001.rg: Reduce stations; remove timing; allow walking; use bigger targets; no racket required.
DRL-WUP-001.pg: Add a ball carry; add reaction cue (color cone to touch); add backward shuffles; add 'catch then go' before next station.
DRL-WUP-001.sm: Players complete loop with control (no collisions), can freeze on cue, improved balance and attention over rounds.
DRL-WUP-001.sf: Maintain spacing; remove trip hazards; keep intensity appropriate; stop if a child shows fatigue or distress.
DRL-WUP-001.ct: Off-court lane or inside service boxes with cones

DRL-WUP-010.n: Dynamic Warm-up + Footwork Ladder Primer (ABC → Tennis)
DRL-WUP-010.o: Prime joints and movement patterns; reinforce deceleration and first-step mechanics.
DRL-WUP-010.su: Set 1 agility ladder, 4 cones for shuffle/crossover patterns, and 2 reaction cones (color).
DRL-WUP-010.ex:
1) 3 minutes dynamic mobility (ankles/hips/T-spine).
2) Ladder patterns (2 rounds): in-in-out-out, lateral, icky shuffle.
3) Cone COD: shuffle-shuffle-crossover, plant and recover.
4) Reaction: coach calls color; athlete sprints 3 steps then split-step.
DRL-WUP-010.cu:
• 'Low hips, quiet upper body.'
• 'Push the ground away.'
• 'Stick the landing on decel.'
• 'Split on cue.'
DRL-WUP-010.ef:
• Heavy steps → cue 'quiet feet'; reduce speed.
• Upright posture → cue 'nose over toes'.
• Sloppy decel → add 1-second hold at plant.
DRL-WUP-010.rg: Remove ladder; use simple line hops; reduce intensity.
DRL-WUP-010.pg: Add racket shadow swings after ladder; add ball feed immediately after reaction.
DRL-WUP-010.sm: Improved movement quality and readiness; fewer early-session mishits due to poor footwork.
DRL-WUP-010.sf: Quality > speed. Stop if pain. Hydration and footwear check.

DRL-WUP-040.o: Prepare shoulders for serving and overhead work; reduce injury risk.
DRL-WUP-040.su: Light bands. 3 exercises: band pull-aparts, external rotations, Y-T-W scap sequence.
DRL-WUP-040.ex:
1) 12 pull-aparts.
2) 10 external rotations each side.
3) 6 Y-T-W each.
4) Repeat once. Finish with 5 shadow serves (slow).
