DRL-WUP-040.ef:
• Using heavy band → lighter band.
• Shrugging → cue shoulders down/back.
• Speeding through → tempo count.
DRL-WUP-040.pg: Add overhead band work; integrate into prehab circuit.
DRL-WUP-040.sm: Shoulders feel warm and stable; reduced early-session serving discomfort.
DRL-WUP-040.sf: General guidance only; stop if pain; consult medical professional for injury.
DRL-WUP-040.st: STG-09-11_GREEN, STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-ADULT_COMP, STG-09-13_HP, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT

DRL-ADULT-001-V1.n: 2-Pattern Points (Plan A / Plan B for Adults) — Simplified
DRL-ADULT-001-V1.o: Give adult competitors 2 simple patterns that win at their level and practice switching under pressure.
DRL-ADULT-001-V1.su: Define Pattern A and Pattern B (e.g., A: heavy crosscourt to backhand; B: serve body then +1 to open). Write on whiteboard. Points start with serve.
DRL-ADULT-001-V1.ex:
1) Play points to 11.
2) Odd points: must attempt Pattern A.
3) Even points: must attempt Pattern B.
4) Coach tags pattern appearance and UFEs.
5) Debrief: which pattern is more reliable?
DRL-ADULT-001-V1.ef:
• Over-complication → simplify patterns to 2 shots.
• Pattern forgotten → verbalize before serve.
• Too aggressive → enforce big targets and net clearance.
DRL-ADULT-001-V1.rg: Only Pattern A for full game.
DRL-ADULT-001-V1.pg: Add opponent style constraints; add time clock; start games down 0-3.
DRL-ADULT-001-V1.sm: Higher pattern execution rate and reduced unforced errors.
DRL-ADULT-001-V1.sf: Adults: monitor injury risk; include warm-up and mobility.

DRL-ADULT-002-V1.n: Serve+1 / Return+1 Game (First 4 Shots Domination) — Simplified
DRL-ADULT-002-V1.o: Prioritize the highest leverage phase: first 4 shots. Force intentionality on serve/return and next ball.
DRL-ADULT-002-V1.su: Play points but stop after 4 shots and award point based on advantage: Winner/Forced error = point; Neutral = replay; UFE = opponent.
DRL-ADULT-002-V1.ex:
1) Start points with serve.
2) Count shots: serve, return, +1, +1.
3) Stop at shot 4 if neutral; replay (keeps reps high).
4) Track 1st serve in%, return depth%, and pattern appearance.
DRL-ADULT-002-V1.cu:
• 'Own the first four.'
• 'Deep return.'
• '+1 with margin.'
DRL-ADULT-002-V1.ef:
• Players frustrated by replays → explain rep goal; keep tempo.
• Too many UFEs early → bigger targets, simplify patterns.
DRL-ADULT-002-V1.pg: Play full point after 4 shots; add pressure scoring.
DRL-ADULT-002-V1.sm: Improved serve/return KPIs and better early-point execution.
DRL-ADULT-002-V1.sf: High intensity; manage rest and hydration.

DRL-ADULT-003-V1.o: Improve doubles communication, shifting, and coverage responsibilities.
DRL-ADULT-003-V1.su: 4 players. Start crosscourt 'crossfire' rally: deuce-court crosscourt only. Net players must call 'mine/yours' and 'switch' on lobs.
DRL-ADULT-003-V1.ex:
1) Start cooperative crosscourt.
2) After 6 balls, make it live.
3) Rule: each team must make a verbal call every point (mine/yours/switch).
4) Rotate after 10 points.
DRL-ADULT-003-V1.cu:
• 'Talk early.'
• 'Shift with the ball.'
• 'Cover the line when partner moves.'
DRL-ADULT-003-V1.ef:
• Silence → enforce point loss if no call.
• Both go for ball → define priority (forehand/middle).
• Net player drifting → mark net home base spot.
DRL-ADULT-003-V1.rg: No live points; cooperative only with calls.
DRL-ADULT-003-V1.pg: Add poach signals; add Australian/I formation sequences.
DRL-ADULT-003-V1.sm: More consistent communication and fewer doubles confusion errors.
DRL-ADULT-003-V1.sf: Racket clashes: define spacing and calling priority.

DRL-ANALYTICS-020-V1.n: Stats-Tagged Set Play (Serve/Return + Pattern Tags) — Simplified
DRL-ANALYTICS-020-V1.o: Turn match play into development by tagging key stats and patterns.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020-V1.su: Play a short set (first to 4 games). Coach/assistant tags: 1st serve in%, return depth%, pattern appeared, UFEs by pattern.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020-V1.ex:
1) Brief pre-set goal: 1 pattern + 1 KPI.
2) Play set.
3) Between changeovers: 30-second coaching on KPI only.
4) Post-set: review tag sheet and choose next session focus.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020-V1.cu:
• 'Plan before point.'
• 'Deep returns.'
• 'Pattern first, improvise later.'
DRL-ANALYTICS-020-V1.ef:
• Too much feedback → constrain coaching to one KPI.
• Players forget plan → write plan on towel/whiteboard.
• Tagging inconsistency → standardize tags and definitions.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020-V1.pg: Add opponent scouting notes; add video clips; expand stats set.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020-V1.sm: Clear KPI trends and actionable next-step focus from set play.
DRL-ANALYTICS-020-V1.sf: Match intensity: monitor heat, hydration, and injury risk.

DRL-ANALYTICS-040-V1.o: Turn video into a habit: tag clips to KPIs and convert into next session action items.
DRL-ANALYTICS-040-V1.su: Capture 10–15 minutes of serve/return or match play. Use tagging sheet: serve routine, return depth, pattern adherence, reset routine.
DRL-ANALYTICS-040-V1.ex:
1) Watch 10 clips.
2) Tag each clip with 1 KPI label.
3) Identify 1 technical cue + 1 tactical decision rule.
4) Write next-session drill prescription (IDs).
DRL-ANALYTICS-040-V1.cu:
• 'One cue, one rule.'
• 'Tag, then act.'
• 'Keep it short.'
DRL-ANALYTICS-040-V1.ef:
• Overanalyzing → timebox to 20 minutes.
• Too many cues → choose the one with biggest ROI.
• No action plan → require drill prescription output.
DRL-ANALYTICS-040-V1.pg: Athlete self-tags; compare coach vs athlete tags; integrate into RAG research notes.
DRL-ANALYTICS-040-V1.sm: Consistent video habit and clear carryover into practice plan.
DRL-ANALYTICS-040-V1.sf: Ensure privacy/permissions for recording; follow facility policies.

DRL-COMP-010-V1.n: Points to 7 — One Constraint (Skill Transfer) — Simplified
DRL-COMP-010-V1.o: Transfer a single skill focus into live points with simple scoring and high engagement.
DRL-COMP-010-V1.su: Choose ONE constraint aligned to the day: e.g., 'crosscourt only', 'must hit deep middle on defense', 'serve to body'.
DRL-COMP-010-V1.ex:
1) Play points to 7.
2) If a player violates constraint, point counts for opponent OR replay (choose based on level).
3) Rotate opponents every 7 points.
4) Coach tracks one KPI during play.
DRL-COMP-010-V1.cu:
• 'One thing today.'
• 'Commit to the rule.'
• 'Reset and go again.'
DRL-COMP-010-V1.ef:
• Too many rules → remove extras; keep one.
• Players gaming the rule → clarify allowed shots.
• Low engagement → shorten games and rotate more.
DRL-COMP-010-V1.rg: Cooperative points; larger court scaling; underhand serve start.
DRL-COMP-010-V1.pg: Add score deficits (start 0-3); add time clock; add consequences.
DRL-COMP-010-V1.sm: Players demonstrate skill in live context; KPI improves over multiple rounds.
DRL-COMP-010-V1.sf: Ensure intensity appropriate; rotate rest and hydration.

DRL-COMP-010-V2.n: Points to 7 — One Constraint (Skill Transfer) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-COMP-010-V2.o: Transfer a single skill focus into live points with simple scoring and high engagement.
DRL-COMP-010-V2.su: Choose ONE constraint aligned to the day: e.g., 'crosscourt only', 'must hit deep middle on defense', 'serve to body'.
DRL-COMP-010-V2.ex:
1) Play points to 7.
2) If a player violates constraint, point counts for opponent OR replay (choose based on level).
3) Rotate opponents every 7 points.
4) Coach tracks one KPI during play.
DRL-COMP-010-V2.cu:
• 'One thing today.'
• 'Commit to the rule.'
• 'Reset and go again.'
DRL-COMP-010-V2.ef:
• Too many rules → remove extras; keep one.
• Players gaming the rule → clarify allowed shots.
• Low engagement → shorten games and rotate more.
DRL-COMP-010-V2.rg: Cooperative points; larger court scaling; underhand serve start.
DRL-COMP-010-V2.pg: Add score deficits (start 0-3); add time clock; add consequences.
DRL-COMP-010-V2.sm: Players demonstrate skill in live context; KPI improves over multiple rounds.
DRL-COMP-010-V2.sf: Ensure intensity appropriate; rotate rest and hydration.

DRL-COMP-030-V1.n: Short Set Starting Down 0–30 (Scoreboard Management) — Simplified
DRL-COMP-030-V1.o: Train calm decision-making when behind in games and reduce panic errors.
DRL-COMP-030-V1.su: Play short sets to 4 games. Every service game starts at 0-30 for the server.
DRL-COMP-030-V1.ex:
1) Start each game at 0-30.
2) Server focuses on high percentage targets and routine.
3) Coach tracks: first serve in%, UFEs, and routine compliance.
4) Debrief after set: what changed under pressure?
DRL-COMP-030-V1.ef:
• Panic aggression → enforce deep-middle rule on neutral balls.
• Double faults → move forward and prioritize 2nd serve shape.
• Emotional tilt → use reset routine.
DRL-COMP-030-V1.pg: Start at 0-40; add time clock; add consequence ladder.
DRL-COMP-030-V1.sm: Reduced UFEs when behind; improved break point conversion and MPI stability.
DRL-COMP-030-V1.sf: High stress and load; balance with recovery and positive reinforcement.

DRL-COMP-030-V2.n: Short Set Starting Down 0–30 (Scoreboard Management) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-COMP-030-V2.o: Train calm decision-making when behind in games and reduce panic errors.
DRL-COMP-030-V2.su: Play short sets to 4 games. Every service game starts at 0-30 for the server.
DRL-COMP-030-V2.ex:
1) Start each game at 0-30.
2) Server focuses on high percentage targets and routine.
3) Coach tracks: first serve in%, UFEs, and routine compliance.
4) Debrief after set: what changed under pressure?
DRL-COMP-030-V2.ef:
• Panic aggression → enforce deep-middle rule on neutral balls.
• Double faults → move forward and prioritize 2nd serve shape.
• Emotional tilt → use reset routine.
DRL-COMP-030-V2.pg: Start at 0-40; add time clock; add consequence ladder.
DRL-COMP-030-V2.sm: Reduced UFEs when behind; improved break point conversion and MPI stability.
DRL-COMP-030-V2.sf: High stress and load; balance with recovery and positive reinforcement.

DRL-COMP-040-V1.n: Serve Spots Only Set Play (Tactical Serving Discipline) — Simplified
DRL-COMP-040-V1.o: Force serve discipline: choose spot with intent and execute under match scoring.
DRL-COMP-040-V1.su: Play a short set. Server must call serve spot (W/B/T) before serving. If serve does not land in called zone, point is replayed (or lost based on level).
DRL-COMP-040-V1.ex:
1) Start set.
2) Before each serve, call spot.
3) Track called vs hit.
4) After set, review serve distribution and effectiveness.
DRL-COMP-040-V1.cu:
• 'Call it, then own it.'
• 'Routine first.'
• 'Use body serve under pressure.'
DRL-COMP-040-V1.ef:
• Too strict for level → use replay instead of point loss.
• Serve clusters of misses → enlarge zones and reduce speed.
DRL-COMP-040-V1.rg: Call only side (deuce/ad), not exact zone.
DRL-COMP-040-V1.pg: Add second serve call; add serve+1 requirement.
DRL-COMP-040-V1.sm: Improved serve placement intent and reduced random serving.

DRL-COMP-040-V2.n: Serve Spots Only Set Play (Tactical Serving Discipline) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-COMP-040-V2.o: Force serve discipline: choose spot with intent and execute under match scoring.
DRL-COMP-040-V2.su: Play a short set. Server must call serve spot (W/B/T) before serving. If serve does not land in called zone, point is replayed (or lost based on level).
DRL-COMP-040-V2.ex:
1) Start set.
2) Before each serve, call spot.
3) Track called vs hit.
4) After set, review serve distribution and effectiveness.
DRL-COMP-040-V2.cu:
• 'Call it, then own it.'
• 'Routine first.'
• 'Use body serve under pressure.'
DRL-COMP-040-V2.ef:
• Too strict for level → use replay instead of point loss.
• Serve clusters of misses → enlarge zones and reduce speed.
DRL-COMP-040-V2.rg: Call only side (deuce/ad), not exact zone.
DRL-COMP-040-V2.pg: Add second serve call; add serve+1 requirement.
DRL-COMP-040-V2.sm: Improved serve placement intent and reduced random serving.

DRL-DBL-020-V1.n: Poach Decision Game (Read the Receiver) — Simplified
DRL-DBL-020-V1.o: Train poach timing and decision-making with clear cues and communication.
DRL-DBL-020-V1.su: 4 players: server, returner, net player, opponent net. Net player has 2 options: stay or poach. Use hand signals.
DRL-DBL-020-V1.ex:
1) Server serves to planned target.
2) Returner returns crosscourt.
3) Net player decides to poach or stay based on return quality and cues.
4) Play out point. Score net player's decision correctness (not just outcome).
5) Rotate positions every 6 points.
DRL-DBL-020-V1.cu:
• 'Poach on a weak return.'
• 'Stay on a strong return.'
• 'Signal early; commit.'
DRL-DBL-020-V1.ef:
• Random poaching → define cues (slow/floaty return).
• Late move → start earlier and split-step.
• Poor communication → enforce signals and partner calls.
DRL-DBL-020-V1.rg: Coach calls poach/stay; return is fed predictably.
DRL-DBL-020-V1.pg: Add Australian formation; add fake poach; add return variety.
DRL-DBL-020-V1.sm: Higher correct decision rate; improved net aggression with discipline.
DRL-DBL-020-V1.sf: Net player safety; avoid racket clashes; enforce spacing.

DRL-DBL-021-V1.n: Doubles Serve + First Volley Pattern (Serve & Close) — Simplified
DRL-DBL-021-V1.o: Develop serve-and-volley patterns and partner movement coordination.
DRL-DBL-021-V1.su: 4 players. Server starts, partner at net. Mark serve target and first volley target (deep middle).
DRL-DBL-021-V1.ex:
1) Server serves and immediately closes.
2) Returner returns crosscourt.
3) Server hits first volley to deep middle, partner adjusts.
4) Play out point. Repeat 8 points then rotate.
DRL-DBL-021-V1.cu:
• 'Serve then close hard.'
• 'First volley deep middle.'
• 'Partner shifts with the ball.'
DRL-DBL-021-V1.ef:
• Server not closing → add sprint to service line before volley.
• First volley too low → cue 'punch through'.
• Partner static → teach 'shift and cover line'.
DRL-DBL-021-V1.rg: Coach hand-feeds return; shorter court start.
DRL-DBL-021-V1.pg: Add poach; add serve variety; add Australian/I formation options.
DRL-DBL-021-V1.sm: Higher net conversion; improved partner coordination and positioning.

DRL-DBL-040-V1.o: Train doubles serving formations and communication to create confusion and first-strike advantages.
DRL-DBL-040-V1.su: Server and net partner practice signals (poach/stay; direction). Use Australian and I-formation positioning.
DRL-DBL-040-V1.ex:
1) Run 8 serves in Australian: serve target + net partner movement.
2) Run 8 serves in I-formation: net player crouches center, moves based on signal.
3) Add returner and play out point.
4) Rotate roles.
DRL-DBL-040-V1.cu:
• 'Signal early and commit.'
• 'Serve target supports formation.'
• 'Partner shifts with movement.'
DRL-DBL-040-V1.ef:
• Confusion on signals → simplify to 2 signals first.
• Net player late → start movement earlier.
• Server misses targets → enlarge target and reduce speed.
DRL-DBL-040-V1.pg: Add fake signals; add return variety; add pressure scoring.
DRL-DBL-040-V1.sm: Cleaner communication and successful formation execution under live returns.
DRL-DBL-040-V1.sf: Net safety; communication to avoid collisions.

DRL-FITNESS-030-V2.n: Prehab Circuit (Shoulder + Core + Hips) — 10 Minutes — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-FITNESS-030-V2.o: Reduce common tennis overuse risk by strengthening rotator cuff/scap/core/hips.
DRL-FITNESS-030-V2.su: Bands + mini-bands + light med ball. 3 stations: (1) band external rotations, (2) scap retractions, (3) dead bug or Pallof press, (4) hip airplanes or lateral band walks.
DRL-FITNESS-030-V2.ex:
1) 30 seconds work / 15 seconds rest per station.
2) 2 rounds.
3) Finish with 60 seconds breathing + thoracic rotation.
DRL-FITNESS-030-V2.cu:
• 'Control, not speed.'
• 'Ribs down, breathe.'
• 'Feel shoulder blade move.'
DRL-FITNESS-030-V2.ef:
• Using too heavy band → reduce resistance.
• Poor posture → coach sets alignment.
• Rushing → add tempo count.
DRL-FITNESS-030-V2.pg: Add eccentric control; add single-leg balance; add med-ball rotational throws (advanced).
DRL-FITNESS-030-V2.sm: Consistent compliance and improved movement quality; fewer soreness flags over cycles.
DRL-FITNESS-030-V2.sf: General fitness guidance only. For pain/injury, consult qualified medical professional.

DRL-FITNESS-040-V1.o: Improve reaction time and split-step timing with unpredictable bounces.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V1.su: Pairs. One athlete drops reaction ball; other starts in athletic stance with split-step on drop.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V1.ex:
1) Dropper says 'ready' and drops ball.
2) Receiver split-steps as ball hits ground and reacts to catch after bounce.
3) 6 reps then switch.
4) Progress: add lateral start positions or add racket catch on strings.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V1.ef:
• Upright posture → cue low hips.
• Late split → slow down, exaggerate timing.
• Reaching → move feet first.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V1.rg: Use normal tennis ball; predictable bounce.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V1.pg: Add second ball; add sprint to cone after catch; add feed and hit after reaction.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V1.sm: Faster first step and better split timing in returns/rallies.

DRL-FITNESS-040-V2.o: Improve reaction time and split-step timing with unpredictable bounces.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V2.su: Pairs. One athlete drops reaction ball; other starts in athletic stance with split-step on drop.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V2.ex:
1) Dropper says 'ready' and drops ball.
2) Receiver split-steps as ball hits ground and reacts to catch after bounce.
3) 6 reps then switch.
4) Progress: add lateral start positions or add racket catch on strings.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V2.ef:
• Upright posture → cue low hips.
• Late split → slow down, exaggerate timing.
• Reaching → move feet first.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V2.rg: Use normal tennis ball; predictable bounce.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V2.pg: Add second ball; add sprint to cone after catch; add feed and hit after reaction.
DRL-FITNESS-040-V2.sm: Faster first step and better split timing in returns/rallies.

DRL-FITNESS-041-V1.n: Medicine Ball Rotational Throws (Power & Sequencing) — Simplified
DRL-FITNESS-041-V1.o: Develop rotational power and sequencing safely to support serve and groundstroke speed.
DRL-FITNESS-041-V1.su: Wall space or partner. Use appropriate med ball weight. Mark safe throw line.
DRL-FITNESS-041-V1.ex:
1) 6 standing rotational throws each side (hip-shoulder separation).
2) 6 step-in rotational throws each side.
3) 6 overhead slams.
4) Rest as needed; prioritize quality.
DRL-FITNESS-041-V1.cu:
• 'Hips lead, shoulders follow.'
• 'Brace core; exhale on throw.'
• 'Quality reps only.'
DRL-FITNESS-041-V1.ef:
• Using arms only → reduce weight; cue hip drive.
• Losing balance → widen stance; slow down.
• Over-arching back → ribs down; brace.
DRL-FITNESS-041-V1.rg: Lighter ball; no wall; mimic pattern with band.
DRL-FITNESS-041-V1.pg: Add shot-put throws; add reactive catches.
DRL-FITNESS-041-V1.sm: Improved coordination and power without pain; better sequencing carryover to tennis.
DRL-FITNESS-041-V1.sf: General fitness guidance; supervise closely; avoid if pain; keep volume modest.

DRL-FOOT-001-V1.n: Split-Step Freeze Dance (Timing + Ready Position) — Simplified
DRL-FOOT-001-V1.o: Teach split-step timing in a fun, low-threat format.
DRL-FOOT-001-V1.su: Coach plays music or claps rhythm. Players bounce in athletic stance on their home spot.
DRL-FOOT-001-V1.ex:
1) Players move lightly; on clap/stop-music they perform a split step and freeze in ready position.
2) Add ball cue: coach tosses a ball left/right after freeze; player makes 1st step to catch.
3) Repeat 6–10 reps.
DRL-FOOT-001-V1.cu:
• 'Land soft.'
• 'Feet under hips.'
• 'Freeze with racket up.'
DRL-FOOT-001-V1.ef:
• Jumping too high → cue 'small hop'.
• Feet too wide → mark two footprints on court.
• Late reaction → slow down cue, then speed up.
DRL-FOOT-001-V1.pg: Add racket; after freeze, hit a fed ball; add split-step on partner contact in mini rally.
DRL-FOOT-001-V1.sm: More consistent split-step timing and faster first step response.
DRL-FOOT-001-V1.sf: Ensure safe spacing; avoid slippery surfaces.

DRL-GAME-001-V1.o: Increase movement intensity while reinforcing listening, teamwork, and boundaries.
DRL-GAME-001-V1.su: Scatter 30–50 balls on half court. Place 4 'treasure chests' (baskets) in corners. Split into 2 teams.
DRL-GAME-001-V1.ex:
1) On go, players collect one ball at a time and place in their team's basket.
2) Add movement constraints each round: shuffles only, hop to ball, sprint back.
3) After 3 rounds, count treasure; discuss effort + teamwork.
DRL-GAME-001-V1.cu:
• 'One ball at a time.'
• 'Eyes up, avoid collisions.'
• 'Quick feet, soft hands.'
DRL-GAME-001-V1.ef:
• Players grabbing many balls → enforce rule and restart.
• Collisions → assign lanes and 'no crossing' zones.
• Over-excitement → shorten rounds; add calm breath between.
DRL-GAME-001-V1.rg: Smaller area; fewer balls; walking only.
DRL-GAME-001-V1.pg: Add racket carry; add 'collect only red balls' (sorting); add time clock.
DRL-GAME-001-V1.sm: High engagement, good spacing, improved agility and compliance to rules.
DRL-GAME-001-V1.sf: Strict spacing and boundaries; keep baskets away from fences to avoid crashes.

DRL-GAME-001-V2.o: Increase movement intensity while reinforcing listening, teamwork, and boundaries.
DRL-GAME-001-V2.su: Scatter 30–50 balls on half court. Place 4 'treasure chests' (baskets) in corners. Split into 2 teams.
DRL-GAME-001-V2.ex:
1) On go, players collect one ball at a time and place in their team's basket.
2) Add movement constraints each round: shuffles only, hop to ball, sprint back.
3) After 3 rounds, count treasure; discuss effort + teamwork.
DRL-GAME-001-V2.cu:
• 'One ball at a time.'
• 'Eyes up, avoid collisions.'
• 'Quick feet, soft hands.'
DRL-GAME-001-V2.ef:
• Players grabbing many balls → enforce rule and restart.
• Collisions → assign lanes and 'no crossing' zones.
• Over-excitement → shorten rounds; add calm breath between.
DRL-GAME-001-V2.rg: Smaller area; fewer balls; walking only.
DRL-GAME-001-V2.pg: Add racket carry; add 'collect only red balls' (sorting); add time clock.
DRL-GAME-001-V2.sm: High engagement, good spacing, improved agility and compliance to rules.
DRL-GAME-001-V2.sf: Strict spacing and boundaries; keep baskets away from fences to avoid crashes.

DRL-GAME-002-V2.n: King of the Castle (Target Knockdown) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-GAME-002-V2.o: Build aim + controlled power via target games with simple scoring.
DRL-GAME-002-V2.su: Place 3–6 cone 'castles' on far side. Players hit from close distance. Teams or individual.
DRL-GAME-002-V2.ex:
1) Players feed themselves or coach feeds.
2) Each hit that knocks a cone earns 1 point.
3) After score threshold, step back 2 feet.
4) Rotate roles (feed/hit) if in pairs.
DRL-GAME-002-V2.cu:
• 'Aim small, miss small.'
• 'Smooth swing; hit through the target.'
• 'Recover to ready.'
DRL-GAME-002-V2.ef:
• Over-hitting → move closer; lower swing speed.
• Missing wide → add larger target zone; focus on contact.
DRL-GAME-002-V2.rg: Bigger cones; closer; allow two bounces.
DRL-GAME-002-V2.pg: Smaller targets; require crosscourt only; add rally before target shot.
DRL-GAME-002-V2.sm: Improving accuracy over rounds; players can modulate speed to hit targets.
DRL-GAME-002-V2.sf: Ensure targets are safe cones, not hard objects; manage retrieval.

DRL-LIVE-001-V1.o: High-tempo doubles reps without traditional serving; develop reactions and teamwork.
DRL-LIVE-001-V1.su: 4 players per court. Coach feeds in to start point. Rules: challengers need 3 points to replace champions; defenders need 2 points to stay.
DRL-LIVE-001-V1.ex:
1) Coach feeds ball into play.
2) Play point to completion.
3) Keep running score per rules.
4) Rotate players as winners/losers based on format.
5) Coach provides quick tactical cues between points.
DRL-LIVE-001-V1.cu:
• 'Make the first ball.'
• 'High percentage targets.'
• 'Communicate and cover.'
DRL-LIVE-001-V1.ef:
• Chaos in rotation → clearly designate champ/challenger sides.
• Overhitting → reinforce big targets and net clearance.
• Fatigue → add 60-second water break every 15 minutes.
DRL-LIVE-001-V1.rg: Challengers need 2, defenders need 2; slower feeds.
DRL-LIVE-001-V1.sm: High engagement and improvement in doubles positioning/reaction decisions.
DRL-LIVE-001-V1.sf: High intensity; ensure warm-up; manage heat and hydration; screen for injury risk.

DRL-LIVE-001-V2.o: High-tempo doubles reps without traditional serving; develop reactions and teamwork.
DRL-LIVE-001-V2.su: 4 players per court. Coach feeds in to start point. Rules: challengers need 3 points to replace champions; defenders need 2 points to stay.
DRL-LIVE-001-V2.ex:
1) Coach feeds ball into play.
2) Play point to completion.
3) Keep running score per rules.
4) Rotate players as winners/losers based on format.
5) Coach provides quick tactical cues between points.
DRL-LIVE-001-V2.cu:
• 'Make the first ball.'
• 'High percentage targets.'
• 'Communicate and cover.'
DRL-LIVE-001-V2.ef:
• Chaos in rotation → clearly designate champ/challenger sides.
• Overhitting → reinforce big targets and net clearance.
• Fatigue → add 60-second water break every 15 minutes.
DRL-LIVE-001-V2.rg: Challengers need 2, defenders need 2; slower feeds.
DRL-LIVE-001-V2.sm: High engagement and improvement in doubles positioning/reaction decisions.
DRL-LIVE-001-V2.sf: High intensity; ensure warm-up; manage heat and hydration; screen for injury risk.

DRL-MENTAL-001-V1.n: 3-Breath Reset Game (Between-Point Routine for Kids) — Simplified
DRL-MENTAL-001-V1.o: Install a simple reset habit (breath + cue) that keeps sessions positive.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V1.su: Coach teaches: (1) hands on racket, (2) 3 slow breaths, (3) cue word ('next ball').
DRL-MENTAL-001-V1.ex:
1) After any miss in drills/games, player steps to a marked 'reset spot'.
2) Performs 3 breaths and says cue word.
3) Returns to ready stance and continues.
4) Coach reinforces with praise for compliance, not outcome.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V1.cu:
• 'Reset spot, 3 breaths.'
• 'Say it: NEXT BALL.'
• 'Strong body language.'
DRL-MENTAL-001-V1.ef:
• Forgetting routine → coach uses silent hand signal.
• Overthinking → keep it playful: 'blow out candles'.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V1.rg: 1 breath + cue for very young; parent models off-court.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V1.pg: Add visual focus point; add 'plan' (target) after cue for older players.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V1.sm: Fewer emotional spirals; quicker return to task; improved resilience in points.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V1.sf: Keep it supportive; avoid shaming. If a child is distressed, pause and address safety/comfort first.

DRL-MENTAL-001-V2.n: 3-Breath Reset Game (Between-Point Routine for Kids) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-MENTAL-001-V2.o: Install a simple reset habit (breath + cue) that keeps sessions positive.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V2.su: Coach teaches: (1) hands on racket, (2) 3 slow breaths, (3) cue word ('next ball').
DRL-MENTAL-001-V2.ex:
1) After any miss in drills/games, player steps to a marked 'reset spot'.
2) Performs 3 breaths and says cue word.
3) Returns to ready stance and continues.
4) Coach reinforces with praise for compliance, not outcome.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V2.cu:
• 'Reset spot, 3 breaths.'
• 'Say it: NEXT BALL.'
• 'Strong body language.'
DRL-MENTAL-001-V2.ef:
• Forgetting routine → coach uses silent hand signal.
• Overthinking → keep it playful: 'blow out candles'.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V2.rg: 1 breath + cue for very young; parent models off-court.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V2.pg: Add visual focus point; add 'plan' (target) after cue for older players.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V2.sm: Fewer emotional spirals; quicker return to task; improved resilience in points.
DRL-MENTAL-001-V2.sf: Keep it supportive; avoid shaming. If a child is distressed, pause and address safety/comfort first.

DRL-MENTAL-010-V1.o: Create a repeatable routine that stabilizes performance and reduces emotional volatility.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V1.su: Define 3-step routine: (1) physical reset (turn away), (2) breath, (3) plan + cue word. Write on whiteboard.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V1.ex:
1) During any point game, coach tracks compliance.
2) Every time athlete uses full routine, they earn a 'routine point'.
3) First to 8 routine points wins (independent of tennis score).
4) Debrief: what step was hardest?
DRL-MENTAL-010-V1.cu:
• 'Turn away, breathe, plan.'
• 'Cue word: heavy / early / trust.'
• 'Routine points matter.'
DRL-MENTAL-010-V1.ef:
• Skipping steps → start with 2-step routine.
• Overthinking plan → make plan one word.
• Negative self-talk → replace with cue word.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V1.rg: Routine only after errors; 1 breath + cue.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V1.pg: Add pressure scenarios; require routine on every point; integrate 'quiet eye' for serve/return.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V1.sm: Routine compliance % increases and emotional recovery improves.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V1.sf: Avoid punitive tone; routine is skill acquisition.

DRL-MENTAL-010-V2.o: Create a repeatable routine that stabilizes performance and reduces emotional volatility.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V2.su: Define 3-step routine: (1) physical reset (turn away), (2) breath, (3) plan + cue word. Write on whiteboard.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V2.ex:
1) During any point game, coach tracks compliance.
2) Every time athlete uses full routine, they earn a 'routine point'.
3) First to 8 routine points wins (independent of tennis score).
4) Debrief: what step was hardest?
DRL-MENTAL-010-V2.cu:
• 'Turn away, breathe, plan.'
• 'Cue word: heavy / early / trust.'
• 'Routine points matter.'
DRL-MENTAL-010-V2.ef:
• Skipping steps → start with 2-step routine.
• Overthinking plan → make plan one word.
• Negative self-talk → replace with cue word.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V2.rg: Routine only after errors; 1 breath + cue.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V2.pg: Add pressure scenarios; require routine on every point; integrate 'quiet eye' for serve/return.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V2.sm: Routine compliance % increases and emotional recovery improves.
DRL-MENTAL-010-V2.sf: Avoid punitive tone; routine is skill acquisition.

DRL-MENTAL-020-V1.n: Pressure Routine + Quiet Eye (Serve/Return Focus) — Simplified
DRL-MENTAL-020-V1.o: Improve attention control by pairing a routine with a stable final visual focus (quiet eye).
DRL-MENTAL-020-V1.su: Define quiet-eye spot: serve = back of ball at toss; return = server contact zone. Use 3-step routine + 1-second quiet eye.
DRL-MENTAL-020-V1.ex:
1) Athlete rehearses routine without ball 5 times.
2) Athlete serves 10 balls: must pause 1 second on quiet-eye spot before motion.
3) Athlete returns 10 feeds: must hold gaze on contact zone before split.
4) Reflect: rate focus 1–5.
DRL-MENTAL-020-V1.cu:
• 'One calm look.'
• 'Breath, focus, go.'
• 'Eyes steady, body calm.'
DRL-MENTAL-020-V1.ef:
• Rushing → enforce pause count.
• Overthinking → keep cue simple.
• Eyes wandering → use visual marker.
DRL-MENTAL-020-V1.pg: Add pressure scoring; integrate into tiebreak; add distractions.
DRL-MENTAL-020-V1.sm: Better routine compliance and reduced rushed errors on serve/return.
DRL-MENTAL-020-V1.sf: Avoid making it rigid; routine must remain functional in match tempo.

DRL-MENTAL-020-V2.n: Pressure Routine + Quiet Eye (Serve/Return Focus) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-MENTAL-020-V2.o: Improve attention control by pairing a routine with a stable final visual focus (quiet eye).
DRL-MENTAL-020-V2.su: Define quiet-eye spot: serve = back of ball at toss; return = server contact zone. Use 3-step routine + 1-second quiet eye.
DRL-MENTAL-020-V2.ex:
1) Athlete rehearses routine without ball 5 times.
2) Athlete serves 10 balls: must pause 1 second on quiet-eye spot before motion.
3) Athlete returns 10 feeds: must hold gaze on contact zone before split.
4) Reflect: rate focus 1–5.
DRL-MENTAL-020-V2.cu:
• 'One calm look.'
• 'Breath, focus, go.'
• 'Eyes steady, body calm.'
DRL-MENTAL-020-V2.ef:
• Rushing → enforce pause count.
• Overthinking → keep cue simple.
• Eyes wandering → use visual marker.
DRL-MENTAL-020-V2.pg: Add pressure scoring; integrate into tiebreak; add distractions.
DRL-MENTAL-020-V2.sm: Better routine compliance and reduced rushed errors on serve/return.
DRL-MENTAL-020-V2.sf: Avoid making it rigid; routine must remain functional in match tempo.

DRL-MENTAL-040-V1.n: Goal Setting + Implementation Intentions (If–Then Plans) — Simplified
DRL-MENTAL-040-V1.o: Translate goals into actionable behaviors using if–then plans that hold up under pressure.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V1.su: Whiteboard: KPI goals and 3 'if–then' plans (e.g., If I feel tight, then I breathe + aim big).
DRL-MENTAL-040-V1.ex:
1) Athlete chooses 1 KPI for the week.
2) Athlete writes 3 if–then plans for common scenarios.
3) Athlete rehearses plans verbally.
4) Integrate into pressure points session; coach tags plan usage.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V1.cu:
• 'One KPI.'
• 'If–then must be simple.'
• 'Practice the plan, not the hope.'
DRL-MENTAL-040-V1.ef:
• Plans too complex → reduce to 3 words.
• Athlete forgets → write on towel card.
• Judgment tone → keep it curious and constructive.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V1.rg: Coach provides template; athlete fills blanks.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V1.pg: Add post-match review; adjust plans based on data.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V1.sm: Improved routine compliance and more consistent responses to common triggers.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V1.sf: Not therapy; refer to qualified professional for mental health concerns.

DRL-MENTAL-040-V2.n: Goal Setting + Implementation Intentions (If–Then Plans) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-MENTAL-040-V2.o: Translate goals into actionable behaviors using if–then plans that hold up under pressure.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V2.su: Whiteboard: KPI goals and 3 'if–then' plans (e.g., If I feel tight, then I breathe + aim big).
DRL-MENTAL-040-V2.ex:
1) Athlete chooses 1 KPI for the week.
2) Athlete writes 3 if–then plans for common scenarios.
3) Athlete rehearses plans verbally.
4) Integrate into pressure points session; coach tags plan usage.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V2.cu:

• 'If–then must be simple.'
• 'Practice the plan, not the hope.'
DRL-MENTAL-040-V2.ef:

• Judgment tone → keep it curious and constructive.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V2.rg: Coach provides template; athlete fills blanks.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V2.pg: Add post-match review; adjust plans based on data.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V2.sm: Improved routine compliance and more consistent responses to common triggers.
DRL-MENTAL-040-V2.sf: Not therapy; refer to qualified professional for mental health concerns.

DRL-MENTAL-041-V2.o: Improve confidence and decision clarity by mentally rehearsing high-quality reps.
DRL-MENTAL-041-V2.su: Athlete chooses one skill (serve target, return depth, pattern). Find quiet spot.
DRL-MENTAL-041-V2.ex:
1) Close eyes and visualize 10 perfect reps with sensory detail (feel, sound, target).
2) Immediately perform 10 real reps.
3) Compare: what matched? what drifted?
4) Repeat once.
DRL-MENTAL-041-V2.cu:
• 'See it, feel it, do it.'
• 'One image, one cue.'
• 'Then execute.'
DRL-MENTAL-041-V2.ef:
• Vague images → add target and body sensation details.
• Overthinking → keep to 10 reps.
• No transfer → pair immediately with real reps.
DRL-MENTAL-041-V2.pg: Use before pressure tiebreak; integrate with quiet eye.
DRL-MENTAL-041-V2.sm: Better focus and smoother execution in subsequent reps.
DRL-MENTAL-041-V2.sf: Keep it practical; avoid making it feel forced.

DRL-MINI-001-V1.n: Throw–Catch Rally (Mini Tennis Rally Builder) — Simplified
DRL-MINI-001-V1.o: Teach rally rhythm and spacing before full hitting.
DRL-MINI-001-V1.su: Pairs across mini-net or rope; each pair has one foam/red ball.
DRL-MINI-001-V1.ex:
1) Player A underhand tosses to partner; partner catches after one bounce.
2) Partner tosses back; continue cooperative 'rally'.
3) Add target: toss must land on 'island' (cone square).
4) Optional: toss → trap on racket → lift to partner.
DRL-MINI-001-V1.cu:
• 'Soft toss to the island.'
• 'Ready hands.'
• 'Bounce, catch, look.'
DRL-MINI-001-V1.ef:
• Wild tosses → shorten distance; teach 'pendulum arm'.
• Not moving feet → add 'step to the bounce'.
• Poor catching → use bigger ball, two hands.
DRL-MINI-001-V1.rg: Catch without bounce; closer distance; no targets.
DRL-MINI-001-V1.pg: Catch with one hand; add side shuffle; transition to gentle hit after bounce.
DRL-MINI-001-V1.sm: Longer cooperative sequences; improved attention and movement to the bounce spot.
DRL-MINI-001-V1.sf: Maintain pair spacing; avoid crossing courts during retrieval.

DRL-MINI-001-V2.n: Throw–Catch Rally (Mini Tennis Rally Builder) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-MINI-001-V2.o: Teach rally rhythm and spacing before full hitting.
DRL-MINI-001-V2.su: Pairs across mini-net or rope; each pair has one foam/red ball.
DRL-MINI-001-V2.ex:
1) Player A underhand tosses to partner; partner catches after one bounce.
2) Partner tosses back; continue cooperative 'rally'.
3) Add target: toss must land on 'island' (cone square).
4) Optional: toss → trap on racket → lift to partner.
DRL-MINI-001-V2.cu:
• 'Soft toss to the island.'
• 'Ready hands.'
• 'Bounce, catch, look.'
DRL-MINI-001-V2.ef:
• Wild tosses → shorten distance; teach 'pendulum arm'.
• Not moving feet → add 'step to the bounce'.
• Poor catching → use bigger ball, two hands.
DRL-MINI-001-V2.rg: Catch without bounce; closer distance; no targets.
DRL-MINI-001-V2.pg: Catch with one hand; add side shuffle; transition to gentle hit after bounce.
DRL-MINI-001-V2.sm: Longer cooperative sequences; improved attention and movement to the bounce spot.
DRL-MINI-001-V2.sf: Maintain pair spacing; avoid crossing courts during retrieval.

DRL-MINI-002-V2.n: Mini Tennis Rally — 1 Bounce Rule — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-MINI-002-V2.o: Build rally tolerance and basic directional control with a simple constraint.
DRL-MINI-002-V2.su: Pairs rally mini tennis. Mark a large target rectangle in each service box (e.g., 2 cones deep-middle).
DRL-MINI-002-V2.ex:
1) Cooperative rally: ball must bounce once and land inside target zone.
2) After 5 in a row, players can 'unlock' and hit to full service box.
3) Add challenge: every 3rd ball must go crosscourt (if using half boxes).
4) Rotate partners every 4 minutes.
DRL-MINI-002-V2.cu:
• 'High over the net.'
• 'Aim middle-deep.'
• 'Recover to home base.'
DRL-MINI-002-V2.ef:
• Too hard → cue '80% swing'; use slower ball.
• Net errors → cue 'aim higher'; add net clearance rope.
• Standing still → cue 'split then step'.
DRL-MINI-002-V2.rg: Allow two bounces; use foam ball; remove target zones.
DRL-MINI-002-V2.pg: Shrink target; add scoring; add 'change direction' ball after 6 in a row.
DRL-MINI-002-V2.sm: Average rally length increases; fewer net errors; better spacing and recovery.

DRL-MOVE-001-V1.o: Automate recovery steps after each hit and reduce drifting.
DRL-MOVE-001-V1.su: Place a 'home base' cone slightly behind baseline center. Coach feeds alternating FH/BH.
DRL-MOVE-001-V1.ex:
1) Athlete hits ball, then must take 3 recovery steps back toward home base cone before next feed.
2) Coach feeds next ball only when athlete touches cone (or crosses a line).
3) After 10 reps, remove cone and keep habit.
DRL-MOVE-001-V1.cu:
• 'Hit, recover, ready.'
• '3 steps to home.'
• 'Split before the bounce.'
DRL-MOVE-001-V1.ef:
• Athlete watches shot → cue 'eyes back to coach' after hit.
• Lazy steps → shorten recovery distance; add timing goal.
• Over-recovering → set cone slightly forward.
DRL-MOVE-001-V1.rg: 2 recovery steps; slower feeds; smaller movement.
DRL-MOVE-001-V1.pg: Random feeds; add live points; add serve start and +1 recovery.
DRL-MOVE-001-V1.sm: Faster ready position, improved defense, fewer late contacts in rallies.

DRL-NET-001-V1.n: Catch Volley → Punch Volley Progression — Simplified
DRL-NET-001-V1.o: Introduce net positioning and volley contact without swing complexity.
DRL-NET-001-V1.su: Pairs at 6–10 ft distance. Start with hands; then rackets.
DRL-NET-001-V1.ex:
1) Hand volley: partner tosses softly; player catches and 'pushes' back with two hands.
2) Racket trap: catch ball on strings (no swing) and pop it back.
3) Punch volley: short backswing, 'block' to target.
4) Add split-step before contact for older stages.
DRL-NET-001-V1.cu:
• 'Racket out front.'
• 'Step to the ball.'
• 'Short punch, no big swing.'
DRL-NET-001-V1.ef:
• Swinging too much → cue 'block like a wall'.
• Racket drops → cue 'hands up'.
• Late contact → start closer; slower toss.
DRL-NET-001-V1.rg: Use balloon; allow catch with hands only.
DRL-NET-001-V1.pg: Add direction targets; add 2-volley combo (FH then BH); add approach + volley sequence.
DRL-NET-001-V1.sm: Cleaner volley contact, improved ready position, and better split-step timing.
DRL-NET-001-V1.sf: Keep volleys controlled; avoid face-level tosses for kids.

DRL-NET-001-V2.n: Catch Volley → Punch Volley Progression — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-NET-001-V2.o: Introduce net positioning and volley contact without swing complexity.
DRL-NET-001-V2.su: Pairs at 6–10 ft distance. Start with hands; then rackets.
DRL-NET-001-V2.ex:
1) Hand volley: partner tosses softly; player catches and 'pushes' back with two hands.
2) Racket trap: catch ball on strings (no swing) and pop it back.
3) Punch volley: short backswing, 'block' to target.
4) Add split-step before contact for older stages.
DRL-NET-001-V2.cu:
• 'Racket out front.'
• 'Step to the ball.'
• 'Short punch, no big swing.'
DRL-NET-001-V2.ef:
• Swinging too much → cue 'block like a wall'.
• Racket drops → cue 'hands up'.
• Late contact → start closer; slower toss.
DRL-NET-001-V2.rg: Use balloon; allow catch with hands only.
DRL-NET-001-V2.pg: Add direction targets; add 2-volley combo (FH then BH); add approach + volley sequence.
DRL-NET-001-V2.sm: Cleaner volley contact, improved ready position, and better split-step timing.
DRL-NET-001-V2.sf: Keep volleys controlled; avoid face-level tosses for kids.

DRL-NET-010-V1.o: Train approach shot selection + forward movement + split-step timing + first volley quality.
DRL-NET-010-V1.su: Coach feeds short ball to baseline. Mark approach target deep corner and volley target open court.
DRL-NET-010-V1.ex:
1) Athlete hits approach (high clearance + depth) to target.
2) Athlete moves forward; split-step at opponent contact (coach toss).
3) Athlete volleys to open court target.
4) Repeat 6 reps per set; 3 sets with rest.
DRL-NET-010-V1.cu:
• 'Approach deep with margin.'
• 'Follow the ball in.'
• 'Split then punch.'
• 'Volley in front.'
DRL-NET-010-V1.ef:
• Approach short → aim higher + deeper target.
• No split-step → add audible cue 'split!'.
• Volley swing → cue 'block and steer'.
DRL-NET-010-V1.rg: Hand-feed; shorter approach distance; trap volley.
DRL-NET-010-V1.pg: Add second volley; add opponent pass attempt; make point live after volley.
DRL-NET-010-V1.sm: Higher approach depth; cleaner first volley; improved net positioning.
DRL-NET-010-V1.sf: Monitor knee/ankle deceleration; keep volume appropriate.

DRL-NET-010-V2.o: Train approach shot selection + forward movement + split-step timing + first volley quality.
DRL-NET-010-V2.su: Coach feeds short ball to baseline. Mark approach target deep corner and volley target open court.
DRL-NET-010-V2.ex:
1) Athlete hits approach (high clearance + depth) to target.
2) Athlete moves forward; split-step at opponent contact (coach toss).
3) Athlete volleys to open court target.
4) Repeat 6 reps per set; 3 sets with rest.
DRL-NET-010-V2.cu:
• 'Approach deep with margin.'
• 'Follow the ball in.'
• 'Split then punch.'
• 'Volley in front.'
DRL-NET-010-V2.ef:
• Approach short → aim higher + deeper target.
• No split-step → add audible cue 'split!'.
• Volley swing → cue 'block and steer'.
DRL-NET-010-V2.rg: Hand-feed; shorter approach distance; trap volley.
DRL-NET-010-V2.pg: Add second volley; add opponent pass attempt; make point live after volley.
DRL-NET-010-V2.sm: Higher approach depth; cleaner first volley; improved net positioning.
DRL-NET-010-V2.sf: Monitor knee/ankle deceleration; keep volume appropriate.

DRL-NET-011-V1.n: Volley Direction Game (Two Targets, One Decision) — Simplified
DRL-NET-011-V1.o: Improve volley control and teach simple decision: volley back behind or away from opponent.
DRL-NET-011-V1.su: Feeder at net with basket. Volleyer at service line. Two targets: deep-middle and angle.
DRL-NET-011-V1.ex:
1) Feeder tosses/feeds.
2) Volleyer must call target ('middle' or 'angle') before contact.
3) Score 1 if ball lands in target.
4) Switch after 12 feeds.
DRL-NET-011-V1.cu:
• 'Racket out front.'
• 'Step to the ball.'
• 'Aim, then punch.'
DRL-NET-011-V1.ef:
• Over-swing → cue 'short punch'.
• Late contact → start closer, slower feed.
• Missing target → enlarge target and re-focus on contact.
DRL-NET-011-V1.rg: Trap and push; use foam ball.
DRL-NET-011-V1.pg: Add moving volleys; add approach into volley; add point live after volley.
DRL-NET-011-V1.sm: Increased target hits and steadier volley contact.
DRL-NET-011-V1.sf: Keep feeds controlled; avoid face-level feeds.

DRL-NET-011-V2.n: Volley Direction Game (Two Targets, One Decision) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-NET-011-V2.o: Improve volley control and teach simple decision: volley back behind or away from opponent.
DRL-NET-011-V2.su: Feeder at net with basket. Volleyer at service line. Two targets: deep-middle and angle.
DRL-NET-011-V2.ex:
1) Feeder tosses/feeds.
2) Volleyer must call target ('middle' or 'angle') before contact.
3) Score 1 if ball lands in target.
4) Switch after 12 feeds.
DRL-NET-011-V2.cu:
• 'Racket out front.'
• 'Step to the ball.'
• 'Aim, then punch.'
DRL-NET-011-V2.ef:
• Over-swing → cue 'short punch'.
• Late contact → start closer, slower feed.
• Missing target → enlarge target and re-focus on contact.
DRL-NET-011-V2.rg: Trap and push; use foam ball.
DRL-NET-011-V2.pg: Add moving volleys; add approach into volley; add point live after volley.
DRL-NET-011-V2.sm: Increased target hits and steadier volley contact.
DRL-NET-011-V2.sf: Keep feeds controlled; avoid face-level feeds.

DRL-NET-020-V1.n: Transition Decision: Approach or Reset (2-Option Rule) — Simplified
DRL-NET-020-V1.o: Teach transition decision: approach only on the right ball; otherwise reset neutral.
DRL-NET-020-V1.su: Coach feeds mixed balls: some short and some deep. Define criteria for approach (short + above net height).
DRL-NET-020-V1.ex:
1) Athlete plays rally.
2) On short ball, athlete must approach and play at net.
3) On deep ball, athlete must rally neutral and stay back.
4) Coach scores decision correctness + point outcome.
DRL-NET-020-V1.cu:
• 'Approach only the right ball.'
• 'Neutral is a weapon.'
• 'Commit forward when you go.'
DRL-NET-020-V1.ef:
• Approaching on deep balls → enforce penalty point.
• Hesitation on short ball → add cue 'green light' and require approach.
• Poor approach quality → set depth target for approach.
DRL-NET-020-V1.pg: Opponent passes/ lobs live; add scoring for correct decisions.
DRL-NET-020-V1.sm: Decision accuracy improves; fewer transition errors; better net positioning.

DRL-PASS-040-V1.n: Passing Shot vs Approach (Drive / Dip / Lob Options) — Simplified
DRL-PASS-040-V1.o: Build passing shot selection and execution against an approaching opponent.
DRL-PASS-040-V1.su: One player approaches after a short ball; defender at baseline. Mark passing targets (down the line and crosscourt) and lob zone.
DRL-PASS-040-V1.ex:
1) Coach feeds short ball to attacker.
2) Attacker approaches.
3) Coach feeds ball to defender.
4) Defender chooses pass: drive, dip (low), or lob.
5) Play out point; rotate roles.
DRL-PASS-040-V1.cu:
• 'Read the net player's position.'
• 'Dip crosscourt is high %.'
• 'Lob when they crowd.'
DRL-PASS-040-V1.ef:
• Defender forces line too early → emphasize crosscourt dip.
• Pass into net → aim higher; add spin.
• Lob short → aim higher/deeper; use topspin lob.
DRL-PASS-040-V1.rg: Attacker starts at service line (less pressure); defender chooses only one option first.
DRL-PASS-040-V1.pg: Attacker chooses approach direction; live feed; add pressure scoring.
DRL-PASS-040-V1.sm: Better passing decisions and reduced errors vs net pressure.
DRL-PASS-040-V1.sf: High intensity; monitor sprinting and decel.

DRL-PRESS-020-V1.n: Deuce Game / Break Point Simulation (Pressure Reps) — Simplified
DRL-PRESS-020-V1.o: Build pressure tolerance by repeating the highest leverage points (deuce/ad, break points).
DRL-PRESS-020-V1.su: Play games that start at Deuce or at 30-40. Track break points converted and routine compliance.
DRL-PRESS-020-V1.ex:
1) Start game at Deuce.
2) Play best of 7 points (deuce/ad cycles).
3) Switch server each game.
4) Add rule: routine must be visible or point doesn't count.
DRL-PRESS-020-V1.cu:
• 'Simplify targets.'
• 'Routine on every point.'
• 'High percentage first 4 shots.'
DRL-PRESS-020-V1.ef:
• Tight swings → cue breath + bigger targets.
• Rushing between points → enforce routine.
• Over-aggression → require deep-middle rule on neutral balls.
DRL-PRESS-020-V1.rg: Start at 15-15; allow coaching between points.
DRL-PRESS-020-V1.pg: Time clock; consequences; play 2 deuce games in a row with stats.
DRL-PRESS-020-V1.sm: Improved routine compliance and break point conversion trend.
DRL-PRESS-020-V1.sf: High stress: keep supportive tone; manage volume and rest.

DRL-PRESS-020-V2.n: Deuce Game / Break Point Simulation (Pressure Reps) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-PRESS-020-V2.o: Build pressure tolerance by repeating the highest leverage points (deuce/ad, break points).
DRL-PRESS-020-V2.su: Play games that start at Deuce or at 30-40. Track break points converted and routine compliance.
DRL-PRESS-020-V2.ex:
1) Start game at Deuce.
2) Play best of 7 points (deuce/ad cycles).
3) Switch server each game.
4) Add rule: routine must be visible or point doesn't count.

• 'Simplify targets.'
• 'Routine on every point.'
• 'High percentage first 4 shots.'
DRL-PRESS-020-V2.ef:

• Rushing between points → enforce routine.
• Over-aggression → require deep-middle rule on neutral balls.
DRL-PRESS-020-V2.rg: Start at 15-15; allow coaching between points.
DRL-PRESS-020-V2.pg: Time clock; consequences; play 2 deuce games in a row with stats.
DRL-PRESS-020-V2.sm: Improved routine compliance and break point conversion trend.
DRL-PRESS-020-V2.sf: High stress: keep supportive tone; manage volume and rest.

DRL-PRESS-021-V1.n: Time-Clock Tiebreak (Fast Decisions Under Pressure) — Simplified
DRL-PRESS-021-V1.o: Increase decision speed and routine discipline by limiting time between points.
DRL-PRESS-021-V1.su: Play a tiebreak to 7 with a 15-second clock between points. Coach times it. Require routine within the clock.
DRL-PRESS-021-V1.ex:
1) Start tiebreak.
2) After each point, players have 15 seconds to start next point.
3) If late, lose 1 point.
4) Track unforced errors and routine compliance.
DRL-PRESS-021-V1.cu:
• 'Breathe quickly, pick a plan.'
• 'Commit to the target.'
• 'Short memory.'
DRL-PRESS-021-V1.ef:
• Panic decisions → simplify to deep-middle.
• Skipping routine → reinforce routine points.
• Too many double faults → allow 20 seconds for serve only initially.
DRL-PRESS-021-V1.sm: Reduced hesitation; improved composure; fewer UFEs late in games.
DRL-PRESS-021-V1.sf: Manage intensity; ensure hydration; stop if heat risk.

DRL-PRESS-021-V2.n: Time-Clock Tiebreak (Fast Decisions Under Pressure) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-PRESS-021-V2.o: Increase decision speed and routine discipline by limiting time between points.
DRL-PRESS-021-V2.su: Play a tiebreak to 7 with a 15-second clock between points. Coach times it. Require routine within the clock.
DRL-PRESS-021-V2.ex:
1) Start tiebreak.
2) After each point, players have 15 seconds to start next point.
3) If late, lose 1 point.
4) Track unforced errors and routine compliance.
DRL-PRESS-021-V2.cu:
• 'Breathe quickly, pick a plan.'
• 'Commit to the target.'
• 'Short memory.'

• Too many double faults → allow 20 seconds for serve only initially.
DRL-PRESS-021-V2.sm: Reduced hesitation; improved composure; fewer UFEs late in games.
DRL-PRESS-021-V2.sf: Manage intensity; ensure hydration; stop if heat risk.

DRL-RKT-001-V1.o: Develop racket comfort, basic control, and listening skills through simple constraints.
DRL-RKT-001-V1.su: Each player with racket and foam/red ball. Mark a 'home spot' with a dot/cone.
DRL-RKT-001-V1.ex:
1) Tap ball up on strings (self-rally). Count taps.
2) On coach call 'Freeze!' players stop the ball on strings and hold still on home spot.
3) Repeat with variations: tap high/low, tap while walking to cone and back.
4) Finish: 3 attempts to beat personal best.
DRL-RKT-001-V1.cu:
• 'Soft hands, quiet wrist.'
• 'Racket like a table.'
• 'Eyes on the ball; freeze like a statue.'
DRL-RKT-001-V1.ef:
• Ball flies off → lower height, bigger ball, two-hand assist on handle.
• Over-gripping → 'hold like a bird'.
• Not freezing → practice freeze without ball first.
DRL-RKT-001-V1.rg: Use balloon; allow bounce between taps; reduce to 3–5 taps goals.
DRL-RKT-001-V1.pg: Add walking, side shuffles, or 180° turn on cue; add tap patterns (2 low + 1 high).
DRL-RKT-001-V1.sm: Tap streak improves; freeze compliance improves; players stay on task with minimal reminders.
DRL-RKT-001-V1.sf: Keep rackets below shoulder height; ensure spacing between players.

DRL-RKT-001-V2.o: Develop racket comfort, basic control, and listening skills through simple constraints.
DRL-RKT-001-V2.su: Each player with racket and foam/red ball. Mark a 'home spot' with a dot/cone.
DRL-RKT-001-V2.ex:
1) Tap ball up on strings (self-rally). Count taps.
2) On coach call 'Freeze!' players stop the ball on strings and hold still on home spot.
3) Repeat with variations: tap high/low, tap while walking to cone and back.
4) Finish: 3 attempts to beat personal best.
DRL-RKT-001-V2.cu:
• 'Soft hands, quiet wrist.'
• 'Racket like a table.'
• 'Eyes on the ball; freeze like a statue.'
DRL-RKT-001-V2.ef:
• Ball flies off → lower height, bigger ball, two-hand assist on handle.
• Over-gripping → 'hold like a bird'.
• Not freezing → practice freeze without ball first.
DRL-RKT-001-V2.rg: Use balloon; allow bounce between taps; reduce to 3–5 taps goals.
DRL-RKT-001-V2.pg: Add walking, side shuffles, or 180° turn on cue; add tap patterns (2 low + 1 high).
DRL-RKT-001-V2.sm: Tap streak improves; freeze compliance improves; players stay on task with minimal reminders.
DRL-RKT-001-V2.sf: Keep rackets below shoulder height; ensure spacing between players.

DRL-RKT-002-V1.o: Build tracking, timing, and hand-eye coordination using predictable bounce/catch rhythm.
DRL-RKT-002-V1.su: Each player with ball (no racket initially). Mark a bounce line with tape/cones.
DRL-RKT-002-V1.ex:
1) Drop ball, let it bounce once, catch with two hands.
2) Progress: bounce on the line, catch above waist.
3) Add movement: take 2 steps back after each catch.
4) Add racket: bounce, catch on strings (trap), then lift.
DRL-RKT-002-V1.cu:
• 'Watch it bounce.'
• 'Hands together like a basket.'
• 'Catch soft, like an egg.'
DRL-RKT-002-V1.ef:
• Reaching late → start closer and slow down.
• Slapping at ball → cue 'receive' not 'grab'.
• Poor bounce height → demonstrate consistent drop.
DRL-RKT-002-V1.rg: Use balloon; allow two bounces; remove movement.
DRL-RKT-002-V1.pg: Add alternating hands catch; add clap once before catch; add partner toss-bounce-catch.
DRL-RKT-002-V1.sm: Consistent bounce-and-catch rhythm; improved focus and calm hands.
DRL-RKT-002-V1.sf: Space players; avoid chasing balls into other groups.

DRL-RKT-002-V2.o: Build tracking, timing, and hand-eye coordination using predictable bounce/catch rhythm.
DRL-RKT-002-V2.su: Each player with ball (no racket initially). Mark a bounce line with tape/cones.
DRL-RKT-002-V2.ex:
1) Drop ball, let it bounce once, catch with two hands.
2) Progress: bounce on the line, catch above waist.
3) Add movement: take 2 steps back after each catch.
4) Add racket: bounce, catch on strings (trap), then lift.
DRL-RKT-002-V2.cu:
• 'Watch it bounce.'
• 'Hands together like a basket.'
• 'Catch soft, like an egg.'
DRL-RKT-002-V2.ef:
• Reaching late → start closer and slow down.
• Slapping at ball → cue 'receive' not 'grab'.
• Poor bounce height → demonstrate consistent drop.
DRL-RKT-002-V2.rg: Use balloon; allow two bounces; remove movement.
DRL-RKT-002-V2.pg: Add alternating hands catch; add clap once before catch; add partner toss-bounce-catch.
DRL-RKT-002-V2.sm: Consistent bounce-and-catch rhythm; improved focus and calm hands.
DRL-RKT-002-V2.sf: Space players; avoid chasing balls into other groups.

DRL-RKT-003-V1.n: Roll to Targets (Directional Control Foundation) — Simplified
DRL-RKT-003-V1.o: Teach 'aiming' and spacing using the simplest ball trajectory (rolling).
DRL-RKT-003-V1.su: Create 3 big targets (hoops/flat cones) at 8–12 feet. Players in pairs with one ball per pair.
DRL-RKT-003-V1.ex:
1) Player A rolls ball with hand toward a target.
2) Player B traps with hand or racket, returns roll to a chosen target.
3) Add 'coach call' target color to train listening.
4) Score: 1 point for hitting target; first to 5.
DRL-RKT-003-V1.cu:
• 'Point your belly button at the target.'
• 'Roll it like bowling.'
• 'Trap, then aim.'
DRL-RKT-003-V1.ef:
• Rolling too hard → shorten distance, cue 'gentle'.
• Not trapping → teach 'stop the ball' first.
• Chaos around targets → assign lanes and clear rules.
DRL-RKT-003-V1.rg: Bigger targets, shorter distance, no scoring.
DRL-RKT-003-V1.pg: Roll with racket push; add backhand-side rolls; add 'fake then go' decision.
DRL-RKT-003-V1.sm: Players consistently hit big targets and demonstrate turn-taking.
DRL-RKT-003-V1.sf: Ensure players roll within lanes; avoid collisions.

DRL-RKT-003-V2.n: Roll to Targets (Directional Control Foundation) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-RKT-003-V2.o: Teach 'aiming' and spacing using the simplest ball trajectory (rolling).
DRL-RKT-003-V2.su: Create 3 big targets (hoops/flat cones) at 8–12 feet. Players in pairs with one ball per pair.
DRL-RKT-003-V2.ex:
1) Player A rolls ball with hand toward a target.
2) Player B traps with hand or racket, returns roll to a chosen target.
3) Add 'coach call' target color to train listening.
4) Score: 1 point for hitting target; first to 5.
DRL-RKT-003-V2.cu:

• 'Roll it like bowling.'
• 'Trap, then aim.'

• Not trapping → teach 'stop the ball' first.
• Chaos around targets → assign lanes and clear rules.
DRL-RKT-003-V2.rg: Bigger targets, shorter distance, no scoring.
DRL-RKT-003-V2.pg: Roll with racket push; add backhand-side rolls; add 'fake then go' decision.
DRL-RKT-003-V2.sm: Players consistently hit big targets and demonstrate turn-taking.
DRL-RKT-003-V2.sf: Ensure players roll within lanes; avoid collisions.

DRL-SERVE-001-V1.n: Toss–Catch & Overhand Throw Pattern (Serve Foundation) — Simplified
DRL-SERVE-001-V1.o: Build the overhand throwing pattern and consistent toss pathway before serving.
DRL-SERVE-001-V1.su: Each player with ball; optional targets on opposite side. No racket initially for younger players.
DRL-SERVE-001-V1.ex:
1) Toss up and catch at full reach (no jump).
2) Step and throw to a big target (underhand then overhand).
3) Add racket: shadow serve motion with pause at trophy; then gentle serve from service line.
DRL-SERVE-001-V1.cu:
• 'Reach tall.'
• 'Throw up then forward.'
• 'Front shoulder points at target.'
DRL-SERVE-001-V1.ef:
• Toss too far → set toss 'window' cone in front.
• Sidearm throw → cue 'elbow leads, hand follows'.
• Jumping wildly → require 'feet stay down' until consistent.
DRL-SERVE-001-V1.rg: Use balloon; toss lower; throw from closer distance.
DRL-SERVE-001-V1.pg: Add accuracy scoring; add serve to deuce/ad boxes; add pre-serve routine (bounce-breath).
DRL-SERVE-001-V1.sm: Consistent toss into window; improved throw accuracy; smoother serve rhythm.
DRL-SERVE-001-V1.sf: Shoulder safety: keep volume low for young kids; stop if pain; emphasize smooth rhythm.

DRL-SERVE-001-V2.n: Toss–Catch & Overhand Throw Pattern (Serve Foundation) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-SERVE-001-V2.o: Build the overhand throwing pattern and consistent toss pathway before serving.
DRL-SERVE-001-V2.su: Each player with ball; optional targets on opposite side. No racket initially for younger players.
DRL-SERVE-001-V2.ex:
1) Toss up and catch at full reach (no jump).
2) Step and throw to a big target (underhand then overhand).
3) Add racket: shadow serve motion with pause at trophy; then gentle serve from service line.
DRL-SERVE-001-V2.cu:
• 'Reach tall.'
• 'Throw up then forward.'
• 'Front shoulder points at target.'
DRL-SERVE-001-V2.ef:
• Toss too far → set toss 'window' cone in front.
• Sidearm throw → cue 'elbow leads, hand follows'.
• Jumping wildly → require 'feet stay down' until consistent.
DRL-SERVE-001-V2.rg: Use balloon; toss lower; throw from closer distance.
DRL-SERVE-001-V2.pg: Add accuracy scoring; add serve to deuce/ad boxes; add pre-serve routine (bounce-breath).
DRL-SERVE-001-V2.sm: Consistent toss into window; improved throw accuracy; smoother serve rhythm.
DRL-SERVE-001-V2.sf: Shoulder safety: keep volume low for young kids; stop if pain; emphasize smooth rhythm.

DRL-SERVE-002-V1.n: Underhand Serve Start Game (Start the Point) — Simplified
DRL-SERVE-002-V1.o: Teach point initiation with high success and early return habits.
DRL-SERVE-002-V1.su: Pairs. Server underhand serves from service line to a big target zone. Receiver starts in ready position.
DRL-SERVE-002-V1.ex:
1) Underhand serve into box.
2) Receiver catches after bounce OR blocks back gently (depending on stage).
3) Play point out to 3 shots max, then reset.
4) Rotate server/receiver every 6 points.
DRL-SERVE-002-V1.cu:
• 'Serve to the island.'
• 'Receiver: split then step.'
• 'Keep it simple: big targets.'
DRL-SERVE-002-V1.ef:
• Missed serves → move closer; bigger target.
• Receiver frozen → add 'split-step clap' cue.
• Too long points → cap at 3–5 shots for success.
DRL-SERVE-002-V1.pg: Move serve back; require return beyond service line; play full point to 7.
DRL-SERVE-002-V1.sm: High serve-in rate; improved readiness for points; reduced fear of serving.
DRL-SERVE-002-V1.sf: Keep points short for young kids to avoid fatigue and chaos.

DRL-SERVE-002-V2.n: Underhand Serve Start Game (Start the Point) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-SERVE-002-V2.o: Teach point initiation with high success and early return habits.
DRL-SERVE-002-V2.su: Pairs. Server underhand serves from service line to a big target zone. Receiver starts in ready position.
DRL-SERVE-002-V2.ex:
1) Underhand serve into box.
2) Receiver catches after bounce OR blocks back gently (depending on stage).
3) Play point out to 3 shots max, then reset.
4) Rotate server/receiver every 6 points.

• 'Receiver: split then step.'
• 'Keep it simple: big targets.'

DRL-SERVE-002-V2.sf: Keep points short for young kids to avoid fatigue and chaos.

DRL-SERVE-020-V1.n: Serve Spots Under Pressure (30-Ball Protocol) — Simplified
DRL-SERVE-020-V1.o: Improve serve reliability + placement under a structured pressure protocol.
DRL-SERVE-020-V1.su: Create 3 targets per box (wide/body/T). Athlete serves 5 balls to each target (deuce then ad) = 30 serves total. Track in% and target hits.
DRL-SERVE-020-V1.ex:
1) Athlete runs full routine each serve.
2) Serve 5 to wide, 5 to body, 5 to T (deuce).
3) Repeat on ad side.
4) Pressure rule: if athlete misses 3 in a row, they must do 10-second reset and re-start that target set.
DRL-SERVE-020-V1.cu:
• 'Routine wins.'
• 'Toss to the same window.'
• 'Hit shape to target (not just speed).'
DRL-SERVE-020-V1.ef:
• Target obsession reduces in% → separate metrics and scale target size.
• Tightness under pressure → add breath + longer exhale.
• Toss drift → toss marker drill.
DRL-SERVE-020-V1.rg: 20-ball protocol; bigger targets; serve from 1 step inside baseline for younger.
DRL-SERVE-020-V1.pg: Add second serve requirement; add scoreboard: down 30-40 must hit T; add serve+1 after each make.
DRL-SERVE-020-V1.sm: First serve in% and target hit% trend upward; reduced 'clusters' of misses.
DRL-SERVE-020-V1.sf: Serve volume management; stop if shoulder pain; prioritize quality.

DRL-SERVE-020-V2.n: Serve Spots Under Pressure (30-Ball Protocol) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-SERVE-020-V2.o: Improve serve reliability + placement under a structured pressure protocol.
DRL-SERVE-020-V2.su: Create 3 targets per box (wide/body/T). Athlete serves 5 balls to each target (deuce then ad) = 30 serves total. Track in% and target hits.
DRL-SERVE-020-V2.ex:

2) Serve 5 to wide, 5 to body, 5 to T (deuce).
3) Repeat on ad side.
4) Pressure rule: if athlete misses 3 in a row, they must do 10-second reset and re-start that target set.

DRL-SERVE-020-V2.sf: Serve volume management; stop if shoulder pain; prioritize quality.

DRL-SERVE-030-V1.o: Add variability to serve placement while maintaining routine and shape.
DRL-SERVE-030-V1.su: Create a randomizer: draw cards (W/B/T) or use phone spinner. Mark targets accordingly.
DRL-SERVE-030-V1.ex:
1) Athlete draws target.
2) Executes full routine and serves to target.
3) Track in% and target hit%.
4) After 10 serves, increase pressure: two misses in a row = reset routine + 10-second pause.
DRL-SERVE-030-V1.cu:
• 'Routine stays constant; target changes.'
• 'Hit shape to target.'
• 'Breathe before motion.'
DRL-SERVE-030-V1.ef:
• Rushing after draw → enforce pause.
• Target misses → adjust target size and aim window.
• Tight arm → cue smooth acceleration.
DRL-SERVE-030-V1.pg: Add serve+1 after each serve; add score-based randomizer (choose target based on score).
DRL-SERVE-030-V1.sm: Stable serve in% with improved adaptability and placement.

DRL-SERVE-030-V2.o: Add variability to serve placement while maintaining routine and shape.
DRL-SERVE-030-V2.su: Create a randomizer: draw cards (W/B/T) or use phone spinner. Mark targets accordingly.
DRL-SERVE-030-V2.ex:
1) Athlete draws target.
2) Executes full routine and serves to target.
3) Track in% and target hit%.
4) After 10 serves, increase pressure: two misses in a row = reset routine + 10-second pause.
DRL-SERVE-030-V2.cu:
• 'Routine stays constant; target changes.'
• 'Hit shape to target.'
• 'Breathe before motion.'
DRL-SERVE-030-V2.ef:
• Rushing after draw → enforce pause.
• Target misses → adjust target size and aim window.
• Tight arm → cue smooth acceleration.
DRL-SERVE-030-V2.pg: Add serve+1 after each serve; add score-based randomizer (choose target based on score).
DRL-SERVE-030-V2.sm: Stable serve in% with improved adaptability and placement.

DRL-TECH-BH-001-V1.o: Create early backhand shape with two-hand stability and directional intent.
DRL-TECH-BH-001-V1.su: Players with two-handed backhand grip; big target in opposite service box.
DRL-TECH-BH-001-V1.ex:
1) Coach underhand toss to backhand side; player catches ball lightly on strings (trap) then pushes it over mini-net to target.
2) Progress to bounce-hit (no trap) using compact swing.
3) Add footwork: 'turn + step' before contact.
4) Score: 5 pushes to target then 5 bounce-hits.
DRL-TECH-BH-001-V1.cu:
• 'Two hands, one unit.'
• 'Turn your shoulders.'
• 'Step, then push through.'
DRL-TECH-BH-001-V1.ef:
• Arms separate → cue 'glue hands together'.
• No shoulder turn → show 'logo to the fence'.
• Hitting with wrists → cue 'quiet wrists'.
DRL-TECH-BH-001-V1.rg: Trap and push only; use foam ball; shorter distance.
DRL-TECH-BH-001-V1.pg: Add topspin brush; require target zones; alternate FH/BH every ball.
DRL-TECH-BH-001-V1.sm: Stable two-hand contact with predictable direction; improved readiness to rally.
DRL-TECH-BH-001-V1.sf: Use slower balls; avoid overloading with long lines.

DRL-TECH-FH-001-V2.n: Drop–Hit to Big Target (Forehand Foundation) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-TECH-FH-001-V2.o: Develop consistent contact point and simple swing path to a large target.
DRL-TECH-FH-001-V2.su: Targets: 2 large cones/hoops in opposite service box. Players start 6–10 feet from mini-net. One ball each or coach feed.
DRL-TECH-FH-001-V2.ex:
1) Player drops ball, lets it bounce, and hits forehand over mini-net into big target zone.
2) Collect and repeat; aim for 10 quality reps.
3) Add movement: start behind cone, shuffle to contact spot, then hit.
4) Scoring: 1 point if ball lands in target; first to 7.
DRL-TECH-FH-001-V2.cu:
• 'Turn, drop, hit.'
• 'Contact in front.'
• 'Finish to the shoulder.'
• External cue: 'Brush the ball up the mountain' (topspin feel).
DRL-TECH-FH-001-V2.ef:
• Late contact → move closer; cue 'hit earlier'.
• Over-swing → cue 'smooth, not hard'; use slower ball.
• Ball into net → raise target zone, cue 'hit up'.
DRL-TECH-FH-001-V2.rg: Allow catch after bounce then gentle push; use foam ball; shorten distance.
DRL-TECH-FH-001-V2.pg: Coach feeds; require 3-in-a-row to advance; shrink target; add crosscourt vs DTL targets.
DRL-TECH-FH-001-V2.sm: Increasing target hits and cleaner contact; fewer mishits; more consistent height over net.
DRL-TECH-FH-001-V2.sf: Ensure safe spacing; players retrieve balls after 'freeze' call.

DRL-WUP-001-V1.o: Build physical literacy (run, hop, balance, rotate) while establishing attention + safety routines.
DRL-WUP-001-V1.su: Create 4–6 stations in a loop: (1) hopscotch dots, (2) cone weave, (3) balance beam (tape line), (4) two-hand throw to target, (5) sprint to 'home base' spot. Use visual cues (animal pictures).
DRL-WUP-001-V1.ex:
1) Coach demonstrates each station with an 'animal'.
2) Players travel the loop for 2–3 rounds.
3) Add a simple command: when coach calls 'Freeze!' players stop in athletic stance.
4) Finish with a 20-second 'fast feet' and then a calm breath.
DRL-WUP-001-V1.cu:
• 'Quiet feet on the line.'
• 'Eyes forward, soft knees.'
• 'Freeze like a statue: racket up / hands ready.'
• Use stories: 'cheetah sprint', 'flamingo balance'.
DRL-WUP-001-V1.ef:
• Chaos/spacing → set clear start spots and one-way traffic.
• Falling off balance line → widen the line or allow 'toe touches'.
• Throwing wildly → use bigger target, shorter distance, two-hand chest pass.
DRL-WUP-001-V1.rg: Reduce stations; remove timing; allow walking; use bigger targets; no racket required.
DRL-WUP-001-V1.pg: Add a ball carry; add reaction cue (color cone to touch); add backward shuffles; add 'catch then go' before next station.
DRL-WUP-001-V1.sm: Players complete loop with control (no collisions), can freeze on cue, improved balance and attention over rounds.
DRL-WUP-001-V1.sf: Maintain spacing; remove trip hazards; keep intensity appropriate; stop if a child shows fatigue or distress.
DRL-WUP-001-V1.ct: Off-court lane or inside service boxes with cones

DRL-WUP-001-V2.o: Build physical literacy (run, hop, balance, rotate) while establishing attention + safety routines.
DRL-WUP-001-V2.su: Create 4–6 stations in a loop: (1) hopscotch dots, (2) cone weave, (3) balance beam (tape line), (4) two-hand throw to target, (5) sprint to 'home base' spot. Use visual cues (animal pictures).
DRL-WUP-001-V2.ex:
1) Coach demonstrates each station with an 'animal'.
2) Players travel the loop for 2–3 rounds.
3) Add a simple command: when coach calls 'Freeze!' players stop in athletic stance.
4) Finish with a 20-second 'fast feet' and then a calm breath.
DRL-WUP-001-V2.cu:
• 'Quiet feet on the line.'
• 'Eyes forward, soft knees.'
• 'Freeze like a statue: racket up / hands ready.'
• Use stories: 'cheetah sprint', 'flamingo balance'.
DRL-WUP-001-V2.ef:
• Chaos/spacing → set clear start spots and one-way traffic.
• Falling off balance line → widen the line or allow 'toe touches'.
• Throwing wildly → use bigger target, shorter distance, two-hand chest pass.
DRL-WUP-001-V2.rg: Reduce stations; remove timing; allow walking; use bigger targets; no racket required.
DRL-WUP-001-V2.pg: Add a ball carry; add reaction cue (color cone to touch); add backward shuffles; add 'catch then go' before next station.
DRL-WUP-001-V2.sm: Players complete loop with control (no collisions), can freeze on cue, improved balance and attention over rounds.
DRL-WUP-001-V2.sf: Maintain spacing; remove trip hazards; keep intensity appropriate; stop if a child shows fatigue or distress.
DRL-WUP-001-V2.ct: Off-court lane or inside service boxes with cones

DRL-WUP-010-V1.n: Dynamic Warm-up + Footwork Ladder Primer (ABC → Tennis) — Simplified
DRL-WUP-010-V1.o: Prime joints and movement patterns; reinforce deceleration and first-step mechanics.
DRL-WUP-010-V1.su: Set 1 agility ladder, 4 cones for shuffle/crossover patterns, and 2 reaction cones (color).
DRL-WUP-010-V1.ex:
1) 3 minutes dynamic mobility (ankles/hips/T-spine).
2) Ladder patterns (2 rounds): in-in-out-out, lateral, icky shuffle.
3) Cone COD: shuffle-shuffle-crossover, plant and recover.
4) Reaction: coach calls color; athlete sprints 3 steps then split-step.
DRL-WUP-010-V1.cu:
• 'Low hips, quiet upper body.'
• 'Push the ground away.'
• 'Stick the landing on decel.'
• 'Split on cue.'
DRL-WUP-010-V1.ef:
• Heavy steps → cue 'quiet feet'; reduce speed.
• Upright posture → cue 'nose over toes'.
• Sloppy decel → add 1-second hold at plant.
DRL-WUP-010-V1.rg: Remove ladder; use simple line hops; reduce intensity.
DRL-WUP-010-V1.pg: Add racket shadow swings after ladder; add ball feed immediately after reaction.
DRL-WUP-010-V1.sm: Improved movement quality and readiness; fewer early-session mishits due to poor footwork.
DRL-WUP-010-V1.sf: Quality > speed. Stop if pain. Hydration and footwear check.

DRL-WUP-040-V2.o: Prepare shoulders for serving and overhead work; reduce injury risk.
DRL-WUP-040-V2.su: Light bands. 3 exercises: band pull-aparts, external rotations, Y-T-W scap sequence.
DRL-WUP-040-V2.ex:
1) 12 pull-aparts.
2) 10 external rotations each side.
3) 6 Y-T-W each.
4) Repeat once. Finish with 5 shadow serves (slow).
DRL-WUP-040-V2.ef:
• Using heavy band → lighter band.
• Shrugging → cue shoulders down/back.
• Speeding through → tempo count.
DRL-WUP-040-V2.pg: Add overhead band work; integrate into prehab circuit.
DRL-WUP-040-V2.sm: Shoulders feel warm and stable; reduced early-session serving discomfort.
DRL-WUP-040-V2.sf: General guidance only; stop if pain; consult medical professional for injury.

DRL-LIVE-002.n: Liveball Serve-Start Rotation (Serve Reps Under Chaos)
DRL-LIVE-002.o: Blend liveball intensity with real serving/returning so serve+return skills transfer under fatigue and scoreboard pressure.
DRL-LIVE-002.su:
4 players per court (2 teams). Use standard Liveball champions/challengers rotation.
Optional: designate a 'serve KPI' target zone with cones (wide/body/T).
DRL-LIVE-002.ex:
1) Champions serve the first point (normal serve).
2) Play out the point (full doubles rules).
3) Alternate server each point on the same team (or alternate deuce/ad).
4) Mini-game: Challengers need 3 points to replace champions; champions need 2 to stay.
5) Rotate challengers every mini-game; track first-serve-in and return-depth quick tallies.
DRL-LIVE-002.cu:
• 'Own the first ball: first serve in to a big target.'
• 'Return deep through the middle to start.'
• 'Call it early: mine/yours/leave.'
• 'After serve: recover + split.'
DRL-LIVE-002.ef:
• Double-fault streaks slow the game → switch to 'one serve' (single attempt) then coach feeds if missed.
• Returns float short → enforce return target beyond service line; reduce serve pace.
• Confusion on rotation → use cones or wristbands to mark champions; coach calls rotations loudly.
DRL-LIVE-002.rg:
Start points with coach feed (no serve). Or use underhand serve.
Challengers need 2; champions need 2 (lower pressure).
DRL-LIVE-002.pg:
Second-serve only round; require serve to a called target.
Award 1 bonus point if serve+1 pattern is executed (e.g., serve wide then volley behind).
DRL-LIVE-002.sm: Improved first-serve-in % under pressure; improved return depth %; fewer unforced errors in first 4 shots.
DRL-LIVE-002.sf: High intensity and shoulder load; ensure full warm-up (band activation), hydration, and adequate rest intervals.
DRL-LIVE-002.tg: Liveball, Serve, Return, Doubles, Competition, Pressure

DRL-LIVE-003.n: Liveball Second-Serve Pressure Round (Spin + Height Wins)
DRL-LIVE-003.o: Turn the second serve into a reliable weapon by training shape (spin/height), target discipline, and emotional control in a liveball format.
DRL-LIVE-003.su:
4 players per court. Mark 2–3 large second-serve targets per box (body + wide).
All points start with a second serve (no first serves).
DRL-LIVE-003.ex:
1) Server must hit a second serve with shape (kick/slice) to a declared target.
2) If the serve misses: immediate point to return team (or replay 1 time, depending on level).
3) Play out point.
4) Mini-game to 5 points; rotate servers each point; rotate teams every 5–7 minutes.
DRL-LIVE-003.cu:
• 'Spin first, speed second.'
• 'Same toss, different shape.'
• 'Breathe + cue word before serve.'
• 'Return team: neutralize deep, middle first.'
DRL-LIVE-003.ef:
• Players guide the serve → cue 'swing up and out', aim big target.
• Toss drifts → mark toss drop spot with a cone/spot.
• Return team over-attacks → require 2 neutral balls before attacking.
DRL-LIVE-003.rg:
Allow 2 attempts before point awarded.
Use green/yellow balls for juniors; move serve starting point forward 1–2 steps.
DRL-LIVE-003.pg:
Add bonus point for second serve to a small target.
Require server to play serve+1 to a planned zone.
Add scoreboard pressure: start down 0–2 each mini-game.
DRL-LIVE-003.sm: Higher second-serve make rate; better serve target hit %; fewer double-fault equivalents under stress.
DRL-LIVE-003.sf: Manage shoulder/elbow load; stop if pain. Build volume gradually; prioritize technique and recovery.
DRL-LIVE-003.tg: Liveball, Serve, Second serve, Spin, Pressure, Competition
