DRL-LIVE-003-V2.pg:
Add bonus point for second serve to a small target.
Require server to play serve+1 to a planned zone.
Add scoreboard pressure: start down 0–2 each mini-game.
DRL-LIVE-003-V2.sm: Higher second-serve make rate; better serve target hit %; fewer double-fault equivalents under stress.
DRL-LIVE-003-V2.sf: Manage shoulder/elbow load; stop if pain. Build volume gradually; prioritize technique and recovery.
DRL-LIVE-003-V2.tg: Liveball, Serve, Second serve, Spin, Pressure, Competition

DRL-LIVE-004.o: Improve doubles anticipation and poach timing by forcing clear decision rules and coordinated team movement.
DRL-LIVE-004.su:
4 players per court. Define a simple poach trigger (choose ONE):
A) If return/groundstroke is crosscourt and lands short (inside service line), net player must poach.
B) Net player must poach at least once per point (anytime).
Use hand signals before point (poach/stay).
DRL-LIVE-004.ex:
1) Coach feeds or start with serve.
2) Teams play live points.
3) If the trigger happens and net player does NOT poach → automatic point to opponents.
4) If net player poaches but misses → point continues (or count as error, coach choice).
5) Rotate after 7 minutes; track successful poach attempts and communication quality.
DRL-LIVE-004.cu:
• 'Small split step; move on opponent contact.'
• 'Poach is a team move: partner covers the line.'
• 'Show early: call “go” or “stay”.'
• 'Volleys to big targets (middle/feet).'
DRL-LIVE-004.ef:
• Net player leaves alley exposed → rehearse partner slide step and covering.
• Over-poaching → reinforce trigger rule and patience.
• Late movement → cue “move on contact,” not after bounce.
DRL-LIVE-004.rg:
Coach feed slower; remove penalty rule and simply count poach attempts.
Use bigger target zones; allow net player to catch-volley for reps (juniors).
DRL-LIVE-004.pg:
Add bonus point for poach winner.
Use smaller targets; require poach to volley behind receiver.
Add fake-poach (stunt) rule to train deception.
DRL-LIVE-004.sm: Higher poach success rate; clearer signals; improved pattern execution in doubles points.
DRL-LIVE-004.sf: Ensure safe spacing at net; emphasize controlled movement and awareness to avoid collisions.
DRL-LIVE-004.tg: Liveball, Doubles, Poaching, Decision-making, Communication, Patterns

DRL-LIVE-005.n: Liveball Crosscourt Lock + DTL Trigger (Patience to Attack)
DRL-LIVE-005.o: Train high-percentage point construction: build with crosscourt tolerance, then change direction only on a clear trigger.
DRL-LIVE-005.su:
Set 'crosscourt lanes' with cones for deuce and ad sides.
Define ONE trigger for down-the-line (DTL):
• Ball lands inside baseline (short) OR
• Opponent pulled wide (outside singles line).
DRL-LIVE-005.ex:
1) Start point with coach feed or serve.
2) During neutral rally, all shots must be crosscourt.
3) When trigger appears, player may go DTL once.
4) After DTL, point becomes open.
5) Score to 7; rotate teams/players every 7–10 minutes.
DRL-LIVE-005.cu:
• 'High net clearance, deep crosscourt.'
• 'Recover to home base after every shot.'
• 'Trigger first, then commit.'
• 'DTL is a shape ball, not a snipe.'
DRL-LIVE-005.ef:
• Players go DTL early → enforce penalty (loss of point) for non-trigger DTL.
• Crosscourt balls land short → set depth target beyond service line.
• Over-aiming corners → cue 'big target crosscourt' (inside singles).
DRL-LIVE-005.rg:
Allow DTL after 5-ball rally regardless of trigger.
Use green balls; shrink court to service boxes for younger players.
DRL-LIVE-005.pg:
Require DTL to land in a specific zone.
Add bonus point if DTL leads to net finish.
Add time pressure (30-sec points).
DRL-LIVE-005.sm: Longer average rally length without errors; improved decision quality; fewer early-direction-change unforced errors.
DRL-LIVE-005.sf: Manage intensity; remind players to decelerate safely when changing directions.
DRL-LIVE-005.tg: Liveball, Direction, Decision rule, Consistency, Patterns, Rally

DRL-LIVE-006.o: Increase willingness and skill to move forward by rewarding transition patterns and net finishing under live scoring.
DRL-LIVE-006.su:
4 players per court (can be doubles or singles rotation).
Mark 'attack zone' (any ball landing inside baseline) as an approach trigger.
Define bonus scoring: +1 bonus point if point is won at net after an approach.
DRL-LIVE-006.ex:
1) Start points with coach feed or serve.
2) If a player receives an attackable ball (inside baseline), they must approach.
3) Play out point.
4) Scoring: normal point = 1; net-finish point = 2.
5) First to 11 wins; rotate roles every game.
DRL-LIVE-006.cu:
• 'Approach through the ball—deep and inside target.'
• 'Split-step before first volley.'
• 'Volley to open court or through the middle.'
• 'Close: don’t admire.'
DRL-LIVE-006.ef:
• Approaches land short → require depth beyond service line.
• Player runs past the ball → cue 'plant outside foot' and hit under control.
• Late split-step → coach calls 'split' on opponent contact for timing.
DRL-LIVE-006.rg:
Remove forced approach rule; keep bonus only.
Start with approach feeds from service line (easier).
DRL-LIVE-006.pg:
Add mandatory 2nd volley finish.
Smaller bonus target (must volley behind).
Add defender passing-shot bonus to train both sides.
DRL-LIVE-006.sm: Higher approach frequency; improved first-volley quality; increased points finished at net with acceptable error rate.
DRL-LIVE-006.sf: High sprint and decel load; enforce rest and hydration; monitor for ankle/knee discomfort.
DRL-LIVE-006.tg: Liveball, Transition, Approach, Volley, Finishing, Patterns
DRL-LIVE-006.ct: Full court (coach feeding or live ball)

DRL-LIVE-006-V1.n: Liveball Transition Bonus (Approach + Volley Reward) — Simplified
DRL-LIVE-006-V1.o: Increase willingness and skill to move forward by rewarding transition patterns and net finishing under live scoring.
DRL-LIVE-006-V1.su:

DRL-LIVE-007.n: Liveball Lob & Overhead Round (Net Survival Skills)
DRL-LIVE-007.o: Build confidence in lobbing under pressure and executing overheads with recovery footwork and smart target selection.
DRL-LIVE-007.su:
4 players. Declare the theme before the round:
Option A (Lob theme): Baseline team must hit at least 1 lob per point.
Option B (Overhead theme): Net team must take any ball above shoulder height as an overhead (no let-it-drop).
DRL-LIVE-007.ex:
1) Start point with coach feed or serve.
2) Apply the round rule (A or B).
3) If a team violates the rule → immediate loss of point.
4) Play mini-games to 7; rotate roles after each game.
DRL-LIVE-007.cu:
• 'Lob: high, deep, middle first.'
• 'Overhead: turn sideways, point to ball, shuffle back.'
• 'Hit to big targets; don’t chase lines.'
• 'Recover after overhead—don’t freeze.'
DRL-LIVE-007.ef:
• Lobs too flat → cue 'lift with legs,' aim higher.
• Overhead backpedaling → teach drop-step + crossover.
• Overhead misses long → aim middle 60% power, focus contact in front.
DRL-LIVE-007.rg:
Allow lobs without penalty; simply count successful lobs.
For overhead: allow catch-and-throw overhead pattern for younger players.
DRL-LIVE-007.pg:
Require lob to land past service line.
Overhead must be placed to a target.
Add second overhead after a defensive lob (repeat).
DRL-LIVE-007.sm: More successful defensive lobs; improved overhead technique under movement; fewer panic errors at net.
DRL-LIVE-007.sf: Overheads carry shoulder risk; stop if pain. Ensure spacing and clear 'mine' calls to prevent collisions.
DRL-LIVE-007.tg: Liveball, Lob, Overhead, Defense, Net, Decision-making

DRL-LIVE-008.n: Liveball Narrow Court Doubles (Singles Lines + Big Targets)
DRL-LIVE-008.o: Increase rally tolerance and shot quality by reducing available court width, forcing margin and disciplined target selection.
DRL-LIVE-008.su:
Use singles lines as boundaries (doubles played inside singles court).
Optional: add 'middle bonus'—+1 if ball goes through middle and wins.
DRL-LIVE-008.ex:
1) Start points with serve or coach feed.
2) Play doubles but all balls must land inside singles court.
3) Score to 11; rotate pairs every 10 minutes.
4) Keep a running tally of unforced errors caused by over-aiming corners.
DRL-LIVE-008.cu:
• 'Aim inside the lines—big targets.'
• 'Use height and spin to create safety.'
• 'Move your feet; don’t reach.'
• 'Volley deep/through middle.'
DRL-LIVE-008.ef:
• Players try to paint lines → set “2-feet inside” target zones.
• Net player crowding → remind spacing and reaction time.
• Too many short balls → enforce depth target past service line.
DRL-LIVE-008.rg:
Allow doubles alleys; use cones to slightly narrow instead of singles lines.
Use green balls for juniors.
DRL-LIVE-008.pg:
Add 'no lob' or 'lob only' rounds.
Add serve+1 pattern requirement.
Add time clock: 60 seconds to win 2 points.
DRL-LIVE-008.sm: Lower unforced error rate; improved accuracy to big targets; better decision discipline under pressure.
DRL-LIVE-008.sf: High intensity; ensure lanes and spacing; manage heat and hydration.

DRL-LIVE-009.o: Create 'match-tight' focus by making unforced errors immediately consequential, reinforcing margin management and reset routines.
DRL-LIVE-009.su:
Standard liveball teams. Define unforced error: missed ball on a neutral/offensive situation (coach judgment).
Set turnover rule: any unforced error = immediate loss of mini-game point AND instant rotation.
DRL-LIVE-009.ex:
1) Start with coach feed.
2) Play out points.
3) If a team commits an unforced error, stop immediately and rotate them off (new challengers in).
4) Keep the score continuously for champions to 11 points.
DRL-LIVE-009.cu:
• 'Margin over ego.'
• 'Reset fast: breath + cue word.'
• 'Neutral balls must be deep with spin.'
• 'Communication stays positive.'
DRL-LIVE-009.ef:
• Players argue calls → pre-define coach as final decision.
• Players play too safe → define 'good aggressive miss' vs 'careless miss'; reward purposeful aggression.
• Spiraling after error → enforce 5-second reset rule before next point.
DRL-LIVE-009.rg:
Remove instant rotation; just count unforced errors per 10 points.
Use bigger targets and slower feed.
DRL-LIVE-009.pg:
Add bonus for winners through middle.
Require a specific pattern (serve+1, return+1).
Time-pressure: 30 seconds per point start-to-finish.
DRL-LIVE-009.sm: Reduced unforced errors per 10 points; improved reset compliance; better emotional stability under consequence.
DRL-LIVE-009.sf: High stress format; keep coaching tone constructive. Ensure warm-up and manage fatigue.
DRL-LIVE-009.tg: Liveball, Consistency, Pressure, Emotional regulation, Competition

DRL-LIVE-010.o: Train scoreboard management and composure by repeatedly starting points/mini-games in a deficit and learning to simplify patterns.
DRL-LIVE-010.su:
Standard liveball court. Choose a simple 'Plan A' pattern for both teams (e.g., deep middle return, crosscourt first).
Each mini-game starts with one team down 0–2.
DRL-LIVE-010.ex:
1) Assign Team A to start each mini-game down 0–2.
2) Play mini-game to 5.
3) Swap which team starts down every mini-game.
4) Between mini-games: 30-second coaching prompt—'What pattern will you commit to?'
DRL-LIVE-010.cu:
• 'Simplify when down: big targets, deep middle.'
• 'Play the next point only.'
• 'Use routines to control arousal.'
DRL-LIVE-010.ef:
• Players rush and miss early → require 2 neutral balls before attacking when down.
• Negative self-talk → cue word list on whiteboard.
• Forgetting plan → require verbal plan statement before first point.
DRL-LIVE-010.rg:
Start down 0–1 instead of 0–2.
Use coach feed and cooperative start.
DRL-LIVE-010.pg:
Start down 0–3.
Add time constraint (must win 5 points in 4 minutes).
Track MPI and reset compliance visibly.
DRL-LIVE-010.sm: Improved performance in deficit situations; better pattern adherence; increased reset compliance under stress.
DRL-LIVE-010.sf: Monitor emotional temperature; keep it developmental, not punitive.
DRL-LIVE-010.tg: Liveball, Pressure, Scoreboard management, Mental, Competition

DRL-LIVE-011.o: Develop aggressive net play patterns by making serve-and-volley a repeatable option with clear targets and scoring incentives.
DRL-LIVE-011.su:
Standard doubles. Choose one server each point.
Rule: serving team must serve-and-volley (server must come in behind serve).
DRL-LIVE-011.ex:
1) Start with serve.
2) Server must approach the net immediately after serve (no staying back).
3) Play out point.
4) Scoring: serving team earns 2 points if they win at net within first 3 shots; otherwise normal 1 point.
5) Rotate servers every 2 points; rotate teams every 10 minutes.
DRL-LIVE-011.cu:
• 'Serve to set up first volley (body or wide).' 
• 'Split before returner hits.'
• 'First volley through middle/feet.'
• 'Partner closes with server.'
DRL-LIVE-011.ef:
• Server hesitates → cue 'commit to the net'.
• First volley too big → cue 'punch, don’t swing'.
• Returners panic → require deep middle return first; then pass/lob.
DRL-LIVE-011.rg:
Allow server to stop at service line (half-volley zone).
Coach feeds return to start (reduce serve pressure).
DRL-LIVE-011.pg:
Returners get bonus for successful pass/lob.
Serve must hit a called target.
Add 2nd volley requirement.
DRL-LIVE-011.sm: Higher success rate of first volley and net finishes; better serve placement; improved transition footwork.
DRL-LIVE-011.sf: High sprint load and shoulder volume. Enforce rest and safe net spacing.
DRL-LIVE-011.tg: Liveball, Serve, Volley, Transition, Doubles, Patterns

DRL-CARDIO-003.n: Cardio Tennis: Crosscourt + Sideline Touch Intervals
DRL-CARDIO-003.o: Drive conditioning while maintaining crosscourt consistency and recovery positioning.
DRL-CARDIO-003.su:
Players in 2 lines behind baseline (deuce/ad). Coach feeds crosscourt.
Place a cone on each sideline near doubles alley (or near singles line for narrow).
DRL-CARDIO-003.ex:
1) Player hits 1 crosscourt ball to big target.
2) Immediately sprint to touch sideline cone on their side.
3) Sprint back to home base and split.
4) Coach feeds next ball (alternate sides).
5) Work: 30–40 seconds; Rest: 20–30 seconds; rotate.
DRL-CARDIO-003.cu:
• 'Hit then recover—don’t watch.'
• 'Big targets; heavy breath.'
• 'Decelerate under control.'
DRL-CARDIO-003.ef:
• Technique breaks → slow feed, enlarge targets.
• Players cut corners → mark a clear touch point.
• Late split-step → coach calls 'split' rhythmically.
DRL-CARDIO-003.rg:
Remove sprint; replace with quick shuffle.
Use green balls and shorten distance for juniors.
DRL-CARDIO-003.pg:
Add 2-ball sequence before touch.
Add down-the-line change after 4 crosscourts.
Add scoring: point only counts if ball lands in target AND touch completed.
DRL-CARDIO-003.sm: Sustains quality shots under high HR; improved footwork efficiency and conditioning tolerance.
DRL-CARDIO-003.sf: Not for beginners or injury flare-ups. Enforce safe deceleration and hydration.

DRL-CARDIO-004.o: Blend quick volley reactions with repeated net-to-base movement to build tennis-specific conditioning.
DRL-CARDIO-004.su:
Players start at service line with rackets. Coach at net feeding volleys.
Mark a cone at baseline as the 'touch line'.
DRL-CARDIO-004.ex:
1) Player hits 2 controlled volleys to big targets.
2) Immediately backpedal/shuffle to baseline cone and touch.
3) Sprint forward to service line, split-step.
4) Repeat for 30–45 seconds, then rotate.
DRL-CARDIO-004.cu:
• 'Short punch volley.'
• 'Turn and run—don’t backpedal fast.'
• 'Split on feed.'
DRL-CARDIO-004.ef:
• Players backpedal dangerously → teach turn-and-run recovery.
• Volleys sprayed → slow feed, bigger targets, stabilize racquet face.
• Fatigue causes sloppy footwork → shorten interval.
DRL-CARDIO-004.rg:
Only 1 volley then touch.
Touch service line instead of baseline.
DRL-CARDIO-004.pg:
Add 3 volleys (forehand/backhand/angle).
Add overhead on 3rd feed.
Add scoring: count only clean volley reps.
DRL-CARDIO-004.sm: Maintains volley quality under fatigue; improved net recovery habits and conditioning.
DRL-CARDIO-004.sf: High change-of-direction volume. Emphasize safe turning, no reckless backpedaling.

DRL-CARDIO-004-V1.o: Blend quick volley reactions with repeated net-to-base movement to build tennis-specific conditioning.
DRL-CARDIO-004-V1.su:
Players start at service line with rackets. Coach at net feeding volleys.
Mark a cone at baseline as the 'touch line'.
DRL-CARDIO-004-V1.ex:

DRL-CARDIO-004-V2.sf: High change-of-direction volume. Emphasize safe turning, no reckless backpedaling.

DRL-CARDIO-005.o: Train serve/return execution when the body is taxed by adding a short sprint before live first-4-shot play.
DRL-CARDIO-005.su:
Pairs or groups. Place a cone on baseline center mark and a cone near doubles alley.
Server starts at baseline; returner normal return position.
DRL-CARDIO-005.ex:
1) Server sprint-touch the alley cone and returns to serve position.
2) Immediately serve.
3) Play out to 4 shots only (serve, return, +1, +1). Stop and score.
4) Scoring: +1 if server wins advantage by shot 4; +1 if returner neutralizes with depth.
5) Rotate every 6 points.
DRL-CARDIO-005.cu:
• 'Breathe before toss.'
• 'Serve to big target; spin if needed.'
• 'Return deep first.'
• 'After sprint: get feet quiet at contact.'
DRL-CARDIO-005.ef:
• Serve pace drops → prioritize placement/spin.
• Returner blocks short → aim deep middle.
• Players forget count → coach calls shot numbers aloud.
DRL-CARDIO-005.rg:
Remove sprint; do quick shuffle only.
Play 2-shot version (serve + return).
DRL-CARDIO-005.pg:
Increase sprint distance; add time limit.
Play full point after shot 4.
Require specific pattern (serve wide, +1 to open court).
DRL-CARDIO-005.sm: Improved first-serve-in and return depth under fatigue; better early-point focus.
DRL-CARDIO-005.sf: High shoulder load and sprint load. Ensure warm-up and manage total serve volume.
DRL-CARDIO-005.tg: Cardio, Serve, Return, First 4 shots, Pressure, Conditioning

DRL-CARDIO-007.o: Stress stroke stability and recovery by pairing rapid technical reps with a short high-intensity run.
DRL-CARDIO-007.su:
Coach with basket at baseline center.
Mark a touch point at back fence or baseline cone.
DRL-CARDIO-007.ex:
1) Coach feeds 2 balls quickly (FH then BH or random).
2) Player hits both to big targets.
3) Immediately sprint to touch fence/cone and return to ready.
4) Rest 20–30 seconds; repeat 6–10 reps.
DRL-CARDIO-007.cu:
• 'Compact swing under speed.'
• 'Recover and breathe.'
• 'Big targets; accept less pace.'
DRL-CARDIO-007.ef:
• Player sprays balls → slow feed; enforce height/spin.
• Sprint form sloppy → shorten sprint; focus on safe decel.
• Over-gripping racket → cue 'soft hands'.
DRL-CARDIO-007.rg:
Only 1 fed ball then touch.
Walk back recovery instead of sprint.
DRL-CARDIO-007.pg:
3-ball sequence; random feeds.
Add point scoring: 1 point per target hit.
Add competitive ladder (highest score wins).
DRL-CARDIO-007.sm: Maintained shot quality under fatigue; improved ability to reset quickly between reps.
DRL-CARDIO-007.sf: Not for beginners. Monitor for calf/hamstring risk; warm up thoroughly.

DRL-CARDIO-008.o: Develop transition fitness and first-volley consistency with repeated approach sequences under interval demands.
DRL-CARDIO-008.su:
Coach feeds short balls from basket. Place a sprint cone at baseline corner.
Players rotate every 45–60 seconds.
DRL-CARDIO-008.ex:
1) Coach feeds short ball.
2) Player hits approach to big target and moves in.
3) Coach feeds volley; player volleys to open court.
4) Player sprints to baseline corner cone and returns to ready.
5) Repeat until interval ends; rotate.
DRL-CARDIO-008.cu:
• 'Approach deep; close the net.'
• 'Split before volley.'
• 'Turn and run to cone; safe decel.'
DRL-CARDIO-008.ef:
• Approaches float → cue 'drive through contact'.
• Volleys sprayed → reduce pace; aim through middle.
• Fatigue causes sloppy movement → shorten interval.
DRL-CARDIO-008.rg:
Remove sprint; walk recovery.
Feed from service line to reduce movement distance.
DRL-CARDIO-008.pg:
Add second volley.
Add defensive passing shot for opponent (live).
Add scoring: must finish at net to score.
DRL-CARDIO-008.sm: Higher transition frequency with stable first volley; improved conditioning for closing points.
DRL-CARDIO-008.sf: High plyometric/stop-start load. Monitor knees/ankles and adjust.
DRL-CARDIO-008.ct: Full court (coach feeding or live ball)

DRL-CARDIO-008-V1.n: Cardio Tennis: Continuous Approach Circuit (Approach–Volley–Sprint) — Simplified
DRL-CARDIO-008-V1.o: Develop transition fitness and first-volley consistency with repeated approach sequences under interval demands.

DRL-CARDIO-009.n: Cardio Tennis: Doubles Switch & Talk (Communication Under Fatigue)
DRL-CARDIO-009.o: Force purposeful communication and movement (switching) in doubles while heart rate is elevated.
DRL-CARDIO-009.su:
4 players. Coach feeds to start each point.
Rule: after every ball hit through the middle (between partners), the receiving team must SWITCH positions (call 'switch').
DRL-CARDIO-009.ex:
1) Coach feeds ball in.
2) Play out point.
3) Enforce switch rule whenever middle ball occurs.
4) Score to 11; rotate players every 10 minutes.
DRL-CARDIO-009.cu:
• 'Call early: switch/mine/yours.'
• 'Sprint then settle: stop on balance.'
• 'Play through middle to create chaos for them.'
DRL-CARDIO-009.ef:
• Players forget to switch → coach stops point and replays.
• Collisions → define clear lanes and switching responsibilities.
• Over-hitting middle → remind to still pick good targets.
DRL-CARDIO-009.rg:
Switch only on coach call.
Allow no-switch but count communications instead.
DRL-CARDIO-009.pg:
Switch on any lob.
Add bonus if you win a point immediately after switching.
Add time rounds (3 min) to raise intensity.
DRL-CARDIO-009.sm: More effective doubles communication; better movement coordination under fatigue.
DRL-CARDIO-009.sf: Collision risk—coach must enforce spacing and clear rules.

DRL-CARDIO-010.n: Cardio Tennis: 30/30 Baseline Intervals (Targets + HR Control)
DRL-CARDIO-010.o: Simple, repeatable conditioning set that keeps technical integrity: hit to targets for 30 seconds, rest for 30 seconds, repeat.
DRL-CARDIO-010.su:
Set 2 large targets crosscourt and 1 deep-middle target.
Pairs rally crosscourt or coach feeds.
DRL-CARDIO-010.ex:
1) 30 seconds: rally to target with controlled pace (count hits-in-target).
2) 30 seconds: rest/breathe.
3) Repeat 6–10 rounds.
4) Track best round score and average score.
DRL-CARDIO-010.cu:
• 'Quality over chaos.'
• 'Breathe out on contact.'
• 'Big targets; height and spin.'
DRL-CARDIO-010.ef:
• Players swing too hard → cap pace; reward target hits.
• Score drops quickly → add 15 seconds rest.
• Technique breaks → shorten interval to 20 seconds.
DRL-CARDIO-010.rg:
Use mini tennis (service boxes) for beginners.
Use green balls; allow one bounce extra (catch-bounce-hit).
DRL-CARDIO-010.pg:
Decrease target size.
Add direction change on coach call.
Add pressure: last 2 rounds count double.
DRL-CARDIO-010.sm: Sustained rally quality at high heart rate; improved conditioning without sacrificing control.
DRL-CARDIO-010.sf: Hydration and heat management required. Stop if dizziness or excessive fatigue.

DRL-PREHAB-001.n: Shoulder Band RAMP (External Rotation + Scap Set)
DRL-PREHAB-001.o: Reduce shoulder injury risk and improve serve readiness with a consistent, low-cost band activation sequence.
DRL-PREHAB-001.su:
Light resistance bands. 6–8 stations along fence.
Sequence is done as part of warm-up (RAMP).
DRL-PREHAB-001.ex:
1) Scap retraction rows: 12 reps.
2) External rotation (elbow at side): 10 reps/side.
3) External rotation at 90/90 (if pain-free): 8 reps/side.
4) Serratus wall slide or punch: 10 reps.
5) Finish with 5 shadow serves focusing on smooth rhythm.
DRL-PREHAB-001.cu:
• 'Slow control—no shaking.'
• 'Shoulder blade moves first, arm follows.'
• 'Pain-free range only.'
DRL-PREHAB-001.ef:
• Too much resistance → use lighter band.
• Shrugging → cue 'down and back'.
• Elbow flares → keep elbow tucked or supported.
DRL-PREHAB-001.rg:
Do only rows + elbow-at-side ER.
Cut reps in half.
DRL-PREHAB-001.pg:
Add 2nd set.
Add isometric holds (5 seconds) at end range.
Pair with serve volume tracking.
DRL-PREHAB-001.sm: Better serve warm-up readiness; reduced shoulder soreness trends; improved load/recovery flags.
DRL-PREHAB-001.sf: Not medical advice. Stop if pain. Refer to physio/medical professional for persistent symptoms.
DRL-PREHAB-001.st: STG-09-11_GREEN, STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-09-13_HP, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT, STG-ADULT_COMP
DRL-PREHAB-001.tg: Prehab, Injury prevention, Shoulder, Serve, Warm-up, Mobility

DRL-PREHAB-001-V2.tg: Prehab, Injury prevention, Shoulder, Serve, Warm-up, Mobility

DRL-PREHAB-002.n: Scap + Core Integrity Circuit (Y-T-W + Dead Bug)
DRL-PREHAB-002.o: Improve posture, scap control, and trunk stability that support healthy serving and stroke mechanics.
DRL-PREHAB-002.su:
Mats or towels; light bands optional.
Circuit: 2 rounds with 30–45 sec rest.
DRL-PREHAB-002.ex:
1) Prone Y: 8 reps.
2) Prone T: 8 reps.
3) Prone W: 8 reps.
4) Dead bug (slow): 6 reps/side.
5) Side plank: 20–30 sec/side.
6) Finish with 10 controlled shadow swings (FH/BH).
DRL-PREHAB-002.cu:
• 'Ribs down; breathe behind the shield.'
• 'Quality reps > quantity.'
• 'No neck tension.'
DRL-PREHAB-002.ef:
• Lower back arches in dead bug → reduce range; exhale.
• Shoulder pinch in prone work → reduce range or switch to band rows.
• Side plank collapse → shorten time.
DRL-PREHAB-002.rg:
1 round only.
Replace prone YTW with band rows.
DRL-PREHAB-002.pg:
Add light weights (0.5–1 kg) if clean.
Add anti-rotation press (Pallof) 10 reps/side.
DRL-PREHAB-002.sm: Reduced shoulder/low-back tightness; improved readiness; better movement quality during tennis sessions.
DRL-PREHAB-002.sf: Avoid painful ranges. Supervise form. Not a rehab protocol for acute injury.
DRL-PREHAB-002.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-09-13_HP, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT, STG-ADULT_COMP
DRL-PREHAB-002.tg: Prehab, Injury prevention, Core, Shoulder, Strength, Mobility

DRL-PREHAB-004.n: Hip & Groin Prep (Glute Med + Copenhagen Progression)
DRL-PREHAB-004.o: Support lateral movement and deceleration with targeted hip and groin strength prep.
DRL-PREHAB-004.su:
Mini-bands + bench (or partner) for Copenhagen.
Circuit 2 rounds.
DRL-PREHAB-004.ex:
1) Mini-band lateral walks: 10 steps each way.
2) Single-leg glute bridge: 8 reps/side.
3) Copenhagen side plank (short lever): 10–20 sec/side.
4) Adductor rock-back mobility: 6 reps/side.
5) Finish with 4 lateral shuffle accelerations (5 m).
DRL-PREHAB-004.cu:
• 'Knees track over toes.'
• 'Hips level; no collapsing.'
• 'Short lever first—earn long lever.'
DRL-PREHAB-004.ef:
• Knee valgus in walks → lighter band, smaller steps.
• Copenhagen too hard → shorten lever or reduce time.
• Low back arch → cue ribs down, squeeze glutes.
DRL-PREHAB-004.rg:
Skip Copenhagen; do side plank only.
One round only.
DRL-PREHAB-004.pg:
Long-lever Copenhagen.
Add decel drills on court after (controlled).
DRL-PREHAB-004.sm: Improved movement quality; fewer hip/groin tightness complaints; better readiness scores.
DRL-PREHAB-004.sf: Do not perform with acute groin pain. Scale aggressively for safety.
DRL-PREHAB-004.tg: Prehab, Injury prevention, Hip, Groin, Strength, Mobility

DRL-RECOV-001.o: Standardize recovery behaviors so athletes leave sessions calmer, looser, and better prepared for next-day training.
DRL-RECOV-001.su:
No equipment required (optional foam roller).
Run this at end of every session.
DRL-RECOV-001.ex:
1) 3 minutes: downshift breathing (4-sec inhale, 6-sec exhale) + attention on slow exhale.
2) 2 minutes: mobility flow (hips, thoracic rotation, calves).
3) 1 minute: hydration check—drink water; confirm electrolyte plan if hot day.
Optional: quick check-in question: '0–10 soreness today?'
DRL-RECOV-001.cu:
• 'Longer exhale = downshift.'
• 'Move through pain-free range.'
• 'Hydrate before you’re thirsty.'
DRL-RECOV-001.ef:
• Athletes rush off → make it part of session structure and attendance.
• Poor compliance → track as a habit KPI (simple check mark).
DRL-RECOV-001.rg:
Do only 60-sec breathing + water.
Do mobility at home with video.
DRL-RECOV-001.pg:
Add 2–3 min foam rolling.
Add sleep target and next-day soreness note.
DRL-RECOV-001.sm: Improved recovery consistency; fewer load/recovery red flags; better attendance consistency.
DRL-RECOV-001.sf: General wellness guidance only. Hydration needs vary; consult professional for medical needs.
DRL-RECOV-001.st: STG-03-04_LTS, STG-05-07_RED, STG-07-09_ORANGE, STG-09-11_GREEN, STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-09-13_HP, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT, STG-ADULT_BEGINNER, STG-ADULT_COMP
DRL-RECOV-001.tg: Recovery, Mobility, Breath, Hydration, Injury prevention

DRL-RECOV-001-V1.o: Standardize recovery behaviors so athletes leave sessions calmer, looser, and better prepared for next-day training.
DRL-RECOV-001-V1.su:

1) 3 minutes: downshift breathing (4-sec inhale, 6-sec exhale) + attention on slow exhale.
2) 2 minutes: mobility flow (hips, thoracic rotation, calves).
3) 1 minute: hydration check—drink water; confirm electrolyte plan if hot day.
Optional: quick check-in question: '0–10 soreness today?'
DRL-RECOV-001-V1.cu:
• 'Longer exhale = downshift.'
• 'Move through pain-free range.'
• 'Hydrate before you’re thirsty.'
DRL-RECOV-001-V1.ef:
• Athletes rush off → make it part of session structure and attendance.
• Poor compliance → track as a habit KPI (simple check mark).
DRL-RECOV-001-V1.rg:
Do only 60-sec breathing + water.
Do mobility at home with video.
DRL-RECOV-001-V1.pg:
Add 2–3 min foam rolling.
Add sleep target and next-day soreness note.
DRL-RECOV-001-V1.sm: Improved recovery consistency; fewer load/recovery red flags; better attendance consistency.
DRL-RECOV-001-V1.sf: General wellness guidance only. Hydration needs vary; consult professional for medical needs.

No equipment required (optional foam roller).

DRL-RECOV-001-V2.ex:

DRL-RECOV-001-V2.tg: Recovery, Mobility, Breath, Hydration, Injury prevention

DRL-RECOV-002.o: Improve tournament performance by standardizing what athletes do between matches to recover and refocus quickly.
DRL-RECOV-002.su:
Used during tournaments: between matches or after a set.
Needs water, snack, shade/towel.
DRL-RECOV-002.ex:
1) 60 sec: breathing + calm down (slow exhale).
2) 90 sec: mobility (hips, calves, thoracic rotations).
3) 60 sec: hydrate + electrolytes if hot.
4) 60 sec: quick refuel (carb + small protein).
5) 30 sec: plan A reminder + cue word for next match.
DRL-RECOV-002.cu:
• 'Recover first, analyze second.'
• 'Simple plan A.'
• 'Stay off your phone for 5 minutes.'
DRL-RECOV-002.ef:
• Athletes over-analyze → enforce the 5-minute structure.
• Under-fueling → pre-pack snack kit.
• Staying in sun → move to shade.
DRL-RECOV-002.rg:
Only hydrate + breathe (2 minutes).
Coach-led prompts.
DRL-RECOV-002.pg:
Add 3-minute light jog/walk if stiff.
Add quick stat review (serve %, return depth).
DRL-RECOV-002.sm: Better next-match start quality; improved emotional regulation and energy management.
DRL-RECOV-002.sf: Heat risk: prioritize shade, electrolytes, and cooling strategies.

DRL-NUTR-001.n: Match Fueling Plan Builder (Pre / During / Post Checklist)
DRL-NUTR-001.o: Reduce performance dips and cramping by giving athletes/parents a repeatable fueling plan template for practice and match days.
DRL-NUTR-001.su:
Whiteboard or printed checklist. Coach provides examples of simple options.
Athlete fills in their plan for next match/training block.
DRL-NUTR-001.ex:
1) Pre-match meal (3–4 hrs): choose carb + protein + fluids.
2) 30–60 min pre: small carb snack + water.
3) During match: sip schedule + carb/electrolyte plan.
4) Post-match (0–60 min): protein + carbs + fluids.
5) Write a 'non-negotiable' for next week (e.g., 1 bottle/hour in heat).
DRL-NUTR-001.cu:
• 'Practice your match fueling in practice.'
• 'Simple beats perfect.'
• 'Hydration is scheduled, not optional.'
DRL-NUTR-001.ef:
• Athletes choose new foods on match day → remind: no surprises.
• Too much sugar at once → smaller, frequent doses.
• No plan → create a default kit list.
DRL-NUTR-001.rg:
Coach provides a default plan and athlete just commits.
Parents lead for younger athletes.
DRL-NUTR-001.pg:
Track compliance and adjust weekly.
Add electrolyte testing and heat plan for summer.
DRL-NUTR-001.sm: More stable energy; fewer cramps; improved readiness and recovery trend.
DRL-NUTR-001.sf: General guidance only; athletes with medical conditions should consult a professional.
DRL-NUTR-001.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-09-13_HP, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT, STG-ADULT_COMP

DRL-NUTR-002.n: Hydration Heat Protocol (Bottle System + Electrolyte Triggers)
DRL-NUTR-002.o: Prevent heat-related performance drops by standardizing hydration behaviors and making them measurable.
DRL-NUTR-002.su:
Coach sets a simple bottle rule per session (e.g., 1 bottle per hour) and defines electrolyte triggers (hot day, heavy sweater, cramps history).
Use a quick pre-session check: urine color scale (optional education).
DRL-NUTR-002.ex:
1) Pre: confirm athlete has bottle; define target intake for session.
2) During: drink at every 2nd changeover or every 10 minutes (timer).
3) Post: refill and finish bottle within 60 minutes.
4) Log: did athlete meet bottle target? (Yes/No).
DRL-NUTR-002.cu:
• 'Hydrate early.'
• 'Electrolytes when heat/sweat is high.'
• 'Cooling strategies: shade, towel, ice if available.'
DRL-NUTR-002.ef:
• Athletes forget → coach uses timer and makes it routine.
• Bottles too small → standardize size.
• Over-drinking discomfort → smaller sips more often.
DRL-NUTR-002.rg:
Only pre + post hydration checks.
No logging; just reminders.
DRL-NUTR-002.pg:
Add compliance tracking for teams.
Add heat acclimation plan (progressive exposure).
DRL-NUTR-002.sm: Fewer heat-related dips; better recovery and training quality in summer conditions.
DRL-NUTR-002.sf: Heat illness risk is serious—follow local safety guidelines. Stop activity if symptoms appear.
DRL-NUTR-002.st: STG-03-04_LTS, STG-05-07_RED, STG-07-09_ORANGE, STG-09-11_GREEN, STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-09-13_HP, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT, STG-ADULT_BEGINNER, STG-ADULT_COMP

DRL-WUP-002.o: Build physical literacy (agility, balance, coordination) using playful movement patterns that transfer to tennis footwork.
DRL-WUP-002.su:
Set 4 stations (10–15 m each):
1) Bear crawl → sprint.
2) Frog jumps.
3) Balance beam (line) + single-leg freeze.
4) Overhand throw to target (cone tower).
DRL-WUP-002.ex:
1) Split players into groups.
2) 45 seconds per station, 15 seconds rotate.
3) Repeat 2 rounds.
4) Finish with 30 sec 'ready position freeze' game.
DRL-WUP-002.cu:
• 'Soft knees, quiet head.'
• 'Freeze like a statue.'
• 'Throw like a serve.'
DRL-WUP-002.ef:
• Kids crash/chaos → reduce distance, add clear lanes.
• Overhand throw is sidearm → demonstrate simple 'elbow up' cue.
• Low engagement → use team points per station.
DRL-WUP-002.rg:
Do only 2 stations.
Walk instead of jump.
DRL-WUP-002.pg:
Add reaction cue (coach call left/right).
Add racket carry during movement.
Add time challenge with quality standards.
DRL-WUP-002.sm: Better coordination and engagement; improved readiness for on-court hitting blocks.
DRL-WUP-002.sf: Ensure safe surfaces; avoid slipping; scale jumps for younger kids.

DRL-RKT-004.o: Improve racket familiarity and self-control by linking movement speed with racket/ball control tasks.
DRL-RKT-004.su:
Each player with racket + ball.
Coach calls colors:
Green = move fast (dribble/tap while moving).
Yellow = slow control.
Red = freeze (ball balanced on strings).
DRL-RKT-004.ex:
1) Players start in a box.
2) Coach calls Green/Yellow/Red every 5–8 seconds.
3) On Red: freeze and hold balance 3 seconds.
4) Add challenges: switch hands, change direction, stop-start.
DRL-RKT-004.cu:
• 'Soft hands, quiet racket.'
• 'Eyes on ball.'
• 'Freeze = still body.'
DRL-RKT-004.ef:
• Ball drops constantly → use foam ball, slow down.
• Kids collide → bigger space and clear lanes.
• Over-gripping → cue 'butterfly hands'.
DRL-RKT-004.rg:
No movement; stationary taps only.
No ball: just racket carry + freeze.
DRL-RKT-004.pg:
Add partner tag while controlling ball.
Add 'spin call' (topspin taps vs slice taps).
Add timed balance record.
DRL-RKT-004.sm: Longer continuous control times; improved confidence with racket handling.
DRL-RKT-004.sf: Keep spacing; use soft balls; avoid aggressive swinging in crowded space.

DRL-RKT-005.o: Build contact confidence by progressing from simple taps to controlled self-rallies with clear numeric goals.
DRL-RKT-005.su:
Each player with racket + ball.
Set ladder goals: 1 bounce, 2 bounces, 3 bounces, up to 5 (or 10 for adults).
DRL-RKT-005.ex:
1) Start with drop-hit-catch (1 bounce) × 5.
2) Progress to drop-hit, let it bounce twice, catch.
3) Continue increasing bounce goal.
4) Once 5 achieved: transition to partner rally in mini-court.
DRL-RKT-005.cu:
• 'Contact in front.'
• 'Small swing, high success.'
• 'Bend knees to track bounce.'
DRL-RKT-005.ef:
• Players swing too big → cue 'pancake swing' (small).
• Ball goes everywhere → use foam/red ball; reduce height.
• Frustration → set micro-goals and celebrate.
DRL-RKT-005.rg:
Use hand toss + catch only.
Use larger foam ball.
DRL-RKT-005.pg:
Add target zones for where bounce must land.
Add alternate forehand/backhand.
Add time challenge: best ladder in 60 sec.
DRL-RKT-005.sm: Increased contact consistency and confidence; faster transition into cooperative rallies.
DRL-RKT-005.sf: Ensure safe spacing; players keep swings compact.

DRL-GAME-003.o: Develop stop-start agility and confidence with a tennis-flavored tag game that keeps kids moving.
DRL-GAME-003.su:
Define a rectangle play area. 'Sharks' are taggers; 'Minnows' carry ball on racket strings or dribble taps.
If tagged, do 3 quick 'tennis jacks' then re-enter.
DRL-GAME-003.ex:
1) Start with 1–2 sharks.
2) Minnows cross the area while controlling ball.
3) Tagged minnows become sharks (or do penalty and continue).
4) Change constraints each round: dribble taps, balance carry, bounce-catch.
DRL-GAME-003.cu:
• 'Head up: see space.'
• 'Soft touches.'
• 'Freeze control when crowded.'
DRL-GAME-003.ef:
• Chaos → shrink number of sharks and enlarge space.
• Ball drops → switch to foam ball and simple carry.
• Kids upset when tagged → use playful penalty, not elimination.
DRL-GAME-003.rg:
No racket: run only.
Walk speed.
DRL-GAME-003.pg:
Add throwing to target after crossing.
Add backpedal rounds.
Add team scoring: total successful crossings.
DRL-GAME-003.sm: High engagement and improved movement skills; increased comfort moving with racket/ball.
DRL-GAME-003.sf: Avoid collisions; enforce 'no diving' rule; use soft balls.

DRL-GAME-003-V1.sm: High engagement and improved movement skills; increased comfort moving with racket/ball.
DRL-GAME-003-V1.sf: Avoid collisions; enforce 'no diving' rule; use soft balls.

DRL-GAME-003-V2.o: Develop stop-start agility and confidence with a tennis-flavored tag game that keeps kids moving.
DRL-GAME-003-V2.su:
Define a rectangle play area. 'Sharks' are taggers; 'Minnows' carry ball on racket strings or dribble taps.
If tagged, do 3 quick 'tennis jacks' then re-enter.
DRL-GAME-003-V2.ex:
1) Start with 1–2 sharks.
2) Minnows cross the area while controlling ball.
3) Tagged minnows become sharks (or do penalty and continue).
4) Change constraints each round: dribble taps, balance carry, bounce-catch.
DRL-GAME-003-V2.cu:
• 'Head up: see space.'
• 'Soft touches.'
• 'Freeze control when crowded.'
DRL-GAME-003-V2.ef:
• Chaos → shrink number of sharks and enlarge space.
• Ball drops → switch to foam ball and simple carry.
• Kids upset when tagged → use playful penalty, not elimination.
DRL-GAME-003-V2.rg:
No racket: run only.
Walk speed.
DRL-GAME-003-V2.pg:
Add throwing to target after crossing.
Add backpedal rounds.
Add team scoring: total successful crossings.
DRL-GAME-003-V2.sm: High engagement and improved movement skills; increased comfort moving with racket/ball.
DRL-GAME-003-V2.sf: Avoid collisions; enforce 'no diving' rule; use soft balls.

DRL-GAME-004.n: Target City Build & Knockdown (Team Accuracy Game)
DRL-GAME-004.o: Teach direction and height control by turning targets into a collaborative 'build then knockdown' challenge.
DRL-GAME-004.su:
Each team builds a 'city' of cone towers (3–5 cones each) on opposite side of net.
Players rally/throw-feed aiming to knock down opponent city.
DRL-GAME-004.ex:
1) Teams start by building 3 towers.
2) Coach feeds; players hit controlled shots to knock targets.
3) When a tower is knocked, opponent must rebuild (10-sec rebuild window).
4) Play for 5 minutes; team with most knockdowns wins.
DRL-GAME-004.cu:
• 'Aim big target first (middle of tower).'
• 'Use height to clear net.'
• 'Small swings = accuracy.'
DRL-GAME-004.ef:
• Players swing too hard → enforce 'control only' rule; no winners.
• Towers too easy → move them back or make smaller.
• Waiting lines → set stations or 2 courts.
DRL-GAME-004.rg:
Throw-feed instead of hit.
Bigger targets (hula hoops).
DRL-GAME-004.pg:
Require a specific direction (crosscourt only).
Add bonus if you knock down with volley.
Add time pressure rebuild.
DRL-GAME-004.sm: Improved accuracy to targets; high engagement; more cooperative energy.
DRL-GAME-004.sf: Use safe spacing; avoid running into net/cones.

DRL-MINI-003.n: Mini Tennis: Crosscourt Corner Challenge (Big Targets)
DRL-MINI-003.o: Build crosscourt rally habits and directional control with scaled court constraints.
DRL-MINI-003.su:
Pair players in opposite service boxes. Mark a big target zone crosscourt (inside service box).
Optional: use mini-net.
DRL-MINI-003.ex:
1) Cooperative rally crosscourt only.
2) Goal: 10 in a row into target.
3) After goal: 1 player may try controlled down-the-line change; if missed, back to crosscourt.
4) Rotate partners every 5 minutes.
DRL-MINI-003.cu:
• 'Up and over—use height.'
• 'Recover to middle of box.'
• 'Small swing, quick feet.'
DRL-MINI-003.ef:
• Ball netting → increase net clearance cue.
• Players aim too sharp → widen targets.
• Standing still → require mini split-step on bounce.
DRL-MINI-003.pg:
Smaller target.
Add scoring game (first to 7) after cooperative phase.
Add approach/volley finish when ball is short.
DRL-MINI-003.sm: Higher mini-court rally max; improved directional control confidence.
DRL-MINI-003.sf: Keep distances safe; avoid crowded courts.
DRL-MINI-003.tg: Mini tennis, Direction, Consistency, Rally, Targets

DRL-MINI-004.n: Mini Tennis: Underhand Serve Start Points (Score + Rhythm)
DRL-MINI-004.o: Teach point starts, simple scoring, and early decision-making using an underhand serve/toss to keep rallies alive.
DRL-MINI-004.su:
Players in service boxes. Use underhand serve or drop-hit as point start.
Coach sets simple scoring (first to 7).
DRL-MINI-004.ex:
1) Start each point with underhand serve to big target.
2) Play out point in mini-court.
3) After every 2 points, players switch sides.
4) Coach prompts one rule per round: crosscourt only, deep-only, or 3-ball minimum.
DRL-MINI-004.cu:
• 'Start the point safely.'
• 'Big targets; keep ball in play.'
• 'Next ball reset.'
DRL-MINI-004.ef:
• Players 'slam' underhand serve → cue 'roll it in'.
• Confusion on score → use visible scoreboard or coins.
• Short attention → shorten games to 5.
DRL-MINI-004.rg:
Coach feeds every point.
Use cooperative scoring (team goal).
DRL-MINI-004.pg:
Add overhand serve for older players.
Require 2nd ball to go crosscourt.
Add tie-break at end.
DRL-MINI-004.sm: More confident point starts; better understanding of scoring and competitive behaviors.
DRL-MINI-004.sf: Supervise swings; keep it low-threat; emphasize sportsmanship.
DRL-MINI-004.tg: Mini tennis, Serve, Scoring, Fun, Competition

DRL-MINI-004-V1.n: Mini Tennis: Underhand Serve Start Points (Score + Rhythm) — Simplified
DRL-MINI-004-V1.o: Teach point starts, simple scoring, and early decision-making using an underhand serve/toss to keep rallies alive.

DRL-MOVE-002.n: Home Base Tag (Recover-to-Cone Habit for Kids)
DRL-MOVE-002.o: Teach recovery positioning by making 'home base' a game—kids learn to return to the middle after every shot.
DRL-MOVE-002.su:
Place a cone as 'home base' in the middle of each player's side (service box or behind baseline).
Coach feeds or players rally.
DRL-MOVE-002.ex:
1) Player hits a ball.
2) Immediately touch home base cone before next ball.
3) If player forgets to touch, opponent earns 1 bonus point.
4) Play short games to 7; rotate partners.
DRL-MOVE-002.cu:
• 'Hit → recover.'
• 'Small steps; stay balanced.'
• 'Touch the cone fast then get ready.'
DRL-MOVE-002.ef:
• Kids run wildly → cue controlled shuffle, not sprint.
• Too hard to touch cone → move cone closer.
• Lines form → create stations with multiple cones.
DRL-MOVE-002.rg:
No scoring penalty; just reminders.
Use walking speed.
DRL-MOVE-002.pg:
Move cone farther back.
Add direction rules (crosscourt only).
Add reaction feed (coach calls side).
DRL-MOVE-002.sm: Improved recovery habit and footwork efficiency in points and rallies.
DRL-MOVE-002.sf: Ensure cones are flat/low to avoid tripping; manage spacing.
DRL-MOVE-002.tg: Footwork, Recovery, Decision rule, Fun, Athletic

DRL-DBL-022.n: Doubles Return Team Shape (Return Deep + Partner Active)
DRL-DBL-022.o: Improve doubles return games by pairing a deep return target with clear partner responsibilities and first-ball patterns.
DRL-DBL-022.su:
4 players. Mark a deep-middle return target (beyond service line, inside singles).
Returner's partner starts active (split step, ready to poach).
DRL-DBL-022.ex:
1) Start each point with serve.
2) Returner must hit first return to deep-middle target.
3) Return partner must call 'mine/yours' and look for 1 poach opportunity.
4) Play out point.
5) Score only counts if return met target; otherwise replay or point loss.
DRL-DBL-022.cu:
• 'Return deep first; offense later.'
• 'Partner stays active and communicates.'
• 'If you poach, partner covers line.'
DRL-DBL-022.ef:
• Returner tries to go for winner → enforce deep-middle rule.
• Partner ball-watches → coach cues 'split on contact'.
• Too many replays → widen target for success.
DRL-DBL-022.rg:
Allow return anywhere beyond service line.
Partner stays back (no poach requirement).
DRL-DBL-022.pg:
Narrow target.
Add planned pattern after return (e.g., next ball to open court).
Add pressure score: start down 0–2.
DRL-DBL-022.sm: Higher return depth % and better first-ball control; improved doubles communication.
DRL-DBL-022.sf: Standard doubles safety; manage serving volume and shoulder load.
DRL-DBL-022.tg: Doubles, Return, First 4 shots, Communication, Patterns

DRL-DBL-023.o: Train the team skill of approaching the net together (not one-up/one-back) with clear triggers and spacing.
DRL-DBL-023.su:
4 players. Start with both teams 2-back at baseline.
Define approach trigger: any short ball (inside baseline) or weak float.
DRL-DBL-023.ex:
1) Coach feeds ball to start.
2) When a team gets an approach trigger, BOTH players must move forward (2-up).
3) If only one player comes in, point stops and counts as loss.
4) Play out point.
5) Rotate after 10 minutes.
DRL-DBL-023.cu:
• 'Approach together; close together.'
• 'Split as a unit.'
• 'Volley through middle/feet.'
DRL-DBL-023.ef:
• One player hesitates → rehearse verbal cue 'up-up'.
• Players crowd → teach staggered depth (one slightly deeper).
• Approaches too soft → require depth target beyond service line.
DRL-DBL-023.rg:
Allow 1-up/1-back but reward 2-up with bonus point.
Start from service line instead of baseline.
DRL-DBL-023.pg:
Add mandatory first volley to a target.
Add passing/lob bonus for defenders.
Add serve start instead of feed.
DRL-DBL-023.sm: Higher frequency of coordinated net transitions; improved volley decision-making in doubles.
DRL-DBL-023.sf: Collision risk when both move—emphasize communication and lanes.

DRL-DBL-023-V1.n: 2-Up / 2-Back Transition Drill (Win the Net Together) — Simplified
DRL-DBL-023-V1.o: Train the team skill of approaching the net together (not one-up/one-back) with clear triggers and spacing.
DRL-DBL-023-V1.su:
4 players. Start with both teams 2-back at baseline.

DRL-DBL-023-V2.n: 2-Up / 2-Back Transition Drill (Win the Net Together) — Advanced/Competitive
DRL-DBL-023-V2.o: Train the team skill of approaching the net together (not one-up/one-back) with clear triggers and spacing.
DRL-DBL-023-V2.su:

DRL-DBL-023-V2.pg:
Add mandatory first volley to a target.
Add passing/lob bonus for defenders.
Add serve start instead of feed.
DRL-DBL-023-V2.sm: Higher frequency of coordinated net transitions; improved volley decision-making in doubles.
DRL-DBL-023-V2.sf: Collision risk when both move—emphasize communication and lanes.

DRL-DBL-024.o: Improve doubles shot selection by making the middle of the court the primary offensive and defensive target.
DRL-DBL-024.su:
4 players. Mark a 'middle lane' (2–3 feet wide) with cones.
Scoring: if a team wins a point with a ball that lands in middle lane, they earn 2 points.
DRL-DBL-024.ex:
1) Start points with serve or coach feed.
2) Play doubles.
3) Apply scoring bonus for middle-lane winners/forced errors.
4) First to 11 wins.
DRL-DBL-024.cu:
• 'Middle is safe and creates confusion.'
• 'Talk: mine/yours/leave.'
• 'Volley through the middle.'
DRL-DBL-024.ef:
• Players aim corners and miss → reinforce bonus points and big targets.
• Confusion causes collisions → define priority rules.
• Too many short balls → set depth target beyond service line.
DRL-DBL-024.rg:
Make middle lane wider.
Remove bonus scoring; just count middle hits.
DRL-DBL-024.pg:
Make lane narrower.
Add rule: at least 1 middle ball per rally.
Add pressure tie-break at end.
DRL-DBL-024.sm: More intentional targeting; reduced unforced errors; better doubles communication.
DRL-DBL-024.sf: Ensure clear partner communication to avoid collisions.

DRL-DBL-025.o: Train doubles lob defense: calling, switching, executing overheads, and resetting the point after the lob.
DRL-DBL-025.su:
4 players. Coach feeds alternating lobs to one team.
Rule: if lob goes over one player, partners must call 'switch' and exchange sides.
DRL-DBL-025.ex:
1) Coach starts rally with a feed.
2) On a lob: closest player calls 'mine' or 'switch'.
3) Execute overhead or defensive high ball.
4) Recover to correct formation.
5) Play out point; rotate roles every 5 minutes.
DRL-DBL-025.cu:
• 'Early call.'
• 'Turn, point, shuffle back.'
• 'Overhead to big target; then recover.'
DRL-DBL-025.ef:
• Late calls → stop and replay with louder calls.
• Players backpedal → teach drop-step.
• Overheads miss long → aim middle, 70% power.
DRL-DBL-025.rg:
Allow catch-and-throw overhead for reps.
Remove switch rule and focus on overhead technique.
DRL-DBL-025.pg:
Add live lob from opponents (not coach).
Add scoring bonus for clean overhead finish.
Add second lob in same point.
DRL-DBL-025.sm: Fewer breakdowns on lobs; improved overhead execution and partner coordination.
DRL-DBL-025.sf: Overhead and collision risk. Enforce clear communication and spacing.

DRL-DBL-027.n: Return vs Australian/I-Formation Solutions (Angle, Lob, Middle)
DRL-DBL-027.o: Train return options against Australian/I-formation so returners can neutralize and create advantage with a clear decision tree.
DRL-DBL-027.su:
4 players. Serving team uses Australian or I-formation each point.
Return team must choose from 3 planned solutions: angle return, lob return, or hard middle return.
DRL-DBL-027.ex:
1) Start with serve.
2) Returner calls solution before point (angle/lob/middle).
3) Play out point.
4) After 6 points, swap returner.
5) Score solution success rate (did it create neutral/advantage?).
DRL-DBL-027.cu:
• 'Decide early, commit.'
• 'Middle is safe under pressure.'
• 'Lob when net player over-commits.'
DRL-DBL-027.ef:
• Returner changes mind mid-swing → require pre-call.
• Lobs too flat → cue higher net clearance.
• Angle return misses → enlarge target and reduce pace.
DRL-DBL-027.rg:
Serving team uses standard formation.
Returner only uses middle return.
DRL-DBL-027.pg:
Serving team mixes formations randomly.
Smaller targets and added pressure scoring.
Add 2nd ball pattern requirement after return.
DRL-DBL-027.sm: Improved return decision-making vs formations; better return quality and reduced panic errors.
DRL-DBL-027.sf: Serve/return intensity—manage rest. Ensure clear net-player positioning for safety.

DRL-KIDS-001.o: Athletic base (stop-start), listening, and court space awareness
DRL-KIDS-001.su: Scatter 6–10 cones as 'islands'. Coach stands central with whistle/clap.
DRL-KIDS-001.ex:
1) Coach calls an animal (bear crawl, frog jumps, crab walk, cheetah run).
2) On clap/whistle, players sprint to an island and freeze in athletic 'ready' pose (knees bent, hands up).
3) Add tennis layer: each player holds racket; freeze with racket up.
4) Add ball: coach tosses a foam ball; frozen player reacts with 1st step to catch (then reset).
DRL-KIDS-001.cu:
• Quiet feet on freeze
• Athletic posture: knees soft, chest tall
• Eyes up - scan space
DRL-KIDS-001.ef:
• Kids collide -> reduce speed, assign lanes/islands, increase spacing
• Freeze sloppy -> demonstrate 'ready statue' (feet under hips, racket up)
DRL-KIDS-001.rg:
• Walk instead of run
• Fewer islands, larger spacing
• No racket (hands only)
DRL-KIDS-001.pg:
• Color islands: go only to called color
• Add 'split-step' before sprint
• Add 1 touch hit after catch (tap-up)
DRL-KIDS-001.sm: All players freeze within 2 seconds and hold posture 3 seconds; 80% correct reaction step on toss.
DRL-KIDS-001.sf: Use age-appropriate movements; avoid high-speed collisions. Keep jumps low for LTS.
DRL-KIDS-001.tg: Kids, Play-based, ABC, Agility, Balance, Coordination

DRL-KIDS-002.su: Create a start line and 2–3 'finish zones' using cones. Coach holds red/yellow/green cards.
DRL-KIDS-002.ex:
1) GREEN = go (sprint or shuffle as called).
2) YELLOW = slow feet (small steps).
3) RED = stop + split-step freeze.
4) Add tennis: on RED, coach points left/right and players take 1st step that direction.
5) Add ball for Orange: on GREEN, coach hand-feeds a ball; player must move and tap it back to a big target.
DRL-KIDS-002.cu:
• Small steps to stop
• Land soft, knees bent
• First step is explosive, not big
DRL-KIDS-002.ef:
• Sliding stops -> teach 'choppy steps' on YELLOW
• Late reactions -> make cues longer/clearer; reduce speed
DRL-KIDS-002.rg:
• Walk-to-stop only
• Use only GREEN/RED first
• No ball contact
DRL-KIDS-002.pg:
• Add backward shuffle on YELLOW
• Add 'turn and go' (change direction on call)
• Add scoring: 1 point for perfect stop posture
DRL-KIDS-002.sm: 80% correct stop posture; improved reaction speed; for Orange: 6/10 taps to big target after move.
DRL-KIDS-002.sf: Surface check for slipping; keep head up; no diving.
DRL-KIDS-002.tg: Kids, Play-based, ABC, Speed, Deceleration, Coordination

DRL-KIDS-003.o: Agility, scanning, and simple team scoring
DRL-KIDS-003.su: Scatter 20+ cones ('treasure') across half court. Make 2–3 team 'home bases' with hoops.
DRL-KIDS-003.ex:
1) On GO, players run to pick up ONE cone and bring it to their team's home base.
2) Change movement mode each round (shuffle, hop, bear crawl).
3) Tennis layer: must carry racket in non-dominant hand; keep eyes up.
4) Orange add-on: before dropping treasure, player must tap a fed ball into a big target.
DRL-KIDS-003.cu:
• One cone at a time
• Eyes up - avoid collisions
• Team cheers - keep it positive
DRL-KIDS-003.ef:
• Cheating (multiple cones) -> rule: 'one hand, one treasure'
• Chaos -> create lanes and spacing
DRL-KIDS-003.rg: • Reduce cones; walk only; smaller groups
DRL-KIDS-003.pg:
• Time trials (60s rounds)
• Add 'steal' round (tag to steal treasure)
• Add skill requirement (5 tap-ups before collecting)
DRL-KIDS-003.sm: Team collects X cones in 60s; Orange: 5/10 tap-ups before deposit.
DRL-KIDS-003.sf: No sliding; ensure cones are soft; coach controls intensity.

DRL-KIDS-003-V1.o: Agility, scanning, and simple team scoring
DRL-KIDS-003-V1.su:
Scatter 20+ cones ('treasure') across half court. Make 2–3 team 'home bases' with hoops.

Simplified setup notes:
• Reduce cones; walk only; smaller groups
DRL-KIDS-003-V1.ex:
1) On GO, players run to pick up ONE cone and bring it to their team's home base.
2) Change movement mode each round (shuffle, hop, bear crawl).
3) Tennis layer: must carry racket in non-dominant hand; keep eyes up.
4) Orange add-on: before dropping treasure, player must tap a fed ball into a big target.

• Chaos -> create lanes and spacing

• Add 'steal' round (tag to steal treasure)
• Add skill requirement (5 tap-ups before collecting)
DRL-KIDS-003-V1.sm: Simplified target: Team collects X cones in 60s; Orange: 5/10 tap-ups before deposit.
DRL-KIDS-003-V1.sf: No sliding; ensure cones are soft; coach controls intensity.

DRL-KIDS-003-V2.o: Agility, scanning, and simple team scoring
DRL-KIDS-003-V2.su:

Advanced setup notes:

DRL-KIDS-003-V2.ex:

2) Change movement mode each round (shuffle, hop, bear crawl).
3) Tennis layer: must carry racket in non-dominant hand; keep eyes up.
4) Orange add-on: before dropping treasure, player must tap a fed ball into a big target.

Advanced options:
• Time trials (60s rounds)
• Add 'steal' round (tag to steal treasure)
• Add skill requirement (5 tap-ups before collecting)
DRL-KIDS-003-V2.cu:

• Eyes up - avoid collisions
• Team cheers - keep it positive

• Chaos -> create lanes and spacing
DRL-KIDS-003-V2.rg: • Reduce cones; walk only; smaller groups
DRL-KIDS-003-V2.pg:
