• Add skill requirement (5 tap-ups before collecting)
DRL-KIDS-003-V2.sm: Advanced target: Team collects X cones in 60s; Orange: 5/10 tap-ups before deposit.
DRL-KIDS-003-V2.sf: No sliding; ensure cones are soft; coach controls intensity.

DRL-KIDS-004.o: Improve balance, jumping and safe landing
DRL-KIDS-004.su: Lay 2 lines of cones 2–4 feet apart as a 'river'. Create multiple rivers if space.
DRL-KIDS-004.ex:
1) Players jump across the river and stick the landing (freeze 2s).
2) Progress: hop on one foot, lateral jumps, forward/back.
3) Tennis layer: hold racket like 'airplane wings' (arms out) without dropping posture.
4) Add ball for Red: after landing, catch a tossed foam ball.
DRL-KIDS-004.cu:
• Land like a ninja (quiet)
• Knees soft, hips back
• Freeze to own balance
DRL-KIDS-004.ef:
• Hard landings -> reduce distance; demonstrate soft landing
• Wobble -> shorter jumps, two-foot land
DRL-KIDS-004.rg:
• Step across instead of jump
• Hold coach's hand for LTS
DRL-KIDS-004.pg:
• Add reaction: coach points which river
• Add 'split-step' before jump
DRL-KIDS-004.sm: Players hold landing freeze 2s on 8/10 reps without falling; quiet landing.
DRL-KIDS-004.sf: Keep distances small; no max jumping contests.

DRL-KIDS-005.o: Lateral movement and ready position while tracking partner
DRL-KIDS-005.su: Pair players facing each other across a line (service line). Make a 'mirror lane' with cones.
DRL-KIDS-005.ex:
1) Leader moves side to side, forward/back, with split steps.
2) Follower mirrors movement staying in lane.
3) Add racket: both in ready position.
4) Orange/Green add ball: leader tosses underhand to side; follower catches or blocks volley back.
DRL-KIDS-005.cu:
• Stay low and balanced
• Small adjustment steps
• Split-step on change
DRL-KIDS-005.ef:
• Crossing feet too much -> teach shuffle then crossover only when needed
• Eyes down -> cue 'see partner's chest'
DRL-KIDS-005.pg:
• Add reaction call: 'split' then move
• Add ball: volley block on the move
DRL-KIDS-005.sm: Follower stays in lane 80% of time; correct split-step timing on change.

DRL-KIDS-006.o: Develop racket-face control and quick reactions
DRL-KIDS-006.su: Place 4–6 colored cones in a semicircle in front of player. Each player has 1 foam/red ball.
DRL-KIDS-006.ex:
1) Players tap the ball up on racket strings (or bounce + catch for LTS).
2) Coach calls a color; player moves to cone while maintaining taps (or carry ball on strings).
3) Stop at cone, freeze, show 'strings to sky'.
4) Orange: require 5 continuous taps before moving.
DRL-KIDS-006.cu:
• Soft hands - absorb ball
• Eyes on ball
• Strings stay level
DRL-KIDS-006.ef:
• Ball flying -> slow down, smaller taps, softer grip
• Looking away -> cue 'nose over ball'
DRL-KIDS-006.rg:
• Bounce and catch only
• Carry ball on strings (no taps)
• Fewer cones
DRL-KIDS-006.pg:
• Use backhand side taps
• Add alternating forehand/backhand taps
• Time challenge: 3 colors in 30s
DRL-KIDS-006.sm: LTS: carry to 3 colors without dropping; Red/Orange: 10 taps in a row and move to called cone.
DRL-KIDS-006.sf: Keep cones spaced to avoid collisions.
DRL-KIDS-006.tg: Kids, Play-based, Racket Skills, Coordination, Reaction

DRL-KIDS-009.n: Pancake Carry Obstacle Course (Racket Face + Balance)
DRL-KIDS-009.o: Racket-face awareness and balance while moving
DRL-KIDS-009.su: Create simple obstacle lane with cones: zig-zag, step-over line, around cone.
DRL-KIDS-009.ex:
1) Place ball on strings (pancake carry).
2) Walk the obstacle course without dropping.
3) If dropped, stop, reset, continue.
4) Red add-on: at end, gentle tap-up x3 then pancake carry back.
DRL-KIDS-009.cu:
• Strings flat like a table
• Slow is smooth
• Eyes forward occasionally - scan
DRL-KIDS-009.ef:
• Ball rolls -> level strings; slower steps
• Kids stare down -> cue 'peek up every 3 steps'
DRL-KIDS-009.rg:
• Use bigger foam ball
• Shorter lane
DRL-KIDS-009.pg:
• Add backward walk
• Add turn around cone with ball stable
DRL-KIDS-009.sm: Complete course with <=2 drops; Red: 3 tap-ups at finish.
DRL-KIDS-009.sf: Avoid tripping hazards; keep obstacles simple.
DRL-KIDS-009.tg: Kids, Play-based, Racket Skills, Balance, Coordination

DRL-KIDS-011.o: Build rhythm, confidence, and basic ball handling
DRL-KIDS-011.su: Each player has 1 ball. Mark a 'score line' with cones.
DRL-KIDS-011.ex:
1) Level 1: bounce on ground and catch (count to 10).
2) Level 2: bounce on racket strings and catch.
3) Level 3: tap-up on racket.
4) Players advance to next cone each time they complete the count.
DRL-KIDS-011.cu:
• Soft hands
• Eyes on ball
• Celebrate small wins
DRL-KIDS-011.ef:
• Kids frustrated -> reduce count target
• Ball too bouncy -> use foam ball
DRL-KIDS-011.pg:
• Alternate forehand/backhand taps
• Add movement: 2 steps between taps
DRL-KIDS-011.sm: Players reach cone 3+ with confidence; fewer drops over rounds.
DRL-KIDS-011.sf: Space players out to avoid balls crossing.
DRL-KIDS-011.tg: Kids, Play-based, Coordination, Rhythm, Racket Skills

DRL-KIDS-012.o: Teach tracking and controlling ball with racket
DRL-KIDS-012.su: Pairs 6–10 feet apart. Each has a racket; one ball per pair.
DRL-KIDS-012.ex:
1) Player A rolls ball along ground to Player B.
2) Player B traps ball softly with racket strings (no bounce).
3) Player B rolls back.
4) Progress: roll to cones as targets; trap and push to target.
DRL-KIDS-012.cu:
• Soft trap (don't slap)
• Strings open like a net
• Watch ball all the way
DRL-KIDS-012.ef:
• Slapping -> cue 'pillow hands'
• Trapping with edge -> show flat strings
DRL-KIDS-012.rg:
• Use hands to stop ball first
• Shorter distance
DRL-KIDS-012.pg:
• Trap then gentle push to target gate
• Add forehand/backhand side traps
DRL-KIDS-012.sm: 10 clean traps each; hit 3/5 target gates on rolls.
DRL-KIDS-012.sf: Keep distances short; avoid wild swings.
DRL-KIDS-012.tg: Kids, Play-based, Tracking, Coordination, Racket Skills

DRL-KIDS-013.o: Build volley contact in front with short punch motion
DRL-KIDS-013.su: Pairs at service line with coach feeding or partner toss. Use big targets (cones).
DRL-KIDS-013.ex:
1) Catch volley: partner tosses; player catches with non-dominant hand on racket throat position.
2) Block volley: same toss, player blocks ball back with short punch.
3) Add target scoring: 1 point if volley lands in big target zone.
4) Alternate forehand/backhand volley every rep.
DRL-KIDS-013.cu:
• Racket out in front
• Short punch, no swing
• Split-step before volley
DRL-KIDS-013.ef:
• Big swings -> 'punch not swing'
• Ball hits body -> start closer, slower toss
DRL-KIDS-013.pg:
• Add move: shuffle then volley
• Add volley-volley rally to 5
DRL-KIDS-013.sm: 8/10 blocks land inside service boxes; maintain ready position.
DRL-KIDS-013.sf: Keep tosses controlled; no wild feeds near faces.
DRL-KIDS-013.tg: Kids, Play-based, Volleys, Hand-Eye, Net Play

DRL-KIDS-013-V1.o: Build volley contact in front with short punch motion
DRL-KIDS-013-V1.su:
Pairs at service line with coach feeding or partner toss. Use big targets (cones).

Simplified setup notes:
• Catch only
• Allow 1 bounce volley
DRL-KIDS-013-V1.ex:
1) Catch volley: partner tosses; player catches with non-dominant hand on racket throat position.
2) Block volley: same toss, player blocks ball back with short punch.
3) Add target scoring: 1 point if volley lands in big target zone.
4) Alternate forehand/backhand volley every rep.

Simplified options:
• Catch only
• Allow 1 bounce volley
DRL-KIDS-013-V1.cu:
• Racket out in front
• Short punch, no swing
• Split-step before volley
DRL-KIDS-013-V1.ef:
• Big swings -> 'punch not swing'
• Ball hits body -> start closer, slower toss
DRL-KIDS-013-V1.pg:
• Add move: shuffle then volley
• Add volley-volley rally to 5
DRL-KIDS-013-V1.sm: Simplified target: 8/10 blocks land inside service boxes; maintain ready position.
DRL-KIDS-013-V1.sf: Keep tosses controlled; no wild feeds near faces.
DRL-KIDS-013-V1.tg: Kids, Play-based, Volleys, Hand-Eye, Net Play

DRL-KIDS-013-V2.o: Build volley contact in front with short punch motion
DRL-KIDS-013-V2.su:
Pairs at service line with coach feeding or partner toss. Use big targets (cones).

Advanced setup notes:
• Add move: shuffle then volley
• Add volley-volley rally to 5
DRL-KIDS-013-V2.ex:
1) Catch volley: partner tosses; player catches with non-dominant hand on racket throat position.
2) Block volley: same toss, player blocks ball back with short punch.
3) Add target scoring: 1 point if volley lands in big target zone.
4) Alternate forehand/backhand volley every rep.

Advanced options:
• Add move: shuffle then volley
• Add volley-volley rally to 5
DRL-KIDS-013-V2.cu:
• Racket out in front
• Short punch, no swing
• Split-step before volley
DRL-KIDS-013-V2.ef:
• Big swings -> 'punch not swing'
• Ball hits body -> start closer, slower toss
DRL-KIDS-013-V2.pg:
• Add move: shuffle then volley
• Add volley-volley rally to 5
DRL-KIDS-013-V2.sm: Advanced target: 8/10 blocks land inside service boxes; maintain ready position.
DRL-KIDS-013-V2.sf: Keep tosses controlled; no wild feeds near faces.
DRL-KIDS-013-V2.tg: Kids, Play-based, Volleys, Hand-Eye, Net Play

DRL-KIDS-014.su: Set 3–5 cones as 'bowling pins' on service line. Players stand 6–10 feet away.
DRL-KIDS-014.ex:
1) Coach hand-feeds or player self-feeds (bounce then hit).
2) Player hits ball to knock down a cone.
3) Reset cones; count pins.
4) Orange: require 2-shot combo (crosscourt then down-the-line to pin).
DRL-KIDS-014.cu:
• Aim big (middle of cone area)
• Finish balanced
• See the target before swing
DRL-KIDS-014.ef:
• Hard swings -> cue 'smooth to target'
• Missing wildly -> bring player closer; bigger targets
DRL-KIDS-014.rg:
• Roll ball to target instead of hit
• Hit from closer distance
DRL-KIDS-014.pg:
• Smaller targets (flat cones)
• Add time pressure (30s rounds)
DRL-KIDS-014.sm: Knock 3 cones in 10 balls; improved accuracy over rounds.
DRL-KIDS-014.sf: Keep players spaced; retrieve balls on coach signal only.
DRL-KIDS-014.tg: Kids, Play-based, Racket Skills, Accuracy, Confidence

DRL-KIDS-015.o: Teach court lines and stopping control
DRL-KIDS-015.su: Players each with a ball. Identify lines: baseline, service line, sideline.
DRL-KIDS-015.ex:
1) Players dribble/bounce ball while moving.
2) Coach calls a line; players move to touch that line and stop ball dead (trap under hand).
3) Add racket dribble for Orange (tap ball on strings while moving).
4) Add scoring: first 3 to line gets 1 point (no pushing).
DRL-KIDS-015.cu:
• Control before speed
• Stop ball like a brake
• Eyes up to find lines
DRL-KIDS-015.ef:
• Kids race dangerously -> remove racing; make it cooperative
• Losing control -> slower dribble
DRL-KIDS-015.pg:
• Call two lines sequence
• Add 'split-step' at line
DRL-KIDS-015.sm: Players identify correct lines; control ball on stop 8/10 reps.
DRL-KIDS-015.sf: No diving; keep heads up; avoid collisions.
DRL-KIDS-015.tg: Kids, Play-based, Ball Control, Court Awareness, Coordination

DRL-KIDS-016.o: Teach early preparation and contact in front
DRL-KIDS-016.su: Pairs on mini court. Use underhand feeds or partner toss.
DRL-KIDS-016.ex:
1) Player self-feeds (bounce) and hits gentle forehand to partner.
2) Partner lets ball bounce, hits back, then immediately catches next ball with off-hand at contact height (simulation).
3) Rotate roles.
4) Orange: require alternating forehand/backhand after coach call.
DRL-KIDS-016.cu:
• Ready early (unit turn)
• Contact in front
• Smooth swing to big target
DRL-KIDS-016.ef:
• Late prep -> cue 'turn on bounce'
• Overhitting -> use slower ball, smaller backswing
DRL-KIDS-016.rg:
• Catch at contact without hitting first
• Shorter distance
DRL-KIDS-016.pg:
• Add movement: start at cone, move to ball
• Add target zones for depth
DRL-KIDS-016.sm: Players demonstrate contact in front; achieve 6-ball cooperative mini rally with smooth tempo.
DRL-KIDS-016.sf: Use soft balls; keep swing sizes appropriate.

DRL-KIDS-017.o: Improve rally control and aiming through big targets
DRL-KIDS-017.su: Set 2–4 'gates' (two cones) on opposite side. Use mini court first.
DRL-KIDS-017.ex:
1) Players rally cooperatively aiming to send the ball through a gate.
2) Score 1 point each time ball goes through any gate.
3) After 5 points, shrink gate width or move gate deeper.
4) Orange/Green: require crosscourt gate only for 3 points then free.
DRL-KIDS-017.cu:
• High net clearance
• Aim through the gate, not at the cone
• Recover to home base
DRL-KIDS-017.ef:
• Too flat -> cue 'arc over net'
• Rushing -> slow tempo; cooperative first
DRL-KIDS-017.rg:
• Allow 2 bounces per side
• Shorter distance
DRL-KIDS-017.pg:
• One-bounce only
• Add 'change direction' gate after 4 balls
DRL-KIDS-017.sm: Cooperative gate score 10+ in 2 minutes; rally length increases.
DRL-KIDS-017.sf: Ensure spacing between courts; retrieve balls safely.
DRL-KIDS-017.tg: Kids, Play-based, Rally, Direction, Big Targets

DRL-KIDS-018.o: Create motivation for directional control under fun pressure
DRL-KIDS-018.su: Build 'castles' with 3–5 cones per team on baseline of mini court.
DRL-KIDS-018.ex:
1) Teams rally/hand-feed to try to knock over opponent castle cones.
2) Any hit that lands in court counts; if it hits castle, remove 1 cone.
3) Reset and swap sides.
4) Orange: only forehand to knock, backhand to defend (constraint).
DRL-KIDS-018.cu:
• Aim big to middle first
• Use control over power
• Cheer teammates
DRL-KIDS-018.ef:
• Kids blast ball -> lower ball pressure; emphasize 'smooth'
• Chaos -> assign positions and rotations
DRL-KIDS-018.rg:
• Hand-roll balls to hit castle
• Use larger targets (flat cones)
DRL-KIDS-018.pg:
• Smaller castles
• Add 'must rally 3 balls before attack'
DRL-KIDS-018.sm: Team knocks 3 cones in 5 minutes; improved accuracy and engagement.
DRL-KIDS-018.sf: Keep players behind safety line when not hitting; coach controls feeding.

DRL-KIDS-018-V1.o: Create motivation for directional control under fun pressure
DRL-KIDS-018-V1.su:
Build 'castles' with 3–5 cones per team on baseline of mini court.

Simplified setup notes:
• Hand-roll balls to hit castle
• Use larger targets (flat cones)
DRL-KIDS-018-V1.ex:
1) Teams rally/hand-feed to try to knock over opponent castle cones.
2) Any hit that lands in court counts; if it hits castle, remove 1 cone.
3) Reset and swap sides.
4) Orange: only forehand to knock, backhand to defend (constraint).

Simplified options:
• Hand-roll balls to hit castle
• Use larger targets (flat cones)
DRL-KIDS-018-V1.cu:
• Aim big to middle first
• Use control over power
• Cheer teammates
DRL-KIDS-018-V1.ef:
• Kids blast ball -> lower ball pressure; emphasize 'smooth'
• Chaos -> assign positions and rotations
DRL-KIDS-018-V1.rg:
• Hand-roll balls to hit castle
• Use larger targets (flat cones)
DRL-KIDS-018-V1.pg:
• Smaller castles
• Add 'must rally 3 balls before attack'
DRL-KIDS-018-V1.sm: Simplified target: Team knocks 3 cones in 5 minutes; improved accuracy and engagement.
DRL-KIDS-018-V1.sf: Keep players behind safety line when not hitting; coach controls feeding.

DRL-KIDS-018-V2.o: Create motivation for directional control under fun pressure
DRL-KIDS-018-V2.su:
Build 'castles' with 3–5 cones per team on baseline of mini court.

Advanced setup notes:
• Smaller castles
• Add 'must rally 3 balls before attack'
DRL-KIDS-018-V2.ex:
1) Teams rally/hand-feed to try to knock over opponent castle cones.
2) Any hit that lands in court counts; if it hits castle, remove 1 cone.
3) Reset and swap sides.
4) Orange: only forehand to knock, backhand to defend (constraint).

Advanced options:
• Smaller castles
• Add 'must rally 3 balls before attack'
DRL-KIDS-018-V2.cu:
• Aim big to middle first
• Use control over power
• Cheer teammates
DRL-KIDS-018-V2.ef:
• Kids blast ball -> lower ball pressure; emphasize 'smooth'
• Chaos -> assign positions and rotations
DRL-KIDS-018-V2.rg:
• Hand-roll balls to hit castle
• Use larger targets (flat cones)
DRL-KIDS-018-V2.pg:
• Smaller castles
• Add 'must rally 3 balls before attack'
DRL-KIDS-018-V2.sm: Advanced target: Team knocks 3 cones in 5 minutes; improved accuracy and engagement.
DRL-KIDS-018-V2.sf: Keep players behind safety line when not hitting; coach controls feeding.

DRL-KIDS-019.o: Develop consistent toss and overhand pattern
DRL-KIDS-019.su: Place hula hoop or cone 'landing zones' in front of player (1–2 feet into court).
DRL-KIDS-019.ex:
1) Players practice toss so ball lands in landing zone.
2) Add overhand throw without racket (throw to target wall/coach).
3) Add racket: shadow serve with trophy pose.
4) Orange/Green: hit serves to big target after 5 good tosses.
DRL-KIDS-019.cu:
• Toss in front, not behind
• Reach up tall
• Same release point
DRL-KIDS-019.ef:
• Toss too high -> lower target; cue 'just above reach'
• Side toss -> align shoulder to target
DRL-KIDS-019.rg:
• Toss and catch only
• Bigger target (hoop)
DRL-KIDS-019.pg:
• Add score: 10 tosses in hoop
• Add serve to target after toss
DRL-KIDS-019.sm: 8/10 tosses land in zone; Orange/Green: 6/10 serves start point safely.
DRL-KIDS-019.sf: No chasing balls across courts; toss in controlled lane.

DRL-KIDS-020.n: Underhand Serve Start + Rally (Start Points Fast)
DRL-KIDS-020.o: Teach point start, tracking, and simple scoring
DRL-KIDS-020.su: Use mini court. Mark big target zones (middle) with cones.
DRL-KIDS-020.ex:
1) Point starts with underhand serve from service line.
2) Players must rally 2 balls cooperatively before point is live.
3) Then play out to 1 point.
4) Rotate servers every point; first to 7.
DRL-KIDS-020.cu:
• Big target serve
• Keep ball in play first
• Say score out loud
DRL-KIDS-020.ef:
• Serve misses -> move closer; allow drop hit
• Arguing score -> coach models 'call it together'
DRL-KIDS-020.rg:
• Allow bounce-catch then throw serve
• Play cooperative only
DRL-KIDS-020.pg:
• Make rally requirement 3 balls
• Add direction constraint (crosscourt)
DRL-KIDS-020.sm: Players start points without delay; 70%+ of serves start rally; can keep score.
DRL-KIDS-020.sf: Keep distances appropriate; ensure safe swing sizes.

DRL-KIDS-020-V1.n: Underhand Serve Start + Rally (Start Points Fast) (Simplified)
DRL-KIDS-020-V1.o: Teach point start, tracking, and simple scoring
DRL-KIDS-020-V1.su:
Use mini court. Mark big target zones (middle) with cones.

Simplified setup notes:
• Allow bounce-catch then throw serve
• Play cooperative only
DRL-KIDS-020-V1.ex:
1) Point starts with underhand serve from service line.
2) Players must rally 2 balls cooperatively before point is live.
3) Then play out to 1 point.
4) Rotate servers every point; first to 7.

Simplified options:
• Allow bounce-catch then throw serve
• Play cooperative only
DRL-KIDS-020-V1.cu:
• Big target serve
• Keep ball in play first
• Say score out loud
DRL-KIDS-020-V1.ef:
• Serve misses -> move closer; allow drop hit
• Arguing score -> coach models 'call it together'
DRL-KIDS-020-V1.rg:
• Allow bounce-catch then throw serve
• Play cooperative only
DRL-KIDS-020-V1.pg:
• Make rally requirement 3 balls
• Add direction constraint (crosscourt)
DRL-KIDS-020-V1.sm: Simplified target: Players start points without delay; 70%+ of serves start rally; can keep score.
DRL-KIDS-020-V1.sf: Keep distances appropriate; ensure safe swing sizes.

DRL-KIDS-020-V2.n: Underhand Serve Start + Rally (Start Points Fast) (Advanced)
DRL-KIDS-020-V2.o: Teach point start, tracking, and simple scoring
DRL-KIDS-020-V2.su:
Use mini court. Mark big target zones (middle) with cones.

Advanced setup notes:
• Make rally requirement 3 balls
• Add direction constraint (crosscourt)
DRL-KIDS-020-V2.ex:
1) Point starts with underhand serve from service line.
2) Players must rally 2 balls cooperatively before point is live.
3) Then play out to 1 point.
4) Rotate servers every point; first to 7.

Advanced options:
• Make rally requirement 3 balls
• Add direction constraint (crosscourt)
DRL-KIDS-020-V2.cu:
• Big target serve
• Keep ball in play first
• Say score out loud
DRL-KIDS-020-V2.ef:
• Serve misses -> move closer; allow drop hit
• Arguing score -> coach models 'call it together'
DRL-KIDS-020-V2.rg:
• Allow bounce-catch then throw serve
• Play cooperative only
DRL-KIDS-020-V2.pg:
• Make rally requirement 3 balls
• Add direction constraint (crosscourt)
DRL-KIDS-020-V2.sm: Advanced target: Players start points without delay; 70%+ of serves start rally; can keep score.
DRL-KIDS-020-V2.sf: Keep distances appropriate; ensure safe swing sizes.

DRL-KIDS-021.o: Build cooperative rally skill and enjoyment
DRL-KIDS-021.su: Pairs on mini court. Use service boxes. Set a big 'middle target' with cones.
DRL-KIDS-021.ex:
1) Players rally cooperatively aiming over the net into big target.
2) Count aloud together to 10.
3) If they reach 10, they earn a 'power-up' (choose next rule).
4) Rotate partners every 2–3 minutes.
DRL-KIDS-021.cu:
• High arc for safety
• Slow tempo first
• Celebrate every improvement
DRL-KIDS-021.ef:
• Overhitting -> cue '50% power'
• Too many misses -> allow 2 bounces
DRL-KIDS-021.rg:
• Catch and toss over net (LTS)
• Allow 2 bounces per side
DRL-KIDS-021.pg:
• Shrink target
• Add 'must hit crosscourt only'
• Add scoring: first pair to 3 power-ups
DRL-KIDS-021.sm: Pairs achieve 10-ball rally at least once; average rally length increases over rounds.
DRL-KIDS-021.sf: Keep lines short; rotate quickly to maintain engagement.

DRL-KIDS-022.o: Teach 'safe crosscourt' as default decision rule
DRL-KIDS-022.su: Set up a crosscourt lane using cones (deuce-to-deuce).
DRL-KIDS-022.ex:
1) Rally crosscourt only for 6 balls cooperatively.
2) After 6, point becomes live (can change direction) OR stay crosscourt and score bonus.
3) Score: 1 point for winning point; +1 bonus if you win without leaving the lane.
4) Switch to ad side after 3 minutes.
DRL-KIDS-022.cu:
• Aim high and crosscourt
• Recover to middle of lane
• Patience first
DRL-KIDS-022.ef:
• Players go down-the-line early -> remind of bonus
• Lane unclear -> widen with more cones
DRL-KIDS-022.pg:
• Reduce bounces to 1
• Require 8 crosscourt balls before live
DRL-KIDS-022.sm: Players keep 70% of rally balls inside lane; improved rally tolerance.
DRL-KIDS-022.sf: Keep cones flat to prevent trips.
DRL-KIDS-022.ct: Mini court / half court crosscourt lanes

DRL-KIDS-023.n: DTL Gate Trigger (Change of Direction Timing)
DRL-KIDS-023.o: Teach when to change direction (only on a short ball)
DRL-KIDS-023.su: Set crosscourt rally lane. Place a 'short ball line' with cones inside baseline.
DRL-KIDS-023.ex:
1) Players must rally crosscourt by default.
2) If a ball lands short (inside the short line), the hitter is allowed to go down-the-line through a gate.
3) If they go DTL on a deep ball, they lose the point.
4) Play points to 7 then rotate partners.
DRL-KIDS-023.cu:
• Crosscourt first
• Change direction only when ball is short
• Step in - hit through gate
DRL-KIDS-023.ef:
• Forcing DTL early -> enforce rule strictly
• No short recognition -> mark short line clearly
DRL-KIDS-023.rg:
• Coach calls 'short' when it happens
• Allow DTL any time for 1 round
DRL-KIDS-023.pg:
• Require DTL to specific target
• Add approach + volley after DTL
DRL-KIDS-023.sm: Correct decisions on DTL triggers 70%+; fewer errors when changing direction.
DRL-KIDS-023.sf: Monitor intensity and spacing; keep ball speeds appropriate.
DRL-KIDS-023.tg: Kids, Play-based, Direction Change, Decision-making, Patterns

DRL-KIDS-024.o: Encourage rally success and tracking by allowing 2 bounces
DRL-KIDS-024.su: Pairs on mini court. Mark big landing zone with cones.
DRL-KIDS-024.ex:
1) Each side is allowed up to 2 bounces before hitting.
2) Goal is to complete a 12-hit cooperative rally.
3) Optional: after 12, play 1 point with 1 bounce only.
4) Rotate partners frequently.
DRL-KIDS-024.cu:
• Move to ball early
• Big target middle
• Smooth swing
DRL-KIDS-024.ef:
• Kids wait too long -> cue 'move on first bounce'
• Confusion -> coach counts bounces aloud
DRL-KIDS-024.rg:
• Catch and toss after second bounce (LTS)
• Shorter target goal (6 hits)
DRL-KIDS-024.pg:
• Reduce to 1 bounce
• Add direction constraint (crosscourt)
DRL-KIDS-024.sm: Rally length increases; players successfully move and hit after second bounce.
DRL-KIDS-024.sf: Keep spacing; avoid running into net.

DRL-KIDS-026.n: Short Ball = Approach Tag (Transition Skill Game)
DRL-KIDS-026.o: Teach recognition of short ball and moving forward
DRL-KIDS-026.su: Coach or partner feeds alternating deep and short balls. Place a cone as 'net target'.
DRL-KIDS-026.ex:
1) Players rally crosscourt (or cooperative) until coach feeds a short ball.
2) On short ball, hitter must approach to net and try to tag cone with racket after hitting.
3) Point plays out; bonus point if player reaches net position before opponent hits.
4) Rotate roles every 5 feeds.
DRL-KIDS-026.cu:
• Recognize short early
• Move forward - don't back up
• Split-step at service line
DRL-KIDS-026.ef:
• Hesitation -> cue 'go on bounce'
• Approach too hard -> 'smooth deep then go'
DRL-KIDS-026.rg:
• Allow approach from inside service line only
• Coach calls 'short'
DRL-KIDS-026.pg:
• Require volley finish
• Add target for approach direction
DRL-KIDS-026.sm: Players move forward on short ball 70%+; improved transition timing.
DRL-KIDS-026.sf: Net safety: no running into net; teach controlled stopping.
DRL-KIDS-026.tg: Kids, Play-based, Transition, Approach, Net Play

DRL-KIDS-028.o: Develop soft hands and 'block' skills near net
DRL-KIDS-028.su: Pairs at service line. Use underhand feeds that bounce once before player.
DRL-KIDS-028.ex:
1) Ball bounces; player blocks it back like a volley (short punch).
2) Keep rally cooperative to 8.
3) Alternate forehand/backhand.
4) Add movement: shuffle one step then block.
DRL-KIDS-028.cu:
• Short punch
• Racket out front
• Soft hands - absorb
DRL-KIDS-028.ef:
• Swinging -> remind 'block'
• Ball too fast -> slow feed, closer distance
DRL-KIDS-028.rg:
• Catch after bounce instead
• Allow 2 bounces
DRL-KIDS-028.pg:
• Add target zones
• Add competition: first to 3 rallies wins
DRL-KIDS-028.sm: Achieve 8-ball cooperative block rally; improved volley control.
DRL-KIDS-028.sf: Keep feeds controlled; protect faces; no hard hits near net.
DRL-KIDS-028.tg: Kids, Play-based, Net Play, Touch, Coordination

DRL-KIDS-030.o: Teach return position, split-step, and first step
DRL-KIDS-030.su: Coach stands at net or service line and simulates serve with toss/throw.
DRL-KIDS-030.ex:
1) Returner starts in ready position at appropriate depth.
2) On coach 'serve motion', returner split-steps.
3) Coach tosses ball left/right; returner catches at contact height.
4) Orange/Green: returner blocks ball back to big target instead of catch.
DRL-KIDS-030.cu:
• Split-step as ball leaves hand
• Small first step
• Racket up
DRL-KIDS-030.ef:
• Early split -> cue 'split on toss release'
• Flat feet -> light bounce pre-serve
DRL-KIDS-030.pg:
• Add target zones for return
• Add serve speed variability
DRL-KIDS-030.sm: Returners split correctly on 8/10 reps; Orange/Green: 6/10 blocks beyond service line.
DRL-KIDS-030.sf: Keep tosses controlled; avoid hard feeds.

DRL-KIDS-031.o: Teach recovery to home base between hits
DRL-KIDS-031.su: Place a 'home base' marker (flat cone) behind service line for each player.
DRL-KIDS-031.ex:
1) Players rally cooperatively.
2) After each hit, player must touch home base with racket tip before next ball.
3) If they miss home base, point resets.
4) Progress: remove touch and require recover within 1 step of home base.
DRL-KIDS-031.cu:
• Hit then recover
• Small steps to arrive balanced
• Eyes on ball while recovering
DRL-KIDS-031.ef:
• Too slow -> shorten distance; move home base closer
• Confusing -> demonstrate once
DRL-KIDS-031.rg:
• Walk recover
• No racket touch required
DRL-KIDS-031.pg:
• Add direction constraint (crosscourt)
• Add scoring race to 7
DRL-KIDS-031.sm: Players consistently recover; fewer 'stuck' positions; improved movement efficiency.
DRL-KIDS-031.sf: Keep touch markers flat; no tripping.

DRL-KIDS-032.o: Smooth transition from cooperative rally to live points
DRL-KIDS-032.su: Pairs on scaled court. Use big targets.
DRL-KIDS-032.ex:
1) Players must complete 3 cooperative hits (count 1-2-3).
2) On 4th ball, point becomes live.
3) Winner gets 1 point; first to 7.
4) Rotate partners after 7 or 10 minutes.
DRL-KIDS-032.cu:
• Cooperative first - build rhythm
• Then compete with control
• Reset quickly after errors
DRL-KIDS-032.ef:
• Kids rush competition -> remind cooperative requirement
• Score confusion -> keep it simple
DRL-KIDS-032.rg:
• Require only 1 cooperative hit
• Allow 2 bounces
DRL-KIDS-032.pg:
• Require 5 cooperative hits
• Add direction constraint on live ball
DRL-KIDS-032.sm: Most points begin with cooperative 3-ball; rally length increases; kids can keep score.
DRL-KIDS-032.sf: Coach keeps tempo; prevent long waits.

DRL-KIDS-032-V1.o: Smooth transition from cooperative rally to live points
DRL-KIDS-032-V1.su:
Pairs on scaled court. Use big targets.

Simplified setup notes:
• Require only 1 cooperative hit
• Allow 2 bounces
DRL-KIDS-032-V1.ex:
1) Players must complete 3 cooperative hits (count 1-2-3).
2) On 4th ball, point becomes live.
3) Winner gets 1 point; first to 7.

DRL-KIDS-033.n: Build the House (Earn Cones with Rally Points)
DRL-KIDS-033.o: Motivate rallying and teamwork with visual progress
DRL-KIDS-033.su: Each team has a 'house base' with 10 cones. Cones are stacked to build a tower.
DRL-KIDS-033.ex:
1) Teams play cooperative rallies (or target hits) to earn cones.
2) Example: 5-ball cooperative rally = earn 1 cone.
3) First team to build tower of 10 wins.
4) Switch roles/partners to keep everyone involved.
DRL-KIDS-033.cu:
• Focus on keeping ball in play
• Cheer teammates
• Use big targets
DRL-KIDS-033.ef:
• Dominant player takes over -> require alternating hits
• Tower falls -> wider base, calmer stacking
DRL-KIDS-033.rg:
• Lower rally requirement (3 balls)
• Use hand toss + hit
DRL-KIDS-033.pg:
• Increase rally requirement (7 balls)
• Add direction target requirement
DRL-KIDS-033.sm: Team completes multiple cooperative rallies; visible progress keeps engagement high.
DRL-KIDS-033.sf: Keep stacks away from active hitting lanes.

DRL-KIDS-034.o: Introduce competitive points with fast rotations
DRL-KIDS-034.su: One mini court per 4 players. Two play, two wait behind baseline cones.
DRL-KIDS-034.ex:
1) Point starts with underhand serve.
2) Winner stays as 'King' for up to 3 points.
3) Challenger rotates in after each point.
4) Track total points won by each player over 10 minutes.
DRL-KIDS-034.cu:
• Start points fast
• Big targets
• Respect rotation rules
DRL-KIDS-034.ef:
• Waiting too long -> use multiple courts or shorter points
• Arguments -> coach is scorekeeper
DRL-KIDS-034.rg:
• Cooperative rally instead of points
• Allow 2 bounces
DRL-KIDS-034.pg:
• Add constraint: crosscourt first
• Add bonus for net finish
DRL-KIDS-034.sm: High reps per player; clear rotations; points won tracked accurately.
DRL-KIDS-034.sf: Ensure waiting players stay behind safety line.
DRL-KIDS-034.tg: Kids, Play-based, Competition, Live Points, Rotation

DRL-KIDS-034-V1.o: Introduce competitive points with fast rotations

One mini court per 4 players. Two play, two wait behind baseline cones.

DRL-KIDS-036.o: Build confidence through achievable challenge vs coach
DRL-KIDS-036.su: Coach plays from inside service boxes; kids play from baseline or closer based on level.
DRL-KIDS-036.ex:
1) Coach starts each point with gentle feed.
2) Kids earn a point if they complete rally of 4 balls OR win the point.
3) Coach earns a point only by hitting a clear winner to a target (not errors).
4) First to 11 (kids vs coach).
DRL-KIDS-036.cu:
• Big targets
• Work together
• Cheer for effort
DRL-KIDS-036.ef:
• Coach too strong -> reduce pace, make coach target smaller
• Kids discouraged -> make rally goal smaller
DRL-KIDS-036.rg:
• Kids need only 2-ball rally for point
• Coach must hit to big target only
DRL-KIDS-036.pg:
• Kids must win point to score
• Add direction constraints
DRL-KIDS-036.sm: Kids team wins/keeps close; high smiles; rally length improves.
DRL-KIDS-036.sf: Coach controls pace; keep it supportive.

DRL-KIDS-036-V1.o: Build confidence through achievable challenge vs coach
DRL-KIDS-036-V1.su:
Coach plays from inside service boxes; kids play from baseline or closer based on level.

Simplified setup notes:

DRL-KIDS-037.o: Reward good decisions and bravery, not just outcomes
DRL-KIDS-037.su: Coach defines 1 'focus decision' (e.g., attack short ball) and 1 'safe default' (crosscourt).
DRL-KIDS-037.ex:
1) Play points to 11.
2) Normal point = 1.
3) Courage point = +1 when player attempts correct decision (even if misses) with good margin.
4) Coach announces courage points immediately.
DRL-KIDS-037.cu:
• Right decision over perfect shot
• Big targets
• Reset fast
DRL-KIDS-037.ef:
• Players force shots -> define 'correct trigger' clearly
• Arguing courage -> coach owns call
DRL-KIDS-037.rg:
• Only award courage for effort (no decision rule)
• Lower score target to 7
DRL-KIDS-037.pg:
• Require decision to happen within first 4 shots
• Add 2 patterns (Plan A/B)
DRL-KIDS-037.sm: Players attempt correct decisions more often; confidence improves; reduced fear of errors.
DRL-KIDS-037.sf: Keep feedback positive; avoid shaming errors.

DRL-KIDS-039.n: Poison Cones (Avoid the Zone) Control Game
DRL-KIDS-039.o: Improve aiming by avoiding a 'no-hit' zone
DRL-KIDS-039.su: Place 2–3 flat cones in center as 'poison'.
DRL-KIDS-039.ex:
1) Players rally cooperatively.
2) Any ball landing on/inside poison zone = point for the other side (or reset).
3) Goal: rally while aiming away from poison.
4) Rotate poison location each round.
DRL-KIDS-039.cu:
• Aim away from poison
• Use smooth swing
• High net clearance
DRL-KIDS-039.ef:
• Kids hit poison often -> make poison smaller or move it
• Confusion -> demonstrate scoring
DRL-KIDS-039.rg:
• Remove scoring; just cooperative
• Make poison very small
DRL-KIDS-039.pg:
• Make poison bigger
• Add two poison zones
DRL-KIDS-039.sm: Players improve directional control; fewer balls land in center poison over time.
DRL-KIDS-039.sf: Keep cones flat; watch foot placement.

DRL-KIDS-040.n: Spin Wizard (Brush Up vs Brush Down Feel)
DRL-KIDS-040.o: Introduce topspin/slice feel with simple tasks
DRL-KIDS-040.su: Use low-compression balls. Place a 'spin target' on opposite service box.
DRL-KIDS-040.ex:
1) Topspin round: players try to make ball bounce high over a cone gate (brush up).
2) Slice round: players try to make ball stay low under a rope/tape line (brush down).
3) Score 1 point per successful 'high bounce' or 'low skid' in target zone.
4) Rotate tasks every 2 minutes.
DRL-KIDS-040.cu:
• Topspin: brush up
• Slice: carve under
• Finish balanced
DRL-KIDS-040.ef:
• Over-coaching -> keep it as 'feel' not mechanics
• Ball too fast -> slower swings
DRL-KIDS-040.rg:
• Hand toss + catch to understand bounce
• Use foam/red for feel
DRL-KIDS-040.pg:
• Add movement before hit
• Add live point: must use called spin
DRL-KIDS-040.sm: Players demonstrate different trajectories; can self-report 'high' vs 'low' bounce outcomes.
DRL-KIDS-040.sf: Keep swings controlled; emphasize feel over power.
DRL-KIDS-040.tg: Kids, Ball Controls, Spin, Technique Feel, Play-based

DRL-ADULT-005.n: Adult Tournament Pattern Points (Plan A / Plan B)
DRL-ADULT-005.o: Give adult competitors two reliable patterns and practice switching under pressure (like match play).
DRL-ADULT-005.su: Write Plan A and Plan B on whiteboard. Example: A = heavy cross to BH; B = attack short ball to open court.
DRL-ADULT-005.ex:
1) Play points to 11.
2) Odd points: player must attempt Plan A.
2) Even points: player must attempt Plan B.
2) Coach tags pattern attempts and UFEs; quick debrief every 5 points.
DRL-ADULT-005.cu:
• One plan per point.
• Big targets.
• Commit to pattern before serve/return.
• Reset quickly.
DRL-ADULT-005.ef:
• Overcomplication → simplify to 2-shot patterns.
• Forgetting plan → verbalize before point.
• Too aggressive → enforce margin targets.
DRL-ADULT-005.rg:
• Only Plan A for full game.
• Stop point after 4 shots and replay if neutral.
DRL-ADULT-005.pg:
• Add opponent style constraint (pusher/big hitter).
• Start games down 0-3 for pressure.
DRL-ADULT-005.sf:
• Adults: enforce warm-up, shoulder prep, and hydration.
• Scale intensity and movement for mixed ability levels.

DRL-ADULT-006.n: USTA Doubles Warmup to Match Start (6-Point Script)
DRL-ADULT-006.o: Standardize the first 6 points of a doubles match to reduce early chaos and settle teams.
DRL-ADULT-006.su: 4 players. Define a 6-point script: 2 points crosscourt start, 2 points serve+1, 2 points full.
DRL-ADULT-006.ex:
1) Point 1–2: start with coach feed, crosscourt only then live.
2) Point 3–4: serve-start, server must hit +1 through middle.
2) Point 5–6: full doubles point with one goal (return deep).
2) Repeat script twice with partner change.
DRL-ADULT-006.cu:
• Start calm; find rhythm.
• Talk early (mine/yours).
• Middle ball is safe.
• Reset between points.
DRL-ADULT-006.ef:
• Rushing to compete → enforce scripted sequence.
• Overhitting → 70% pace; big targets.
• Confusion on roles → assign clear positions.
DRL-ADULT-006.rg:
• No serving; coach feeds all points.
• Only 4-point script.
DRL-ADULT-006.pg:
• Add poach signals on points 5–6.
• Add no-ad point at end.
DRL-ADULT-006.sf:
• Adults: enforce warm-up, shoulder prep, and hydration.
• Scale intensity and movement for mixed ability levels.

DRL-ADULT-006-V1.n: USTA Doubles Warmup to Match Start (6-Point Script) — Simplified
DRL-ADULT-006-V1.o: Standardize the first 6 points of a doubles match to reduce early chaos and settle teams.
DRL-ADULT-006-V1.su: 4 players. Define a 6-point script: 2 points crosscourt start, 2 points serve+1, 2 points full.
DRL-ADULT-006-V1.ex:
1) Point 1–2: start with coach feed, crosscourt only then live.
2) Point 3–4: serve-start, server must hit +1 through middle.
2) Point 5–6: full doubles point with one goal (return deep).
2) Repeat script twice with partner change.
DRL-ADULT-006-V1.cu:
• Start calm; find rhythm.
• Talk early (mine/yours).
• Middle ball is safe.
• Reset between points.
DRL-ADULT-006-V1.ef:
• Rushing to compete → enforce scripted sequence.
• Overhitting → 70% pace; big targets.
• Confusion on roles → assign clear positions.
DRL-ADULT-006-V1.rg:
• No serving; coach feeds all points.
• Only 4-point script.
DRL-ADULT-006-V1.pg:
• Add poach signals on points 5–6.
• Add no-ad point at end.
DRL-ADULT-006-V1.sf:
• Adults: enforce warm-up, shoulder prep, and hydration.
• Scale intensity and movement for mixed ability levels.

DRL-ADULT-006-V2.o: Standardize the first 6 points of a doubles match to reduce early chaos and settle teams.
DRL-ADULT-006-V2.su: 4 players. Define a 6-point script: 2 points crosscourt start, 2 points serve+1, 2 points full.

DRL-ADULT-007.o: Give adults a workout while raising rally tolerance: cooperate first, then compete.
DRL-ADULT-007.su: Pairs on full court. Timer. Mark a deep zone beyond service line.
DRL-ADULT-007.ex:
1) Each point begins cooperatively: must complete 6 balls in play landing beyond service line.
2) After 6, point becomes live.
2) If an error occurs before 6, restart count (no point).
2) Play to 11 then rotate opponents.
DRL-ADULT-007.cu:
• Start smooth; build rhythm.
• Big targets beyond service line.
• After count, compete with intention.
• Reset quickly.
DRL-ADULT-007.ef:
• Count arguing → one player counts aloud; coach final.
• Too many restarts → reduce to 4-ball count.
• Players go too hard after count → keep margin.
DRL-ADULT-007.pg:
• Increase to 8-ball count.
• Add constraint: one ball must be crosscourt, one to middle.
DRL-ADULT-007.sm:
• Rally success rate.
• UFE per 10 after live phase.
DRL-ADULT-007.sf:
• Adults: enforce warm-up, shoulder prep, and hydration.
• Scale intensity and movement for mixed ability levels.

DRL-ADULT-008.o: Reduce doubles faults and build confidence by improving second-serve shape and routine.
DRL-ADULT-008.su: Each player has 15 balls. Mark one big second-serve target per box.
DRL-ADULT-008.ex:
1) Player hits 10 second serves to big target.
2) If 7/10 land in, move target smaller; if <7, keep target big and add spin cue.
2) Then play 10 serve-start points where only second serve is allowed.
DRL-ADULT-008.cu:
• Same routine every time.
• Spin and margin.
• Aim to big target.
• Commit—no pushing.
DRL-ADULT-008.ef:
• Pushing serve → cue 'swing with spin'.
• Toss inconsistent → toss-and-catch reps.
• Fear of double fault → shorten routine and trust spin.
DRL-ADULT-008.rg:
• Underhand serve start for beginners.
• Serve from closer line.
DRL-ADULT-008.pg:
• Add pressure: 3-in-a-row to finish.
• Add serve+1 pattern.
DRL-ADULT-008.sm:
• Second serve in %.
• Double faults per set.
DRL-ADULT-008.sf:
• Adults: enforce warm-up, shoulder prep, and hydration.
• Scale intensity and movement for mixed ability levels.

DRL-ADULT-009.o: Make return depth measurable and game-like—so adult players stop giving away easy first balls.
DRL-ADULT-009.su: Server serves at 60–70%. Mark deep zone beyond service line and deep-middle bonus zone.
DRL-ADULT-009.ex:
1) Play points.
2) Returner scores +1 if return lands deep zone; +2 if deep-middle bonus; -1 if short.
2) Switch returners every 8 returns; play to +10.
DRL-ADULT-009.cu:
• Split-step timing.
• Aim deep middle.
• Height buys depth.
• Recover after return.
DRL-ADULT-009.ef:
• Returner tries winner → reframe scoring (depth wins).
• Server too hard → reduce pace.
• Zone disputes → use cones and clear boundaries.
DRL-ADULT-009.rg:
• Coach feeds serve-like ball.
• Deep zone larger.
DRL-ADULT-009.pg:
• Shrink zones; add no-ad pressure points.
• Add return +1 pattern requirement.
DRL-ADULT-009.sf:
• Adults: enforce warm-up, shoulder prep, and hydration.
• Scale intensity and movement for mixed ability levels.

DRL-ADULT-013.o: Give adults a simple repeatable between-point routine to reduce unforced errors in matches.
DRL-ADULT-013.su: During point play or drills, coach enforces a 10-second routine after every point.
DRL-ADULT-013.ex:
1) After each point, players: 1) turn away, 2) one deep breath, 3) say one cue ('big target'), 4) state next plan (serve target/pattern).
2) If routine skipped, point doesn't count (or team loses a point).
2) Run for 10 minutes inside any point game.
DRL-ADULT-013.cu:
• Turn away to reset.
• Breath down-regulates.
• One cue only.
• Plan before point.
DRL-ADULT-013.ef:
• Players feel silly → normalize; keep it short.
• Routine too long → compress to breath + cue.
• Overthinking → plan must be 1 sentence.
DRL-ADULT-013.rg:
• Coach prompts routine verbally.
• No penalty—just reminders.
DRL-ADULT-013.pg:
• Add pressure scoring; routine required on big points only.
• Add journaling after session (1 lesson, 1 goal).
DRL-ADULT-013.sf:
• Adults: enforce warm-up, shoulder prep, and hydration.
• Scale intensity and movement for mixed ability levels.

DRL-CARDIO-015.o: Train change-of-direction and recovery with representative corner movement and controlled hitting.
DRL-CARDIO-015.su: Coach at center with basket. Place cones at 4 corners (2 wide, 2 deep).
DRL-CARDIO-015.ex:
1) Work 40 sec / rest 20 sec for 8 rounds.
2) Coach feeds to a random corner. Player hits to big crosscourt target then sprints through center cone before next feed.
2) Every third feed is a 'short ball'—player must approach and touch net strap after volley.
2) Rotate players each round.
DRL-CARDIO-015.cu:
• Turn shoulders early.
• Run through the middle to recover.
• Big targets under fatigue.
• Split-step before each feed.
DRL-CARDIO-015.ef:
• Player guesses feed → cue 'react not predict'.
• Errors explode → slow feeds; widen targets.
• Unsafe sprinting → require controlled decel at cones.
DRL-CARDIO-015.rg:
• Predictable pattern feeds (two corners only).
• Shorter work (30 sec).
DRL-CARDIO-015.pg:
• Increase randomness and pace.
• Add scoring for correct 'short ball' decisions.
DRL-CARDIO-015.sm:
• Hits in play per interval.
• Recovery efficiency (coach).
DRL-CARDIO-015.sf:
• High intensity: enforce warm-up and hydration breaks.
• Manage shoulder/knee load; swap sprints for shuffles if needed.
DRL-CARDIO-015.tg: Cardio, Fitness, Workout, Movement, Agility, Pace control

DRL-CARDIO-017.n: Cardio Tennis: Serve-Spot Sprint (Placement Under Fatigue)
DRL-CARDIO-017.o: Train serving under fatigue with a repeatable sprint-reset routine.
DRL-CARDIO-017.su: Players serve in pairs. Mark 3 serve targets (T/body/wide) with cones.
DRL-CARDIO-017.ex:
1) Work 25 sec / rest 20 sec for 10 rounds.
2) During work: server hits 2 serves (call target first). After each serve, server sprint-shuffles to touch doubles alley cone and returns to serve.
2) Returner blocks return safely; focus is serve quality.
2) Switch server every 2 rounds.
DRL-CARDIO-017.cu:
• Call target, then commit.
• Land balanced.
• Quick feet, controlled breathing.
• Second serve has shape (spin).
DRL-CARDIO-017.ef:
• Serve breaks down → reduce sprint distance; keep routine.
• Rushing toss → pause 1 second before toss.
• Returner too aggressive → block deep middle.
DRL-CARDIO-017.rg:
• No sprints; walk reset.
• Only 1 target (big target).
DRL-CARDIO-017.pg:
• Add scoring: must hit target 3 times to 'win' round.
• Add pressure: last round is win-by-2 targets.
DRL-CARDIO-017.sm:
• Serve target hit %.
• Serve in % under fatigue.
DRL-CARDIO-017.sf:
• High intensity: enforce warm-up and hydration breaks.
• Manage shoulder/knee load; swap sprints for shuffles if needed.
DRL-CARDIO-017.tg: Cardio, Fitness, Workout, Serve, Pressure, Footwork

DRL-CARDIO-020.n: Cardio Tennis: 'Hit & Hydrate' Intervals (Workout + Recovery Habit)
DRL-CARDIO-020.o: Deliver a strong workout while building a disciplined hydration/reset habit.
DRL-CARDIO-020.su: 6–12 players. Set water break station and cones for intervals. Use simple feed + rally tasks.
DRL-CARDIO-020.ex:
1) Run 5 blocks of 6 minutes: 4 min work (intervals) + 2 min coached hydrate/stretch.
2) Work: 20-sec rally to big target + 10-sec quick reset (repeat).
2) Rotate partners every 2 minutes.
2) During hydrate block: coach cues breathing, quick shoulder/hip mobility, and refocus.
DRL-CARDIO-020.cu:
• Work hard, then recover on purpose.
• Big targets when tired.
• Breath down-regulates.
• Hydrate consistently.
DRL-CARDIO-020.ef:
• Players skip recovery → make hydrate block mandatory.
• Intensity too high for beginners → reduce to 15/15 intervals.
• Form breakdown → stop and reset cues.
DRL-CARDIO-020.rg:
• Shorter blocks: 3 x 5 min.
• No running—only hitting.
DRL-CARDIO-020.pg:
• Increase intensity or add movement tasks.
• Add scoring for rally streaks.
DRL-CARDIO-020.sm:
• Player-reported readiness after blocks.
• Rally success during later blocks.
DRL-CARDIO-020.sf:
• Heat management: schedule hydration breaks and shade as needed.
• Modify impact: replace sprints with shuffles for knee/ankle issues.
DRL-CARDIO-020.tg: Cardio, Fitness, Workout, Recovery, Habits, Fun

DRL-CARDIO-024.n: Cardio Tennis: Partner Hand-Off Rally (Social + Sweat)
DRL-CARDIO-024.o: Create a fun adult workout with constant movement and simple rally goals.
DRL-CARDIO-024.su: Pairs on each half-court. After every 3 hits, partners switch sides (hand-off through middle).
DRL-CARDIO-024.ex:
1) 12-minute continuous block.
2) Rally crosscourt; after 3 hits, both partners run through middle and swap positions.
2) If rally breaks, quick restart with coach feed.
DRL-CARDIO-024.cu:
• Talk during swaps.
• Keep ball in play.
• Run through middle, not around partner.
• Smile—keep it social.
DRL-CARDIO-024.ef:
• Collisions → enforce 'call yours' and widen lanes.
• Too many errors → slow pace and allow 1 extra bounce.
• Confusion → count hits aloud.
DRL-CARDIO-024.rg:
• No swaps; rally only.
• Swap after 5 hits.
DRL-CARDIO-024.pg:
• Make it competitive: points for successful swaps without miss.
• Add DTL trigger on coach call.
DRL-CARDIO-024.sm:
• Successful swaps per minute.
• Rally length.
DRL-CARDIO-024.sf:
• High intensity: enforce warm-up and hydration breaks.
• Manage shoulder/knee load; swap sprints for shuffles if needed.

DRL-CARDIO-030.n: Cardio Tennis: 'Champions Court' HIIT Liveball (15-Min Burner)
DRL-CARDIO-030.o: Blend cardio and competition using fast-rotation liveball with short rounds.
DRL-CARDIO-030.su: 6–12 players. Use takeover-style liveball with 2-minute rounds and quick rotation.
DRL-CARDIO-030.ex:
1) Play 5 rounds of 2 minutes with 1-minute rest.
2) Challengers need 2 points to take; defenders need 1 point to hold (fast).
2) Coach keeps tempo high with quick feeds.
DRL-CARDIO-030.cu:
• Fast reset; next point.
• Big targets under fatigue.
• Communicate.
DRL-CARDIO-030.ef:
• Arguing about score → coach controls score; keep simple.
• Too intense for some → add longer rest or slower feed.
• Unsafe dives → no diving rule.
DRL-CARDIO-030.rg:
• Longer rounds with less intensity.
• No competitive takeover; cooperative.
DRL-CARDIO-030.pg:
• Add themed round: return must be deep.
• Add golden point at end.
DRL-CARDIO-030.sf:
• High intensity: enforce warm-up and hydration breaks.
• Manage shoulder/knee load; swap sprints for shuffles if needed.

DRL-DBL-042.n: Return Team Shape (Chip Middle + Net Crash)
DRL-DBL-042.o: Create a simple doubles return pattern that works for USTA teams: deep middle chip + net crash.
DRL-DBL-042.su: 4 players. Returner starts slightly inside baseline. Return partner starts at service line ready to move.
DRL-DBL-042.ex:
1) Server serves at 70% pace.
2) Returner chips/blocks deep middle (beyond service line).
2) Return team both move forward: returner to baseline-approach line; partner to net.
2) Point becomes live after return. Score only counts if return is deep middle.
DRL-DBL-042.cu:
• Return deep middle buys time.
• Move together—same direction.
• First volley to big target.
• Communicate: 'mine/yours/switch'.
DRL-DBL-042.ef:
• Return floats short → aim higher and through the middle.
• Partner stays back → assign a 'crash line' they must cross.
• Too aggressive volleys → bigger target; play through middle.
DRL-DBL-042.rg:
• Coach feeds instead of serve.
• Returner allowed to hit crosscourt instead of middle.
DRL-DBL-042.pg:
• Add lob defense after crash.
• Add poach on 2nd volley with signal.
DRL-DBL-042.sm:
• Return depth %.
• Points won after crash.
DRL-DBL-042.sf:
• Define calling priority to avoid racket clashes.
• No reckless dives; keep spacing at net.

DRL-DBL-045.o: Make formations usable for league play by practicing clear calls, movement, and coverage responsibilities.
DRL-DBL-045.su: 4 players. Server + partner practice I-formation or Australian. Use clear hand signals.
DRL-DBL-045.ex:
1) Play 10 points from deuce side in I-formation, then 10 from ad side.
2) After serve, net player commits to called side; server covers opposite.
2) Return team tries to hit to open space (including lobs).
2) Coach pauses every 3 points to adjust positioning.
DRL-DBL-045.cu:
• Signal early and confirm.
• Net player commits—no half poach.
• Server covers line with urgency.
• Return team uses height and crosscourt.
DRL-DBL-045.ef:
• Net player hesitates → reduce options; only 'left/right'.
• Server doesn’t cover line → mark line coverage cone.
• Miscommunication → require verbal confirmation before serve.
DRL-DBL-045.rg:
• Start without returners (shadow movements).
• No lobs allowed initially.
DRL-DBL-045.pg:
• Allow returners to choose aggressive options.
• Add scoring: formation point counts double.
DRL-DBL-045.sm:
• Call accuracy.
• Point outcomes by formation.
DRL-DBL-045.sf:
• Define calling priority to avoid racket clashes.
• No reckless dives; keep spacing at net.

DRL-DBL-048.n: Body Serve + Middle Ball (USTA Doubles Bread & Butter)
DRL-DBL-048.o: Build a reliable doubles pattern: body serve to jam → next ball through the middle.
DRL-DBL-048.su: 4 players. Server targets body; net partner ready to intercept middle ball.
DRL-DBL-048.ex:
1) Server calls 'body' and serves at 70% pace.
2) Returner aims deep middle.
2) Server hits +1 through middle; net partner looks to poach on any float.
2) Play point. Score only counts if serve target was attempted and +1 went through middle.
DRL-DBL-048.cu:
• Body serve jams; keeps return predictable.
• Middle is safe—reduces angles.
• Net partner hunts floaters.
• Stay disciplined.
DRL-DBL-048.ef:
• Serving too hard → prioritize placement.
• +1 tries to paint lines → bigger middle target.
• Net partner poaches blindly → poach only on float or signal.
DRL-DBL-048.rg:
• Allow returner to block crosscourt.
• Coach feed instead of serve.
DRL-DBL-048.pg:
• Increase serve pace; add no-ad pressure points.
• Add 'Plan B' (T serve + volley) if returner adjusts.
DRL-DBL-048.sf:
• Define calling priority to avoid racket clashes.
• No reckless dives; keep spacing at net.

DRL-DBL-049.n: Two-Up / Two-Back Switch Trigger (Win the Net Together)
DRL-DBL-049.o: Train coordinated team transitions from 2-back to 2-up on a short ball trigger.
DRL-DBL-049.su: 4 players. Start with both teams 2-back. Coach feeds neutral ball.
DRL-DBL-049.ex:
1) Rally crosscourt for 4 balls then live.
2) Trigger: if your team hits a ball that lands inside opponent’s service line, both players must move to net.
2) Point continues; if team fails to move together, automatic point loss.
DRL-DBL-049.cu:
• Move as a pair.
• Trigger is ball depth, not mood.
• Split-step at net.
• Volley to big targets.
DRL-DBL-049.ef:
• One player goes alone → enforce rule, reset roles.
• Trigger unclear → mark service line with cones.
• Players stuck in no-man's-land → define approach depth (stop at service line then split-step).
DRL-DBL-049.rg:
• Coach calls trigger instead of depth rule.
• Only one player moves (net player) at first.
DRL-DBL-049.pg:
• Add counter: opponent can lob; must switch and cover.
• Add pressure scoring: win-by-2.
DRL-DBL-049.sm:
• Correct transition compliance.
• Net points won.
DRL-DBL-049.sf:
• Define calling priority to avoid racket clashes.
• No reckless dives; keep spacing at net.

DRL-DBL-051.n: Doubles Tiebreak Serve Order Simulator (Mini-Break Awareness)
DRL-DBL-051.o: Make tiebreak serve order automatic and train mini-break awareness for team tennis.
DRL-DBL-051.su: 4 players. Play tiebreak to 10 (super tiebreak) with correct serve order.
DRL-DBL-051.ex:
1) Play super tiebreak to 10.
2) Coach calls out 'mini-break' whenever return team wins a point.
2) Between points, each team must verbalize the current score and who serves next.
2) At 8-8, enforce 'win-by-2'.
DRL-DBL-051.cu:
• Know the score and server.
• Reset between points.
• Play big targets on pressure points.
• Serve routines stay constant.
DRL-DBL-051.ef:
• Players forget order → use a serve-order card on fence.
• Score confusion → stop and reset; teach quickly.
• Over-tightness → breathing cue.
DRL-DBL-051.rg:
• Play to 7 instead of 10.
• Coach feeds instead of serve.
DRL-DBL-051.pg:
• Add constraint (returns must be deep).
• Start down 0-3.
DRL-DBL-051.sm:
• Serve order accuracy.
• Mini-break conversion rate.
DRL-DBL-051.sf:
• Define calling priority to avoid racket clashes.
• No reckless dives; keep spacing at net.

DRL-DBL-051-V1.o: Make tiebreak serve order automatic and train mini-break awareness for team tennis.

DRL-DBL-052.n: Net Player 'Hands First' Challenge (No Back Swing Volleys)
DRL-DBL-052.o: Improve volley stability by removing backswings and training hands-first reactions.
DRL-DBL-052.su: 4 players. 2 at net, 2 feeding from service line with controlled pace. Mark volley target zones.
DRL-DBL-052.ex:
1) Volleyers must keep racket in front—no backswing (coach watches).
2) Feeders alternate forehand/backhand volleys and occasional body volleys.
2) Score 1 point for each volley landing in target; -1 if backswing occurs.
2) Rotate after 2 minutes.
DRL-DBL-052.cu:
• Racket out front.
• Punch, don't swing.
• Absorb pace (soft hands).
• Stay balanced.
DRL-DBL-052.ef:
• Backswing habit → have player start with racket touching net strap line (visual).
• Stiff hands → cue 'catch with strings'.
• Feet stuck → add split-step each feed.
DRL-DBL-052.rg:
• Slower feeds.
• No target scoring—just reps.
DRL-DBL-052.pg:
• Increase feed pace.
• Add 'poach step' then volley.
DRL-DBL-052.sf:
• Define calling priority to avoid racket clashes.
• No reckless dives; keep spacing at net.

DRL-DBL-053.o: Prioritize the highest leverage phase in doubles: serve, return, +1 volley.
DRL-DBL-053.su: 4 players. Mark return depth zone and first-volley target (deep middle).
DRL-DBL-053.ex:
1) Play points but stop after first volley (+1).
2) If server team wins advantage by +1 volley (winner/forced error), award point.
2) If neutral, replay.
2) Rotate servers every 6 points.
DRL-DBL-053.cu:
• Serve with target.
• Return deep.
• First volley to middle.
• Reset quickly.
DRL-DBL-053.ef:
• Players want full points → explain rep goal; keep tempo.
• Return floats short → aim higher; bigger target.
• Volley too risky → middle target only.
DRL-DBL-053.rg:
• Stop after serve+return only.
• Allow coach feed.
DRL-DBL-053.pg:
• Play full point after +1 for last 5 minutes.
• Add pressure: win-by-2.
DRL-DBL-053.sf:
• Define calling priority to avoid racket clashes.
• No reckless dives; keep spacing at net.

DRL-DBL-055.n: Defense-to-Offense Doubles Reset (High Cross then Counter)
DRL-DBL-055.o: Teach teams to reset with a high crosscourt ball, then counterattack as a unit.
DRL-DBL-055.su: 4 players. Coach feeds a wide defensive ball to one baseliner.
DRL-DBL-055.ex:
1) Defensive team must hit a high, deep crosscourt reset (ball #1).
2) After reset, they must recover and win point using a 2-ball pattern: next ball through middle then net finish.
2) Play point live after reset. Score only counts if reset met height/depth criteria.
DRL-DBL-055.cu:
• Defense first: high and deep.
• Recover together.
• Attack through middle.
• Finish at net when you have advantage.
DRL-DBL-055.ef:
• Reset too low → enforce height window; add rope/visual cue.
• Players attack too early → require through-middle ball.
• Partner doesn't move up → define trigger (after through-middle ball).
DRL-DBL-055.rg:
• Coach calls 'reset' ball; allow any deep ball.
• Remove net finish requirement.
DRL-DBL-055.pg:
• Add pressure: start at 30-40.
• Add opponent poach/lob responses.
DRL-DBL-055.sm:
• Reset compliance %.
• Point win rate after reset.
DRL-DBL-055.sf:
• Define calling priority to avoid racket clashes.
• No reckless dives; keep spacing at net.

DRL-GAME-005.n: Tennis Golf (9 Holes: Targets Around the Court)
DRL-GAME-005.o: Create a fun, repeatable accuracy game that works for mixed groups and keeps everyone engaged.
DRL-GAME-005.su:
Set 9 targets ('holes') using cones/hoops: deep middle, deep cross, short angle, volley target, overhead target, serve target, etc.
Players keep their own score (strokes).
DRL-GAME-005.ex:
1) Each hole has a start location and target. Players take turns (or stations) hitting until they 'sink' the ball (hit target).
2) Count strokes: each attempt = 1 stroke; max strokes per hole = 6.
2) Winner is lowest total strokes after 9 holes.
2) Rotate to keep tempo; coach feeds where needed.
DRL-GAME-005.cu:
• Aim big; be smart.
• Routine and focus.
• Celebrate small wins.
• Keep pace—don’t overthink.
DRL-GAME-005.ef:
• Slow pace → run stations simultaneously.
• Targets too hard → enlarge targets.
• Score confusion → use simple scorecards.
DRL-GAME-005.rg:
• Only 3 holes (short version).
• Allow hand toss starts for kids.
DRL-GAME-005.pg:
• Shrink targets; add time limit per hole.
• Add doubles team golf.
DRL-GAME-005.sf:
• Keep rotations organized to avoid collisions.
• Explain scoring clearly before starting.
DRL-GAME-005.tg: Games, Competition, Targets, Fun, All levels

DRL-GAME-006.o: High-energy takeover game that produces lots of point reps and pressure moments.
DRL-GAME-006.su: One 'champion' side and one 'challenger' side. 6–12 players rotate in quickly.
DRL-GAME-006.ex:
1) Challengers must win 2 points to take the champion side.
2) Champions need only 1 point to hold.
2) Coach feeds fast between points.
2) After a player plays 2 consecutive rounds on champion side, they must rotate out (prevent hogging).
DRL-GAME-006.cu:
• Fast reset—next point.
• Play big targets under pressure.
• Champions defend; challengers attack smart.
• Keep energy high.
DRL-GAME-006.ef:
• Rotation confusion → label spots; coach calls names.
• Too many errors → start with cooperative 4-ball then live.
• Over-competitive → enforce sportsmanship rule.
DRL-GAME-006.rg:
• Challengers need 1 point (easier).
• Use half-court only.
DRL-GAME-006.pg:
• Add themed rounds (returns deep, middle only).
• Add tiebreak final between top two.
DRL-GAME-006.sm:
• Points played per minute.
• UFE per 10.
DRL-GAME-006.sf:
• Keep rotations organized to avoid collisions.
• Explain scoring clearly before starting.

DRL-GAME-007.n: World Cup Teams (Country vs Country Points)
DRL-GAME-007.o: Create team energy and a global theme that works for juniors and adults.
DRL-GAME-007.su: Split into 2–4 teams (countries). Each court runs the same mini-game; points add to team total.
DRL-GAME-007.ex:
1) Play 3 rounds of 8 minutes: Round 1 rally points, Round 2 serve-start points, Round 3 doubles points.
2) Each round winner earns 3 team points, second earns 2, third earns 1.
2) Final: one sudden-death point per court for bonus.
DRL-GAME-007.cu:
• Cheer teammates.
• Big targets.
• Fast reset between rounds.
• Have a clear team role.
DRL-GAME-007.ef:
• Confusion → keep rules simple and posted.
• Uneven teams → assign handicaps.
• Slow transitions → timekeeper + whistle.
DRL-GAME-007.pg:
• Add themed constraint per round (middle only, deep returns).
• Add prize for sportsmanship.
DRL-GAME-007.sf:
• Keep rotations organized to avoid collisions.
• Explain scoring clearly before starting.

DRL-GAME-007-V1.n: World Cup Teams (Country vs Country Points) — Simplified
DRL-GAME-007-V1.o: Create team energy and a global theme that works for juniors and adults.
DRL-GAME-007-V1.su: Split into 2–4 teams (countries). Each court runs the same mini-game; points add to team total.
DRL-GAME-007-V1.ex:

DRL-GAME-007-V1.sf:

• Explain scoring clearly before starting.

DRL-GAME-008.o: Increase volley reps and footwork while keeping rotation fast and fun.
DRL-GAME-008.su: 6–12 players. Two lines at net positions; feeder at service line. When you miss, rotate to end of opposite line.
DRL-GAME-008.ex:
1) Feeder tosses/feeds to volleyer.
2) Volleyer must volley to target then immediately switch sides (cross behind net strap safely).
2) If volleyer misses, they rotate out; next person steps in.
2) Score team streaks (max consecutive successful volleys).
DRL-GAME-008.cu:
• Hands in front.
• Split-step before feed.
• Punch to big target.
• Move with control around net.
DRL-GAME-008.ef:
• Collisions at net → one-way traffic rule; cross behind net strap only.
• Volley swings → punch cue.
• Feeder too fast → slow down; quality reps.
DRL-GAME-008.rg:
• Catch then punch.
• No crossing; stay on one side.
DRL-GAME-008.pg:
• Add movement: volley then touch service line cone.
• Add overhead feed every 5th ball.
DRL-GAME-008.sm:
• Volley streak max.
• Touches per minute.
DRL-GAME-008.sf:
• Keep rotations organized to avoid collisions.
• Explain scoring clearly before starting.
DRL-GAME-008.tg: Games, Competition, Net play, Workout, Rotation

DRL-GAME-009.o: Create variety and pressure by starting tiebreaks at random scores (match realism).
DRL-GAME-009.su: Write 6 start scores on cards (0-3, 1-4, 4-4, 5-5, 6-6, 2-2). Draw a card each round.
DRL-GAME-009.ex:
1) Draw a start score and set that score immediately.
2) Play tiebreak to 7 (win-by-2 at 6-6).
2) After each tiebreak, switch partners/opponents.
DRL-GAME-009.cu:
• Reset quickly—score is just information.
• Big targets.
• Know serve order.
• Body language discipline.
DRL-GAME-009.ef:
• Score confusion → serve-order card and coach calls out server.
• Over-tightness → breath cue.
• Arguing about start score → card is final.
DRL-GAME-009.pg:
• Add constraint: return must be deep.
• Add one 'golden point' at end.
DRL-GAME-009.sm:
• Tiebreak win rate from behind.
• UFE under pressure.
DRL-GAME-009.sf:
• Keep rotations organized to avoid collisions.
• Explain scoring clearly before starting.

DRL-GAME-009-V2.o: Create variety and pressure by starting tiebreaks at random scores (match realism).
DRL-GAME-009-V2.su: Write 6 start scores on cards (0-3, 1-4, 4-4, 5-5, 6-6, 2-2). Draw a card each round.
DRL-GAME-009-V2.ex:
1) Draw a start score and set that score immediately.
2) Play tiebreak to 7 (win-by-2 at 6-6).
2) After each tiebreak, switch partners/opponents.
DRL-GAME-009-V2.cu:
• Reset quickly—score is just information.
• Big targets.
• Know serve order.
• Body language discipline.
DRL-GAME-009-V2.ef:
• Score confusion → serve-order card and coach calls out server.
• Over-tightness → breath cue.
• Arguing about start score → card is final.
DRL-GAME-009-V2.pg:
• Add constraint: return must be deep.
• Add one 'golden point' at end.
DRL-GAME-009-V2.sm:
• Tiebreak win rate from behind.
• UFE under pressure.
DRL-GAME-009-V2.sf:
• Keep rotations organized to avoid collisions.
• Explain scoring clearly before starting.

DRL-GAME-010.n: Beat the Pro (Coach as Boss Level)
DRL-GAME-010.o: Make a challenging but inclusive game: players vs coach with scalable handicaps.
DRL-GAME-010.su: Coach plays 1 side; players rotate as challengers. Use handicaps: coach must hit to big target only; players can have extra bounce.
DRL-GAME-010.ex:
1) Challenger plays 2 points vs coach.
2) If challenger wins 1 point, they earn a team point; if they win 2, they earn 3 team points.
2) Rotate quickly; keep scoreboard.
DRL-GAME-010.cu:
• Play smart, not hard.
• Big targets.
• Coach models patterns.
• Have fun with it.
DRL-GAME-010.ef:
• Coach dominates too much → add handicaps (coach must hit middle, must slice, etc.).
• Players intimidated → allow extra bounce or green dot.
• Slow rotation → 2-point max then rotate.
DRL-GAME-010.rg:
• Cooperative rally with coach only.
• Shorter court.
DRL-GAME-010.pg:
• Add doubles challengers vs coach.
• Add theme: only crosscourt.
DRL-GAME-010.sf:
• Keep rotations organized to avoid collisions.
• Explain scoring clearly before starting.

DRL-GAME-011.o: A universal team game that teaches consistency and scoring with a familiar structure.
DRL-GAME-011.su: Split into 2 teams. Use a simple 'bases' system with cones (1st, 2nd, 3rd, home).
DRL-GAME-011.ex:
1) Team hits 3 'outs' per inning (misses).
2) A successful rally exchange or target hit advances runners one base.
2) A point won with a winner advances two bases.
2) Play 3 innings; switch roles.
DRL-GAME-011.cu:
• Keep ball in play.
• Advance runners with smart shots.
• Team communication.
• Celebrate teammates.
DRL-GAME-011.ef:
• Rules too complex → simplify: every made ball advances 1 base.
• Slow pace → use timed innings instead of outs.
• Arguments → coach is umpire.
DRL-GAME-011.rg:
• No bases; just team points.
• Shorter innings.
DRL-GAME-011.pg:
• Add constraints per inning (crosscourt only, deep middle only).
• Add 'steal base' fitness option.
DRL-GAME-011.sf:
• Keep rotations organized to avoid collisions.
• Explain scoring clearly before starting.

DRL-GAME-012.n: Doubles 'Sudden Death Alley' (One Ball, One Life)
DRL-GAME-012.o: Create instant pressure: lose one point and rotate out; teaches focus and doubles court awareness.
DRL-GAME-012.su: 8–16 players. One doubles court. Losers rotate out, winners stay (but max 3 rounds).
DRL-GAME-012.ex:
1) Play one sudden-death point.
2) If ball lands in alley = automatic win (encourages awareness).
2) Losers rotate; winners stay up to 3 rounds then must rotate out.
DRL-GAME-012.cu:
• Treat every point like match point.
• Big targets.
• Know your alley responsibilities.
• Reset quickly.
DRL-GAME-012.ef:
• Rotation confusion → clear waiting spots.
• Too stressful → allow best-of-3 points before rotation.
• Unsafe dives → no diving rule.
DRL-GAME-012.rg:
• Alley rule removed.
• Play two-point mini-games.
DRL-GAME-012.pg:
• Add no-ad serve starts only.
• Add 'middle bonus' instead of alley bonus.
DRL-GAME-012.sm:
• Points played per minute.
• UFE per 10.
DRL-GAME-012.sf:
• Keep rotations organized to avoid collisions.
• Explain scoring clearly before starting.

DRL-GAME-013.o: Make serving practice game-like and repeatable with a bingo card of targets.
DRL-GAME-013.su: Create a 3x3 bingo grid with targets (T/body/wide on both sides). Use cones/hoops.
DRL-GAME-013.ex:
1) Players serve and mark a square when they hit a target (or within a zone).
2) First to complete a row wins.
2) Add bonus: after bingo, must hit 2 consecutive second serves in to finish.
DRL-GAME-013.cu:
• Call target first.
• Same routine.
• Spin second serves.
• Celebrate progress.
DRL-GAME-013.ef:
• Targets too hard → enlarge zones.
• Players chase lines → aim 3 feet inside.
• Slow pace → run 2 servers at once.
DRL-GAME-013.rg:
• Only 3 targets (no bingo).
• Underhand serve for younger.
DRL-GAME-013.pg:
• Shrink targets; add pressure time limit.
• Add serve+1 after each target hit.
DRL-GAME-013.sm:
• Targets hit per 20 serves.
• Serve in %.
DRL-GAME-013.sf:
• Keep rotations organized to avoid collisions.
• Explain scoring clearly before starting.

DRL-GAME-014.n: Grand Slam 'Night Session' (Lights Out Pressure Round)
DRL-GAME-014.o: Create a high-pressure round that simulates 'night session' intensity—short, clutch, and emotional control.
DRL-GAME-014.su: Set a 10-minute round where every point is worth double and starts with serve/return.
DRL-GAME-014.ex:
1) All points are no-ad.
2) Every point starts with serve; return must be deep or point loss.
2) At 8 minutes, announce 'last 2 minutes' where missed routine = point loss.
2) Winning team gets 'night session champ' title for the day.
DRL-GAME-014.cu:
• Stay calm; breathe.
• Routines are non-negotiable.
• Big targets on returns.
• Embrace pressure.
DRL-GAME-014.ef:
• Too intense → shorten round to 6 minutes.
• Rule confusion → post rules clearly.
• Behavior issues → enforce sportsmanship.
DRL-GAME-014.rg:
• Remove return depth rule.
• Normal scoring.
DRL-GAME-014.pg:
• Add crowd noise/music.
• Start down 0-3 in tiebreak.
DRL-GAME-014.sm:
• Return depth %.
• UFE under pressure.
• Routine compliance.
DRL-GAME-014.sf:
• Keep rotations organized to avoid collisions.
• Explain scoring clearly before starting.
DRL-GAME-014.tg: Games, Competition, Pressure, Elite vibe, Competition

DRL-HP-003.n: Backhand Line Under Pressure (DTL Target + Recover)
DRL-HP-003.o: Train a high-level backhand DTL change with margin, plus the recovery that makes it usable in matches.
DRL-HP-003.su: Start with backhand crosscourt rally. Mark a DTL target 3 feet inside sideline and deep. Place a recovery cone at center.
DRL-HP-003.ex:
1) Rally crosscourt for 4 balls.
2) On coach call (or on short ball), hitter changes DTL to target.
2) After DTL, hitter must recover to touch center cone before next ball.
2) Point continues live after DTL. Score only counts if DTL was attempted to target.
DRL-HP-003.cu:
• Prepare early; clean contact.
• Aim inside the line.
• Recover immediately—don’t admire.
• Expect the counter.
DRL-HP-003.ef:
• DTL misses wide → increase net clearance; aim bigger target.
• Late contact → start closer; reduce pace.
• Recovery slow → enforce touch-cone rule.
DRL-HP-003.rg:
• Coach feeds a controlled short ball for DTL.
• Stop point after DTL attempt.
DRL-HP-003.pg:
• Add opponent counter pattern (inside-out).
• Add scoreboard: only DTL at 30-30 or later.
DRL-HP-003.sm:
• DTL target hit %.
• Recovery compliance.
• Point win rate after DTL.
DRL-HP-003.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-003.tg: High Performance, Elite, Backhand, Change direction, Pressure

DRL-HP-004.o: Rehearse the inside-out forehand pattern at elite pace while managing fatigue and footwork quality.
DRL-HP-004.su: Feed to backhand corner. Mark inside-out target (deep crosscourt) and inside-in target (DTL).
DRL-HP-004.ex:
1) Work 40 sec / rest 20 sec x 8.
2) Player runs around and hits inside-out forehand to target.
2) Every 3rd ball: coach calls 'inside-in' and player must go line to target.
2) Track quality: only count reps that land in target zones.
DRL-HP-004.cu:
• First step is explosive.
• Get outside ball.
• Height + spin for margin.
• Recover through the middle.
DRL-HP-004.ef:
• Footwork breaks down → reduce pace; add rest.
• Inside-out sprays → aim higher, use more spin.
• Inside-in forced → allow one extra step and bigger target.
DRL-HP-004.rg:
• Only inside-out (no inside-in).
• Start closer to corner.
DRL-HP-004.pg:
• Add live point after inside-in.
• Add pressure: must hit 3 targets in a row to finish.
DRL-HP-004.sm:
• Target hit %.
• Rep quality under fatigue.
DRL-HP-004.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-004.tg: High Performance, Elite, Forehand, Footwork, Offense

DRL-HP-006.n: Short Ball Kill Tree (Drive / Angle / Drop)
DRL-HP-006.o: Develop 3 elite short-ball options with decision triggers and finish patterns.
DRL-HP-006.su: Mark 3 targets: deep corner (drive), short angle (angle), short drop box (drop). Coach feeds short balls.
DRL-HP-006.ex:
1) Player receives short ball.
2) Player must call option (Drive/Angle/Drop) before contact.
2) After shot, player must complete finish pattern: Drive → recover; Angle → cover open court; Drop → close net for volley/lob response.
2) Score 1 point for correct option + shot in target; play to 15.
DRL-HP-006.cu:
• Call it early.
• Move through ball; don’t reach.
• Finish pattern matters (don’t watch).
• Choose simple option under pressure.
DRL-HP-006.ef:
• Drop attempted from too far → enforce trigger: only inside service line.
• Angles miss → aim inside target; add net clearance.
• Drive too risky → deep middle first.
DRL-HP-006.rg:
• Only Drive option first 5 min.
• Coach calls option for athlete.
DRL-HP-006.pg:
• Add opponent response live (coach feeds pass/lob).
• Add pressure: start at 13-13.
DRL-HP-006.sm:
• Option execution %.
• Conversion rate on short balls.
DRL-HP-006.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-006.tg: High Performance, Elite, Offense, Decision-making, Short ball

DRL-HP-008.o: Weaponize the kick serve and +1 ball with repeatable targets and pressure scoring.
DRL-HP-008.su: Mark ad-side kick target (wide) and +1 target (open court). Returner starts neutral.
DRL-HP-008.ex:
1) Server serves kick wide from ad side.
2) Returner blocks return crosscourt deep.
2) Server hits +1 to open court target (inside-out or DTL depending on plan).
2) Score: +2 if serve hits target; +1 if +1 hits target; -1 for double fault. Play to 20.
DRL-HP-008.cu:
• Same routine every serve.
• Spin + height.
• +1 with margin.
• Expect the return; move early.
DRL-HP-008.ef:
• Serve lands short → toss higher; brush more; aim higher.
• +1 rushed → split-step after serve.
• Overthinking → simplify to one +1 target.
DRL-HP-008.rg:
• Underhand or slice serve for juniors.
• No scoring; just reps.
DRL-HP-008.pg:
• Add 2nd serve pressure only set.
• Add returner aggression (step in) for adaptation.
DRL-HP-008.sm:
• Serve target %.
• +1 target %.
• Double faults per 10.
DRL-HP-008.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-008.tg: High Performance, Elite, Serve, Patterns, Lefty/righty

DRL-HP-011.o: Teach elite rhythm control: vary pace intentionally without losing shape or targets.
DRL-HP-011.su: Crosscourt rally. Define three tempos: 1) 'Slow heavy' (high, spin), 2) 'Fast drive' (flatter), 3) 'Reset' (deep middle).
DRL-HP-011.ex:
1) Coach calls tempo every 2 balls (e.g., slow, slow, fast, reset).
2) Players must adjust pace while keeping ball in play to big targets.
2) After 2 minutes, play live points where a player earns bonus point if they successfully execute a 'slow-fast' change before winning.
DRL-HP-011.cu:
• Same swing shape; adjust acceleration.
• Height buys control.
• Fast doesn’t mean risky.
• Reset after miss.
DRL-HP-011.ef:
• Players change technique too much → cue 'same shape, different speed'.
• Fast shots miss → bigger targets; reduce speed delta.
• Slow shots sit short → aim deeper/higher.
DRL-HP-011.rg:
• Only slow tempo first 3 min.
• Coach calls slower switches.
DRL-HP-011.pg:
• Add direction changes during tempo shifts.
• Add pressure scoring.
DRL-HP-011.sm:
• Tempo compliance (coach).
• Error rate during fast phase.
DRL-HP-011.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-011.tg: High Performance, Elite, Tactics, Rhythm, Control

DRL-HP-012.o: Practice elite composure and simplification when behind in games and sets.
DRL-HP-012.su: Play games that start at 0–30 (server down). Choose 1 pattern per player.
DRL-HP-012.ex:
1) Start each game at 0–30.
2) Server must play pattern on first two points (serve+1 or rally pattern).
2) Receiver focuses on deep return and big targets.
2) Play 8 games, switch servers.
DRL-HP-012.cu:
• No panic—big targets.
• Process routine between points.
• Play your best pattern under stress.
• Make opponent play.
DRL-HP-012.ef:
• Players go for too much → enforce pattern; remove hero shots.
• Emotional swings → breath + cue word.
• Coaching overload → 1 cue only.
DRL-HP-012.rg:
• Start at 15–30.
• Coach feeds instead of serve.
DRL-HP-012.pg:
• Start at 0–40.
• Add no-ad at deuce for more pressure.
DRL-HP-012.sm:
• Games held from 0–30.
• UFE rate when behind.
DRL-HP-012.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-012.tg: High Performance, Elite, Pressure, Mental, Tactics

DRL-HP-013.o: Train the tactical and emotional mechanics of mini-breaks in tiebreaks.
DRL-HP-013.su: Play repeated 7-point tiebreaks. Coach tags mini-breaks, serve locations, return depth.
DRL-HP-013.ex:
1) Play tiebreak to 7.
2) After any mini-break, the team that won it must call their next-point plan out loud.
2) At 5-5, enforce win-by-2.
2) Repeat 3 tiebreaks with short rest.
DRL-HP-013.cu:
• Know score/server.
• Plan after mini-break.
• Big targets.
• Breath to control arousal.
DRL-HP-013.ef:
• Confusion on serve order → serve-order card.
• Over-tight serving → routine + spin serve option.
• Tilting after lost mini-break → reset routine.
DRL-HP-013.pg:
• Add constraint: return must be deep.
• Start each tiebreak down 0-3.
DRL-HP-013.sm:
• Mini-break conversion rate.
• Serve in % under pressure.
DRL-HP-013.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-013.tg: High Performance, Elite, Tiebreak, Pressure, Serve/return
