DRL-HP-013-V1.tg: High Performance, Elite, Tiebreak, Pressure, Serve/return

DRL-HP-013-V2.o: Train the tactical and emotional mechanics of mini-breaks in tiebreaks.
DRL-HP-013-V2.su: Play repeated 7-point tiebreaks. Coach tags mini-breaks, serve locations, return depth.
DRL-HP-013-V2.ex:
1) Play tiebreak to 7.
2) After any mini-break, the team that won it must call their next-point plan out loud.
2) At 5-5, enforce win-by-2.
2) Repeat 3 tiebreaks with short rest.
DRL-HP-013-V2.cu:
• Know score/server.
• Plan after mini-break.
• Big targets.
• Breath to control arousal.
DRL-HP-013-V2.ef:
• Confusion on serve order → serve-order card.
• Over-tight serving → routine + spin serve option.
• Tilting after lost mini-break → reset routine.
DRL-HP-013-V2.pg:
• Add constraint: return must be deep.
• Start each tiebreak down 0-3.
DRL-HP-013-V2.sm:
• Mini-break conversion rate.
• Serve in % under pressure.
DRL-HP-013-V2.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-013-V2.tg: High Performance, Elite, Tiebreak, Pressure, Serve/return

DRL-HP-014.o: Simulate grass-court priorities: low bounce, quick points, first-strike tennis with net pressure.
DRL-HP-014.su: Rules: keep points short; reward forward movement. Mark serve targets and approach lane.
DRL-HP-014.ex:
1) Play points starting with serve.
2) Server must hit serve to target, then +1 must be aggressive to open court or approach.
2) Bonus point for finishing at net within first 4 shots.
2) First to 15 wins.
DRL-HP-014.cu:
• Attack with margin (big targets).
• Move forward behind good ball.
• Keep volley simple.
• Don’t overplay slices—use when appropriate.
DRL-HP-014.ef:
• Overforcing winners → remind big targets; middle is acceptable.
• No net movement → enforce approach trigger on short ball.
• Volleys missed → first volley deep middle.
DRL-HP-014.rg:
• No serve; coach feeds and start with approach.
• Remove bonus points.
DRL-HP-014.pg:
• Add 'returner chip and charge' option.
• Add scoreboard: no-ad deuce points.
DRL-HP-014.sm:
• Net finishes per 10 points.
• First-four win rate.
DRL-HP-014.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-014.tg: High Performance, Elite, Surface, First strike, Serve+1

DRL-HP-017.o: Prepare for lefty/righty matchup patterns (especially ad-side wide serve and crosscourt exchanges).
DRL-HP-017.su: Set two scenarios: vs lefty server on ad side; vs righty server on ad side. Mark return targets and +1 targets.
DRL-HP-017.ex:
1) Block 1: return reps vs ad-side wide serve (coach simulates). Returner aims deep middle; +1 hitter executes plan.
2) Block 2: serve reps from ad side: wide slice/kick then +1 to open.
2) Block 3: play points where only ad-side serves count (deuce side coach feed).
DRL-HP-017.cu:
• Know opponent patterns.
• Return deep first.
• Serve wide opens court.
• +1 into big target.
DRL-HP-017.ef:
• Players overcomplicate → pick one plan per block.
• Return misses → enlarge deep-middle target.
• Serve lacks shape → reduce speed, increase spin.
DRL-HP-017.rg:
• Coach feeds instead of serve.
• Only practice one scenario (righty vs righty).
DRL-HP-017.pg:
• Add pressure scoring: win-by-2 on ad-side points.
• Add 'plan B' if opponent adjusts.
DRL-HP-017.sm:
• Return depth %.
• Serve target %.
• Pattern rate.
DRL-HP-017.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-017.tg: High Performance, Elite, Opponent styles, Serve patterns, Return patterns

DRL-HP-020.o: Train attacking high balls (common on clay and vs heavy topspin) with safe targets and footwork.
DRL-HP-020.su: Coach feeds high topspin balls (or uses a spin machine). Mark attack targets deep cross and deep middle.
DRL-HP-020.ex:
1) Player practices 10 high balls: 5 forehand, 5 backhand.
2) Rule: must move back/around ball to hit at peak/descending, not jammed.
2) Score 1 if ball lands deep target; 2 if creates advantage (coach tag).
2) Finish with live points where opponent intentionally hits high ball to trigger attack.
DRL-HP-020.cu:
• Create space; don’t get jammed.
• Use legs and core.
• Aim big; add spin.
• Recover after attack.
DRL-HP-020.ef:
• Player hits too flat → add spin cue; aim higher over net.
• Late and jammed → start farther back; early prep.
• Overforcing → deep middle target.
DRL-HP-020.rg:
• Lower feeds.
• No scoring—just quality reps.
DRL-HP-020.pg:
• Add movement: high ball on run.
• Add approach after high-ball attack.
DRL-HP-020.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-020.tg: High Performance, Elite, Technique, Offense, Spin

DRL-HP-023.n: Serve Targets Under Pressure (3-in-a-Row or Restart)
DRL-HP-023.o: Build a champion-level serving focus: repeatable targets under restart pressure.
DRL-HP-023.su: Mark 3 serve targets per box (T/body/wide). Choose one target per set.
DRL-HP-023.ex:
1) Server must hit chosen target 3 times in a row.
2) If miss, restart count at 0.
2) After completing, switch target and repeat.
2) Track attempts and time to complete each target.
DRL-HP-023.cu:
• Same routine every serve.
• Commit to target.
• Breathe and visualize.
• Spin second serve; no fear.
DRL-HP-023.ef:
• Tightness → longer exhale.
• Chasing lines → target is 3 feet inside.
• Rushing resets → keep rhythm steady.
DRL-HP-023.pg:
• Add consequence: miss after 2-in-row = restart and 10-sec wall sit (optional).
• Do under fatigue after rally block.
DRL-HP-023.sm:
• Targets completed per 10 minutes.
• Serve in % and target hit %.
DRL-HP-023.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-023.tg: High Performance, Elite, Serve, Pressure, Consistency

DRL-HP-024.o: Turn second-serve returns into offensive sequences with clear targets and constraints.
DRL-HP-024.su: Server hits only second serves (spin). Mark return target (deep cross) and +1 target (open court).
DRL-HP-024.ex:
1) Returner must step in and return aggressively to return target.
2) Then must hit +1 to open-court target within next ball.
2) Stop point after +1; if sequence succeeds, score 2 points; if return in but +1 fails, score 1.
2) Play to 20.
DRL-HP-024.cu:
• Step in on second serve.
• Return deep with margin.
• +1 to open court; don’t rush.
• Recover after return.
DRL-HP-024.ef:
• Return misses long → aim higher; add spin.
• +1 too risky → bigger target; deep middle allowed.
• Returner passive → enforce stepping position.
DRL-HP-024.rg:
• Return from baseline; no step-in requirement.
• Stop after return only.
DRL-HP-024.pg:
• Play full points after +1.
• Add pressure: must win by 2.
DRL-HP-024.sm:
• Aggressive return success %.
• +1 target %.
• Points per sequence.
DRL-HP-024.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-024.tg: High Performance, Elite, Return, Offense, Pattern

DRL-HP-025.o: Make routines automatic by practicing them as a 'skill'—not a concept—inside real points.
DRL-HP-025.su: Choose one pre-point routine (breath + cue + visualize) and one between-point reset (turn away + breath + plan). Coach observes.
DRL-HP-025.ex:
1) Play a 10-minute set of points.
2) Every point begins only after athlete completes pre-point routine (coach can hold serve).
2) After point, athlete must execute reset routine before next feed/serve.
2) Coach gives feedback only on routine quality, not outcome.
DRL-HP-025.cu:
• Routines are non-negotiable.
• Breath controls arousal.
• Plan before point.
• Reset quickly after loss or win.
DRL-HP-025.ef:
• Athlete rushes → coach slows start; enforce routine.
• Routine becomes long → simplify to 1 breath + 1 cue.
• Athlete resists → explain performance ROI; keep consistent.
DRL-HP-025.rg:
• Coach guides athlete through routine verbally.
• Shorter block: 5 minutes.
DRL-HP-025.pg:
• Add pressure scoring: points only count if routine executed.
• Add crowd noise/music to simulate distractions.
DRL-HP-025.sm:
• Routine compliance %.
• Error rate trend under routine constraint.
DRL-HP-025.sf:
• High intensity and high load: manage volume, rest, and hydration.
• Quality over quantity—stop if mechanics break down.
DRL-HP-025.tg: High Performance, Elite, Mental, Routines, Consistency

DRL-KIDS-041.o: Build tracking and soft-contact skills using a balloon, then transfer to a foam/red ball.
DRL-KIDS-041.su: Each athlete has a racket; 1 balloon per 2 players (or per player). Mark a 'parade lane' with cones.
DRL-KIDS-041.ex:
1) Coach demos 'tap up' with a short swing (pancake face).
2) Athletes keep balloon in the air while walking through the lane to the finish cone.
2) Add challenge: stop at each cone and perform 3 controlled taps before moving on.
2) Transfer: replace balloon with foam/red ball bounce-taps (1 bounce between hits).
DRL-KIDS-041.cu:
• Quiet hands, quiet feet.
• Watch balloon/ball until contact.
• Small swing; control over power.
• Celebrate streaks, not perfection.
DRL-KIDS-041.ef:
• Big baseball swings → 'tiny taps' cue; hold finish.
• Eyes up early → call 'watch it' and pause before next tap.
• Crowding → assign personal lane and spacing cones.
DRL-KIDS-041.rg:
• Do balloon only, allow hand catches.
• One bounce allowed between racket taps.
DRL-KIDS-041.pg:
• Add direction: tap balloon to partner then move to next cone.
• Add 'ready position freeze' at each cone (split-step).
DRL-KIDS-041.sm:
• Longest controlled tap streak.
• Can move while controlling without dropping.
DRL-KIDS-041.sf:
• Use balloons/foam balls to reduce fear.
• Keep parade lanes separated to avoid collisions.

DRL-KIDS-043.o: Build athletic ABCs and a tennis-ready stop position using color/line cues.
DRL-KIDS-043.su: Lay 4 colored cones in a square. Coach calls a color; athletes move to it and freeze in 'ready position' (athletic stance + racket out).
DRL-KIDS-043.ex:
1) Start with walk/jog to called cone and freeze.
2) Add tennis movement: side shuffle to right/left colors; forward sprint to front cone; backpedal to back cone.
2) Add ball: coach tosses a foam ball after freeze; athlete catches with two hands.
2) Add racket: after catch, do 1 controlled bounce on strings.
DRL-KIDS-043.cu:
• Low base, nose over toes.
• Freeze like a statue—quiet feet.
• Hands/racket in front.
• Eyes on coach cue then ball.
DRL-KIDS-043.ef:
• Standing tall → 'sit in chair' cue.
• Feet crossing when shuffling → demonstrate side shuffle.
• Sloppy freeze → use 2-second hold rule.
DRL-KIDS-043.rg:
• Reduce to 2 cones only.
• Remove ball toss—movement only.
DRL-KIDS-043.pg:
• Add reaction: coach fakes one color then switches.
• Add split-step before first move.
DRL-KIDS-043.sm:
• Can freeze in balanced ready position on command.
• Reaction speed improves (coach observation).
DRL-KIDS-043.sf:
• Clear movement lanes; no crossing paths.
• Keep stops controlled—no sliding on hard courts.
DRL-KIDS-043.tg: ABC, Agility, Balance, Coordination, Ready position

DRL-KIDS-045.o: Build racket-face awareness (open/closed) and balance with simple carry challenges.
DRL-KIDS-045.su: Mark a short course with cones. Each athlete has a racket and foam/red ball.
DRL-KIDS-045.ex:
1) Phase 1: Pancake carry—ball on strings, walk to cone and back.
2) Phase 2: Pizza carry—flip racket (backhand side) and carry again.
2) Phase 3: Pancake bounce—1 bounce on strings per step.
2) Phase 4: Add 'tennis stop' at each cone (split-step + ready).
DRL-KIDS-045.cu:
• Flat face; quiet steps.
• Hold racket out in front.
• Soft hands—don't squeeze.
• Eyes down to keep balance.
DRL-KIDS-045.ef:
• Ball falls often → slow down; shorten course.
• Grip too tight → 'baby bird hands' cue.
• Racket face tilting → 'table top' cue.
DRL-KIDS-045.rg:
• Allow ball to be trapped against throat of racket.
• Shorter distance.
DRL-KIDS-045.pg:
• Add obstacle: step over mini hurdles while carrying.
• Race relay (team) without dropping.
DRL-KIDS-045.sm:
• Distance completed without drops.
• Number of controlled bounces.
DRL-KIDS-045.sf:
• No running with rackets near others.
• Use soft balls; clear lanes.
DRL-KIDS-045.tg: Racket skills, Balance, Control, Grip comfort

DRL-KIDS-046.o: Teach the idea of 'ready feet' (split-step) using bubbles as a timing cue.
DRL-KIDS-046.su: Coach blows bubbles or tosses light objects; athletes start in a line with space between them.
DRL-KIDS-046.ex:
1) Coach calls 'Ready!' and athletes do a small split-step.
2) On 'Go!', athletes pop a bubble with racket tip (or hand for LTS).
2) Add movement: coach points left/right; athlete shuffle then split-step then pop.
2) Transfer: coach tosses foam ball; athlete split-steps then catches.
DRL-KIDS-046.cu:
• Little hop—land quiet.
• Racket out front.
• React after you land.
• Smile—make it playful.
DRL-KIDS-046.ef:
• Big jumps → cue 'tiny hop'.
• Swinging wildly → use racket tip only.
• Kids collide → give each a lane and pop only in lane.
DRL-KIDS-046.rg:
• Hands only (no racket).
• No movement; split-step in place.
DRL-KIDS-046.pg:
• Add 'two pops' then recover to home base spot.
• Add competition: most pops in 30 sec.
DRL-KIDS-046.sm:
• Split-step on cue rate (coach observation).
• Pops per 30 sec.
DRL-KIDS-046.sf:
• Maintain spacing; assign lanes.
• Avoid slippery bubble residue on court—use sparingly.

DRL-KIDS-048.o: Teach basic swing shapes (forehand/backhand) and balanced finish positions without ball pressure.
DRL-KIDS-048.su: Athletes spaced out. Coach demos one simple shape: unit turn → swing → finish → freeze.
DRL-KIDS-048.ex:
1) Call 'Forehand!' athletes do the motion and freeze 2 seconds.
2) Call 'Backhand!' repeat.
2) Add 'Ready!' athletes reset stance with split-step.
2) Optional: add foam ball drop-hit for Red (bounce, tap to partner).
DRL-KIDS-048.cu:
• Turn shoulders first.
• Finish to the target.
• Freeze balanced (no wobble).
• Reset fast to ready.
DRL-KIDS-048.ef:
• Arms only → cue 'shoulders turn'.
• Falling over → widen base; shorten swing.
• Copying too fast → slow demo; one rep at a time.
DRL-KIDS-048.rg:
• No freeze; just move through shape.
• Use only forehand shape.
DRL-KIDS-048.pg:
• Add 'move then swing': shuffle to cone then swing.
• Add 'finish over shoulder' for topspin feel (older Red).
DRL-KIDS-048.sm:
• Can hold balanced finish.
• Improved shape on next ball-fed reps.
DRL-KIDS-048.sf:
• Spacing: rackets shouldn't touch.
• Keep motions controlled (no full-speed swings near others).

DRL-KIDS-048-V1.o: Teach basic swing shapes (forehand/backhand) and balanced finish positions without ball pressure.
DRL-KIDS-048-V1.su: Athletes spaced out. Coach demos one simple shape: unit turn → swing → finish → freeze.

DRL-KIDS-048-V2.sf:

DRL-KIDS-049.o: Create cooperative rally habits with immediate positive reinforcement (high five).
DRL-KIDS-049.su: Mini-net set up. Partners 6–10 feet apart. Use foam/red balls.
DRL-KIDS-049.ex:
1) Start with catch-and-throw over mini-net for 5 reps.
2) Progress to bounce-catch: 1 bounce then catch and toss back.
2) Progress to racket tap over net (1 bounce allowed).
2) Rule: after every successful send/receive, both players high-five and reset.
DRL-KIDS-049.cu:
• Big target over middle of net.
• Soft touch; arc the ball.
• High five = reset routine.
• Stay positive after misses.
DRL-KIDS-049.ef:
• Hitting too hard → cue 'rainbow ball'.
• Standing still → add 'home base' spot to recover.
• Getting frustrated → emphasize streak goal and high fives.
DRL-KIDS-049.pg:
• Make target smaller (cones).
• Add scoring: first to 10 successful exchanges.
DRL-KIDS-049.sm:
• Max cooperative exchanges.
• Body language stays positive.
DRL-KIDS-049.sf:
• Use soft balls.
• Keep net area clear; no running through net.

DRL-KIDS-050.n: Coach Says: Tennis Edition (Listening + Movement + Cues)
DRL-KIDS-050.o: Build attention control and basic tennis commands through a 'Simon Says' style game.
DRL-KIDS-050.su: Coach is caller. Athletes in a line with spacing and rackets if appropriate.
DRL-KIDS-050.ex:
1) Coach calls commands: 'Ready position', 'Split-step', 'Forehand turn', 'Shuffle left', 'Touch net', 'Freeze'.
2) Only do command if coach says 'Coach Says'.
2) Add ball: on 'Coach Says Catch', coach tosses foam ball to athlete.
2) Rotate caller for older Red players.
DRL-KIDS-050.cu:
• Fast reset to ready.
• Quiet feet on split-step.
• Eyes on coach.
• Play fair—follow the rule.
DRL-KIDS-050.ef:
• Kids always move → slow down; demonstrate example.
• Crowding → assign lanes.
• Loss of focus → keep rounds short (30–45 sec).
DRL-KIDS-050.rg:
• Use fewer commands.
• No rackets; movement only.
DRL-KIDS-050.pg:
• Add combination commands: 'split-step then shuffle'.
• Add point for correct execution (team score).
DRL-KIDS-050.sf:
• Keep movements controlled; no sliding.
• Ensure space for shuffles/sprints.

DRL-KIDS-050-V2.o: Build attention control and basic tennis commands through a 'Simon Says' style game.
DRL-KIDS-050-V2.su: Coach is caller. Athletes in a line with spacing and rackets if appropriate.

DRL-KIDS-051.o: Increase rally reps using quick rotation while learning crosscourt direction.
DRL-KIDS-051.su: Use service boxes only. 4–8 players per court. Mark waiting spots with cones.
DRL-KIDS-051.ex:
1) Play cooperative crosscourt rally (deuce-to-deuce) to 6 exchanges.
2) On 6th exchange, rally becomes live point to win.
2) Winner stays; challenger rotates in from line.
2) Switch to ad side after 5 minutes.
DRL-KIDS-051.cu:
• Aim crosscourt to big target.
• Recover to middle of box.
• Soft hands—keep it in.
• Say 'mine' before contact.
DRL-KIDS-051.ef:
• Players hit down the line accidentally → put cone 'wall' on line; remind crosscourt lane.
• Too hard → enforce arc over net.
• Long lines → run two boxes simultaneously.
DRL-KIDS-051.rg:
• Start with catch-throw over mini-net.
• Allow two bounces for 60 seconds then one bounce.
DRL-KIDS-051.pg:
• Add scoring: win only if point ends with ball landing in deep half of box.
• Add 'change direction' bonus on 3rd ball.
DRL-KIDS-051.sm:
• Average exchanges before error.
• Participation rate (no long waits).
DRL-KIDS-051.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-052.n: Big Target Bingo (Hit Zones + Simple Scoring)
DRL-KIDS-052.o: Teach aiming to large safe zones with a bingo-style scorecard.
DRL-KIDS-052.su: Create 3 large zones with cones: Middle-Deep, Crosscourt-Deep, Short-Crosscourt. Give each player a simple bingo card (3x3) with zones.
DRL-KIDS-052.ex:
1) Coach feeds or partner tosses ball.
2) Player hits to called zone; if ball lands in zone, they mark a square.
2) First to complete a row = 'Bingo'.
2) Rotate feeders every 2 minutes.
DRL-KIDS-052.cu:
• Aim big; clear the net.
• Finish to the target.
• Call the zone before swinging.
• Celebrate the decision, not the outcome.
DRL-KIDS-052.ef:
• Too precise too early → make zones bigger.
• Players forget zones → keep cone colors consistent.
• Rushing → add 2-second set routine.
DRL-KIDS-052.rg:
• Only 1 zone (middle-deep).
• Underhand toss feed close range.
DRL-KIDS-052.pg:
• Shrink zones; add requirement: must use forehand/backhand alternation.
• Add movement: start at cone then recover.
DRL-KIDS-052.sm:
• Zones hit per 10 balls.
• Bingo completion time.
DRL-KIDS-052.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-053.o: Improve toss consistency and confidence with a simple bingo scoring system.
DRL-KIDS-053.su: Place a hoop or target on court at ideal toss location (slightly in front). Each player has 10 balls.
DRL-KIDS-053.ex:
1) Player performs toss and catches at full reach.
2) If toss lands in hoop zone (or within cone square), mark a bingo square.
2) After 5 tosses, switch to 'toss + gentle serve' (underhand or abbreviated overhand).
2) Rotate: coach checks grip and trophy position quickly.
DRL-KIDS-053.cu:
• Same routine every time.
• Reach high—catch at full extension.
• Toss in front of hitting shoulder.
• Relaxed arm.
DRL-KIDS-053.ef:
• Toss too high → 'shoulder height + reach' cue.
• Toss drifting → mark foot position; repeat from same spot.
• Overthinking → keep reps fast.
DRL-KIDS-053.rg:
• Toss and catch only (no serve).
• Bigger hoop/zone.
DRL-KIDS-053.pg:
• Serve to big target after each good toss.
• Add pressure: 3-in-a-row needed.
DRL-KIDS-053.sm:
• % tosses in target zone.
• Serve start success rate.
DRL-KIDS-053.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-056.o: Develop volley basics (ready position, short punch) using a clear progression.
DRL-KIDS-056.su: Players at net with partner/coach 8–12 feet away. Place 2 cone targets near service line.
DRL-KIDS-056.ex:
1) Phase 1: Catch the toss at arm’s length in front (volley position).
2) Phase 2: Catch then 'punch' the catch downward into target (teaches firm wrist).
2) Phase 3: Toss then volley with racket to target.
2) Play Volcano: each target hit = 1 lava rock; first to 8 wins.
DRL-KIDS-056.cu:
• Hands in front; no backswing.
• Step and punch.
• Firm wrist—racket face stable.
• Aim to target, not 'hard'.
DRL-KIDS-056.ef:
• Swinging volley → cue 'punch, not swing'.
• Contact too close to body → catch in front drill.
• Feet stuck → add step cue and pause.
DRL-KIDS-056.rg:
• Stay stationary; underhand toss slower.
• Bigger targets.
DRL-KIDS-056.pg:
• Add moving volleys (shuffle then volley).
• Add 'low volley' variation.
DRL-KIDS-056.sm:
• Targets hit per 10 volleys.
• Volley form consistency (coach).
DRL-KIDS-056.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-056-V1.su: Players at net with partner/coach 8–12 feet away. Place 2 cone targets near service line.

2) Phase 2: Catch then 'punch' the catch downward into target (teaches firm wrist).

2) Phase 3: Toss then volley with racket to target.
2) Play Volcano: each target hit = 1 lava rock; first to 8 wins.

DRL-KIDS-057.o: Teach moving forward on a short ball and finishing at the net.
DRL-KIDS-057.su: Coach feeds short ball to service line. Place a 'high-five cone' near net post.
DRL-KIDS-057.ex:
1) Player hits approach to big deep target, then runs to net.
2) Player performs split-step; coach tosses a volley which player punches to target.
2) Player high-fives cone and resets to baseline.
2) After 5 reps, rotate.
DRL-KIDS-057.cu:
• See short ball—go forward.
• Approach with margin (arc).
• Split-step before volley.
• Celebrate the finish—high five.
DRL-KIDS-057.ef:
• Approach too short → cue 'aim past service line'.
• No split-step → freeze rule: must split or redo rep.
• Overrunning net → mark volley contact spot.
DRL-KIDS-057.rg:
• Underhand feed; allow catch volley.
• Remove volley; just approach + run to net.
DRL-KIDS-057.pg:
• Make it competitive: time trial of 5 quality reps.
• Add second volley or overhead.
DRL-KIDS-057.sm:
• Approach depth quality.
• Volley target hits.
DRL-KIDS-057.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-057-V1.n: Approach & High-Five Finish (Move Forward + Net) — Simplified
DRL-KIDS-057-V1.o: Teach moving forward on a short ball and finishing at the net.
DRL-KIDS-057-V1.su: Coach feeds short ball to service line. Place a 'high-five cone' near net post.

DRL-KIDS-058.o: Build scoring comfort and a simple between-point reset habit.
DRL-KIDS-058.su: Play mini-games to 7 points. Add a 'scoreboard cone' where winner must touch cone and call the score aloud.
DRL-KIDS-058.ex:
1) Play point.
2) Winner touches scoreboard cone and says the score (e.g., '4–2 me').
2) Both players do a 1-breath reset and get ready for next point.
2) Rotate opponents every 7 minutes.
DRL-KIDS-058.cu:
• Say score confidently.
• One breath, eyes forward—next ball.
• Keep body language positive.
• Simple targets.
DRL-KIDS-058.ef:
• Players forget score → use visible flip cards.
• Rushing next point → enforce breath reset.
• Negative talk → replace with cue word ('next').
DRL-KIDS-058.rg:
• Shorter games to 5.
• Coach calls score for first 2 minutes.
DRL-KIDS-058.pg:
• Add pressure: at 6-6, play 'golden point'.
• Add serve-start points.
DRL-KIDS-058.sm:
• Reset compliance (coach observed).
• Ability to keep score.
DRL-KIDS-058.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-059.o: Improve crosscourt control with a channel and depth bonus scoring.
DRL-KIDS-059.su: Create a crosscourt channel using cones (3–6 feet wide). Mark deep bonus zone near baseline.
DRL-KIDS-059.ex:
1) Cooperative rally crosscourt through channel.
2) Score 1 point for ball in channel; 2 points if it also lands in deep zone.
2) First pair to 15 points wins.
2) Switch to other side of court halfway.
DRL-KIDS-059.cu:
• Aim big—inside the channel edges.
• Arc over the net.
• Recover to middle of box/court.
• Use legs for height.
DRL-KIDS-059.ef:
• Hitting wide → widen channel then shrink gradually.
• Too flat → cue 'rainbow'.
• Standing still → add 'hit then recover' rule.
DRL-KIDS-059.rg:
• Allow one extra bounce.
• Shorten distance (service line).
DRL-KIDS-059.pg:
• Make channel narrower; add live point after 6 exchanges.
• Add DTL change only on coach call.
DRL-KIDS-059.sm:
• Points per minute.
• Average exchanges before miss.
DRL-KIDS-059.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-061.o: Teach players to intentionally rally short or deep based on a called constraint.
DRL-KIDS-061.su: Mark two target lanes: Short lane (service boxes) and Deep lane (beyond service line). Players rally crosscourt.
DRL-KIDS-061.ex:
1) Round 1: Deep only (all balls must land deep lane).
2) Round 2: Short only (all balls must land short lane).
2) Round 3: Coach calls 'short' or 'deep' mid-rally; players must adjust next ball.
2) Finish: live points where winning requires 1 short ball then 1 deep ball (sequence).
DRL-KIDS-061.cu:
• See the target; change height not just power.
• Use legs for depth.
• Recover to home base.
• Make it obvious—don't be subtle.
DRL-KIDS-061.ef:
• Players can't keep it short → slow swing, open face slightly.
• Players can't keep it deep → aim higher, bigger target.
• Late adjustment on coach call → simplify: call earlier or give extra bounce.
DRL-KIDS-061.rg:
• Only deep lane first 5 minutes.
• Short lane is 'anywhere in service box'.
DRL-KIDS-061.pg:
• Make lanes narrower.
• Add 'change direction' after short ball.
DRL-KIDS-061.sm:
• % success in called lane.
• Decision speed on coach call.
DRL-KIDS-061.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-062.n: Serve +1 Big Target (First 3 Shots for Orange)
DRL-KIDS-062.o: Introduce serve +1 with big targets and simplified scoring.
DRL-KIDS-062.su: Mark 2 big targets: return target (deep middle) and +1 target (open court). Use underhand or abbreviated serve if needed.
DRL-KIDS-062.ex:
1) Player A serves to big target.
2) Player B returns to deep middle target.
2) Player A hits +1 to open-court target (crosscourt).
2) Stop point after +1: success if all 3 balls meet target; otherwise replay. Rotate server every 6 reps.
DRL-KIDS-062.cu:
• Routine before serve.
• Return deep middle.
• +1 with margin—no hero shots.
• Quick recover after serve.
DRL-KIDS-062.ef:
• Serve misses → allow two serves or underhand.
• Return floats short → start closer; emphasize split-step.
• +1 rushed → cue 'see it, then swing'.
DRL-KIDS-062.rg:
• Serve is underhand; return is catch-and-toss for first 2 minutes.
• Bigger targets.
DRL-KIDS-062.pg:
• Play full point after +1.
• Shrink targets; add pressure scoring.
DRL-KIDS-062.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-064.o: Teach run-around forehand positioning and inside-out direction with control.
DRL-KIDS-064.su: Place a cone 'run-around marker' on backhand corner. Feed balls to backhand side.
DRL-KIDS-064.ex:
1) Player starts at home base.
2) On feed to backhand corner, player moves around to hit forehand inside-out to crosscourt target.
2) Recover to home base; repeat 6 reps then switch.
2) Add live point: if player hits inside-out to target, point continues; if not, replay.
DRL-KIDS-064.cu:
• First step is explosive.
• Get outside the ball.
• Aim big crosscourt.
• Recover through the middle.
DRL-KIDS-064.ef:
• Late to ball → start closer; reduce feed speed.
• Over-rotation → cue 'finish to target'.
• Pulling ball wide → aim higher, more spin.
DRL-KIDS-064.rg:
• Feed slower; allow ball to bounce twice.
• Smaller movement: start closer to backhand corner.
DRL-KIDS-064.pg:
• Add inside-in option on coach call.
• Add pressure: 3-in-a-row needed to 'level up'.
DRL-KIDS-064.sm:
• Inside-out target hit %.
• Recovery time efficiency (coach).
DRL-KIDS-064.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-068.n: Tiebreak to 7 with One Rule (Pressure Intro)
DRL-KIDS-068.o: Introduce tiebreak scoring and pressure with a single simple constraint.
DRL-KIDS-068.su: Explain tiebreak serve order quickly (1 then 2). Choose one rule (e.g., returns must be deep).
DRL-KIDS-068.ex:
1) Play tiebreak to 7.
2) Constraint rule stays for entire tiebreak (e.g., return beyond service line).
2) Between points: 1-breath reset required.
2) After tiebreak, quick debrief: what rule helped?
DRL-KIDS-068.cu:
• Know the score.
• One breath reset.
• Big targets under pressure.
• Play the rule first, then play.
DRL-KIDS-068.ef:
• Players forget serve order → use visual card.
• Emotional swings → reinforce reset routine.
• Ignoring constraint → point loss.
DRL-KIDS-068.rg:
• Play to 5 instead of 7.
• No serves; coach feeds.
DRL-KIDS-068.pg:
• Add second constraint for advanced players.
• Start tiebreak down 0-3.
DRL-KIDS-068.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-069.n: Attack or Reset Decision Game (Short Ball Choice)
DRL-KIDS-069.o: Train a clear rule: attack the short ball with margin; reset on deep/fast balls.
DRL-KIDS-069.su: Mark 'attack zone' inside service line and 'reset target' deep middle. Coach feeds mixed depth balls.
DRL-KIDS-069.ex:
1) If feed lands in attack zone: player must step in and attack to big corner target.
2) If feed lands deep: player must hit high/deep to reset target.
2) Score 1 point for correct decision + ball in target; play to 15.
2) Finish with live points where coach calls 'attack' or 'reset' only on some balls.
DRL-KIDS-069.cu:
• See ball depth early.
• Step in for attack.
• High and deep to reset.
• Recover after decision.
DRL-KIDS-069.ef:
• Players attack deep balls → enforce rule strictly; point loss.
• Attacks too risky → bigger targets; net clearance goal.
• Resets too soft → aim higher/deeper; use legs.
DRL-KIDS-069.rg:
• Coach calls decision instead of zone rule.
• Bigger targets.
DRL-KIDS-069.pg:
• Add 'attack options': approach vs angle vs drive.
• Add pressure: must win by 2.
DRL-KIDS-069.sm:
• Correct decision %.
• Target hit % for attack vs reset.
DRL-KIDS-069.sf:
• Keep spacing between players (no long lines).
• Use scaled balls/rackets; prioritize confidence and fun.

DRL-KIDS-070.n: World Tour Points (Clay / Hard / Grass Constraints)
DRL-KIDS-070.o: Create variety and adaptability by playing points with 'surface-style' constraints.
DRL-KIDS-070.su: Set 3 rounds (5 minutes each): Clay, Hard, Grass. Write the rules on whiteboard.
DRL-KIDS-070.ex:
1) Round 1 (Clay): ball must clear net by 'window'; bonus for 10+ rally; no drop shots.
2) Round 2 (Hard): normal rules; bonus for first-strike (win within 4 shots).
2) Round 3 (Grass): slice/low ball bonus; bonus for serve+volley or approach finish.
2) Rotate opponents after each round.
DRL-KIDS-070.cu:
• Adapt—same fundamentals, different tactics.
• Clay: height + patience.
• Hard: tempo + first four.
• Grass: keep it low; move forward.
DRL-KIDS-070.ef:
• Players ignore rules → enforce point bonuses/penalties.
• Overcomplicating → keep to 1–2 rules per round.
• Unsafe sliding imitation → remind no sliding on hard courts.
DRL-KIDS-070.rg:
• Use only 2 rounds (Clay/Hard).
• Shorter scoring games to 5.
DRL-KIDS-070.pg:
• Add 'opponent style' rounds (pusher vs big hitter).
• Add tiebreak final.
DRL-KIDS-070.sm:
• Rally tolerance in clay round.
• First-four win rate in hard round.
• Net finishes in grass round.
DRL-KIDS-070.sf:
• No sliding on hard courts.
• Manage competitiveness—keep it playful and educational.

DRL-USTA-001.n: USTA Team Warm-Up Circuit (8 Minutes, Doubles Ready)
DRL-USTA-001.o: Standardize match warm-up so teams start sharp and safe.
DRL-USTA-001.su: 8-minute circuit: 2 min mini tennis, 2 min baseline crosscourt, 2 min volleys/overheads, 2 min serves/returns.
DRL-USTA-001.ex:
1) Run circuit on a timer.
2) Each segment has one cue (big targets, split-step, communication).
3) End with one 'first point' simulation (serve + 3 balls).
4) Players state Plan A before starting.
DRL-USTA-001.cu:
• 'Big targets early.'
• 'Talk on doubles balls.'
• 'Same routine every match.'
DRL-USTA-001.ef:
• Warm-up too intense → keep 70% effort first 4 mins.
• Players skip sections → timer + coach lead.
DRL-USTA-001.rg: Shorten to 5 minutes; remove overheads if unsafe.
DRL-USTA-001.pg: Add 1-minute pressure serve target at end; add return depth challenge.
DRL-USTA-001.sm: Better first 2 games performance; fewer early errors; higher readiness rating.
DRL-USTA-001.sf: Avoid max-intensity early; manage overhead volume; hydrate.
DRL-USTA-001.tg: USTA League, Warm-up, Doubles, Routine, Footwork

DRL-USTA-002.o: Build a repeatable between-game routine that improves composure and decision quality.
DRL-USTA-002.su: During practice sets, enforce a 90-second changeover routine using a script card.
DRL-USTA-002.ex:
1) After each odd game, sit.
2) 3 breaths.
3) State Plan A and one cue.
4) Quick hydration.
5) Stand at 20 seconds and rehearse next return/serve.
DRL-USTA-002.ef:
• Talking about last mistake → redirect to next plan.
• Rushing → coach enforces time.
DRL-USTA-002.pg: Add micro-goal tracking (serve target, return depth) each changeover.
DRL-USTA-002.sm: Higher reset compliance; improved late-set execution.
DRL-USTA-002.sf: Keep it positive; manage heat; ensure hydration.
DRL-USTA-002.tg: USTA League, Reset, Mental, Routine, Pressure

DRL-USTA-002-V1.o: Make super tiebreaks feel normal by repeating them with coaching constraints and stat tags.
DRL-USTA-002-V1.su: Pairs play super tiebreaks to 10. Use correct serve order. Coach tracks mini-breaks and UFEs.
DRL-USTA-002-V1.ex:
1) Play super tiebreak to 10 (win-by-2).
2) Each team chooses 1 'anchor play' (pattern) before starting (e.g., return deep middle).
2) At changeovers (every 6 points), quick 15-sec reset and reminder of anchor play.
2) Repeat 2–3 super tiebreaks, rotating partners.
DRL-USTA-002-V1.cu:
• Know the score and server.
• Anchor play first.
• Breathe and slow heart rate.
• Body language discipline.
DRL-USTA-002-V1.ef:
• Players forget serve order → use a serve-order card on fence.
• Going for too much → enforce big targets.
• Tilt after mini-break → reset routine.
DRL-USTA-002-V1.rg:
• Play to 7.
• Coach feeds instead of serve.
DRL-USTA-002-V1.pg:
• Start down 0-4.
• Add 'one serve' rule to increase pressure.
DRL-USTA-002-V1.sm:
• Mini-break conversion rate.
• UFE per 10 points.
DRL-USTA-002-V1.sf:
• Explain rules clearly to avoid disputes.
• Manage intensity for mixed-level adult groups.
DRL-USTA-002-V1.tg: USTA League, Team Tennis, Tiebreak, Clutch, Momentum

DRL-USTA-002-V2.o: Make super tiebreaks feel normal by repeating them with coaching constraints and stat tags.
DRL-USTA-002-V2.su: Pairs play super tiebreaks to 10. Use correct serve order. Coach tracks mini-breaks and UFEs.
DRL-USTA-002-V2.ex:
1) Play super tiebreak to 10 (win-by-2).
2) Each team chooses 1 'anchor play' (pattern) before starting (e.g., return deep middle).
2) At changeovers (every 6 points), quick 15-sec reset and reminder of anchor play.
2) Repeat 2–3 super tiebreaks, rotating partners.
DRL-USTA-002-V2.cu:
• Know the score and server.
• Anchor play first.
• Breathe and slow heart rate.
• Body language discipline.
DRL-USTA-002-V2.ef:
• Players forget serve order → use a serve-order card on fence.
• Going for too much → enforce big targets.
• Tilt after mini-break → reset routine.
DRL-USTA-002-V2.rg:
• Play to 7.
• Coach feeds instead of serve.
DRL-USTA-002-V2.pg:
• Start down 0-4.
• Add 'one serve' rule to increase pressure.
DRL-USTA-002-V2.sm:
• Mini-break conversion rate.
• UFE per 10 points.
DRL-USTA-002-V2.sf:
• Explain rules clearly to avoid disputes.
• Manage intensity for mixed-level adult groups.
DRL-USTA-002-V2.tg: USTA League, Team Tennis, Tiebreak, Clutch, Momentum

DRL-USTA-003.o: Remove confusion and maximize advantage in doubles tiebreak serve order.
DRL-USTA-003.su: Set up doubles tiebreak scenario. Pre-decide serve order and receiving positions.
DRL-USTA-003.ex:
1) Start at 0-0 tiebreak.
2) Run full serve order: 1 serve, then 2 each.
3) Before each serve, server calls target and pattern.
4) Track first-serve-in and return depth.
DRL-USTA-003.ef:
• Confusion → stop and rehearse order without ball.
• Poor positioning → mark starting spots with cones.
DRL-USTA-003.pg: Add pressure: start down 0-3; add no-ad tiebreak variant.
DRL-USTA-003.sm: Faster decision-making; fewer 'who serves?' moments; better early tiebreak points.
DRL-USTA-003.tg: USTA League, Doubles, Pressure, Routine, Scoring

DRL-USTA-004.o: Standardize doubles signals to reduce hesitation and improve net pressure.
DRL-USTA-004.su: Net player uses 3-call system: Poach, Stay, Fake. Practice with cooperative serves.
DRL-USTA-004.ex:
1) Server/Net decide signal.
2) Serve to big target.
3) Returner blocks return crosscourt.
4) Net executes signal movement.
5) Stop after 4 shots; reset quickly.
DRL-USTA-004.ef:
• Late signals → set rule: signal before bounce.
• Net player freezes → reduce speed and rehearse without ball.
DRL-USTA-004.rg: No movement; just call signals and shadow step.
DRL-USTA-004.pg: Add live point; add Australian/I formations.
DRL-USTA-004.sm: Improved poach timing and partner trust.
DRL-USTA-004.sf: Avoid collisions; define lanes and responsibilities.
DRL-USTA-004.tg: USTA League, Doubles, Signals, Communication, Poach

DRL-USTA-005.o: Teach I-formation basics for USTA teams: spacing, signals, and first 3 shots.
DRL-USTA-005.su: Server + net player in I. Returner blocks return crosscourt. Use cones for safe lanes.
DRL-USTA-005.ex:
1) Rehearse positions and movement without ball.
2) Run serve + return + 1 + 1.
3) Stop and debrief after 4 shots.
4) Switch sides every 6 reps.
DRL-USTA-005.ef:
• Wrong coverage → simplify: poach only; no fakes.
• Unsafe movement → increase spacing; slow pace.
DRL-USTA-005.rg: Use standard formation only; no I until comfortable.
DRL-USTA-005.pg: Add live points; add receiver changing return direction.
DRL-USTA-005.sm: Players execute formation with clarity and less hesitation.
DRL-USTA-005.sf: Safety first: spacing, controlled pace; avoid collisions at net.
DRL-USTA-005.tg: USTA League, Doubles, Formations, Tactics, Communication

DRL-USTA-006.o: Train serve placement decision under realistic scoreboard pressure.
DRL-USTA-006.su: Server plays games starting at 30-30 and 40-AD scenarios. Must call location before serving.
DRL-USTA-006.ex:
1) Start each point at a score (coach calls).
2) Server calls location and pattern.
3) Play point.
4) Track: called serve hit? first serve in? outcome.
DRL-USTA-006.ef:
• Missing targets → enlarge target; reduce pace.
• Overthinking → simplify to 2 locations.
DRL-USTA-006.rg: Call location but no target bonus.
DRL-USTA-006.pg: Add consequence for missed call; add second serve placement goals.
DRL-USTA-006.sm: Serve target hit rate improves; better clutch execution.
DRL-USTA-006.tg: USTA League, Serve, Targets, Pressure, Routine

DRL-USTA-007.n: Return Games: Deep Middle Only (3-Point Mini Set)
DRL-USTA-007.o: Reinforce the highest percentage return for most levels: deep middle.
DRL-USTA-007.su: Play mini sets to 3 points. Returner only scores if return lands beyond service line in middle third.
DRL-USTA-007.ex:
1) Serve point.
2) Returner aims deep middle.
3) If return meets rule and point won = 2 points; if return meets rule and point lost = 1 point.
4) If return misses rule = 0 points.
DRL-USTA-007.ef:
• Returner over-swings → cue 'block'.
• Serve too hard → reduce serve pace.
DRL-USTA-007.pg: Add 'return +1' rule to lock in pattern.
DRL-USTA-007.sm: Return depth consistency improves; fewer early-point errors.
DRL-USTA-007.tg: USTA League, Return, Depth, Margin, Pressure

DRL-USTA-008.o: Practice doubles crosscourt stability then turn it into a live point.
DRL-USTA-008.su: Start with crosscourt 'crossfire' rally (ad vs ad, deuce vs deuce). After 6 balls, ball is live.
DRL-USTA-008.ex:
1) Rally crosscourt 6 balls.
2) On coach call 'live', all court opens.
3) Play point out.
4) Rotate teams every 10 minutes.
DRL-USTA-008.ef:
• Too many early errors → reduce required balls to 4.
• Players forget live call → use loud cue and hand signal.
DRL-USTA-008.rg: Make it live immediately (no 6-ball requirement) for beginners.
DRL-USTA-008.pg: Add poach triggers; add serve start.
DRL-USTA-008.sm: Higher crosscourt rally tolerance and better transition into point play.
DRL-USTA-008.sf: Avoid racket clashes; define priority on middle balls.
DRL-USTA-008.tg: USTA League, Doubles, Crossfire, Transition, Points

DRL-USTA-009.n: Middle Squeeze Game (Net Players Own the Middle)
DRL-USTA-009.o: Teach net positioning: squeeze middle and force opponents wide.
DRL-USTA-009.su: Doubles. Net players must keep feet inside singles lines when ball is neutral.
DRL-USTA-009.ex:
1) Start point with serve or feed.
2) Net players 'squeeze' middle on neutral balls.
3) If net player drifts wide on neutral, point loss.
4) Play to 11.
DRL-USTA-009.ef:
• Net players feel exposed → slow pace; use bigger targets.
• Confusion on neutral → define neutral ball as any ball above net height and in middle third.
DRL-USTA-009.rg: Remove rule; just coach cues positioning.
DRL-USTA-009.pg: Add lob-defense rotation; add poach bonuses.
DRL-USTA-009.sm: Improved net positioning discipline; more forced errors created.
DRL-USTA-009.sf: Spacing awareness to avoid collisions; communicate.
DRL-USTA-009.tg: USTA League, Doubles, Positioning, Teamwork, Pressure

DRL-USTA-010.o: Train appropriate decisions on second serves: attack when green, neutralize when yellow/red.
DRL-USTA-010.su: Server hits second serves only. Returner must call 'attack' or 'neutral' before returning.
DRL-USTA-010.ex:
1) Server delivers second serve.
2) Returner calls decision.
3) If 'attack' → aim to aggressive target; if 'neutral' → deep middle.
4) Stop after 4 shots; reset quickly.
DRL-USTA-010.ef:
• Returner auto-attacks → require decision call.
• Too risky attacks → enlarge target and add height requirement.
DRL-USTA-010.pg: Play full points; add scoreboard pressure at 30-40.
DRL-USTA-010.sm: Better shot selection and fewer return UFEs on second serves.
DRL-USTA-010.sf: Manage intensity and serve pace; ensure adequate warm-up.
DRL-USTA-010.tg: USTA League, Serve, Return, Decision-making, Aggression

DRL-LTS-001.o: Build tracking + contact confidence with ultra-slow 'rally' using a balloon.
DRL-LTS-001.su: Each player has a balloon (or one per pair). Define a 'home base' spot (cone).
DRL-LTS-001.ex:
1) Players tap balloon upward with hand or racket.
2) After each tap, return feet to home base.
3) Coach calls 'freeze' and checks ready position.
4) Optional: partner keep-up (alternate taps).
DRL-LTS-001.cu:
• 'Eyes on the balloon.'
• 'Soft hands / soft racket.'
• 'Return to home base.'
DRL-LTS-001.ef:
• Swinging too hard → switch to hand taps; use 'feather touch' cue.
• Chasing wildly → add home base cone + 'tap then return'.
DRL-LTS-001.rg: Use bigger balloon; allow 2 taps in a row; remove home-base rule.
DRL-LTS-001.pg: Add mini-net and require balloon crosses 'river'; add alternating forehand/backhand taps.
DRL-LTS-001.sm: Players complete 10 taps without balloon dropping; smiles/engagement stays high.
DRL-LTS-001.sf: Clear space; balloons can pop—keep extras; avoid face collisions in groups.

DRL-LTS-002.o: Introduce swing shapes and ready position through bubble popping.
DRL-LTS-002.su: Coach blows bubbles across mini court. Players start in athletic stance with racket.
DRL-LTS-002.ex:
1) Coach says 'ready' then blows bubbles.
2) Players 'pop' bubbles using short swings.
3) Alternate forehand side then backhand side.
4) Finish with 10-second 'quiet feet' freeze.
DRL-LTS-002.cu:
• 'Short swing.'
• 'Point your strings at the bubble.'
• 'Freeze like a statue.'
DRL-LTS-002.ef:
• Big wild swings → bring players closer; cue 'paintbrush'.
• Forgetting ready position → reset with 'racket in front'.
DRL-LTS-002.rg: Let players pop bubbles with hands first; reduce to forehand only.
DRL-LTS-002.pg: Add target cones: pop bubbles while standing behind cone; add 1-step movement to bubble.
DRL-LTS-002.sm: Players can pop 5 bubbles in a row with controlled swing; maintains stance on cue.
DRL-LTS-002.sf: Keep distance between kids; avoid swinging close to others.
DRL-LTS-002.tg: Contact, Racket skills, Fun, Technique, Warm-up

DRL-LTS-003.o: Teach stop–start control and attention with tennis-ready movement cues.
DRL-LTS-003.su: Cones create a lane. Each player has ball on racket or in hand.
DRL-LTS-003.ex:
1) Green = jog forward.
2) Yellow = side shuffle.
3) Red = stop + balance on one foot (2 seconds).
4) On every 'red', perform 3 controlled ball taps on racket (or bounce-catch).
DRL-LTS-003.cu:
• 'Green go, yellow slide, red freeze.'
• 'Quiet feet on red.'
DRL-LTS-003.ef:
• Falling over on balance → shorten balance time; use two-foot freeze.
• Dropping ball → switch to hand carry then progress.
DRL-LTS-003.rg: No racket/ball during movement; focus only on footwork cues.
DRL-LTS-003.pg: Add 'blue' = backpedal; add quick split-step on each cue change.
DRL-LTS-003.sm: Players respond correctly to 8/10 cues; improved stop–start control.

DRL-LTS-003-V1.n: Traffic Light Footwork + Racket Tap — Simplified
DRL-LTS-003-V1.o: Teach stop–start control and attention with tennis-ready movement cues. (simplified)
DRL-LTS-003-V1.su: Cones create a lane. Each player has ball on racket or in hand.
DRL-LTS-003-V1.ex:
1) Green = jog forward.
2) Yellow = side shuffle.
3) Red = stop + balance on one foot (2 seconds).
4) On every 'red', perform 3 controlled ball taps on racket (or bounce-catch).

Simplify: Shorten distance; allow catch-and-throw or hand taps; reduce targets.
DRL-LTS-003-V1.cu:
• 'Green go, yellow slide, red freeze.'
• 'Quiet feet on red.'
DRL-LTS-003-V1.ef:
• Falling over on balance → shorten balance time; use two-foot freeze.
• Dropping ball → switch to hand carry then progress.
DRL-LTS-003-V1.rg: Use hands first; reduce moving parts; make success easy (70%+).
DRL-LTS-003-V1.pg: Add 'blue' = backpedal; add quick split-step on each cue change.
DRL-LTS-003-V1.sm: Players respond correctly to 8/10 cues; improved stop–start control.

DRL-LTS-004.o: Develop directional control (push/roll) and aiming to big targets.
DRL-LTS-004.su: Set 3–5 'treasure' cones/targets. Players roll foam/red balls from a line.
DRL-LTS-004.ex:
1) Each player gets 3 balls.
2) Roll ball to knock down treasure cone.
3) Score 1 point per hit.
4) Rotate to new distance or angle after each round.
DRL-LTS-004.cu:
• 'Point your belly button to target.'
• 'Smooth roll.'
• 'Aim big.'
DRL-LTS-004.ef:
• Rolling too hard → use 'gentle roll' cue; bring targets closer.
• Not aiming → use a big target zone (hula hoop).
DRL-LTS-004.rg: Use larger target; allow underhand toss instead of roll.
DRL-LTS-004.pg: Progress to racket push (forehand push) instead of roll; move targets to corners.
DRL-LTS-004.sm: Hits increase over rounds; kids can name target and aim intentionally.
DRL-LTS-004.sf: Use foam balls; keep kids behind line until coach says go.
DRL-LTS-004.tg: Direction, Targets, Fun, Game, Court Awareness

DRL-LTS-005.o: Improve balance and racket control carrying a ball safely.
DRL-LTS-005.su: Set a simple obstacle path (cones, lines). Players carry ball on racket (strings up).
DRL-LTS-005.ex:
1) Walk the path without dropping ball.
2) If ball falls, stop, reset ball, continue.
3) Add 'freeze' moments for posture checks.
4) Make it a story: 'cross the bridge' (line), 'go around mountain' (cone).
DRL-LTS-005.ef:
• Racket tilting → cue 'level tray'.
• Running → set 'walking only' rule.
DRL-LTS-005.rg: Carry ball in hand first; shorten path.
DRL-LTS-005.pg: Add backward walk; add gentle taps every 3 steps.
DRL-LTS-005.sm: Players complete path with ≤2 drops; posture and focus improve.
DRL-LTS-005.sf: Keep obstacles safe; no jumping with racket held high.
DRL-LTS-005.tg: Balance, Racket skills, Warm-up, Coordination, Fun

DRL-LTS-006.o: Blend jumping/landing mechanics with simple racket taps for hand-eye.
DRL-LTS-006.su: Place hoops as 'islands' in a line. Player holds racket and ball.
DRL-LTS-006.ex:
1) Jump two-footed from hoop to hoop.
2) After landing, perform 2 controlled taps on racket.
3) Continue through course then high-five coach.
DRL-LTS-006.cu:
• 'Land like a ninja.'
• 'Soft knees.'
• 'Two taps then go.'
DRL-LTS-006.ef:
• Losing balance → reduce jump distance; allow step instead.
• Dropping ball on taps → allow hand taps.
DRL-LTS-006.pg: Add single-leg landings; add forehand/backhand alternation on taps.
DRL-LTS-006.sm: Safe landings + controlled taps; consistent rhythm.
DRL-LTS-006.sf: Check hoops don't slip; supervise spacing.
DRL-LTS-006.tg: ABC, Coordination, Warm-up, Racket skills, Fun

DRL-LTS-007.o: Train quick attention shifts and catching skill (pre-tennis tracking).
DRL-LTS-007.su: 4 colored cones around player. Coach with ball/foam ball.
DRL-LTS-007.ex:
1) Coach calls a color.
2) Player touches that cone.
3) On return, coach tosses ball for catch.
4) Reset to ready position.
DRL-LTS-007.ef:
• Late reaction → start with 2 colors.
• Dropped catches → toss slower and closer.
DRL-LTS-007.rg: Walk to cone; roll ball instead of toss.
DRL-LTS-007.pg: Add racket catch (catch ball on strings); add quick split-step before move.

DRL-LTS-008.n: Rainbow Toss to Hoop (Overhand Pattern Intro)
DRL-LTS-008.o: Introduce overhand throw and 'toss to target' concepts for future serving.
DRL-LTS-008.su: Hoops or buckets as targets. Players hold foam/red ball.
DRL-LTS-008.ex:
1) Show 'rainbow' arc toss.
2) Toss ball into hoop.
3) Celebrate 'swish' points.
4) Alternate arms lightly (build symmetry).
DRL-LTS-008.ef:
• Flat throws → move closer; use 'over the mountain' cue.
• Wild aim → make hoop bigger; reduce distance.
DRL-LTS-008.pg: Add step-in throw; add 'toss then catch' for rhythm; add target zones (near/far hoop).
DRL-LTS-008.sm: More balls land in hoop; improved throw shape and confidence.
DRL-LTS-008.sf: Use soft balls; keep kids behind line.

DRL-LTS-009.o: Create early 'send/receive' skill across a mini-net with success.
DRL-LTS-009.su: Mini-net or rope line. Big target zones marked with cones on both sides.
DRL-LTS-009.ex:
1) Start with throw-catch across.
2) Progress to drop-hit (coach holds ball, player taps over).
3) Goal: land ball in big target zone.
4) Keep score cooperatively: 'team gets 10 crosses'.
DRL-LTS-009.ef:
• Hitting too hard → move closer; use foam balls.
• Missing net → raise rope slightly or allow throw first.
DRL-LTS-009.pg: Add forehand/backhand choice; add 1-step move before hit.
DRL-LTS-009.sm: Team achieves 10 successful crosses; rally length increases.
DRL-LTS-009.sf: Spacing across net; no running into net.
DRL-LTS-009.tg: Rally, Cooperation, Big Targets, Fun, Court Awareness

DRL-LTS-010.o: Build social confidence and rally rhythm through paired success.
DRL-LTS-010.su: Pairs on mini court with foam/red ball. Can use throw-catch or tap-bounce.
DRL-LTS-010.ex:
1) Partner A sends ball (throw or tap).
2) Partner B receives (catch or tap).
3) After each successful exchange, quick high-five.
4) Count to 10 as a team.
DRL-LTS-010.ef:
• Too much celebration breaks flow → 'high five then ready' cue.
• Missed catches → use bigger/softer ball.
DRL-LTS-010.sm: Team reaches 10 exchanges; positive communication observed.
DRL-LTS-010.sf: Keep pairs spaced; avoid swinging near partner.

DRL-LTS-012.o: Improve aiming and directional send using 'bowling' concept.
DRL-LTS-012.su: Set 5–8 cones as bowling pins. Players roll or push ball with racket to knock pins.
DRL-LTS-012.ex:
1) Each player rolls/pushes 2 balls.
2) Count pins knocked.
3) Reset quickly; rotate roles (setter/roller).
DRL-LTS-012.ef:
• Too hard → shorten distance; use foam balls.
• Not aiming → give one 'goal pin' to focus.
DRL-LTS-012.pg: Use racket push; add 'must hit at least 1 pin before moving up a level'.
DRL-LTS-012.sm: Pins knocked per round increases; players identify aim point.
DRL-LTS-012.sf: Keep kids behind line; watch for running into pins.
DRL-LTS-012.tg: Targets, Direction, Game, Fun, Court Awareness

DRL-LTS-013.o: Introduce soft contact and 'touch' by landing ball near a teddy/target.
DRL-LTS-013.su: Place a plush toy/marker in a target square (safe distance). Mini-net optional.
DRL-LTS-013.ex:
1) Coach drop-feeds.
2) Player taps softly so ball lands in target square.
3) Score: 1 point for landing in square, 2 points if it stops near teddy.
DRL-LTS-013.ef:
• Hitting too hard → move closer and use foam ball.
• Missing target → make square bigger.
DRL-LTS-013.rg: Let ball bounce twice; allow hand roll.
DRL-LTS-013.pg: Add backhand touch; add move-in step; add mini-net crossing then stop.
DRL-LTS-013.sm: More balls land in target; improved soft contact.
DRL-LTS-013.sf: Ensure toy is off primary running lane; use safe marker if needed.
DRL-LTS-013.tg: Touch, Feel, Fun, Racket skills, Mini tennis

DRL-LTS-015.o: Keep attention high while getting lots of reps through story-based constraints.
DRL-LTS-015.su: Mini court. Coach narrates: 'jungle' (shuffle), 'river' (jump line), 'castle' (hit to target).
DRL-LTS-015.ex:
1) Coach gives a story cue.
2) Players perform movement action.
3) Then they complete a simple hit/throw to target.
4) Repeat quickly with new cue every 20–30 seconds.
DRL-LTS-015.ef:
• Over-excitement → shorten story; add 'freeze' between cues.
• Confusion → use 2 repeating cues.
DRL-LTS-015.pg: Add partner send/receive after each cue; add scoring for teamwork points.
DRL-LTS-015.sm: Players stay engaged for full block; improved listening and quick transitions.
DRL-LTS-015.sf: Keep rules simple; ensure safe spacing.

DRL-ABEG-001.o: Build reliable crosscourt rally tolerance with safe height/depth targets.
DRL-ABEG-001.su: Pairs rally crosscourt (ad-to-ad or deuce-to-deuce). Place two big target zones deep (beyond service line).
DRL-ABEG-001.ex:
1) Start cooperative: goal = 10-ball rally to big targets.
2) If ball lands short or wide, reset count.
3) After a 10-ball rally, switch to other side (forehand/backhand).
4) Finish: first pair to 3 ladders wins.
DRL-ABEG-001.cu:
• 'High over the net.'
• 'Aim deep-middle.'
• 'Recover to ready after contact.'
DRL-ABEG-001.ef:
• Ball sailing long → cue higher spin + bigger margin; move target shorter.
• Net errors → increase net clearance goal ("3 feet over").
DRL-ABEG-001.rg: Shorten to service boxes; allow drop-hit start; reduce target distance.
DRL-ABEG-001.pg: Add '2-good-balls then change direction' rule; add movement (hit then shuffle to cone).
DRL-ABEG-001.sm: Average rally length increases; fewer UFEs per 10 points.
DRL-ABEG-001.sf: Monitor overuse: keep intensity moderate; rotate sides to avoid repetitive stress.
DRL-ABEG-001.tg: Adult, Rally, Consistency, Big Targets, Cooperation

DRL-ABEG-002.n: Serve Routine + Big Target Game (10 Serves Each)
DRL-ABEG-002.o: Develop a consistent serve routine and improve first-serve-in percentage to big targets.
DRL-ABEG-002.su: Set 2 large target zones (deuce wide/body or ad wide/body). Players serve with a simple pre-serve routine.
DRL-ABEG-002.ex:
1) Each player serves 10 balls.
2) Score 1 point for landing in correct service box, +1 bonus for hitting target zone.
3) Between serves, perform full routine (breath, look target, cue word).
4) Switch sides and repeat.
DRL-ABEG-002.cu:
• 'Same routine every time.'
• 'Toss in front.'
• 'Smooth rhythm.'
DRL-ABEG-002.ef:
• Toss inconsistent → do 5 toss-only reps.
• Double faults → reduce pace; focus on height + spin.
DRL-ABEG-002.rg: Start from service line; allow underhand serve; bigger targets.
DRL-ABEG-002.pg: Add pressure: must make 2 in a row; add 'second serve' shape goal (slice/kick feel).
DRL-ABEG-002.sm: First serve in % improves; routine compliance increases.
DRL-ABEG-002.sf: Limit serve volume for shoulder; alternate servers; include band warm-up.

DRL-ABEG-003.n: Return Block to Deep Zone (Neutralize First)
DRL-ABEG-003.o: Teach a simple, repeatable return: block deep through the middle to neutralize.
DRL-ABEG-003.su: Coach or partner serves moderate pace. Mark a deep target beyond service line in middle third.
DRL-ABEG-003.ex:
1) Returner starts in ready position and split-steps on toss.
2) Goal: block return deep to middle target.
3) Server plays next ball cooperatively (rally 4 balls) then stop.
4) Rotate roles every 8 returns.
DRL-ABEG-003.cu:
• 'Split on contact.'
• 'Short backswing.'
• 'Deep through the middle.'
DRL-ABEG-003.ef:
• Over-swinging → cue 'catch the serve' with strings.
• Returns short → aim higher and longer; move target closer first.
DRL-ABEG-003.rg: Start with hand-fed serves or coach feeds from service line.
DRL-ABEG-003.pg: Add 'return +1' (return then next ball to big crosscourt); add serve placement variety.
DRL-ABEG-003.sm: Return depth % beyond service line increases.
DRL-ABEG-003.sf: Use moderate serve pace; avoid high-risk returns early; manage wrists/elbows.
DRL-ABEG-003.tg: Adult, Return, Depth, Big Targets, Decision-making

DRL-ABEG-004.n: Mini Tennis to Full Court Progression (3 Zones)
DRL-ABEG-004.o: Bridge skill from mini tennis to baseline rally using zone progressions.
DRL-ABEG-004.su: Set 3 zones: service boxes (Zone 1), inside baseline (Zone 2), baseline (Zone 3).
DRL-ABEG-004.ex:
1) Start rally in Zone 1 (mini tennis) to 10.
2) If success, move to Zone 2 and rally to 10.
3) If success, move to Zone 3 and rally to 10.
4) If failure, drop back one zone and rebuild.
DRL-ABEG-004.ef:
• Players rush zones → enforce success gate.
• Ball too flat → cue 'rainbow' over net.
DRL-ABEG-004.rg: Stay in Zone 1 only; allow 2 bounces.
DRL-ABEG-004.pg: Add directional requirement (crosscourt only); add point start after Zone 3 success.
DRL-ABEG-004.sm: Players reach Zone 3 more often over weeks; rally tolerance improves.
DRL-ABEG-004.sf: Watch knees/ankles when moving zones; keep rest breaks.

DRL-ABEG-005.o: Stabilize forehand contact point and swing path using a simple constraint.
DRL-ABEG-005.su: Place two cones forming a 'contact gate' slightly in front of lead foot. Coach drop-feeds or hand-feeds.
DRL-ABEG-005.ex:
1) Player must contact ball in front of body through the cone gate.
2) Finish over shoulder (or across) with balance.
3) Hit 8 balls then rotate.
4) Add target: deep middle beyond service line.
DRL-ABEG-005.cu:
• 'Meet it in front.'
• 'Finish tall.'
• 'Balance on last step.'
DRL-ABEG-005.ef:
• Late contact → earlier unit turn; smaller backswing.
• Off-balance → slower feed; split-step timing.
DRL-ABEG-005.rg: Shorter distance; catch-then-hit; no gate first.
DRL-ABEG-005.pg: Add movement: 1 shuffle step before hit; add crosscourt target.
DRL-ABEG-005.sm: More contacts through gate; improved directional control.
DRL-ABEG-005.sf: Feed at safe pace; keep players out of feeding lane.

DRL-ABEG-006.o: Create a safe backhand that clears the net with margin and lands deep.
DRL-ABEG-006.su: Mark a 'net clearance rope' (imaginary) and a deep target zone. Coach feeds to backhand.
DRL-ABEG-006.ex:
1) Player hits backhand with goal: clear net by ~3 feet.
2) Land in deep zone.
3) Score 1 point for clearance + depth.
4) First to 10 points wins.
DRL-ABEG-006.ef:
• Ball into net → cue higher finish; slower feed.
• Ball long → add more spin; shorten target.
DRL-ABEG-006.rg: Use slice block backhand; shorten court.
DRL-ABEG-006.pg: Add alternating crosscourt/DTL targets; add live rally after 3 feeds.
DRL-ABEG-006.sm: Backhand net clearance improves; fewer net errors.
DRL-ABEG-006.sf: Mind elbow/wrist; keep volume moderate; include breaks.

DRL-ABEG-007.o: Build volley confidence and firm contact using a clear progression.
DRL-ABEG-007.su: Players at net. Coach feeds gently from service line. Two big target cones on opposite service boxes.
DRL-ABEG-007.ex:
1) Start: catch the ball with racket face ("pillow catch").
2) Progress: short punch volley to target.
3) Score: 1 point per target hit.
4) Rotate after 8 feeds.
DRL-ABEG-007.ef:
• Swinging volleys → cue 'block not swing'.
• Racket drops → start closer; reset ready position.
DRL-ABEG-007.rg: Only catch with strings; no target.
DRL-ABEG-007.pg: Add split-step before feed; add alternating forehand/backhand volleys.
DRL-ABEG-007.sm: Target hit rate increases; fewer mishits.
DRL-ABEG-007.sf: Protect faces: controlled feeds only; keep spacing.
DRL-ABEG-007.tg: Adult, Volley, Net play, Hand-eye, Targets

DRL-ABEG-007-V1.n: Volley Catch-to-Punch Progression (2-Target Net Game) — V1 Simplified
DRL-ABEG-007-V1.o: Build volley confidence and firm contact using a clear progression. (simplified)
DRL-ABEG-007-V1.su: Players at net. Coach feeds gently from service line. Two big target cones on opposite service boxes.
DRL-ABEG-007-V1.ex:
1) Start: catch the ball with racket face ("pillow catch").
2) Progress: short punch volley to target.
3) Score: 1 point per target hit.
4) Rotate after 8 feeds.

Simplify: Allow 2 bounces; shorten court to service boxes; use bigger targets; cooperative scoring.
DRL-ABEG-007-V1.ef:
• Swinging volleys → cue 'block not swing'.
• Racket drops → start closer; reset ready position.
DRL-ABEG-007-V1.rg: Only catch with strings; no target.
DRL-ABEG-007-V1.pg: Add split-step before feed; add alternating forehand/backhand volleys.
DRL-ABEG-007-V1.sm: Target hit rate increases; fewer mishits.
DRL-ABEG-007-V1.sf: Protect faces: controlled feeds only; keep spacing.
DRL-ABEG-007-V1.tg: Adult, Volley, Net play, Hand-eye, Targets

DRL-ABEG-007-V2.n: Volley Catch-to-Punch Progression (2-Target Net Game) — V2 Advanced
DRL-ABEG-007-V2.o: Build volley confidence and firm contact using a clear progression. (advanced)
DRL-ABEG-007-V2.su: Players at net. Coach feeds gently from service line. Two big target cones on opposite service boxes.
DRL-ABEG-007-V2.ex:
1) Start: catch the ball with racket face ("pillow catch").
2) Progress: short punch volley to target.
3) Score: 1 point per target hit.
4) Rotate after 8 feeds.

Advance: Add score pressure (win-by-2, time clock); require directional targets; add consequence for UFEs.
DRL-ABEG-007-V2.ef:
• Swinging volleys → cue 'block not swing'.
• Racket drops → start closer; reset ready position.
DRL-ABEG-007-V2.rg: Only catch with strings; no target.
DRL-ABEG-007-V2.pg: Add pressure scoring; add serve start; add 'must hit target before point counts'.
DRL-ABEG-007-V2.sm: Target hit rate increases; fewer mishits.
DRL-ABEG-007-V2.sf: Protect faces: controlled feeds only; keep spacing.
DRL-ABEG-007-V2.tg: Adult, Volley, Net play, Hand-eye, Targets

DRL-ABEG-009.n: Doubles: Crosscourt–Down-the-Line Trigger (2 Good Balls Rule)
DRL-ABEG-009.o: Introduce a simple doubles pattern: build crosscourt then change direction on a trigger.
DRL-ABEG-009.su: Pairs play crosscourt rally. Define trigger: after 2 deep crosscourts, player may go down-the-line.
DRL-ABEG-009.ex:
1) Play points starting with a feed.
2) Must hit 2 deep crosscourts before any DTL.
3) If DTL attempted early, point loss.
4) Track success of trigger and net coverage.
DRL-ABEG-009.cu:
• 'Two good balls first.'
• 'Then change with margin.'
DRL-ABEG-009.ef:
• Going DTL too risky → bigger target; require net clearance.
• Not recognizing trigger → count out loud.
DRL-ABEG-009.rg: Allow DTL anytime but reward after 2 balls (bonus).
DRL-ABEG-009.pg: Start with serve; add poach on DTL; add win-by-2 scoring.
DRL-ABEG-009.sm: Better decision-making; reduced UFEs on direction change.
DRL-ABEG-009.sf: Manage intensity; ensure safe court movement for beginners.
DRL-ABEG-009.tg: Adult, Doubles, Patterns, Direction, Decision rule

DRL-ABEG-010.n: Rally Bingo (Hit Zones to Complete a Card)
DRL-ABEG-010.o: Make accuracy and consistency fun using a bingo-style target card.
DRL-ABEG-010.su: Create 3x3 target grid on court (cones). Give each pair a 'bingo card' (zones).
DRL-ABEG-010.ex:
1) Cooperative rally with target goal.
2) When a player hits a called zone, mark it.
3) First to complete a row wins.
4) Switch targets every 5 minutes.
DRL-ABEG-010.ef:
• Players aim too small → enlarge zones.
• Forgetting target → coach calls out loud each point.
DRL-ABEG-010.pg: Add competitive scoring; require alternating zones; add serve start.
DRL-ABEG-010.sm: More purposeful aiming; improved depth control.

DRL-ABEG-011.o: Create consistent rally habits with simple scoring and consequences.
DRL-ABEG-011.su: Pairs rally crosscourt. Scoring: team starts at 0 and tries to reach 21.
DRL-ABEG-011.ex:
1) Each successful rally of 4 balls = +1.
2) If error before 4 balls, -1 (min 0).
3) First team to 21 wins.
4) Reset sides and repeat.
DRL-ABEG-011.ef:
• Getting frustrated → change -1 to 0; keep it positive.
• Too many errors → shorten court and slow feeds.
DRL-ABEG-011.rg: No negative points; only accumulate positives.
DRL-ABEG-011.pg: Increase required rally length (6 balls) before scoring; add direction change rule.
DRL-ABEG-011.sm: Rally tolerance improves; emotional control improves.
DRL-ABEG-011.sf: Keep intensity moderate; take breaks to avoid fatigue errors.

DRL-ABEG-011-V1.n: 21 Rally Game (Consistency Under Mild Pressure) — V1 Simplified
DRL-ABEG-011-V1.o: Create consistent rally habits with simple scoring and consequences. (simplified)
DRL-ABEG-011-V1.su: Pairs rally crosscourt. Scoring: team starts at 0 and tries to reach 21.
DRL-ABEG-011-V1.ex:
1) Each successful rally of 4 balls = +1.
2) If error before 4 balls, -1 (min 0).
3) First team to 21 wins.
4) Reset sides and repeat.

Simplify: Allow 2 bounces; shorten court to service boxes; use bigger targets; cooperative scoring.
DRL-ABEG-011-V1.ef:
• Getting frustrated → change -1 to 0; keep it positive.
• Too many errors → shorten court and slow feeds.
DRL-ABEG-011-V1.rg: No negative points; only accumulate positives.
DRL-ABEG-011-V1.pg: Increase required rally length (6 balls) before scoring; add direction change rule.
DRL-ABEG-011-V1.sm: Rally tolerance improves; emotional control improves.
DRL-ABEG-011-V1.sf: Keep intensity moderate; take breaks to avoid fatigue errors.

DRL-ABEG-011-V2.n: 21 Rally Game (Consistency Under Mild Pressure) — V2 Advanced
DRL-ABEG-011-V2.o: Create consistent rally habits with simple scoring and consequences. (advanced)
DRL-ABEG-011-V2.su: Pairs rally crosscourt. Scoring: team starts at 0 and tries to reach 21.
DRL-ABEG-011-V2.ex:
1) Each successful rally of 4 balls = +1.
2) If error before 4 balls, -1 (min 0).
3) First team to 21 wins.
4) Reset sides and repeat.

DRL-ABEG-011-V2.pg: Add pressure scoring; add serve start; add 'must hit target before point counts'.
DRL-ABEG-011-V2.sm: Rally tolerance improves; emotional control improves.
DRL-ABEG-011-V2.sf: Keep intensity moderate; take breaks to avoid fatigue errors.

DRL-ABEG-012.o: Teach simple transition: attack short ball, approach deep, then volley to big target.
DRL-ABEG-012.su: Coach feeds short ball. Place big target cone in opposite service box for volley.
DRL-ABEG-012.ex:
1) Player hits approach shot deep crosscourt.
2) Sprint under control to net and split-step.
3) Coach feeds volley; player punches to big target.
4) Rotate after 6 reps.
DRL-ABEG-012.ef:
• Rushing net → enforce split-step.
• Approaches too risky → aim middle/deep first.
DRL-ABEG-012.rg: Start from service line; drop-feed approach; catch volley first.
DRL-ABEG-012.pg: Add live point after volley; add decision: volley short vs deep.
DRL-ABEG-012.sm: Higher transition success; more confident net play.
DRL-ABEG-012.sf: Controlled sprints; avoid sliding; monitor knees.

DRL-ABEG-012-V1.n: Approach + Volley Finish (Big Target Transition) — V1 Simplified
DRL-ABEG-012-V1.o: Teach simple transition: attack short ball, approach deep, then volley to big target. (simplified)
DRL-ABEG-012-V1.su: Coach feeds short ball. Place big target cone in opposite service box for volley.
DRL-ABEG-012-V1.ex:
1) Player hits approach shot deep crosscourt.
2) Sprint under control to net and split-step.
3) Coach feeds volley; player punches to big target.
4) Rotate after 6 reps.

Simplify: Allow 2 bounces; shorten court to service boxes; use bigger targets; cooperative scoring.

• Rushing net → enforce split-step.
• Approaches too risky → aim middle/deep first.
DRL-ABEG-012-V1.rg: Start from service line; drop-feed approach; catch volley first.
DRL-ABEG-012-V1.pg: Add live point after volley; add decision: volley short vs deep.
DRL-ABEG-012-V1.sm: Higher transition success; more confident net play.
DRL-ABEG-012-V1.sf: Controlled sprints; avoid sliding; monitor knees.

DRL-ABEG-012-V2.o: Teach simple transition: attack short ball, approach deep, then volley to big target. (advanced)

DRL-ABEG-012-V2.sf: Controlled sprints; avoid sliding; monitor knees.

DRL-ABEG-013.o: Practice the most common big point format (no-ad) with a simple plan.
DRL-ABEG-013.su: Play repeated no-ad deuce games. Server calls a simple pattern (body serve +1 to open court).
DRL-ABEG-013.ex:
1) Start at deuce.
2) Play one no-ad point only.
3) Rotate server/returner every 2 points.
4) Track: first serve in and plan execution.
DRL-ABEG-013.ef:
• Overthinking → write plan on whiteboard.
• Missing serves → bigger target; reduce pace.
DRL-ABEG-013.rg: Allow second serve only; no-ad point starts with a coach feed.
DRL-ABEG-013.pg: Add receiver choice side; add consequences (down 0-30 start).
DRL-ABEG-013.sm: Better clutch point routines; improved serve KPIs.
DRL-ABEG-013.sf: Keep pace appropriate; hydrate; manage shoulder volume.

DRL-ABEG-013-V1.n: No-Ad Deuce Reps (Serve +1 Simple Pattern) — V1 Simplified
DRL-ABEG-013-V1.o: Practice the most common big point format (no-ad) with a simple plan. (simplified)
DRL-ABEG-013-V1.su: Play repeated no-ad deuce games. Server calls a simple pattern (body serve +1 to open court).
DRL-ABEG-013-V1.ex:
1) Start at deuce.
2) Play one no-ad point only.
3) Rotate server/returner every 2 points.
4) Track: first serve in and plan execution.

Simplify: Allow 2 bounces; shorten court to service boxes; use bigger targets; cooperative scoring.
DRL-ABEG-013-V1.ef:
• Overthinking → write plan on whiteboard.
• Missing serves → bigger target; reduce pace.
DRL-ABEG-013-V1.rg: Allow second serve only; no-ad point starts with a coach feed.

DRL-ABEG-013-V1.sm: Better clutch point routines; improved serve KPIs.
DRL-ABEG-013-V1.sf: Keep pace appropriate; hydrate; manage shoulder volume.

DRL-ABEG-013-V2.n: No-Ad Deuce Reps (Serve +1 Simple Pattern) — V2 Advanced
DRL-ABEG-013-V2.o: Practice the most common big point format (no-ad) with a simple plan. (advanced)
DRL-ABEG-013-V2.su: Play repeated no-ad deuce games. Server calls a simple pattern (body serve +1 to open court).
DRL-ABEG-013-V2.ex:
1) Start at deuce.
2) Play one no-ad point only.
3) Rotate server/returner every 2 points.
4) Track: first serve in and plan execution.

Advance: Add score pressure (win-by-2, time clock); require directional targets; add consequence for UFEs.
DRL-ABEG-013-V2.ef:

• Missing serves → bigger target; reduce pace.
DRL-ABEG-013-V2.rg: Allow second serve only; no-ad point starts with a coach feed.
DRL-ABEG-013-V2.pg: Add pressure scoring; add serve start; add 'must hit target before point counts'.
DRL-ABEG-013-V2.sm: Better clutch point routines; improved serve KPIs.
DRL-ABEG-013-V2.sf: Keep pace appropriate; hydrate; manage shoulder volume.

DRL-ABEG-014.o: Simplify return points: deep return then +1 through the middle to stabilize.
DRL-ABEG-014.su: Server serves moderate. Returner aims deep middle; rally continues for 2 more shots then stop.
DRL-ABEG-014.ex:
1) Serve.
2) Return deep middle.
3) Server hits neutral.
4) Returner plays +1 through middle.
5) Stop and repeat (high reps).
DRL-ABEG-014.ef:
• Return too short → aim higher; shorten target.
• +1 too aggressive → enforce middle corridor.
DRL-ABEG-014.rg: Coach feeds instead of serve; remove +1 rule.
DRL-ABEG-014.pg: Play out point after +1; add target for +1 (deep crosscourt).
DRL-ABEG-014.sm: Return quality improves; fewer early-point UFEs.
DRL-ABEG-014.sf: Avoid max-pace serves; focus on consistency.
DRL-ABEG-014.tg: Adult, Return, Patterns, Margin, Decision rule

DRL-ABEG-015.n: Lob vs Overhead Intro (Safe Defense Skill)
DRL-ABEG-015.o: Teach safe lobs and basic overhead positioning without fear.
DRL-ABEG-015.su: One player at baseline lobs; one at net practices tracking overhead with a catch first.
DRL-ABEG-015.ex:
1) Baseline player tosses high lob.
2) Net player moves back, turns, and catches overhead position (or catches ball).
3) Progress to gentle overhead to big target.
4) Swap roles every 6 reps.
DRL-ABEG-015.cu:
• 'Turn and run.'
• 'Point with off hand.'
• 'Move back early.'
DRL-ABEG-015.ef:
• Fear of ball → start with foam balls; catch only.
• Late footwork → earlier turn cue.
DRL-ABEG-015.rg: Foam ball catch only; smaller lobs.
DRL-ABEG-015.pg: Add live point: lob then recover; overhead to specific target zones.
DRL-ABEG-015.sm: Improved tracking and confidence; fewer panic swings.
DRL-ABEG-015.sf: Use safe feeds; watch for backpedaling—teach turn-and-run.

DRL-ABEG-016.o: Provide a reliable 'save' shot under pressure: simple slice to big target.
DRL-ABEG-016.su: Coach feeds medium pace to backhand. Mark a big crosscourt target zone.
DRL-ABEG-016.ex:
1) Player uses slice (open face, steady through).
2) Goal: clear net by 2–3 feet and land in big zone.
3) Rally 6 balls slice-only then switch.
DRL-ABEG-016.ef:
• Floating long → close face slightly; aim shorter.
• Into net → more height and softer swing.
DRL-ABEG-016.pg: Mix slice and topspin; add approach on short slice.
DRL-ABEG-016.sm: Fewer backhand errors; improved defensive consistency.
DRL-ABEG-016.sf: Monitor elbow; avoid excessive slice reps if pain.
DRL-ABEG-016.tg: Adult, Backhand, Slice, Margin, Defense, Consistency

DRL-ABEG-016-V1.o: Provide a reliable 'save' shot under pressure: simple slice to big target. (simplified)
DRL-ABEG-016-V1.su: Coach feeds medium pace to backhand. Mark a big crosscourt target zone.

DRL-ABEG-017.o: Combine serve routine with safe recovery steps to build match habits and fitness.
DRL-ABEG-017.su: Server at baseline with cone at recovery spot (just inside baseline).
DRL-ABEG-017.ex:
1) Serve to big target.
2) Immediately recover with 3 quick steps to cone.
3) Split-step and freeze.
4) Repeat 8 serves then rotate.
DRL-ABEG-017.ef:
• Forgetting recovery → verbal cue 'hit then three steps'.
• Fatigue → add longer rest.
DRL-ABEG-017.rg: Start from service line; no sprint (walk recover).
DRL-ABEG-017.pg: Add live return and +1 ball; add time clock.
DRL-ABEG-017.sm: Better serve recovery habits; improved footwork rating.
DRL-ABEG-017.sf: Avoid max sprints for beginners; manage heat and hydration.

DRL-ABEG-019.o: Train two gears: soft touch then deep reset to regain control.
DRL-ABEG-019.su: Half court. Target A: short service box; Target B: deep beyond service line.
DRL-ABEG-019.ex:
1) Player A plays touch ball landing in Target A.
2) Player B responds with deep reset to Target B.
3) Alternate roles for 8 exchanges.
4) Switch to competitive: touch earns 2 points, deep reset earns 1.
DRL-ABEG-019.ef:
• Touch too hard → shorten swing.
• Reset ball short → aim higher and longer.
DRL-ABEG-019.pg: Add full-court version with approach after touch; add volley finish.
DRL-ABEG-019.sm: Improved feel + depth control; better rally management.
DRL-ABEG-019.sf: Mindful of knees when moving forward/back; controlled steps.
DRL-ABEG-019.tg: Adult, Touch, Depth, Transition, Decision rule

DRL-ABEG-020.o: Practice score pressure and resets using a realistic comeback scenario.
DRL-ABEG-020.su: Play super tiebreak to 10, but one team starts down 0–4.
DRL-ABEG-020.ex:
1) Team A starts down 0–4.
2) Play super tiebreak to 10 (win by 2).
3) Between points, enforce reset routine (breath + cue).
4) Swap which team starts down each round.
DRL-ABEG-020.cu:
• 'One point at a time.'
• 'Reset after every point.'
DRL-ABEG-020.ef:
• Emotional spiral → stop, breathe, simplify targets.
• Rushing between points → slow routine and enforce time.
DRL-ABEG-020.rg: Play to 7 and no win-by-2; start 0–2 deficit.
DRL-ABEG-020.pg: Add consequences; add specific patterns for big points (serve body, return deep middle).
DRL-ABEG-020.sm: Better composure under deficits; reset compliance improves.
DRL-ABEG-020.sf: Manage intensity; keep positive climate; avoid excessive stress for true beginners.

DRL-ABEG-020-V1.n: Super Tie-Break Momentum Swings (0–4 Comeback) — V1 Simplified
DRL-ABEG-020-V1.o: Practice score pressure and resets using a realistic comeback scenario. (simplified)
DRL-ABEG-020-V1.su: Play super tiebreak to 10, but one team starts down 0–4.
DRL-ABEG-020-V1.ex:

2) Play super tiebreak to 10 (win by 2).

4) Swap which team starts down each round.

Simplify: Allow 2 bounces; shorten court to service boxes; use bigger targets; cooperative scoring.
