DRL-ABEG-020-V1.pg: Add consequences; add specific patterns for big points (serve body, return deep middle).
DRL-ABEG-020-V1.sm: Better composure under deficits; reset compliance improves.
DRL-ABEG-020-V1.sf: Manage intensity; keep positive climate; avoid excessive stress for true beginners.

DRL-ABEG-020-V2.n: Super Tie-Break Momentum Swings (0–4 Comeback) — V2 Advanced
DRL-ABEG-020-V2.o: Practice score pressure and resets using a realistic comeback scenario. (advanced)
DRL-ABEG-020-V2.su: Play super tiebreak to 10, but one team starts down 0–4.
DRL-ABEG-020-V2.ex:
1) Team A starts down 0–4.
2) Play super tiebreak to 10 (win by 2).
3) Between points, enforce reset routine (breath + cue).
4) Swap which team starts down each round.

Advance: Add score pressure (win-by-2, time clock); require directional targets; add consequence for UFEs.
DRL-ABEG-020-V2.cu:
• 'One point at a time.'
• 'Reset after every point.'
DRL-ABEG-020-V2.ef:
• Emotional spiral → stop, breathe, simplify targets.
• Rushing between points → slow routine and enforce time.
DRL-ABEG-020-V2.rg: Play to 7 and no win-by-2; start 0–2 deficit.
DRL-ABEG-020-V2.pg: Add pressure scoring; add serve start; add 'must hit target before point counts'.
DRL-ABEG-020-V2.sm: Better composure under deficits; reset compliance improves.
DRL-ABEG-020-V2.sf: Manage intensity; keep positive climate; avoid excessive stress for true beginners.

DRL-ROG-001.o: Build rally tolerance and court awareness with constant rotation and teamwork.
DRL-ROG-001.su: Mini tennis court. 4–8 players in a line on each side. Coach starts rally with feed.
DRL-ROG-001.ex:
1) Player hits then immediately rotates to end of their line.
2) Next player steps in to receive.
3) Team counts total successful exchanges (goal 20+).
4) Add bonus points for target hits (deep in box).
DRL-ROG-001.ef:
• Chaos on rotation → mark waiting spots; enforce one in/one out.
• Too hard → use lower compression ball and shorter swings.
DRL-ROG-001.rg: No rotation; just cooperative rally pairs.
DRL-ROG-001.pg: Add scoring: first team to 15 exchanges wins; add forehand-only round, then backhand-only.
DRL-ROG-001.sm: Total exchanges increase; improved readiness and spacing.
DRL-ROG-001.tg: Game, Rally, Cooperation, Fun, Court Awareness

DRL-ROG-002.o: Introduce competitive behavior with fast, low-stress scoring and lots of reps.
DRL-ROG-002.su: Mini or half court. One 'champion' side vs challengers. Coach feeds each point.
DRL-ROG-002.ex:
1) Challenger needs 2 points to take the champion spot.
2) Champion needs 1 point to stay.
3) Rotate challengers quickly.
4) Coach sets theme (crosscourt only, big targets).
DRL-ROG-002.ef:
• Arguing calls → coach calls lines.
• Too intense → reduce to 1-point takeover and emphasize fun.
DRL-ROG-002.rg: Cooperative only (no takeover) for 5 minutes first.
DRL-ROG-002.pg: Add serve start; add win-by-2 takeover for advanced groups.
DRL-ROG-002.sm: High engagement; players understand quick scoring and resets.
DRL-ROG-002.sf: Keep points short; manage emotions; rotate quickly.

DRL-ROG-003.o: Teach depth control by earning levels from short to deep targets.
DRL-ROG-003.su: Set 3 target zones: short (service box), mid (between service line and baseline), deep (beyond baseline line marker).
DRL-ROG-003.ex:
1) Start at Level 1 (short target) cooperatively.
2) Hit 3 balls into current level target to level up.
3) Miss = stay or drop a level (coach choice).
4) First player/team to complete Level 3 wins.
DRL-ROG-003.cu:
• 'Aim where you can succeed.'
• 'Make the ball arc.'
DRL-ROG-003.ef:
• Players force depth → enforce level-up rule.
• Targets too hard → enlarge zones.
DRL-ROG-003.pg: Add direction requirement (crosscourt/deuce/ad); add point start after Level 3 achieved.
DRL-ROG-003.sm: Players demonstrate deliberate depth changes; fewer short balls under pressure.
DRL-ROG-003.sf: Use cones safely; keep movement controlled.

DRL-ROG-004.o: Install a simple decision rule: build stability before changing direction.
DRL-ROG-004.su: Half or full court depending on level. Rally starts crosscourt.
DRL-ROG-004.ex:
1) Must hit 2 deep crosscourts before allowed to go down-the-line.
2) If DTL attempted early = point loss.
3) Play points to 7.
4) Rotate opponents every 7 minutes.
DRL-ROG-004.ef:
• Players forget rule → count out loud.
• DTL too risky → require big target corridor.
DRL-ROG-004.rg: Cooperative rally only; DTL never allowed for first 5 minutes.
DRL-ROG-004.pg: Allow 'change direction' only on short ball; add net player for doubles feel.
DRL-ROG-004.sm: Improved patience and lower UFE rate on direction changes.
DRL-ROG-004.sf: Monitor intensity; ensure safe court spacing.
DRL-ROG-004.tg: Decision rule, Direction, Patterns, Rally, Tactics

DRL-ROG-005.o: Create high engagement and confidence by rallying as a team against coach.
DRL-ROG-005.su: Coach on one side, players rotate on other side (line). Mini/half/full court based on level.
DRL-ROG-005.ex:
1) Coach feeds steady ball.
2) Team must reach 20-ball rally.
3) If error, reset to 0 but celebrate best streak.
4) Add themes: only to big target; only crosscourt; only high balls.
DRL-ROG-005.ef:
• Players feel pressure → reframe as teamwork not winning.
• Too hard → slow coach feed; shrink court.
DRL-ROG-005.pg: Add scoring: team earns 'power-up' after 10 balls to attack a short ball.
DRL-ROG-005.sm: Longest streak increases week to week; engagement remains high.
DRL-ROG-005.sf: Coach controls pace; maintain safe feeds.

DRL-ROG-006.n: Serve Start Progression — Underhand to Overhand
DRL-ROG-006.o: Build ability to start points safely with progression to overhand serve.
DRL-ROG-006.su: Players in pairs. Start with underhand serves from service line; progress back.
DRL-ROG-006.ex:
1) 5 minutes underhand serve points.
2) 5 minutes 'toss + tap' serve.
3) 5 minutes overhand serve from inside baseline.
4) Track serve-in percentage and quick reset routine.
DRL-ROG-006.ef:
• Too many faults → move closer; bigger targets.
• Toss issues → do toss-only reps.
DRL-ROG-006.rg: Only underhand serve for entire block.
DRL-ROG-006.pg: Add serve target bonuses; add return depth target.
DRL-ROG-006.sm: More points started successfully; improved routine consistency.
DRL-ROG-006.sf: Manage shoulder volume; focus on smooth motion.

DRL-ROG-007.o: Add athletic stimulus to rally work without losing technique quality.
DRL-ROG-007.su: Pairs rally crosscourt. Cone behind each player (3–5 meters).
DRL-ROG-007.ex:
1) Hit one ball then immediately touch cone and recover.
2) Partner hits back; repeat.
3) After 3 minutes, switch to cooperative rally without run.
4) Repeat 2–3 rounds.
DRL-ROG-007.ef:
• Technique breaks down → reduce sprint distance; slow pace.
• Too tired → increase rest.
DRL-ROG-007.rg: Walk to cone; shorter distance; fewer rounds.
DRL-ROG-007.pg: Add scoring: point only counts if both touched cone; add direction targets.
DRL-ROG-007.sm: Improved footwork rating and fitness while maintaining rally stability.
DRL-ROG-007.sf: Watch for slipping; keep sprints controlled.

DRL-ROG-009.o: Train forward/back transitions: touch shot then recovery vs lob.
DRL-ROG-009.su: Half court. Player A tries touch shot; Player B responds with lob; then reset rally.
DRL-ROG-009.ex:
1) Coach feeds neutral ball.
2) Player A plays touch into service box.
3) Player B lobs high; Player A recovers back.
4) Point ends after 4 balls; score to 11.
DRL-ROG-009.cu:
• 'Soft touch then recover.'
• 'High lob, buy time.'
DRL-ROG-009.ef:
• Touch too hard → shorten swing.
• Lob too flat → 'rainbow' cue.
DRL-ROG-009.rg: Remove lob; just touch and recover.
DRL-ROG-009.pg: Play full point; add approach/volley after touch.
DRL-ROG-009.sm: Improved transition movement and decision-making under changing ball types.
DRL-ROG-009.sf: Teach safe turn-and-run; manage backward movement.

DRL-ROG-010.o: Create repeatable competitive finishers with lots of short reps.
DRL-ROG-010.su: Play mini tiebreaks to 5 points (win by 1) on half/full court.
DRL-ROG-010.ex:
1) Start with a feed or serve.
2) Play to 5 quickly.
3) Between points, enforce reset routine.
4) Rotate opponents every tiebreak.
DRL-ROG-010.ef:
• Too intense → reduce to 3 points.
• Disputes → coach calls lines.
DRL-ROG-010.rg: Feed start only; cooperative for first 5 mins.
DRL-ROG-010.pg: Increase to 7 points win-by-2; add serve start and changeovers.
DRL-ROG-010.sm: Players improve pressure habits and reset compliance.
DRL-ROG-010.sf: Keep emotional climate positive; manage fatigue.

DRL-ROG-010-V1.n: Mini Tie-Break First-to-5 (Fast Competition Finisher) — Simplified
DRL-ROG-010-V1.o: Create repeatable competitive finishers with lots of short reps. (simplified)
DRL-ROG-010-V1.su: Play mini tiebreaks to 5 points (win by 1) on half/full court.
DRL-ROG-010-V1.ex:
1) Start with a feed or serve.
2) Play to 5 quickly.
3) Between points, enforce reset routine.
4) Rotate opponents every tiebreak.

Simplify: Smaller court; allow 2 bounces; cooperative scoring.
DRL-ROG-010-V1.ef:
• Too intense → reduce to 3 points.
• Disputes → coach calls lines.
DRL-ROG-010-V1.rg: Feed start only; cooperative for first 5 mins.
DRL-ROG-010-V1.pg: Increase to 7 points win-by-2; add serve start and changeovers.
DRL-ROG-010-V1.sm: Players improve pressure habits and reset compliance.
DRL-ROG-010-V1.sf: Keep emotional climate positive; manage fatigue.

DRL-USTA-011.o: Practice staying calm and executing a simple plan when down 0–40.
DRL-USTA-011.su: Start each game at 0–40 for server. Play 3 points to reach deuce or lose game.
DRL-USTA-011.ex:
1) Server starts at 0–40.
2) Must call Plan A (serve target +1).
3) Play 3 points.
4) Rotate server every game.
DRL-USTA-011.ef:
• Panic pace → slow routine; enforce breath.
• Over-aggression → enforce safe zones.
DRL-USTA-011.pg: Add no-ad at deuce; add consequences/bonuses.
DRL-USTA-011.sm: Improved composure and plan execution under deficit.
DRL-USTA-011.sf: Keep positive environment; avoid excessive stress.
DRL-USTA-011.tg: USTA League, Pressure, Reset, Mental, Patterns

DRL-USTA-012.n: Break Point Conversion Set (Return Games Focus)
DRL-USTA-012.o: Train converting break points with return quality and first 4 shots discipline.
DRL-USTA-012.su: Play mini set where every return game starts at 30-40 (break point).
DRL-USTA-012.ex:
1) Server starts at 30-40.
2) Returner states plan (deep middle or target).
3) Play point.
4) Track break conversion % over 10 break points.
DRL-USTA-012.ef:
• Returner misses too much → bigger targets; reduce pace.
• Server tight → routine cues.
DRL-USTA-012.pg: Add second consecutive break point if missed; add tiebreak after 10.
DRL-USTA-012.sm: Break point conversion % improves; better return habits.
DRL-USTA-012.sf: Manage emotional pressure; keep reps controlled.
DRL-USTA-012.tg: USTA League, Pressure, Return, Patterns, Competition

DRL-USTA-013.n: Match Clock Set (1 Set to 6 in 20 Minutes)
DRL-USTA-013.o: Simulate league time constraints and improve pace-of-play habits.
DRL-USTA-013.su: Play one set to 6 games with a 20-minute match clock. Changeovers timed.
DRL-USTA-013.ex:
1) Start clock.
2) 90-second changeovers.
3) No coaching during games; only at changeovers.
4) At 20 minutes, stop and record score.
DRL-USTA-013.ef:
• Rushing points → remind quality first.
• Too slow between points → set 20-second serve rule.
DRL-USTA-013.rg: Short set to 4 games in 15 minutes.
DRL-USTA-013.pg: Add stat tracking (1st serve in, UFEs) per 4 games.
DRL-USTA-013.sm: Improved pace habits; better focus under time pressure.
DRL-USTA-013.sf: Hydrate; manage heat; ensure safe pace.
DRL-USTA-013.tg: USTA League, Match play, Pressure, Habits, Routine

DRL-USTA-014.o: Reduce double faults by training safe second serve targets and routines.
DRL-USTA-014.su: Play points where server gets only one serve attempt, but must hit a large 'safety zone'.
DRL-USTA-014.ex:
1) Mark big second-serve target (middle of box).
2) Server gets one attempt.
3) If in = point plays out; if out = automatic loss.
4) Track in% over 20 serves.
DRL-USTA-014.ef:
• Fear → reduce consequence at first.
• Flat serve long → add height + spin cue.
DRL-USTA-014.pg: Add scoreboard pressure (down 30-40) on final 5 serves.
DRL-USTA-014.sf: Limit serve volume; manage shoulder and back.
DRL-USTA-014.tg: USTA League, Serve, Mental, Confidence, Pressure

DRL-USTA-015.n: Doubles Return Formation Adjustments (Stack vs Standard)
DRL-USTA-015.o: Practice adjusting formations based on server/returner strengths and patterns.
DRL-USTA-015.su: Run repeated serve/return reps using both standard and stacked formations.
DRL-USTA-015.ex:
1) 6 points standard formation.
2) 6 points stacked formation.
3) Track return depth and first 4 shots success.
4) Debrief: which formation feels better and why?
DRL-USTA-015.ef:
• Confusion on positions → mark spots with cones.
• Too complex → keep only standard for beginners.
DRL-USTA-015.rg: Only standard formation and deep middle returns.
DRL-USTA-015.sm: Better formation clarity and improved return outcomes.
DRL-USTA-015.tg: USTA League, Doubles, Return, Tactics, Communication

DRL-HC-SRV-004.n: Hard-Court Serve+1: Flat T (Deuce) — +1 to Open Court (Tiebreak Build)
DRL-HC-SRV-004.o: Build a hard-court first-strike pattern: flat serve to the t on the deuce side, then hit +1 into the open court created by serve direction. under tiebreak build scoring.
DRL-HC-SRV-004.su:
• Full court. 2 players (Server vs Returner).
• Server starts on the deuce side. Target the t serve location.
• Place 2 cones: one in deep target zone (last 3 feet) and one in the intended +1 target lane.
• Coach stands behind server to call pattern and monitor toss/tempo.
DRL-HC-SRV-004.ex:
1) Server plays the designated serve (make target first; pace second).
2) Returner returns with intent: deep middle first 3 reps, then game-realistic return.
3) Server must play the +1 as: Hit +1 into the open court created by serve direction.
4) After +1 contact, point is live. Winner earns 1 point.
5) Scoring: Play a 7-point tiebreak; must execute pattern on each service point.
6) Rotate roles every 6 points.
DRL-HC-SRV-004.cu:
• Serve: same toss, same rhythm; hit through the target window.
• Land balanced; recover with a split-step as the returner strikes.
• First strike: prioritize depth + direction; do not overhit on hard court.
• Hard-court cue: take the +1 early (inside baseline if possible) to steal time.
DRL-HC-SRV-004.ef:
• Serve misses wide/long → reduce pace 10%, aim big target (body/T) until 6/10 in.
• Late on +1 → shorten backswing; earlier unit turn; contact in front.
• Over-aiming to corners → re-center to high-margin lane (3 ft inside line).
DRL-HC-SRV-004.rg:
• Allow second serve if first misses; still must hit +1 pattern.
• Returner returns cooperatively crosscourt to start; then progress to random.
DRL-HC-SRV-004.pg:
• Returner may attack second serves; server must defend then re-establish pattern.
• Add 'must win in 4 shots' constraint (serve/return/+1/+2) to emphasize first-strike.
• Add No-Ad game: every point is sudden-death.
DRL-HC-SRV-004.sm:
• First serve in ≥ target for stage; serve hits intended location ≥ 40% (build to 55%+).
• +1 lands in intended lane ≥ 60% on big target.
• Pattern win rate improves across sets (track over 3 tiebreaks).
DRL-HC-SRV-004.sf: Manage volume: cap total serves per athlete; rotate to protect shoulder; keep returner behind baseline for safety.
DRL-HC-SRV-004.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-09-13_HP, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT, STG-ADULT_COMP
DRL-HC-SRV-004.tg: Hard Court, Serve, Serve+1, Patterns, Tiebreak Build, Flat, T, Deuce

DRL-HC-SRV-012.o: Build a hard-court first-strike pattern: flat serve to the t on the deuce side, then run-around to play inside-out forehand to opponent backhand corner. under tiebreak build scoring.

DRL-HC-SRV-020.n: Hard-Court Serve+1: Flat T (Deuce) — +1 Short Angle (Tiebreak Build)
DRL-HC-SRV-020.o: Build a hard-court first-strike pattern: flat serve to the t on the deuce side, then use a controlled short-angle +1 to pull the returner off the court. under tiebreak build scoring.
DRL-HC-SRV-020.su:
• Full court. 2 players (Server vs Returner).
• Server starts on the deuce side. Target the t serve location.
• Place 2 cones: one in deep target zone (last 3 feet) and one in the intended +1 target lane.
• Coach stands behind server to call pattern and monitor toss/tempo.
DRL-HC-SRV-020.ex:
1) Server plays the designated serve (make target first; pace second).
2) Returner returns with intent: deep middle first 3 reps, then game-realistic return.
3) Server must play the +1 as: Use a controlled short-angle +1 to pull the returner off the court.
4) After +1 contact, point is live. Winner earns 1 point.
5) Scoring: Play a 7-point tiebreak; must execute pattern on each service point.
6) Rotate roles every 6 points.
DRL-HC-SRV-020.cu:
• Serve: same toss, same rhythm; hit through the target window.
• Land balanced; recover with a split-step as the returner strikes.
• First strike: prioritize depth + direction; do not overhit on hard court.
• Hard-court cue: take the +1 early (inside baseline if possible) to steal time.

DRL-HC-RET-217.n: Hard-Court Return: Aggressive Down-the-Line from Inside Baseline vs First Serve — Target Deep Middle (Neutral Start)
DRL-HC-RET-217.o: Build a hard-court return identity: Attack down-the-line return to change direction and pressure the server. from inside baseline against first serve conditions, landing to land return within the middle lane, past service line + 3 feet., then play out the point under neutral start scoring.
DRL-HC-RET-217.su:
• Full court. 2 players (Server vs Returner).
• Returner starts Start 1–2 steps inside the baseline to take time away.
• Server hits first serve patterns. If lefty: server serves left-handed patterns (wide on ad, etc).
• Place cone targets for return landing zone and first ball 'safe lane' (3 ft inside line).
• Coach tracks return depth + quality (10-point sample).
DRL-HC-RET-217.ex:
1) Server serves according to the context (first or second serve).
2) Returner executes return type: Attack down-the-line return to change direction and pressure the server.
3) Landing rule: Land return within the middle lane, past service line + 3 feet.
4) After return, point is live. Returner scores bonus +1 if return hits target and point is won within 5 shots.
5) Scoring: Play first to 8 points, normal start.
6) Switch ends every 6 points to train both return sides.
DRL-HC-RET-217.cu:
• Early read: split-step as server hits; first move with eyes first, then feet.
• Compact swing on hard court; finish controlled (no wrap).
• Return depth > angle until stable; win time by taking ball early, not by swinging bigger.
• Recover: after return contact, recover with crossover + split to neutral position.
DRL-HC-RET-217.ef:
• Late contact → start a half-step deeper or shorten backswing; prioritize first step.
• Over-rotating hips → keep chest more square; use stable base; finish to target.
• Return floats short → aim above net strap; extend through contact; earlier preparation.
DRL-HC-RET-217.rg:
• Coach hand-feeds from service line to groove return type.
• Allow returner to start on baseline regardless of position prescription.
DRL-HC-RET-217.pg:
• Add 'server +1 must be to open court' to raise tactical realism.
• Add No-Ad game scoring: every point is sudden death.
• Add fatigue: 10-second shuttle between points; maintain return quality.
DRL-HC-RET-217.sm:
• Return depth ≥ 70% past service line; target hits ≥ 35% building to 50%+.
• Return Quality Index trend up (coach-rated 1–5).
• Reduce 'free points' (return misses) under pressure formats.
DRL-HC-RET-217.sf: Returner spacing: keep extra balls off court; server serves at controlled volume; hydrate in heat.
DRL-HC-RET-217.st: STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-09-13_HP, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT, STG-ADULT_COMP
DRL-HC-RET-217.tg: Hard Court, Return, Aggressive Down-the-Line, Inside Baseline, First Serve, Deep Middle, Neutral Start

DRL-HC-PAT-004.n: Hard-Court Pattern: 2x Cross + DTL Finish (Forehand emphasis) — Tiebreak
DRL-HC-PAT-004.o: Build a repeatable hard-court pattern (Hit 2 crosscourt balls with margin, then change direction down-the-line to finish or take control.) with forehand emphasis under tiebreak scoring.
DRL-HC-PAT-004.su:
• Full court, 2 players. Coach feeds first ball (or serve start for advanced).
• Mark 'safe lane' 3 ft inside sideline for margin-based hits.
• Use depth cones in last 3 feet to reward depth on hard court.
• If group (4+): run two courts or 2-on/2-off rotation every 5 minutes.
DRL-HC-PAT-004.ex:
1) Start with a coach feed to neutral zone (or serve start for advanced groups).
2) Required first sequence: Crosscourt x2 (margin) → Down-the-line x1 (attack) → Play out.
3) After the required sequence is completed, the point is live.
4) Scoring: Play a 7-point tiebreak; pattern must be executed on first 3 shots.
5) Bonus: +1 if the change-of-direction ball lands past service line and within safe lane.
DRL-HC-PAT-004.cu:
• Hard-court cue: win time with early preparation and inside-baseline positioning.
• Margin first: aim 3 ft inside lines until stability is proven.
• Change direction only when balanced (outside leg loaded; hips under control).
• Forehand cue: contact in front; finish to target (no 'wrap' under pace).
DRL-HC-PAT-004.ef:
• Change direction off-balance → require 1 extra crosscourt ball before DTL.
• Ball lands short → cue 'shape then drive'; finish longer; aim deeper target.
• Overhitting to win early → reframe to win by depth + repetition.
DRL-HC-PAT-004.rg:
• Reduce to mini-court or half-court crosscourt to stabilize rally.
• Allow cooperative feed and catch between points for younger stages.
DRL-HC-PAT-004.pg:
• Add 'must win within 6 shots' once pattern execution is consistent.
• Add 'no backhand slice' restriction (unless slice-specific day).
• Add fatigue: 15-second footwork ladder between points while maintaining pattern.
DRL-HC-PAT-004.sm:
• Pattern completion rate ≥ 70% (first 3 shots executed).
• Depth rate ≥ 60% past service line on neutral balls.
• UFE-10 trend down under pressure context.
DRL-HC-PAT-004.sf: Use clear 'ball on court' calls; manage rotations; ensure players not crossing behind hitter during live points.
DRL-HC-PAT-004.st: STG-09-11_GREEN, STG-11-14_YELLOW, STG-14-18_YELLOW, STG-09-13_HP, STG-13P_CBI, STG-12-18_FT, STG-ADULT_COMP
DRL-HC-PAT-004.tg: Hard Court, Patterns, Tiebreak, Forehand, Direction Control, Decision-making

2) Required first sequence: Short angle → Behind ball → Play out.
3) After the required sequence is completed, the point is live.
4) Scoring: Play a 7-point tiebreak; pattern must be executed on first 3 shots.
5) Bonus: +1 if the change-of-direction ball lands past service line and within safe lane.
DRL-HC-PAT-028.cu:
• Hard-court cue: win time with early preparation and inside-baseline positioning.
• Margin first: aim 3 ft inside lines until stability is proven.
• Change direction only when balanced (outside leg loaded; hips under control).
• Forehand cue: contact in front; finish to target (no 'wrap' under pace).
DRL-HC-PAT-028.ef:
• Change direction off-balance → require 1 extra crosscourt ball before DTL.
• Ball lands short → cue 'shape then drive'; finish longer; aim deeper target.
• Overhitting to win early → reframe to win by depth + repetition.
DRL-HC-PAT-028.rg:
• Reduce to mini-court or half-court crosscourt to stabilize rally.
• Allow cooperative feed and catch between points for younger stages.
DRL-HC-PAT-028.pg:
• Add 'must win within 6 shots' once pattern execution is consistent.
• Add 'no backhand slice' restriction (unless slice-specific day).
• Add fatigue: 15-second footwork ladder between points while maintaining pattern.
DRL-HC-PAT-028.sm:
• Pattern completion rate ≥ 70% (first 3 shots executed).
• Depth rate ≥ 60% past service line on neutral balls.
• UFE-10 trend down under pressure context.
DRL-HC-PAT-028.sf: Use clear 'ball on court' calls; manage rotations; ensure players not crossing behind hitter during live points.

LB-LIVE-001.o: Baseline liveball with high reps and simple rules
LB-LIVE-001.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed (no serve) or short feed to start
LB-LIVE-001.ex:
Challengers need 3 points to take court; Defenders need 2 points to stay. Play continuous points.
Winners stay as Defenders; losers rotate off to waiting line (or to Challenger side). Keep rotation fast.
LB-LIVE-001.cu:
• Quick reset after each point
• Doubles spacing: one up/one back when appropriate
• Target the middle under pressure

LB-LIVE-002.o: Adds scoreboard pressure and longer rallies near takeover
LB-LIVE-002.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-002.ex:
Challengers must win by 2 points to take court (e.g., 4-2). If tied, play sudden-death 'golden point' at deuce.
Same as Classic; keep a visible score track (coach calls loud).
LB-LIVE-002.cu:
• Protect the middle when ahead
• Simplify targets at deuce
• Between-point reset: breath + cue

LB-LIVE-003.n: Side-Out Liveball (You Only Score on Defense)
LB-LIVE-003.o: Trains defending, neutralizing, and counterpunching under pressure
LB-LIVE-003.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-003.ex:
Defenders can only score by winning a point while defending. Challengers score normally. First to 5 (or time).
After each point, roles stay the same unless takeover occurs. Rotate challengers every 2 points to keep reps.
LB-LIVE-003.cu:
• High + deep to reset
• Play through the middle when defending
• Use lobs when stretched
LB-LIVE-003.pg: Harder: defenders must win 2 in a row to score
LB-LIVE-003.sf: Works well with a 'defense theme' week.

LB-LIVE-004.n: Golden Point Takeover (Every Takeover Point = Sudden Death)
LB-LIVE-004.o: High-pressure, short-point intensity and clutch execution
LB-LIVE-004.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed or serve start
LB-LIVE-004.ex:
Whenever score reaches 2-2 (or 3-3), next point is golden point. Takeover requires winning golden point.
After golden point, rotate immediately. Keep tempo high.
LB-LIVE-004.cu:
• First volley/return quality matters
• Commit to plan A
• No mental drift between points
LB-LIVE-004.pg: Add serve start on golden points only
LB-LIVE-004.sf: Use sparingly to avoid excessive stress load.

LB-LIVE-005.o: Trains rapid recovery and sustained focus under back-to-back pressure
LB-LIVE-005.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed (two balls ready)
LB-LIVE-005.ex:
At takeover point (e.g., challengers at 2), play TWO points back-to-back without break. If challengers win both, they take; otherwise defenders stay.
Rotate after each two-ball sequence. Keep challengers in line order.
LB-LIVE-005.cu:
• Reset fast: turn away, breath, re-set feet
• Communicate between the two points
LB-LIVE-005.sf: Very effective for advanced adult liveball.

LB-LIVE-006.o: Maximizes touches and keeps energy high (great for larger groups)
LB-LIVE-006.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-006.ex:
Single-point games: winners stay, losers rotate. Track total points per player for the round.
Rotate after every point; keep waiting line tight and ready.
LB-LIVE-006.cu:
• Start in ready position before feed
• Play safe targets on first ball

LB-LIVE-007.o: Creates strong accountability for unforced errors and improves focus
LB-LIVE-007.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-007.ex:
If you make the unforced error, you rotate off immediately (even if your team wins later in rally). Continue point then rotate offender.
Coach calls 'move' and points to rotation. Keep flow fast.
LB-LIVE-007.cu:
• Margin targets
• Early preparation
• High net clearance when neutral
LB-LIVE-007.pg: Harder: rotate on any miss incl. forced error
LB-LIVE-007.sf: Explain rules clearly to avoid disputes.

LB-LIVE-008.n: Serve-Start Champions (Serve to Begin Each Point)
LB-LIVE-008.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve (champs serve every point; rotate servers)
LB-LIVE-008.ex:
Same as Classic takeover scoring, but each point starts with a serve from defenders. Defenders must call serve target (T/body/wide).
Rotate servers after each point (or every 2). Challengers return from consistent position.
LB-LIVE-008.cu:
• Call target before serve
• Return deep beyond service line
• Server recovers to home base
LB-LIVE-008.sf: Best when courts allow safe serving speed.

LB-LIVE-009.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feeds a serve-like ball to returner
LB-LIVE-009.ex:
Standard takeover scoring. Only rule: returner must hit return beyond service line or point = loss.
Rotate returners every point; keep returners in a fixed queue to maximize reps.
LB-LIVE-009.cu:
• Split-step on feed
• Aim big crosscourt
• Height + depth first
LB-LIVE-009.pg: Harder: return must hit a deep target zone
LB-LIVE-009.sf: Great early in session after warm-up.

LB-LIVE-010.o: Encourages forward movement and finishing skills
LB-LIVE-010.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-010.ex:
Normal takeover scoring, plus: if your team wins a point with a volley or overhead, count it as 2 points.
Rotate normally; coach calls 'double' on net-finish points.
LB-LIVE-010.cu:
• Approach on short ball
• Split-step at service line
• Punch volleys to big targets
LB-LIVE-010.pg: Harder: bonus only if net player touches net strap after point
LB-LIVE-010.sf: Good for weeks emphasizing transitions and volleys.

LB-LIVE-011.n: Fixed Partners Ladder (Rotate Opponents Each Mini-Game)
LB-LIVE-011.o: Stable partnerships for communication and pattern building
LB-LIVE-011.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-011.ex:
Play mini-games to 5 points. Winners move up one court (if multiple courts) or stay; losers move down.
Partners stay together for the round. After each mini-game, opponents rotate (or move courts).
LB-LIVE-011.cu:
• Between-point talk: who takes middle, who covers lob
• Play high-percentage patterns

LB-LIVE-012.o: Trains adaptability and communication with new partners
LB-LIVE-012.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-012.ex:
Standard takeover scoring.
After every 2 points, net players switch partners (or all players rotate clockwise).
LB-LIVE-012.cu:
• Quick communication: 'mine/yours', middle rules
• Simple patterns only

LB-LIVE-013.n: Up/Down Court Ladder (Winners Move Up, Losers Move Down)
LB-LIVE-013.o: Competitive ladder with clear progression and motivation
LB-LIVE-013.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court (multi-court ideal). Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-013.ex:
Mini-games to 7 (or timed).
After each mini-game, winning pair moves up a court, losing pair moves down.
LB-LIVE-013.cu:
• Play percentage when up
• Use agreed pattern on big points
LB-LIVE-013.pg: Single court: winners stay, losers rotate; track points for ladder ranking
LB-LIVE-013.sf: Pairs should be grouped by level for quality.

LB-LIVE-014.n: Winner Stays + Partner Stays (Loser Rotates Only One Player)
LB-LIVE-014.o: Balances continuity and variety; helps newer players stay engaged
LB-LIVE-014.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-014.ex:
Standard takeover scoring.
If challengers lose, only the returner-side player rotates; partner stays. Keeps one anchor player.
LB-LIVE-014.cu:
• Anchor player leads communication
• Keep targets simple for rotating player
LB-LIVE-014.pg: Harder: rotate both on loss; easier: rotate only after 2 losses
LB-LIVE-014.sf: Great when group has mixed skill levels.

LB-LIVE-015.n: Clockwise Rotation After Each Point (4-Corner Liveball)
LB-LIVE-015.o: Keeps everyone moving; builds spatial understanding and court roles
LB-LIVE-015.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-015.ex:
Single-point games: winners get 1 point; first to 11 (or time).
After every point, all four players rotate positions clockwise (server side -> net -> opposite).
LB-LIVE-015.cu:
• Know your new role quickly
• Reset and get ready before feed
LB-LIVE-015.pg: Harder: add role-specific constraints (net must volley)
LB-LIVE-015.sf: High tempo; coach must control feed timing.

LB-LIVE-016.o: Creates clear competitive structure with less chaos than point rotations
LB-LIVE-016.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court (2 courts). Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-016.ex:
Play mini-set to 5. Court A winners stay; losers move to Court B. Court B winners move up.
Move courts after each mini-set only (not point-by-point).
LB-LIVE-016.cu:
• Plan your first 2 shots
• Protect serve/return quality

LB-LIVE-017.n: Mix & Match (Coach Calls New Teams Mid-Round)
LB-LIVE-017.o: Allows micro-adjustments for balanced competition and targeted coaching
LB-LIVE-017.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-017.ex:
Standard takeover scoring.
Every 4 minutes, coach calls new pairings based on performance/needs.
LB-LIVE-017.cu:
• Coach sets 1 theme per pairing (e.g., middle control)
• Short feedback windows
LB-LIVE-017.sf: Coach must be decisive to keep flow.

LB-LIVE-018.o: Trains positional awareness and net/baseline role switching
LB-LIVE-018.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-018.ex:
Mini-games to 3 points; winners keep 'king' positions (net).
After each mini-game, all players rotate through: deuce net -> ad net -> deuce baseline -> ad baseline.
LB-LIVE-018.cu:
• Net role: hunt with racket up
• Baseline role: big targets and depth
LB-LIVE-018.pg: Harder: net player must finish within 2 shots
LB-LIVE-018.sf: Great variety without confusion if explained well.

LB-LIVE-019.o: Solves odd numbers and keeps engagement high
LB-LIVE-019.su: Players: 3 per court. Court: Half court doubles (one side). Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-019.ex:
Play single points. If the sitting player calls 'sub', they replace the player who made the last error.
Seat rotates by rule: sitting player becomes baseline on next point.
LB-LIVE-019.pg: Harder: sitting player can sub only after winning rock-paper-scissors

LB-LIVE-020.o: Creates high pace with larger groups on one court
LB-LIVE-020.su: Players: 5 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-020.ex:
Single-point games; winners get 1 point; first to 11 or time.
After every point, one player rotates off and the waiting player rotates in (clockwise).
LB-LIVE-020.cu:
• Be ready to enter instantly
• Know starting positions
LB-LIVE-020.pg: Harder: rotate two players every point
LB-LIVE-020.sf: Coach should slow feed if players look confused.

LB-LIVE-021.n: Crosscourt Lock (2 Balls Crosscourt Before Free Play)
LB-LIVE-021.o: Builds margin and rally rhythm; reduces early direction-change errors
LB-LIVE-021.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-021.ex:
Standard takeover scoring, with rule: first two shots must be crosscourt; then point is live.
Rotate normally.
LB-LIVE-021.cu:
• Big crosscourt targets
• Height + spin for safety
• Recover to middle
LB-LIVE-021.sf: Excellent early session to build rhythm.

LB-LIVE-022.n: DTL Trigger Round (Only Change Direction on Short Ball)
LB-LIVE-022.o: Trains tactical discipline and correct change-of-direction timing
LB-LIVE-022.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-022.ex:
If a player changes direction down-the-line on a deep ball, point is lost automatically. DTL allowed only on ball landing inside service line.
Rotate normally; coach must call 'short' clearly.
LB-LIVE-022.cu:
• Recognize short ball early
• Step in, hit through target
• Crosscourt patience
LB-LIVE-022.pg: Harder: DTL must pass through a cone gate
LB-LIVE-022.sf: Keeps players from forcing low-percentage DTL.

LB-LIVE-023.o: Emphasizes transition timing and net finishing
LB-LIVE-023.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-023.ex:
Win point = 1. If you win point after hitting an approach shot and finishing at net, point = 2.
Rotate normally. Encourage at least one approach attempt per rally when available.
LB-LIVE-023.cu:
• Attack short ball to big target
• Split-step at service line
• Volley to open court
LB-LIVE-023.pg: Harder: must win with volley/overhead for bonus
LB-LIVE-023.sf: Use on weeks focused on net play.

LB-LIVE-024.o: Forces correct net positioning and finishing; reduces backing up
LB-LIVE-024.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-024.ex:
Net players may only hit volleys/overheads (no groundstrokes). Baseline players play normally. Standard takeover scoring.
Rotate normally; ensure net players switch to get reps.
LB-LIVE-024.cu:
• Racket up
• Split-step on opponent hit
• Punch to big targets
LB-LIVE-024.pg: Harder: net player must poach once per point
LB-LIVE-024.sf: Only for groups with safe volley skills.

LB-LIVE-025.o: Teaches spacing, communication, and team shape
LB-LIVE-025.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-025.ex:
Each team must keep one player at net and one at baseline at all times. Switching allowed only after contact. Standard scoring.
Rotate normally; coach enforces role adherence.
LB-LIVE-025.cu:
• Net player: protect middle
• Baseline player: aim deep crosscourt
• Talk: 'mine/yours'
LB-LIVE-025.pg: Harder: net player must touch service line at split-step

LB-LIVE-026.o: Forces intent on serve/return/+1 and improves first-strike execution
LB-LIVE-026.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve or coach feed
LB-LIVE-026.ex:
Point ends after the 3rd shot (serve=1). Award point to side winning at shot 3. Standard takeover scoring.
Rotate normally. Keep score quick.
LB-LIVE-026.cu:
• Commit to plan A
• Big targets on return
• Attack the first short ball
LB-LIVE-026.sf: High intensity; use as late-round finisher.

LB-LIVE-027.n: Return Depth Bonus (Return Must Be Deep)
LB-LIVE-027.o: Incentivizes deep, neutralizing returns without over-coaching technique
LB-LIVE-027.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start (or serve-like feed)
LB-LIVE-027.ex:
Normal scoring plus: if return lands beyond service line, return team earns +1 bonus regardless of point outcome.
Rotate returners frequently.
LB-LIVE-027.cu:
• Split-step timing
• Aim big crosscourt
• Height + depth
LB-LIVE-027.pg: Harder: bonus only if return hits deep target corner
LB-LIVE-027.sf: Excellent to improve returns in adult groups.

LB-LIVE-028.n: Defense Reset Round (High + Deep Only When Stretched)
LB-LIVE-028.o: Trains defensive decision rules and recovery positioning
LB-LIVE-028.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-028.ex:
If a player is pulled outside singles sideline, next ball must be high + deep crosscourt. If not, point is lost. Standard scoring.
Rotate normally. Coach calls 'stretched' cue.
LB-LIVE-028.cu:
• High, heavy crosscourt
• Recover to middle
• Use lobs when needed
LB-LIVE-028.pg: Harder: defense ball must land in deep target

LB-LIVE-029.o: Encourages variety, touch, and tactical creativity
LB-LIVE-029.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-029.ex:
Each team must use at least one slice or drop shot in every point to score. If they win point without using it, point does not count.
Rotate normally.
LB-LIVE-029.cu:
• Disguise with same prep
• Keep drop shots low
• Use slice to change pace
LB-LIVE-029.pg: Harder: must win point within 6 shots after using touch
LB-LIVE-029.sf: Use with advanced groups only to avoid chaos.

LB-LIVE-030.o: Trains lobs and overhead finishing under live pressure
LB-LIVE-030.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-030.ex:
If a team gets pulled wide or sees both opponents at net, they are encouraged to lob; lob winner counts as 2. Overhead winner counts as 2.
Rotate normally.
LB-LIVE-030.cu:
• High lob to middle/deep
• Overhead: move feet, contact high
• Partner calls 'mine'
LB-LIVE-030.pg: Harder: lobs must land in deep zone for bonus
LB-LIVE-030.sf: Keep safety: overhead awareness and spacing.

LB-LIVE-031.o: Creates intent and accountability for serve placement
LB-LIVE-031.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start
LB-LIVE-031.ex:
Server must call T/body/wide before serving. If serve lands outside called target zone, point starts at 0-1 for server team.
Rotate servers each point. Keep returners in fixed rotation.
LB-LIVE-031.cu:
• Commit to target
• Same toss, different intent
• Recover fast after serve
LB-LIVE-031.sf: Keep it positive; use target size appropriate to level.

LB-LIVE-032.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start
LB-LIVE-032.ex:
All serves are 2nd serves (slice/kick intent). Double faults count as 2 points for opponents.
Rotate servers each point (or every 2).
LB-LIVE-032.cu:
• Brush up and out
• Aim big target
• Serve then recover
LB-LIVE-032.pg: Harder: must hit serve to called zone

LB-LIVE-033.n: Return Position Shift Round (Step In / Step Back)
LB-LIVE-033.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start or serve-like feed
LB-LIVE-033.ex:
Every 2 minutes, coach calls a return position: 'Step In', 'Neutral', or 'Step Back'. Returners must adjust and still hit deep.
Rotate returners every point; coach keeps time.
LB-LIVE-033.cu:
• Split-step timing adjusts with position
• Aim big crosscourt
• Short swing on fast serves
LB-LIVE-033.sf: Great for adult comp and juniors.

LB-LIVE-034.n: Chip & Charge Return Round (One Attempt Minimum)
LB-LIVE-034.o: Forces forward pressure and transition skills
LB-LIVE-034.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start
LB-LIVE-034.ex:
Return team must attempt chip-and-charge at least once per point to score. If they win without attempting, point does not count.
Rotate returners quickly to spread reps.
LB-LIVE-034.cu:
• Low chip to middle
• Sprint and split-step
• First volley deep
LB-LIVE-034.pg: Harder: must attempt on 1st serve too

LB-LIVE-035.o: Builds proactive doubles pattern and communication
LB-LIVE-035.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start
LB-LIVE-035.ex:
Serving team must attempt body serve + poach pattern. If net player does not cross at least once, point does not count for server team.
Rotate servers/net players so everyone gets reps.
LB-LIVE-035.cu:
• Signal before serve
• Net player crosses aggressively
• Baseline player covers line
LB-LIVE-035.pg: Harder: poach must result in volley contact
LB-LIVE-035.sf: Coach must ensure safe spacing at net.

LB-LIVE-036.o: Trains first-strike tennis and intent early in rally
LB-LIVE-036.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start
LB-LIVE-036.ex:
Point has two scores: (A) who wins the point and (B) who wins within first 4 shots. First-4 winner earns bonus point.
Rotate normally; coach calls first-4 result.
LB-LIVE-036.cu:
• Return deep, neutral first
• Server commits to +1 plan
• No panic on first ball
LB-LIVE-036.pg: Harder: bonus only if pattern executed
LB-LIVE-036.sf: Great for adult comp and older juniors.

LB-LIVE-037.o: Pattern automation under controlled serve speed
LB-LIVE-037.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start
LB-LIVE-037.ex:
All points start with a second serve. Server must attempt a pre-defined +1 pattern (e.g., serve wide -> forehand to open). If +1 not attempted, point does not count.
Rotate servers every point; coach enforces pattern attempt.
LB-LIVE-037.cu:
• Call pattern before serve
• Recover quickly
• Big target +1 ball
LB-LIVE-037.pg: Harder: require +1 to hit target zone
LB-LIVE-037.sf: Protect shoulder volume; keep serve intensity moderate.

LB-LIVE-038.o: Creates pressure reps for serving team and forces simplification
LB-LIVE-038.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start or coach feed
LB-LIVE-038.ex:
Server team begins each point down 0-1 in takeover game. They must win two points in a row to hold.
Rotate servers frequently.
LB-LIVE-038.cu:
• High percentage serve
• First ball big target
• No over-pressing
LB-LIVE-038.sf: Use in short bursts to avoid frustration.

LB-LIVE-039.o: Forces return focus and aggressive but controlled returns
LB-LIVE-039.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start
LB-LIVE-039.ex:
Return team begins each point down 0-1. They must win two in a row to take court.
Rotate returners quickly; coach calls pressure score.
LB-LIVE-039.cu:
• Deep return first
• Play to opponent weakness
• Reset after miss
LB-LIVE-039.pg: Harder: require return to deep target
LB-LIVE-039.sf: Pairs well with return theme week.

LB-LIVE-040.o: Trains forward intent and volley finishing
LB-LIVE-040.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start
LB-LIVE-040.ex:
Serving player must serve-and-volley at least once per rally for server team to score. If not, server team cannot score the point even if they win.
Rotate servers frequently to spread reps.
LB-LIVE-040.cu:
• Sprint to net
• Split-step timing
• First volley deep
LB-LIVE-040.pg: Harder: must serve-and-volley on 2nd serve too
LB-LIVE-040.sf: Ensure safe net skills; not for beginners.

LB-LIVE-041.n: Two Singles Lanes Liveball (Crosscourt Singles Inside Doubles Court)
LB-LIVE-041.o: Creates singles reps inside a doubles liveball session
LB-LIVE-041.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Singles lanes (two crosscourts). Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-041.ex:
Play 2 simultaneous crosscourt singles points (deuce lane and ad lane). Winners stay; losers rotate. Score individually.
Rotate into lane you lost; keep two lines organized.
LB-LIVE-041.cu:
• Crosscourt margin
• Recover to singles home base
• Change direction only when short
LB-LIVE-041.sf: Great when group wants more singles-specific work.

LB-LIVE-042.o: Improves touch, control, and fast reactions; lowers physical load
LB-LIVE-042.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Service boxes only. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Underhand serve or coach feed
LB-LIVE-042.ex:
Points played only inside service boxes. Winner stays king. First to 7 or time.
Rotate challenger every point; keep quick.
LB-LIVE-042.cu:
• Soft hands
• Use angles
• Stay balanced
LB-LIVE-042.sf: Excellent for mixed levels or tired groups.

LB-LIVE-043.o: Encourages width creation and directional control
LB-LIVE-043.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-043.ex:
Normal scoring, but any ball landing in doubles alley is worth 2 points (alley target cones).
Rotate normally; coach calls bonus points.
LB-LIVE-043.cu:
• Move opponent side-to-side
• Aim with margin
• Don't miss wide trying for alley
LB-LIVE-043.pg: Harder: alley bonus only if preceded by crosscourt
LB-LIVE-043.sf: Use when focusing on court geometry and width.

LB-LIVE-044.o: Trains advanced doubles tactics and partner signals
LB-LIVE-044.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Serve start
LB-LIVE-044.ex:
Serving team must use Australian or I-formation on each point. Points count normally.
Rotate servers and returners. Coach ensures correct formation setup.
LB-LIVE-044.cu:
• Signal clearly
• Server hits called target
• Net player commits
LB-LIVE-044.sf: Only with players who understand formations.

LB-LIVE-045.n: No-Man's-Land Penalty (Stay Out of the Middle Zone)
LB-LIVE-045.o: Encourages correct court positioning (either attack to net or retreat)
LB-LIVE-045.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-045.ex:
If a player hits a groundstroke while standing in no-man's-land (between service line and baseline), point is lost automatically.
Rotate normally; coach calls penalties clearly.
LB-LIVE-045.cu:
• Approach and keep going
• If late, retreat behind baseline
• Split-step at service line
LB-LIVE-045.pg: Harder: penalty applies to volleys too if too deep

LB-LIVE-046.n: Short Court -> Full Court Transition (3+3 Structure)
LB-LIVE-046.o: Smoothly ramps intensity and improves control before full pace
LB-LIVE-046.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Service boxes then full court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-046.ex:
First 3 minutes: service-box points only. Next 3 minutes: half court. Final 9 minutes: full court. Track same score throughout.
Rotate normally at each phase change.
LB-LIVE-046.cu:
• Control first, then expand
• Keep same tactical theme across phases
LB-LIVE-046.pg: Harder: require specific pattern in full-court phase

LB-LIVE-047.o: Improves opponent targeting and tactical awareness
LB-LIVE-047.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed or serve start
LB-LIVE-047.ex:
Teams earn +1 bonus if they successfully execute a planned pattern targeting opponent backhand 2+ times in rally.
Rotate normally; coach tags pattern execution.
LB-LIVE-047.cu:
• Identify weakness fast
• Play to big targets
• Don't force winners
LB-LIVE-047.pg: Harder: require specific sequence (BH-BH then attack)
LB-LIVE-047.sf: Works well with coaching feedback between points.

LB-LIVE-048.n: Passing Shot vs Volley Mini-Game (Net Battle)
LB-LIVE-048.o: Builds passing shots, volleys, and transition defense
LB-LIVE-048.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Half court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-048.ex:
One team starts at net, one at baseline. Baseline team scores by passing; net team scores by volley winner. Switch roles every 5 points.
Rotate players through net/baseline roles.
LB-LIVE-048.cu:
• Baseline: aim low to feet
• Net: close and punch
• Communicate middle
LB-LIVE-048.pg: Harder: baseline must hit within singles lane
LB-LIVE-048.sf: High focus round for specific skills.

LB-LIVE-049.n: Chaos Constraint Call (Coach Calls Mid-Rally Rules)
LB-LIVE-049.o: Trains adaptability and problem-solving under dynamic constraints
LB-LIVE-049.su: Players: 4 per court. Court: Doubles court. Round: 15 min × 1 rounds. Start: Coach feed
LB-LIVE-049.ex:
Coach may call a constraint mid-rally: 'crosscourt only', 'net touch', 'drop', 'lob', 'DTL'. Teams earn bonus if they complete the called task and win point.
Rotate normally. Keep calls simple and infrequent.

LB-CARDIO-001.su: Court: Pairs crosscourt in doubles alley removed (use singles lane).. Level: Adults 3.0+ (scale down for 2.5)
LB-CARDIO-001.ex:
Tennis task: Rally crosscourt aiming deep beyond service line with 70% pace.
Movement task: During rest: jog to baseline corner cone and back.
LB-CARDIO-001.cu:
• High net clearance
• Smooth tempo
• Recover to home base between hits
LB-CARDIO-001.sf: Monitor RPE 6–8/10; hydrate between sets; stop if dizzy.
LB-CARDIO-001.ct: Pairs crosscourt in doubles alley removed (use singles lane).

LB-CARDIO-002.su: Court: One player hits from ad side center; coach feeds to backhand side.. Level: Adults 3.5+ (or strong 3.0)
LB-CARDIO-002.ex:
Tennis task: Hit inside-out forehand to crosscourt target; recover to center each rep.
Movement task: Rest: shuffle to sideline and back (short).
LB-CARDIO-002.cu:
• First step across
• Load outside leg
• Big target crosscourt
LB-CARDIO-002.rg: reduce feed pace, allow set position
LB-CARDIO-002.sf: Watch knees/ankles with repeated lateral moves; reduce volume if pain.
LB-CARDIO-002.ct: One player hits from ad side center; coach feeds to backhand side.

LB-CARDIO-003.su: Court: Two at net, coach feeds from service line.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-003.ex:
Tennis task: Rapid forehand/backhand volleys to big targets in service boxes.
Movement task: Rest: step back to service line and reset stance.
LB-CARDIO-003.cu:
• Racket up
• Short punch
• Split-step every feed
LB-CARDIO-003.pg: add split-step each feed + target corners
LB-CARDIO-003.sf: Keep feeds controlled to protect faces; stop if shoulder pain.
LB-CARDIO-003.ct: Two at net, coach feeds from service line.

LB-CARDIO-004.su: Court: Coach feeds short ball from baseline; player starts at baseline.. Level: Adults 3.5+
LB-CARDIO-004.ex:
Tennis task: Hit approach to big target, sprint to net, finish volley to target.
Movement task: Rest: walk back to baseline and reset.
LB-CARDIO-004.cu:
• Approach smooth and deep
• Split-step at service line
• First volley deep
LB-CARDIO-004.rg: start closer (service line) and use underhand feed
LB-CARDIO-004.sf: Avoid reckless sprinting; insist on controlled stop at net.
LB-CARDIO-004.ct: Coach feeds short ball from baseline; player starts at baseline.

LB-CARDIO-005.su: Court: Server at baseline; returner blocks return; coach assists ball flow.. Level: Adults 3.5+
LB-CARDIO-005.ex:
Tennis task: Serve to called target then play +1 ball to big target; stop point after +1.
Movement task: Rest: jog to fence and back; reset serve routine.
LB-CARDIO-005.cu:
• Call target
• Recover after serve
• Big target +1
LB-CARDIO-005.rg: start at service line and drop-hit
LB-CARDIO-005.sf: Limit serve volume; alternate servers; shoulder-friendly warm-up required.
LB-CARDIO-005.ct: Server at baseline; returner blocks return; coach assists ball flow.

LB-CARDIO-006.su: Court: Pairs rally crosscourt full court.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-006.ex:
Tennis task: Sustain rally with height/depth; count balls; goal 20+ each work interval.
Movement task: Rest: walk, deep breaths, quick coaching cue.
LB-CARDIO-006.cu:
• Arc and depth
• Relax grip
• Stay in rallies
LB-CARDIO-006.sf: Monitor form breakdown; reduce interval length if technique collapses.

LB-CARDIO-007.su: Court: Coach at net with basket; player at service line.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-007.ex:
Tennis task: Random forehand/backhand volley blocks; aim to middle target.
Movement task: Rest: reset stance + split-step rehearsal.
LB-CARDIO-007.cu:
• Split-step timing
• Short punch
• Quiet feet
LB-CARDIO-007.pg: add 2 volleys per rep (FH then BH)
LB-CARDIO-007.sf: Keep feeds at safe height; protect face/eyes.
LB-CARDIO-007.ct: Coach at net with basket; player at service line.

LB-CARDIO-008.su: Court: Two cones on baseline 6–8 feet apart; coach feeds.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-008.ex:
Tennis task: Player shuffles cone-to-cone; on each arrival coach feeds; player hits to big target.
Movement task: Rest: walk to center and breathe.
LB-CARDIO-008.cu:
• Stay low
• Small adjustment steps
• Recover after hit
LB-CARDIO-008.pg: require depth target + add DTL on short feed
LB-CARDIO-008.sf: Avoid overloading knees; keep shuffle distances appropriate.
LB-CARDIO-008.ct: Two cones on baseline 6–8 feet apart; coach feeds.

LB-CARDIO-009.su: Court: Two at net volley-volley; baseline cone behind them.. Level: Adults 3.5+
LB-CARDIO-009.ex:
Tennis task: Volley exchange; every 3rd ball, coach calls 'back' and both sprint to baseline cone then return to net.
Movement task: Rest: walk and reset.
LB-CARDIO-009.sf: Ensure players know safe backpedal mechanics; no turning into partner.
LB-CARDIO-009.ct: Two at net volley-volley; baseline cone behind them.

LB-CARDIO-010.n: 45/15 Crosscourt + DTL on Whistle (Change of Direction)
LB-CARDIO-010.su: Court: Pairs crosscourt; DTL gate marked with cones.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-010.ex:
Tennis task: Rally crosscourt; on whistle, next ball must go DTL through gate; then return to crosscourt.
Movement task: Rest: reset and quick cue review.
LB-CARDIO-010.cu:
• Crosscourt patience
• Step in on direction change
• Aim through gate
LB-CARDIO-010.pg: DTL must be on short ball only
LB-CARDIO-010.sf: Avoid reckless DTL swings; keep targets big.
LB-CARDIO-010.ct: Pairs crosscourt; DTL gate marked with cones.

LB-CARDIO-011.su: Court: Coach feeds alternating to forehand/backhand from service line.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-011.ex:
Tennis task: Player hits alternating FH/BH to big crosscourt targets.
Movement task: Rest: quick reset + 2 deep breaths.
LB-CARDIO-011.cu:
• Early prep
• Stable base
• Big targets
LB-CARDIO-011.sf: Short work bouts reduce injury risk; stop if elbow/shoulder pain.
LB-CARDIO-011.ct: Coach feeds alternating to forehand/backhand from service line.

LB-CARDIO-012.su: Court: Two cones make a figure-8 around baseline center; coach feeds.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-012.ex:
Tennis task: Player runs figure-8; coach feeds at each cone; player hits to middle target.
Movement task: Rest: walk and reset.
LB-CARDIO-012.cu:
• Control the turn
• Stay low at cones
• Recover after hit
LB-CARDIO-012.sf: Avoid sharp cuts if knee pain; widen turns.
LB-CARDIO-012.ct: Two cones make a figure-8 around baseline center; coach feeds.

LB-CARDIO-013.su: Court: 4 players at net (2 per side) crossfire pattern.. Level: Adults 3.5+
LB-CARDIO-013.ex:
Tennis task: Continuous crosscourt volley exchange (no groundstrokes).
Movement task: Rest: rotate positions (deuce/ad) and reset.
LB-CARDIO-013.cu:
• Racket up
• Punch to big targets
• Split-step each contact
LB-CARDIO-013.sf: Eye safety: controlled pace; no reckless swinging.
LB-CARDIO-013.ct: 4 players at net (2 per side) crossfire pattern.

LB-CARDIO-014.su: Court: Coach feeds wide balls alternating sides from baseline.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-014.ex:
Tennis task: Player runs to ball and hits high, deep 'reset' shot crosscourt to deep zone.
Movement task: Rest: walk back to center and reset.
LB-CARDIO-014.cu:
• High and deep
• Recover to middle
• Use margin under stress
LB-CARDIO-014.sf: Watch for slips on wide runs; reduce speed if footing poor.
LB-CARDIO-014.ct: Coach feeds wide balls alternating sides from baseline.

LB-CARDIO-015.su: Court: Coach feeds rally ball; target zones marked.. Level: Adults 3.5+
LB-CARDIO-015.ex:
Tennis task: Player must hit 2 deep balls to big target, then approach on 3rd feed and finish volley.
Movement task: Rest: jog back to baseline and reset.
LB-CARDIO-015.cu:
• Deep first
• Approach smooth
• Split-step at net
LB-CARDIO-015.sf: Net safety and controlled stopping required.
LB-CARDIO-015.ct: Coach feeds rally ball; target zones marked.

LB-CARDIO-016.su: Court: 4 players. Coach feeds crosscourt rally; net player practices timed poach.. Level: Adults 4.0+
LB-CARDIO-016.ex:
Tennis task: Play live for 3 shots; net player must poach on coach cue and intercept.
Movement task: Rest: rotate net player roles.
LB-CARDIO-016.cu:
• Split-step then go
• Cross decisively
• Partner covers line
LB-CARDIO-016.pg: poach must touch ball and land volley in target
LB-CARDIO-016.sf: Ensure safe spacing at net; clear communication.
LB-CARDIO-016.ct: 4 players. Coach feeds crosscourt rally; net player practices timed poach.

LB-CARDIO-017.su: Court: Serve-like feed to returner; +1 feed to same player.. Level: Adults 3.5+
LB-CARDIO-017.ex:
Tennis task: Returner blocks deep crosscourt then hits +1 to open target; stop after +1.
Movement task: Rest: reset and switch returner every 2 rounds.
LB-CARDIO-017.cu:
• Split-step timing
• Short swing on return
• Big target +1
LB-CARDIO-017.pg: return must hit deep target zone
LB-CARDIO-017.sf: Monitor arm fatigue; keep ball speeds manageable.
LB-CARDIO-017.ct: Serve-like feed to returner; +1 feed to same player.

LB-CARDIO-018.su: Court: Coach feeds lob; player starts at service line.. Level: Adults 3.5+
LB-CARDIO-018.ex:
Tennis task: Player retreats, sets feet, hits overhead to big target; then sprint back to net ready position.
Movement task: Rest: walk and reset.
LB-CARDIO-018.cu:
• Turn sideways
• Contact high
• Recover fast
LB-CARDIO-018.sf: Shoulder safety: keep overhead volume moderate; stop if pain.
LB-CARDIO-018.ct: Coach feeds lob; player starts at service line.

LB-CARDIO-019.su: Court: Coach feeds to backhand side; player at baseline.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-019.ex:
Tennis task: Hit backhands crosscourt to big target with margin (height and depth).
Movement task: Rest: reset and shake out arms.
LB-CARDIO-019.cu:
• Early shoulder turn
• Stable base
• Finish balanced
LB-CARDIO-019.pg: add down-the-line every 4th ball to a gate
LB-CARDIO-019.sf: Avoid excessive volume if elbow irritation; switch to slice if needed.
LB-CARDIO-019.ct: Coach feeds to backhand side; player at baseline.

LB-CARDIO-020.su: Court: Player starts at net; coach alternates short and deep feeds.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-020.ex:
Tennis task: Player volleys short feed, then retreats to baseline to hit deep feed, then runs back to net.
Movement task: Rest: walk and reset.
LB-CARDIO-020.cu:
• Turn and run (no backpedal)
• Split-step at each position
• Control targets
LB-CARDIO-020.sf: Teach safe turn-and-run to avoid falls.
LB-CARDIO-020.ct: Player starts at net; coach alternates short and deep feeds.

LB-CARDIO-021.su: Court: Service boxes only (mini tennis).. Level: Adults 3.0+ (also good for juniors)
LB-CARDIO-021.ex:
Tennis task: Cooperative mini rally with touch and control; aim to keep ball in play.
Movement task: Rest: step back and reset grip/breath.
LB-CARDIO-021.cu:
• Soft hands
• Arc over net
• Stay balanced
LB-CARDIO-021.sf: High heart rate fast; take longer break after block if needed.

LB-CARDIO-022.su: Court: Pairs play full-court singles or doubles points.. Level: Adults 3.5+
LB-CARDIO-022.ex:
Tennis task: Play ONE high-quality point starting with serve; stop immediately after point ends.
Movement task: Rest: walk, breathe, quick cue + switch server.
LB-CARDIO-022.cu:
• Match routine before point
• Big targets under fatigue
• Reset after point
LB-CARDIO-022.rg: start with coach feed instead of serve
LB-CARDIO-022.pg: assign pattern goal for each point
LB-CARDIO-022.sf: Ensure adequate warm-up; stop if form breaks down.
LB-CARDIO-022.ct: Pairs play full-court singles or doubles points.

LB-CARDIO-023.su: Court: Pairs crosscourt; middle target marked with cones.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-023.ex:
Tennis task: Rally crosscourt; every 3rd ball must be played through the middle target (neutral reset).
Movement task: Rest: reset stance and breathe.
LB-CARDIO-023.cu:
• Middle is safe reset
• Crosscourt margin
• Recover to home base
LB-CARDIO-023.sf: Avoid forcing pace; focus on control.
LB-CARDIO-023.ct: Pairs crosscourt; middle target marked with cones.

LB-CARDIO-024.su: Court: Server with home-base cone behind baseline.. Level: Adults 3.5+
LB-CARDIO-024.ex:
Tennis task: Serve to big target then sprint to touch home-base cone before next ball (coach feeds return).
Movement task: Rest: walk and switch servers every 4 rounds.
LB-CARDIO-024.cu:
• Land balanced
• Explode to home base
• Eyes up to opponent
LB-CARDIO-024.pg: serve to called zone + touch home base before return contact
LB-CARDIO-024.sf: Limit serve volume; shoulder-friendly warm-up required.
LB-CARDIO-024.ct: Server with home-base cone behind baseline.

LB-CARDIO-025.su: Court: Coach feeds mid-court ball; targets set crosscourt angle zones.. Level: Adults 3.5+
LB-CARDIO-025.ex:
Tennis task: Player hits angle to pull opponent wide (target), then recovers to center; repeat.
Movement task: Rest: reset and breathe.
LB-CARDIO-025.cu:
• Create angle with margin
• Recover fast
• Stay balanced
LB-CARDIO-025.pg: angle then attack open court on next feed
LB-CARDIO-025.sf: Monitor hips/groin with repeated lateral pushes; widen spacing if needed.
LB-CARDIO-025.ct: Coach feeds mid-court ball; targets set crosscourt angle zones.

LB-CARDIO-026.n: 45/15 Drop Shot + Sprint to Net (Touch + Speed)
LB-CARDIO-026.su: Court: Coach feeds neutral ball to baseline.. Level: Advanced (4.0+)
LB-CARDIO-026.ex:
Tennis task: Player hits drop shot to target then sprints to net and performs split-step; coach feeds passing ball to volley.
Movement task: Rest: walk back to baseline.
LB-CARDIO-026.cu:
• Soft hands on drop
• Explode forward
• Split-step at net
LB-CARDIO-026.pg: must volley finish to score rep
LB-CARDIO-026.sf: High sprint volume; ensure athletes are warmed up and surface is safe.
LB-CARDIO-026.ct: Coach feeds neutral ball to baseline.

LB-CARDIO-027.n: 30/30 Baseline to Net Strap Touch (Transition Conditioning)
LB-CARDIO-027.su: Court: Player starts at baseline; coach feeds short ball each rep.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-027.ex:
Tennis task: Player hits controlled approach then runs to touch net strap and back to service line ready.
Movement task: Rest: walk and reset.

• Approach with control
• Stop under control
• Quick recovery
LB-CARDIO-027.rg: start at service line and hand-feed
LB-CARDIO-027.pg: add volley after approach before strap touch
LB-CARDIO-027.sf: Teach safe stopping; no sliding into net.
LB-CARDIO-027.ct: Player starts at baseline; coach feeds short ball each rep.

LB-CARDIO-028.su: Court: Coach feeds one ball per rep to baseline.. Level: Adults 3.0+
LB-CARDIO-028.ex:
Tennis task: Hit 1 controlled ball to big target then perform 40s mobility reset (hip openers, thoracic rotations).
Movement task: Rest period IS mobility work.
LB-CARDIO-028.cu:
• Quality reps over speed
• Use breath during mobility
• Smooth swing
LB-CARDIO-028.sf: Great for mixed fitness groups; reduces overuse risk.
LB-CARDIO-028.ct: Coach feeds one ball per rep to baseline.

LB-CARDIO-029.ex:
Tennis task: Defenders must lob to deep target; then recover to defensive positions.
Movement task: Rest: rotate defenders/attackers.
LB-CARDIO-029.cu:
• High lob to middle/deep
• Recover quickly
• Communicate
LB-CARDIO-029.pg: lob must land in deep target to score
LB-CARDIO-029.sf: Shoulder and overhead safety for attackers; keep pace controlled.
LB-CARDIO-029.ct: 4 players. Coach feeds attacking volley/approach to baseline defenders.

• Brush up
• High clearance
• Finish balanced
LB-CARDIO-030.sf: Monitor elbow/shoulder; switch to slower pace if pain.
LB-CARDIO-030.ct: Coach feeds to forehand; deep targets marked.

LB-CARDIO-031.su: Court: 1 court, cones for targets + movement touch points; basket/feeder. Level: Adult Beginner–Intermediate
LB-CARDIO-031.ex:
Tennis task: Defensive high-ball reset (high/deep crosscourt)
Movement task: Forward/backward shuffle (no blind backpedal)
LB-CARDIO-031.cu:
• Keep swing compact and targets big under fatigue.
• Reset fast between reps (breath + ready position).
• Move with control—decelerate safely.
LB-CARDIO-031.rg: shorter court, slower feeds/green dot, reduce movement distance
LB-CARDIO-031.pg: increase pace, shrink targets, add pressure scoring/time clock
LB-CARDIO-031.sf:
Warm-up required (shoulders/hips/ankles). Hydrate every block.
Scale impact: replace sprints with shuffles for knee/ankle issues.
Stop if mechanics break down—quality first.

scaling: Play-based stations; 1-step instructions; foam/red balls; no formal scoring; serve = toss/throw games; focus on motor skills + fun.
36-ft court; red balls; short lines; underhand to start points; emphasize rally initiation, big targets, and recovery to home base.
60-ft court; orange balls; introduce split-step, crosscourt default, serve routine basics, and simple patterns (build → change).
Full court or green dot; start KPI language; serve/return foundations; pattern basics; introduce pressure games lightly.
Training-heavy; KPI tracking weekly; pattern library; second serve development; physical readiness and recovery habits.
Execution under pressure; plan A/B/C; analytics + match simulations; manage load and recovery; develop autonomy.
2-hour sessions; higher reps; deliberate practice; daily KPIs; integrate conditioning and mental routines; tournament readiness.
Serve/return dominance + first 4 shots; high pressure reps; analytics; doubles systems; college match demands.
Daily periodized blocks (tennis + S&C + mental + academics); manage load; quarterly evaluations; advanced analytics and match play.
Safety-first warm-up; simplify techniques; big targets; green ball optional; fast success and social play confidence.
High leverage: serve/return + 2 patterns + pressure points; injury prevention priority; KPI-based improvement with limited time.
Use big targets; scale pace and scoring to group. Rotate partners often. Keep RPE appropriate.

liveball.name: Classic Champions (Defenders 2 / Challengers 3)
liveball.scoring: Challengers need 3 points to take court; Defenders need 2 points to stay. Play continuous points.
liveball.rotation: Winners stay as Defenders; losers rotate off to waiting line (or to Challenger side). Keep rotation fast.
liveball.theme: Baseline liveball with high reps and simple rules
liveball.cues:
• Quick reset after each point
• Doubles spacing: one up/one back when appropriate
• Target the middle under pressure

liveball.name: Win-by-2 Takeover (Pressure Finish)
liveball.scoring: Challengers must win by 2 points to take court (e.g., 4-2). If tied, play sudden-death 'golden point' at deuce.
liveball.rotation: Same as Classic; keep a visible score track (coach calls loud).
liveball.theme: Adds scoreboard pressure and longer rallies near takeover
liveball.cues:
• Protect the middle when ahead
• Simplify targets at deuce
• Between-point reset: breath + cue

liveball.name: Side-Out Liveball (You Only Score on Defense)
liveball.scoring: Defenders can only score by winning a point while defending. Challengers score normally. First to 5 (or time).
liveball.rotation: After each point, roles stay the same unless takeover occurs. Rotate challengers every 2 points to keep reps.
liveball.theme: Trains defending, neutralizing, and counterpunching under pressure
liveball.cues:
• High + deep to reset
• Play through the middle when defending
• Use lobs when stretched

liveball.name: Golden Point Takeover (Every Takeover Point = Sudden Death)
liveball.scoring: Whenever score reaches 2-2 (or 3-3), next point is golden point. Takeover requires winning golden point.
liveball.rotation: After golden point, rotate immediately. Keep tempo high.
liveball.theme: High-pressure, short-point intensity and clutch execution
liveball.cues:
• First volley/return quality matters
• Commit to plan A
• No mental drift between points

liveball.name: Two-Ball Takeover (Double-Point Burst)
liveball.scoring: At takeover point (e.g., challengers at 2), play TWO points back-to-back without break. If challengers win both, they take; otherwise defenders stay.
liveball.rotation: Rotate after each two-ball sequence. Keep challengers in line order.
liveball.theme: Trains rapid recovery and sustained focus under back-to-back pressure
liveball.cues:
• Reset fast: turn away, breath, re-set feet
• Communicate between the two points

liveball.name: No-Ad Mini-Games (First to 1, Rotate Fast)
liveball.scoring: Single-point games: winners stay, losers rotate. Track total points per player for the round.
liveball.rotation: Rotate after every point; keep waiting line tight and ready.
liveball.theme: Maximizes touches and keeps energy high (great for larger groups)
liveball.cues:
• Start in ready position before feed
• Play safe targets on first ball

liveball.name: Miss = Move (Instant Rotation Accountability)
liveball.scoring: If you make the unforced error, you rotate off immediately (even if your team wins later in rally). Continue point then rotate offender.
liveball.rotation: Coach calls 'move' and points to rotation. Keep flow fast.
liveball.theme: Creates strong accountability for unforced errors and improves focus
liveball.cues:
• Margin targets
• Early preparation
• High net clearance when neutral

liveball.name: Serve-Start Champions (Serve to Begin Each Point)
liveball.scoring: Same as Classic takeover scoring, but each point starts with a serve from defenders. Defenders must call serve target (T/body/wide).
liveball.rotation: Rotate servers after each point (or every 2). Challengers return from consistent position.
liveball.theme: Serve/return emphasis inside liveball format
liveball.cues:
• Call target before serve
• Return deep beyond service line
• Server recovers to home base

liveball.name: Return-Start Liveball (Return Quality Round)
liveball.scoring: Standard takeover scoring. Only rule: returner must hit return beyond service line or point = loss.
liveball.rotation: Rotate returners every point; keep returners in a fixed queue to maximize reps.
liveball.theme: Return depth and first-ball neutralization
liveball.cues:
• Split-step on feed
• Aim big crosscourt
• Height + depth first

liveball.name: Net-Touch Bonus (Finish at Net)
liveball.scoring: Normal takeover scoring, plus: if your team wins a point with a volley or overhead, count it as 2 points.
liveball.rotation: Rotate normally; coach calls 'double' on net-finish points.
liveball.theme: Encourages forward movement and finishing skills
liveball.cues:
• Approach on short ball
• Split-step at service line
• Punch volleys to big targets

liveball.name: Fixed Partners Ladder (Rotate Opponents Each Mini-Game)
liveball.scoring: Play mini-games to 5 points. Winners move up one court (if multiple courts) or stay; losers move down.
liveball.rotation: Partners stay together for the round. After each mini-game, opponents rotate (or move courts).
liveball.theme: Stable partnerships for communication and pattern building
liveball.cues:
• Between-point talk: who takes middle, who covers lob
• Play high-percentage patterns

liveball.name: Rotate Partners Every 2 Points (Social + Adaptation)
liveball.scoring: Standard takeover scoring.
liveball.rotation: After every 2 points, net players switch partners (or all players rotate clockwise).
liveball.theme: Trains adaptability and communication with new partners
liveball.cues:
• Quick communication: 'mine/yours', middle rules
• Simple patterns only

liveball.name: Up/Down Court Ladder (Winners Move Up, Losers Move Down)
liveball.scoring: Mini-games to 7 (or timed).
liveball.rotation: After each mini-game, winning pair moves up a court, losing pair moves down.
liveball.theme: Competitive ladder with clear progression and motivation
liveball.cues:
• Play percentage when up
• Use agreed pattern on big points

liveball.name: Winner Stays + Partner Stays (Loser Rotates Only One Player)
liveball.scoring: Standard takeover scoring.
liveball.rotation: If challengers lose, only the returner-side player rotates; partner stays. Keeps one anchor player.
liveball.theme: Balances continuity and variety; helps newer players stay engaged
liveball.cues:
• Anchor player leads communication
• Keep targets simple for rotating player

liveball.name: Clockwise Rotation After Each Point (4-Corner Liveball)
liveball.scoring: Single-point games: winners get 1 point; first to 11 (or time).
liveball.rotation: After every point, all four players rotate positions clockwise (server side -> net -> opposite).
liveball.theme: Keeps everyone moving; builds spatial understanding and court roles
liveball.cues:
• Know your new role quickly
• Reset and get ready before feed

liveball.name: Two-Court Ladder (Court A/B Promotion/Relegation)
liveball.scoring: Play mini-set to 5. Court A winners stay; losers move to Court B. Court B winners move up.
liveball.rotation: Move courts after each mini-set only (not point-by-point).
liveball.theme: Creates clear competitive structure with less chaos than point rotations
liveball.cues:
• Plan your first 2 shots
• Protect serve/return quality

liveball.name: Mix & Match (Coach Calls New Teams Mid-Round)
liveball.scoring: Standard takeover scoring.
liveball.rotation: Every 4 minutes, coach calls new pairings based on performance/needs.
liveball.theme: Allows micro-adjustments for balanced competition and targeted coaching
liveball.cues:
• Coach sets 1 theme per pairing (e.g., middle control)
• Short feedback windows

liveball.name: King's Cross (Rotate Through 4 Positions)
liveball.scoring: Mini-games to 3 points; winners keep 'king' positions (net).
liveball.rotation: After each mini-game, all players rotate through: deuce net -> ad net -> deuce baseline -> ad baseline.
liveball.theme: Trains positional awareness and net/baseline role switching
liveball.cues:
• Net role: hunt with racket up
• Baseline role: big targets and depth

liveball.name: 3-Player Court (One Sits Out, Fast Sub)
liveball.scoring: Play single points. If the sitting player calls 'sub', they replace the player who made the last error.
liveball.rotation: Seat rotates by rule: sitting player becomes baseline on next point.
liveball.theme: Solves odd numbers and keeps engagement high
liveball.cues:
• Clear 'sub' timing
• Keep rules simple

liveball.name: 5-Player Court (Continuous Sub After Each Point)
liveball.scoring: Single-point games; winners get 1 point; first to 11 or time.
liveball.rotation: After every point, one player rotates off and the waiting player rotates in (clockwise).
liveball.theme: Creates high pace with larger groups on one court
liveball.cues:
• Be ready to enter instantly
• Know starting positions

cardio.name: 30/30 Crosscourt Rhythm (Baseline Endurance)
cardio.task: Rally crosscourt aiming deep beyond service line with 70% pace.
cardio.cues:
• High net clearance
• Smooth tempo
• Recover to home base between hits

cardio.name: 40/20 Inside-Out Forehand Pattern (Footwork + Pattern)
cardio.task: Hit inside-out forehand to crosscourt target; recover to center each rep.
cardio.cues:
• First step across
• Load outside leg
• Big target crosscourt

cardio.name: Tabata Volleys (20/10 x 8)
cardio.task: Rapid forehand/backhand volleys to big targets in service boxes.
cardio.cues:
• Racket up
• Short punch
• Split-step every feed

cardio.name: 45/15 Approach + Volley Finish (Transition HIIT)
cardio.task: Hit approach to big target, sprint to net, finish volley to target.
cardio.cues:
• Approach smooth and deep
• Split-step at service line
• First volley deep

cardio.name: 60/30 Serve +1 (First Four Shots Fitness)
cardio.task: Serve to called target then play +1 ball to big target; stop point after +1.
cardio.cues:
• Call target
• Recover after serve
• Big target +1

cardio.name: 90/30 Long Rally Aerobic (Zone 3–4 Tennis)
cardio.task: Sustain rally with height/depth; count balls; goal 20+ each work interval.
cardio.cues:
• Arc and depth
• Relax grip
• Stay in rallies

cardio.name: 15/15 Reaction Feeds (Quick Feet + Contact)
cardio.task: Random forehand/backhand volley blocks; aim to middle target.
cardio.cues:
• Split-step timing
• Short punch
• Quiet feet

cardio.name: 40/20 Lateral Shuffle + Hit (Baseline Agility)
cardio.task: Player shuffles cone-to-cone; on each arrival coach feeds; player hits to big target.
cardio.cues:
• Stay low
• Small adjustment steps
• Recover after hit

cardio.name: 30/30 Net Exchange + Sprint Back (Net Fitness)
cardio.task: Volley exchange; every 3rd ball, coach calls 'back' and both sprint to baseline cone then return to net.

cardio.name: 45/15 Crosscourt + DTL on Whistle (Change of Direction)
cardio.task: Rally crosscourt; on whistle, next ball must go DTL through gate; then return to crosscourt.
cardio.cues:
• Crosscourt patience
• Step in on direction change
• Aim through gate
