liveball_session.title: LBTA LiveBall
liveball_session.objective: High-intensity, coach-fed doubles session that sharpens positioning, patterns, and finishing while delivering an excellent cardio workout. No serving—coach feeds every ball to maintain constant action.

rules.champions: 2 players who hold the 'king of the court' position
rules.challengers: 2 players attempting to dethrone the Champions
rules.coach: Positioned on Champions side with ball basket, feeds every point (no serves)
rules.scoring: Challengers need 3 points to take court. Champions need 2 points to stay. Champions hold until beaten.
first_ball: Must be hit from baseline to coach's requested target (FH cross, BH cross, inside-out FH, or OPEN BALL)
Cannot be poached by Champions' net player UNLESS it's an OPEN BALL feed
Miss first ball (net or out) → Challengers immediately replaced, go to back of line
Outright winner on first ball → Challengers become Champions immediately (bypass 3-point requirement)
Wrong target → re-feed once, then replacement
rules.after_first: Any Challenger can hit subsequent feeds. Coach feeds random balls with variety. Point ends on winner or error; coach immediately feeds next ball.

blocks.name: Dynamic Warm-Up
blocks.setup: All players on baseline, spread across both sides
blocks.execution:
Movement & Footwork (8 min): Jog to net and back (2 reps) – 1 min. Side shuffle right/left to net and back (2 reps each) – 1 min. Carioca to net and back (2 reps) – 1 min. Split-step + first-step reaction drill – 2 min. High knees to service line and back (2 reps) – 1 min. Shadow swings with movement (FH/BH) – 2 min.

Cooperative Warm-Up Hits (7 min): Groundstrokes (4 min) — pair up crosscourt, rally from baseline, goal 10 consecutive balls. First 2 min FH side, next 2 min BH side. Volleys (3 min) — Minute 1-2: one at net, one at baseline. Minute 3: both at net, short fast reflex volleys.
cues: Low athletic stance, weight on balls of feet
Split-step before every direction change
Eyes up, track the coach's hand

blocks.name: Forehand Crosscourt
blocks.focus: FH crosscourt patterns from deuce side
blocks.setup: Champions: 1 baseline deuce, 1 net deuce. Challengers: 1 baseline deuce, 1 net deuce. Coach feeds from center service line on Champions side.
blocks.execution: Feed to Challenger baseline (deuce side). Must hit forehand crosscourt to requested target. After first ball: point is live, players may change direction after 3 balls if on offense. Standard scoring (3 vs 2).
cues: Heavy topspin crosscourt, aim 3–5 feet inside the line
High net clearance—4–6 feet minimum over the net
Recover outside the singles sideline after each shot
Net player: watch for floating balls to poach
Stay patient with crosscourt until you earn the line

blocks.name: Backhand Crosscourt
blocks.focus: BH crosscourt consistency from ad side
blocks.setup: Champions: 1 baseline ad, 1 net ad. Challengers: 1 baseline ad, 1 net ad. Coach feeds from center service line on Champions side.
blocks.execution: Feed to Challenger baseline (ad side). Must hit backhand crosscourt to requested target. After first ball: point is live, same scoring (3 vs 2).
cues: Use your legs on backhand, don't just arm it
Shape the ball with topspin crosscourt, don't slice short
Net player: protect the middle, anticipate the poach
Baseline player: recover behind baseline after neutral backhands
Depth is more important than pace—make them hit behind the baseline

blocks.name: Forehand Inside-Out
blocks.focus: Create offense by running around BH with IOF
blocks.setup: Champions: 1 baseline ad, 1 net ad. Challengers: 1 baseline ad, 1 net ad. Coach feeds from center service line. Emphasis on strong FH players running around backhand.
blocks.execution: Feed to Challenger baseline (ad side). Must hit inside-out forehand (from ad court hitting crosscourt to deuce side). After first ball: point is live, any direction. Standard scoring (3 vs 2).
cues: Load outside leg, create space to step around the ball
Target: deep crosscourt into deuce corner or heavy to opponent's backhand
After inside-out, recover toward ad corner—not center court
Net partner: close in when you see your partner attack inside-out
This is an offensive pattern—use it to take control of the point

blocks.name: Forehand Anywhere (Deuce)
blocks.setup: Champions: 1 baseline deuce, 1 net deuce. Challengers: 1 baseline deuce, 1 net deuce. Coach feeds from center service line.
blocks.execution: Feed to Challenger baseline (deuce side). Challenger chooses OPEN BALL—any forehand direction (crosscourt OR down the line). Champions net player CAN poach first ball on this drill. After first ball: point is live, any direction. Standard scoring (3 vs 2).
cues: Decide early: crosscourt for high margin, line only when you see open space
Read opponent positioning before you hit—don't telegraph
Don't reveal your direction with preparation—disguise it
Net player (both sides): active hands, split-step and anticipate
If you go line, commit and close behind it

blocks.name: Backhand Anywhere (Ad)
blocks.setup: Champions: 1 baseline ad, 1 net ad. Challengers: 1 baseline ad, 1 net ad. Coach feeds from center service line.
blocks.execution: Feed to Challenger baseline (ad side). Challenger chooses OPEN BALL—any backhand direction (crosscourt OR down the line). Champions net player CAN poach first ball. After first ball: point is live, any direction. Standard scoring (3 vs 2).
cues: Default to crosscourt unless you're clearly on offense
Heavy, high-margin backhand until you earn a short ball
Slice is OK for neutralizing, but follow up with topspin drive when you attack
If you see the net player leaning, rip your backhand line
This is the finishing block—raise the intensity and close out strong
blocks.tempo: 3-5 seconds between points, push pace if energy drops

3.0-3.5: Slow feed pace slightly (5-7 seconds throughout)
Allow 2 first-ball attempts if requested (coach discretion)
Focus more on crosscourt consistency in Blocks 5-6, less on going line
4.0+: Increase feed pace (3-5 seconds in all blocks)
Add spin and pace variety to feeds
Enforce first-ball rules with zero tolerance
Push decision-making in Blocks 5-6: 'Make a choice and commit'
4.5+: Maximum feed pace (2-4 seconds between points)
Add wrong-footed feeds and short angle feeds in Blocks 5-6
Increase consequence of first-ball miss: team sits out 2 rotations

reminders.rotation: Keep rotations fast: 60-90 seconds max. Pre-position next Challenger pair at the fence ready to step in immediately.
reminders.intensity: Coach sets the tone with body language, voice, and pace. Keep ball basket full and close. Last block should be highest intensity.
reminders.feedback: Short, specific cues between points (not mini-lessons). ✅ 'Great depth, now recover wider' ✅ 'Perfect first ball—exactly the target' ❌ 'So what you're doing wrong is your grip and stance and...'
reminders.communication: Demand communication every point: 'Yours/Mine' on every ball, 'Switch' when crossing, 'Stay/Poach' at net. Reward teams who communicate well.
reminders.safety: Call balls clearly to avoid collisions. Watch for fatigue in hot conditions—mandatory 30-second water breaks. Stop play immediately for cramping or injury.
reminders.first_ball_discipline: This is the signature of liveball—enforce strictly every time. Miss = immediate replacement (no 'gimmes'). Winner = instant Champions. Wrong target = re-feed once, then replacement.

equipment: 2 full ball hoppers (min 100 balls per court)
Whiteboard/marker optional for tracking top Champions
liveball_session.progression_logic: Blocks 2-3: Foundation crosscourt patterns (one direction only). Block 4: Offensive forehand pattern (inside-out creates attack). Blocks 5-6: Decision-making and finishing (open ball = game situations).
liveball_session.intensity_arc: Blocks 1-2: Teaching mode—establish patterns and rules. Blocks 3-4: Building mode—increase pace and expectations. Blocks 5-6: Performance mode—maximize intensity, simulate match pressure.

levels.name: 👀 Tracker

Can send ball over mini-net toward target

levels.name: 2️⃣ Builder

1.coach_action: Observe during warm-up. Record initial level. Flag misplacements. 5 min per player embedded in session.

10.coach_action: Observe who executes under pressure. Record KPIs from competition. Flag advancement candidates.

11.coach_action: Full match-play observation. Record: rally tolerance, serve %, reset routine %, pattern execution. Draft advancement recs.

12.coach_action: Formal KPI protocol. Run all stage-relevant KPIs. Write parent reports. Submit advancement recs to Robert. Celebrate.

2-9.coach_action: Track KPIs WITHIN drills. The drill IS the measurement. Note key numbers. Record enjoyment (1-5) after session.

advancement.auto: Player meets ALL readiness gates during Week 12. Coach documents with KPI data. Advances next cycle. No approval needed.
advancement.borderline: Player meets 3 of 5 gates or coach has concerns. Submit to Robert with KPI data. Robert + Andrew review within 48 hours. Decision: Advance / Hold / Trial 2 weeks.
advancement.regression: Enjoyment <3, can't keep up, parent reports frustration. Coach documents with KPI evidence. Framed positively: 'Solidify the foundation to accelerate long-term development.'
advancement.iron_rule: No advancement without at least 3 recorded KPIs. No social promotions. No parent pressure overrides. Data decides.

stations.name: TopSpinPro
stations.desc: 10 reps FH topspin, 10 reps BH. Self-paced.

stations.name: Shadow Swings
stations.desc: 10 FH + 10 BH with full footwork. Use cue card.

stations.name: Wall Rally
stations.desc: Rally against wall/fence to personal best. Track count.

stations.name: Serve Toss Practice
stations.desc: Toss to target (racket face catch). 20 reps.

stations.name: Resistance Band Swings
stations.desc: Band on fence. 10 FH, 10 BH. Feel resistance.

stations.name: Target Practice (Self-Feed)
stations.desc: Self-feed, aim at cone. Track hits out of 10.

parent_comms.week1: Hi [Parent], welcome to [Program]! [Child] is starting at [Level]. This cycle we're focusing on [Phase 1 themes]. Here's what to expect in the first few weeks.
parent_comms.week6: [Child] is doing great with [specific skill]. We're working on [area]. Keep encouraging [home practice]. See you [next session day]!
parent_comms.week10: We're entering our assessment weeks. [Child] will do some fun challenges that help us track progress. Nothing stressful — just games with a purpose!
parent_comms.week12: Written progress report: current level, KPI results, strengths, growth areas, next-cycle goals. Advancement recommendation if applicable.

junior_dev.note: Peter commutes from Temecula — earliest 10 AM. Confirm AM shift start time.

full_day_schedule.detail: Greet by name. Name game or team cheer. Theme. Sunscreen check.
Dynamic warm-up (10 min) → Skill drill from curriculum Code A (30 min). Station rotation if 6+.
Non-tennis team game from Games Catalog on grass field. High energy.
Rally games (15 min) → Mini-tournament / Champions format (25 min).
AM half-day: daily award, cool-down, team cheer, dismiss at gate by 11:45.
Tue/Thu = sprinkler run, water balloons. Mon/Wed = arts & crafts, playground.
AM final pickups at gate. Full-day lunch begins. PM kids arrive + check in. Busiest 20 min of the day.
Packed lunch in shaded picnic area. Coach eats with group. Social bonding.
PM kids welcomed by name. Fresh cheer. Full-day kids rejoin from lunch.
Warm-up (10 min) → Session planner Code B drills (30 min). Different content than AM.
Pickleball, soccer tennis, frisbee, badminton. Rotate every 10 min.
Target Smash, Rally Relay, King/Queen of Court. Competition focus.
PM daily awards (separate from AM). Stretch. Group share. Team cheer.
Supervised play until 3:00. Parent conversations. Conversion moments.

jd_schedule.detail: Jog, ladder, shadow swings, split-step. Match-intensity prep.
Curriculum drills. Patterns, serve/return. Station rotation if 6+.
Conditioned points, pattern games, short sets. Pressure playbook.
Round robin or bracket. Score every match. Friday = Weekly Championship.
Performance notes. Tournament encouragement. Enrollment conversation.

awards.rule: By Friday, every camper recognized for something. Awards reflect genuine progress. No manufactured wins.

critical_hour.action: Greet EVERY kid by name at gate. Crouch to eye level. Name tag. Pair with returning camper buddy.
Circle. Name + favorite food/animal. Coach repeats all names. First belonging moment.
Do NOT hand out rackets. Movement games only: relay races, tag, obstacle course. Every kid can run. Every kid succeeds.
Introduce rackets through play: balance ball on strings, bounce-walk, tap-ups. Foam balls. Impossible to fail.
Hand-feed (not racket-feed). Underhand toss, kid hits foam ball over mini-net. Celebrate EVERY contact at 10/10 energy.

skill_groups.label: Never Played
skill_groups.desc: Foam/red balls. Mini-net. Focus on contact/tracking. TopSpinPro station. 80% play, 20% skill.

skill_groups.label: Some Experience
skill_groups.desc: Red/orange balls. 36–60ft court. Rally building, direction games. 60% play, 40% skill.

skill_groups.label: Can Rally
skill_groups.desc: Orange/green balls. Full court. Patterns, competition, Champions format. 50% play, 50% skill.

dropin.pre_arrival: Robert texts coach by 8 PM: '[Name], age [X], dropping in tomorrow [AM/PM/Full]. [Experience level].'
dropin.arrival: Coach greets by name. Assigns buddy (returning weekly camper). Quick 2-min skill read during warm-up. Place into Group A/B/C.
dropin.awards: Daily awards: YES. Weekly awards: NO (need full week). Thursday participation certificate: YES.
dropin.conversion: Pickup script: 'They picked up [skill] fast. We have spots the rest of this week. They'd be perfect for [program] this fall. Can I text you the link?'
dropin.followup: Robert texts within 24 hours: 'Allison mentioned [Child] had a great time. Want to add more days? Free trial for fall [program].'
dropin.capacity: Hard cap per shift. Robert maintains daily roster. Full shift → offer other shift. Never turn away without alternative.

heat.action: Standard schedule. Water breaks every 25–30 min.
Extended water breaks (10 min every 20 min). Add shade time. Mist/sprinkler available.
Reduce on-court to morning only. Afternoon = shade activities, field games on grass, water play.
Cancel outdoor. Covered/shaded area only. Games, crafts, trivia. Early pickup option.

safety.supplies: 5-gal water cooler + ice, cups, first aid kit, SPF 50 sunscreen (parent consent), emergency contact list, emergency granola bars

rules: Kids bring own lunch (packing list in registration email: lunch, 2 snacks, water bottle, sunscreen, hat, change of clothes for water days)
Emergency granola bars if forgotten — call parent
Hand sanitizer before eating, trash bags, leave no trace
If enrollment >6 full-day, request parent volunteer or Robert for noon coverage

conversion.day1: Pickup: '[Child] did amazing! They picked up [skill] fast. Have you thought about fall programs?' Hand out flyer.
conversion.day3: '[Child] is really developing. They'd be perfect for [Orange Ball / Green Dot] this fall. Want me to save a spot?'
conversion.thursday: During awards, publicly recognize growth. After: 'It would be a shame to lose momentum. Fall starts [date].'
conversion.post_camp: Peter texts: 'Allison mentioned [Child] had a great week. [Program] would be a perfect next step. Want a free trial?'

lbhs_team.name: LBHS Boys Tennis

blocks.activity: Dynamic warm-up + mini tennis rallies. Partners assigned by lineup — S1 with D1 team, S2 with D2, S3 with D3. Focus on rhythm, not intensity.
S1–S3 on courts 1–3: serve+1 pattern reps. Coach feeds from ad/deuce side. 10 reps each side. Focus: first-strike weapons. Alternates on courts 4–6: crosscourt rally tolerance to 20.

D1–D3 on courts 1–3: I-formation reps (10 pts), Australian formation (10 pts), standard (10 pts). Signals practice. Coach at net post calling formations. Singles players move to court 4–6 for serve practice.
All players play tiebreaks to 7. Rotate opponents every tiebreak. Track W/L. Creates pressure reps before match day. Coach observes composure and between-point routines.
Conditioned sets: start every game at 15–30 (server down). Forces hold under pressure. Or: first to 4 games, no-ad scoring. Simulate match pressure points.
Stretch circle. Tomorrow's opponent scouting debrief: who plays what, tendencies, game plan per position. Singles players: know your opponent's backhand. Doubles: who poaches, who stays.
Jog, dynamic stretch, mini-tennis. Progressions: cooperative rally → directional → competitive.
Stations: Court 1–2 serve targets (deuce wide, T, body). Court 3–4 return positioning (split-step timing, block vs swing). Court 5–6 serve+return live points. Rotate every 8 min.

Pattern A: CC rally → inside-out FH → finish. Pattern B: BH CC → short ball → approach → volley. Coach feeds. 6 reps per pattern then rotate. Singles focus on execution %, doubles focus on setting up net player.
blocks.court_assign: Courts 1–3: Pattern A, Courts 4–6: Pattern B, swap at 12 min

blocks.activity: Approach + volley + overhead sequence. Coach feeds short, player approaches, volleys, then overhead lob. 8 reps. Doubles pairs: poach drill — server + net player, 10 pts per rotation.
blocks.court_assign: Courts 1–3: Singles approach, Courts 4–6: Doubles poach

blocks.activity: Challenge ladder sets: play 2 tiebreaks against the player ranked above you. Win both = challenge officially posted. Alternates rotate in for practice sets on remaining courts.
blocks.court_assign: Challenge matches on courts 1–4, practice sets on 5–6

blocks.activity: Court sprints: baseline-to-net × 6, sideline-to-sideline × 6. Stretch. Quick debrief: 1 thing each player will work on tomorrow.
Light jog, dynamic stretch, arm circles, leg swings. No hard hitting yet. Get blood flowing.
Lineup partners. Start at service line, cooperative rally. Extend to baseline after 20 balls. Focus: tracking, timing, feet moving.
Crosscourt FH rallies (2 min), crosscourt BH (2 min), DTL FH (2 min), DTL BH (2 min). No winners — build rhythm and confidence.
10 first serves each side (deuce + ad). Then 10 second serves. Then 5 serve+1 points. Doubles teams: 5 returns each.
Circle up. Quick reminder: game plan per position. Reset routine. 'Control the controllables.' Team cheer. Break.
Standard warm-up. Challenger and challenged player do NOT warm up together — separate courts.
Best of 3 sets, no-ad scoring, 7-point tiebreak at 5-5. Official scoring. Coach observes. Remaining team runs practice sets or drills on other courts.
Non-challenge players: pattern drills, doubles reps, or conditioning depending on weekly focus.
Challenge result posted. If rank changes, new lineup posted by next practice. Sportsmanship discussion. No hard feelings — this is how we get better.

challenge_rules: Any player can challenge the player ranked 1 spot above them.
Challenges must be announced at least 1 practice in advance.
Format: Best of 3 sets, no-ad scoring, tiebreak at 5–5.
Winner takes the higher rank. Loser drops to the lower rank.
A player can only be challenged once per week.
Challenges take priority over normal practice when courts allow.
Coach may initiate challenges based on practice performance.
Results are final. No rematches for 2 weeks.
